source.subject: Inflight token backpressure — customer mitigation checklist
messages:
From: James Liu <james.liu@fastpay.com>
To: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 25 May 2027 09:02:00 -0700
Subject: Chat responses are stalling (high latency)

Hi Laura,

We've been seeing elevated end-to-end latency in our customer-facing chat since ~08:45 PDT. The streaming responses are delayed in 10-30s bursts and some sessions are timing out. This started right after we pushed a new conversational flow that increases context length.

Can you confirm if this is on Redwood's side or anything on our config that would cause backpressure? We're in a demo for a major prospect in 90 minutes and need a short mitigation path.

Thanks,
James
---
James Liu
Director of Product
FastPay
From: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>
To: James Liu <james.liu@fastpay.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 25 May 2027 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Chat responses are stalling (high latency)

Hi James — thanks for the flag and the detail. We're seeing correlated latency spikes for a subset of sessions tied to longer context windows and higher token churn. Short checklist to try immediately (low risk):

- Short-term workaround (client-side): reduce recent context sent in the prompt by trimming older messages; target ~2–3k tokens.
- Streaming fallback: switch to non-streaming (batch) responses for long contexts to avoid partial chunking timeouts; this trades latency for completion reliability.
- Retry policy: implement exponential backoff with jitter at the client for streaming disconnects; don't exceed 3 retries in demo.

We have ops running a trace and will send logs. If you want, we can open a 30-minute bridge now — I can join with our runtime engineers.

Attaching a short playbook and will follow up with logs.

Laura
Laura Bennett
Senior Customer Solutions
Redwood Inference
From: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
To: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwood.com>, Stephanie Nguyen <stephanie.nguyen@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 25 May 2027 09:18:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Chat responses are stalling (high latency) — internal triage

Logging summary from the runtime: we observed increased token enqueue time and repeated KV cache misses for a shard serving FastPay's workspace between 08:40–09:05 PDT. Early hypothesis: a burst of longer sequences caused per-request memory pressure and the scheduler dropped to smaller batch sizes, increasing per-token overhead (backpressure effect).

Immediate steps I'm running:
- Pinned affected shard to lower concurrency to stabilize batching (rolling change).
- Dumped handshake + transport logs (handshake-logs-20270525.txt) and a KV cache snapshot for offline analysis.
- Kickstarted a hotfix candidate (scheduler tweak) to restore longer-batch aggregation; will only enable if we validate no regression on other customers.

If the customer needs a short-term mitigation, ask them to trim context and enable the non-streaming path as Laura suggested.

Marissa
Principal Runtime Engineer
Redwood Inference
From: James Liu <james.liu@fastpay.com>
To: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>
Cc: contracts@fastpay.com
Date: Tue, 25 May 2027 09:27:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Chat responses are stalling (high latency)

Laura — appreciate the quick response. We'll trim context for the demo and enable the non-streaming path as a fallback. Please keep me posted; if you can join the bridge in 10 minutes that would help.

Also, any ETA on whether the scheduler tweak is safe to enable for our dedicated pool? We prefer not to toggle mid-demo unless it's low risk.

James
From: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>
To: James Liu <james.liu@fastpay.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwood.com>, Stephanie Nguyen <stephanie.nguyen@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 26 May 2027 14:20:00 -0700
Subject: Post-incident summary: inflight token backpressure and recommended permanent mitigations

Hi James — following up with a brief post-incident summary and recommended next steps. Thanks again for your patience and for taking the quick fallbacks during the demo.

What happened (short):
- Root cause: a sudden increase in long-context requests created uneven batching and frequent KV cache evictions on one runtime shard serving FastPay's dedicated pool. The scheduler fell back to smaller batch sizes which increased per-token overhead and produced high tail latency (inflight token backpressure).
- Impact window: 2027-05-25 08:40–09:05 PDT. Affected ~4% of requests to chat endpoints; increased 95th/99th percentile latency by ~8–28s for those requests.

Actions we took:
- Immediate: recommended context trimming + non-streaming fallback (client) during demo.
- Runtime: pinned the shard to lower concurrency to reduce memory churn and rolled a scheduler tuning to improve aggregation for long sequences.
- Data capture: uploaded handshake logs and a KV cache snapshot (attached) for your team to review.

Recommended next steps for FastPay (permanent mitigations):
1) Shorten rolling context window for interactive flows where possible (keep recent messages prioritized).
2) Implement the client-side fallback to non-streaming for requests > 3k tokens.
3) Add an adaptive retry with exponential backoff and jitter on streaming disconnects.
4) Share a sample production trace with us (we can redact PII) so we can validate scheduler tuning against your exact distribution.

Redwood next steps for FastPay:
- We'll run a canary of the scheduler tweak across a small subset of shards in your dedicated pool and monitor token enqueue time and tail latency. ETA: 48 hours.
- We'll provide a short config snippet for the non-streaming fallback and a trimmed client playbook (client-playbook-v1.pdf attached).
- We'll follow up with a scheduled 30-minute sync to review traces and confirm the canary results (options: Thu 11am PDT or Fri 2pm PDT).

If you want us to perform the canary sooner, we can prioritize, but it'll require a simple rollback plan in case of regressions.

Attachments: handshake-logs-20270525.txt (handshake + transport traces), kvcache-snapshot-20270525.json, client-playbook-v1.pdf

Thanks — I'm available if you want to discuss the technical details on the bridge.

Best,
Laura Bennett
Senior Customer Solutions
Redwood Inference
laura.bennett@redwood.com
+1 (415) 555-0123

source.subject: Coord: Self-booked On-Prem Demo — ops gates & calendar stitch
messages:
From: No Reply Redwood Inference <no-reply@redwood.ai>
To: Sara Matthews <sara.m@wavehealth.com>
Cc: Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.ai>
Date: 2027-05-15T09:12:00-07:00
Subject: Confirmed: Redwood Private Demo — Wed May 19 10:00 PT (self-book)

Hi Sara —

Thanks for self-booking a Private (on-prem/VPC) demo with Redwood Inference. This message confirms the time and includes a short intake checklist so we can make the session productive.

Event: Redwood Private demo (overview + technical run-through)
When: Wed, May 19 · 10:00 — 10:45 AM PT
Location: Google Meet (link in calendar invite) — https://meet.google.com/xxx-xxxx-xxx
Host (SDR): Karthik Iyer (karthik@redwood.ai)
Intended attendees: WaveHealth - Product & IT (please add any extra names)

Please review the attached intake documents and, if possible, complete the short intake form before the session so we can prepare relevant compliance notes and the ops checklist. Attachments: onprem-demo-intake-form.pdf, onprem-checklist.pdf

If you have any compliance gating items (SOC 2, data residency, encryption at rest), reply to this thread and Karthik will loop in our security/ops contacts.

Looking forward to the demo —
Redwood Inference Events

---
Attachment stubs:
- onprem-demo-intake-form.pdf (1 page intake form)
- onprem-checklist.pdf (ops checklist, short)
From: Sara Matthews <sara.m@wavehealth.com>
To: Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.ai>
Cc: Michael Chen <m.chen@wavehealth.com>
Date: 2027-05-15T09:35:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Confirmed: Redwood Private Demo — Wed May 19 10:00 PT (self-book)

Hi Karthik —

Thanks for the confirmation. A couple quick asks before the demo:  
- Can you add our lead infra contact, Raj Patel (raj.patel@wavehealth.com), to the invite? He needs to join the tech pre-call.  
- We require a redacted SOC 2 summary and a short note on data residency (we host in us-east-1). Can you share those ahead of the meeting?
- Also, would it be possible to do a 20-minute tech pre-call with your engineer on the same day at 9:25 AM PT (before the demo)? That would help our infra team triage network/ports quickly.

I completed the intake form and flagged the compliance items. If the pre-call can't be accommodated, please propose an alternate 30-minute window the morning of the 19th.

Thanks —
Sara Matthews
Director, Product
WaveHealth

---
Quoted (original confirmation):
> Event: Redwood Private demo (overview + technical run-through)
> When: Wed, May 19 · 10:00 — 10:45 AM PT
> Host (SDR): Karthik Iyer (karthik@redwood.ai)

From: Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.ai>
To: Stephanie Nguyen <stephanie@redwood.ai>, Sean Gallagher <sean@redwood.ai>
Cc: (none)
Date: 2027-05-15T11:00:00-07:00
Subject: Fwd: Confirmed: Redwood Private Demo — coordination required (internal only)

Team —

Inbound self-book from WaveHealth. Sara asked for:
- add Raj Patel (raj.patel@wavehealth.com) to the invite
- a redacted SOC 2 summary + quick note on data residency (us-east-1)
- a 20-minute tech pre-call at 9:25 AM PT on 5/19 (before the 10:00 demo) — prefer engineer to present and run through network/ports and quick KV-cache/latency expectations for a 1M token/day workload

Attachments I pulled from portal: redwood-soc2-summary-redacted.pdf (attached). Please review and tell me if you need a fuller package or an SOC 2 cert copy for procurement.

Can someone cover the tech pre-call at 9:25? If not, alternatives I can offer Sara: 8:45 — 9:05 PT or 11:15 — 11:35 PT (same day). Also confirm Ops attendance for the 10:00 demo (we usually put long-term eng + solutions).

Quick notes for whoever joins the pre-call:  
- WaveHealth will expect port 443 egress and we should confirm FQDNs and optional IP allowlist (we can provide ranges)
- They want explicit data residency: confirm model footprint and that logs/audit can be routed to their VPC (note: this is a Private demo with potential Private deployment ask)

I've attached the redacted SOC 2 summary for context.

Karthik
SDR (Redwood Inference)

---
Attachment stub:
- redwood-soc2-summary-redacted.pdf (2 pages, redacted control summaries)
From: Stephanie Nguyen <stephanie@redwood.ai>
To: Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.ai>
Cc: Sean Gallagher <sean@redwood.ai>
Date: 2027-05-15T13:02:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Fwd: Confirmed: Redwood Private Demo — coordination required (internal only)

Karthik —

Priya (ops eng) can take the 9:25 AM PT tech pre-call on 5/19 and will join the main 10:00 demo as the ops lead. I've attached a short preflight agenda she can use.

We should also move the demo 15 minutes later (10:15 start) because of an existing infra sync on our side; please confirm with Sara if that works. If Sara prefers to keep 10:00 we can still do it, but Priya's pre-call handoff will be tighter.

I'll update the calendar invite, add Raj (raj.patel@wavehealth.com) and include the SOC2 redacted summary. I will also add the Ops allowlist ranges doc to the invite notes.

Thanks —
Stephanie
Sr. Solutions Ops
Redwood Inference

---
Attachment stub:
- ops_preflight_agenda.docx (1 page agenda)

On 2027-05-15T11:00:00-07:00, Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.ai> wrote:
> Team —
> 
> Inbound self-book from WaveHealth. Sara asked for:
> - add Raj Patel (raj.patel@wavehealth.com) to the invite
> - a redacted SOC 2 summary + quick note on data residency (us-east-1)
> - a 20-minute tech pre-call at 9:25 AM PT on 5/19 (before the 10:00 demo) — prefer engineer to present and run through network/ports and quick KV-cache/latency expectations for a 1M token/day workload
> 
> Can someone cover the tech pre-call at 9:25?
> 
From: Sean Gallagher <sean@redwood.ai>
To: Stephanie Nguyen <stephanie@redwood.ai>, Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.ai>
Date: 2027-05-15T13:05:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Fwd: Confirmed: Redwood Private Demo — coordination required (internal only)

Ack — Priya is good for 9:25 and I've approved the 10:15 shift. I'll attach the allowlist ranges to the calendar now.

-Sean

source.subject: Post-demo legal timeline and redline status — FintechCo
messages:
From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
To: Dana Li <dana.li@fintechco.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwood.ai>
Date: Mon, 10 May 2027 15:08:00 -0700
Subject: Post-demo recap: integration scope & next steps

Hi Dana — great conversation today. Quick recap and proposed next steps so we stay aligned:

- Integration scope: real-time inference (API), prefix caching for historical prompts, and tokenized embeddings for search ranking.
- Success criteria: sub-250ms 95th perc latency on typical queries, < 0.03 USD per 1k tokens estimated post-quantization.
- Risks called out: audit/logging requirements and on-prem enclave for regulated workflows.

Requested next steps:
1) I attached the draft SOW (RW_SOW_v3.pdf) and our current pricing sheet (pricing-sheet-2027Q2.xlsx). Please share with procurement and counsel.
2) Can you confirm who on your side will own the legal redlines and an expected target date for counsel feedback?
3) If helpful, we can schedule a 30-min legal-to-legal call to walk through the key clauses (data retention, audit logs, indemnity).

If procurement has a preferred timeline, call that out and we’ll try to accelerate. I’ll follow up in a few days if I don’t hear back.

Thanks —
Ben Carter
Enterprise AE | Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.ai | +1 415-555-0198

Attachments: RW_SOW_v3.pdf (docx/pdf), pricing-sheet-2027Q2.xlsx (spreadsheet)
From: Claire Morris <claire.morris@fintechco.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
Cc: Dana Li <dana.li@fintechco.com>, procurement@fintechco.com
Date: Wed, 12 May 2027 09:14:00 -0700
Subject: RE: Post-demo recap: integration scope & next steps

Ben — thanks for the recap. We circulated the SOW to counsel and attached their initial redlines for your review (FintechCo_Redlines_v1.pdf). Summary of the principal items counsel flagged:

- Data retention: request for fixed retention window and stronger controls for deletion (they want a 90-day minimum retention clause changed).
- Audit logs & access: require audit trail of model inputs/outputs for regulated accounts and a list of fields that must be redacted.
- Indemnity & liability cap: asking to narrow Redwood’s indemnity for third-party IP, and proposing a mutual cap tied to annual spend.

Procurement is aiming to complete legal review by end of next week (week of May 17) but that may slip depending on the number of redlines. If Redwood could propose alternative language for the retention clause and provide a short justification for the indemnity position, that would speed things up.

Forwarded message:
> On Mon, 10 May 2027 at 15:08 Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai> wrote:
> > Hi Dana — great conversation today. Quick recap and proposed next steps so we stay aligned:
> > ...

Thanks —
Claire Morris
Senior Counsel, FintechCo
claire.morris@fintechco.com

Attachment: FintechCo_Redlines_v1.pdf (pdf, 12 pages)
From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwood.ai>
Date: Wed, 12 May 2027 09:42:00 -0700
Subject: FW: Post-demo recap: integration scope & next steps

Marissa / Sean — forwarding FintechCo counsel’s redlines we just received. Key asks are retention, audit logging, and indemnity cap. My read: negotiable on retention/logging with concrete language; indemnity will be stickier.

Proposed internal ask:
- Can legal approve our alternative language for retention (90-day retention with customer-controlled purge + audit log scoping)? We already have template language from other regulated customers I can share in a 1-pager.
- Will finance/signing approve a limited concession on liability cap (to mutual cap tied to committed annual spend) if we include a 6-month pilot discount to make the economics palatable?

I attached our current SOW and pricing for context. If you want, I can jump on a call with counsel to walk through the items — FintechCo seemed open to a quick redline-sync meeting.

– Ben
Ben Carter
Enterprise AE | Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.ai

Attachments: RW_SOW_v3.pdf, pricing-sheet-2027Q2.xlsx

Quoted from Claire Morris:
> We circulated the SOW to counsel and attached their initial redlines for your review (FintechCo_Redlines_v1.pdf). Summary of the principal items counsel flagged:
> - Data retention: request for fixed retention window and stronger controls for deletion
> - Audit logs & access: require audit trail of model inputs/outputs for regulated accounts
> - Indemnity & liability cap: asking to narrow Redwood’s indemnity for third-party IP
From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
To: Dana Li <dana.li@fintechco.com>, Claire Morris <claire.morris@fintechco.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>
Date: Fri, 14 May 2027 08:22:00 -0700
Subject: Checking timeline on counsel redlines — quick sync?

Hi Dana / Claire — wanted to check in on redline timing and offer to help move this to a quick resolution. A couple quick notes:

- Our legal team drafted alternate language for the data-retention and audit-log items that keeps you within your compliance needs while preserving our operational guarantees. I can send the two-paragraph proposal if that helps.
- We're happy to set up a 30-minute call with our counsel and yours to run through the three principal asks — retention, audit, indemnity — and land on actionable edits.

Proposed times (PT): Mon 5/17 10:00–10:30, Tue 5/18 14:00–14:30. Tell me what works or suggest a slot that works better for counsel.

Also: can you confirm whether procurement is still targeting the week of May 24 for final approval? If so, we can escalate internally to prioritize the legal review.

Short follow-up — if I don’t hear back I’ll try a one-line nudge next Wednesday.

Appreciate it —
Ben Carter
Enterprise AE | Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.ai | +1 415-555-0198

Attachments referenced earlier: FintechCo_Redlines_v1.pdf

source.subject: Residency matrix and delete-hooks proposal
messages:
From: Amit Mehra <amit@wavepayments.com>
To: Sean Gallagher <sean@redwood.com>
Date: 2027-05-09T09:12:00-07:00
Subject: Can Redwood support per-tenant retention + regional deletes?

Hi Sean,

Quick intro — I'm Amit (Product Ops) at WavePayments. We're evaluating Redwood for a payments-backed fraud product. Two compliance asks came up from Legal / Ops: 1) ability to set per-tenant retention TTLs (e.g., 30d/180d/indefinite) that drive both stored logs and embeddings, and 2) ability to force regional deletion (WIth proof) when a customer chooses EU-only residency — including an audit trail and a way to provide deletion certificates.

A few concrete questions:
- Can retention be configured per-tenant or per-API-key today?
- When a delete request is issued, do you provide a machine-readable certificate (time-stamped) showing which objects were removed and from which regions?
- How do you handle mixed-residency tenants (some subsets of data must stay in EU, others in US)?

If you have a short matrix or checklist we can fill, that would speed internal approvals. Happy to hop on a 30-minute call.

Thanks,
Amit
Product Ops, WavePayments
From: Sean Gallagher <sean@redwood.com>
To: Amit Mehra <amit@wavepayments.com>
Cc: Lauren Bishop <lauren@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.com>
Date: 2027-05-09T11:08:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Can Redwood support per-tenant retention + regional deletes?

Hi Amit — thanks for the clear questions. Short answers up front:
- Per-tenant TTL: not currently exposed as a self-serve admin control, but we have the runtime hooks and storage tagging to enable it.
- Deletion certificates / audit trail: we emit audit logs and can produce time-stamped events; we don't yet produce a formal cert in the UI but can generate a signed report on request.
- Mixed-residency: supported via tenant-level routing policies; need to confirm details for partial-object residency (e.g., embeddings stored in region X, logs in region Y).

I'll loop in Product and Eng to draft a short mutual-action plan and a simple matrix you can fill. Lauren / Karthik — can you help with an initial ask list and a rough implementation approach?

Sean Gallagher
Head of Sales, Redwood Inference
sean@redwood.com
Redwood Inference
From: Lauren Bishop <lauren@redwood.com>
To: Sean Gallagher <sean@redwood.com>, Amit Mehra <amit@wavepayments.com>
Cc: Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.com>, Evelyn Hart <evelyn@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>
Date: 2027-05-09T14:20:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Can Redwood support per-tenant retention + regional deletes?

Thanks Sean / Amit. From a product standpoint here are the minimum asks we'd expect for an enterprise customer to sign off:

- Configurable retention policy surface (admin UI + API): per-tenant default, per-route override, and per-asset-type granularity (raw requests, embeddings, audit logs).
- Residency matrix capture: a simple spreadsheet that maps data class -> allowed regions -> required controls (KMS residency, isolation).
- Delete workflow: synchronous API to request deletion, async worker to purge objects, and a signed deletion report with object ids/timestamps/regions.
- Proof of deletion: append-only audit log entries and an HMAC-signed manifest (or signed PDF) that legal can store.
- RBAC for retention changes and an approval flow for permanent deletion.

Operational notes / questions for Eng:
- Are there storage-level tags today that we can surface as 'region=eu/us' so deletes can be scoped?
- How hard is it to add a signed-manifest generator and retention-enforcement worker?

I'll attach a draft 'residency matrix' for Amit to fill and a short checklist for Legal.

Lauren Bishop
VP, Product
Redwood Inference

Attachment: residency-matrix-v1.xlsx (spreadsheet)
From: Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.com>
To: Lauren Bishop <lauren@redwood.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean@redwood.com>, Amit Mehra <amit@wavepayments.com>
Cc: Evelyn Hart <evelyn@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>
Date: 2027-05-10T10:35:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Can Redwood support per-tenant retention + regional deletes?

Taking an engineering pass on the asks — high level plan below (phase-based):

Phase 1 (MVP, Q3):
- Storage tagging: expose existing region/storage-class tags as metadata on objects (no migration).
- Retention policy layer: add a per-tenant metadata bucket that resolves effective TTL (tenant -> route -> asset type). Policies evaluated at write time and enforcement via TTL index + background purge worker.
- Delete API: endpoint that enqueues deletion jobs; jobs return a job id that can be polled.
- Audit events: emit structured audit events to our logging pipeline (timestamp, object id, region, action).

Phase 2 (Q4+):
- Signed deletion manifest generator (HSM/KMS-backed signature), PDF export for legal, and optional webhook to push manifest to customer-controlled endpoint.
- Cross-region consistency: coordinated purge for partially replicated objects — will need a two-phase delete with verification.
- Admin UI: simple page to set tenant defaults and view deletion job history plus exports.

Technical risks / notes:
- Large historical backfills will be a heavy IO task; recommend a scoped migration approach and rate-limited workers.
- Embeddings: if users have cached vectors in third-party stores, we can only delete what we control — we should document limitations.
- Signed manifests: requires additional KMS access patterns; might need compliance review for key custody.

I've attached a short design doc with the delete-hooks proposal. We can scope this to WavePayments' needs (e.g., mandatory signed manifest + EU-only deletion) if they want priority.

Karthik Iyer
Director, Engineering — Runtime
Redwood Inference

Proposed next steps / mutual action plan:

- Redwood delivers Phase 1 MVP scoped to WavePayments (target: Q3 2027) — includes per-tenant TTLs, delete API, and audit event exports.
- If you require signed manifests / PDF certificates, we scope Phase 2 and aim for late-Q4.

source.subject: URGENT: Stratos Health production queue thrash causing 504s — exec escalation
messages:
From: Michael Price <michael.price@stratoshealth.com>
To: Hannah Schmitt <hannah.schmitt@redwood.com>
Cc: Stratos Health SRE <oncall@stratoshealth.com>
Date: 2027-05-09T08:12:03-07:00
Subject: Re: Intermittent 504s impacting API latency

Hannah —

We started seeing a spike in 504s and 429s around 07:40 PT this morning. Our product team reports timeouts for POST /v1/generate calls and downstream queues filling up. Token success rate dropped to ~72% for the last 30min. We're seeing lots of client retries which is amplifying the issue. Can you confirm if anything changed on your side? This is impacting our triage dashboard and several active users right now.

Attached is a screenshot of our dashboard and a short export of recent request IDs.

—Michael
Michael Price
VP Product, Stratos Health
From: Hannah Schmitt <hannah.schmitt@redwood.com>
To: Michael Price <michael.price@stratoshealth.com>
Cc: Markus Klein <markus.klein@redwood.com>, Priyom Das <priyom.das@redwood.com>
Date: 2027-05-09T08:28:17-07:00
Subject: Re: Intermittent 504s impacting API latency

Michael — thank you for the alert and the snapshots. I'm escalating internally now.

Quick notes: our global telemetry shows a localized latency spike on the US-west serving pool that correlates with heavy prefix-cache invalidations for Stratos' model variant. Initial hypothesis is a batching/KV-cache thrash that degraded tail latency and caused queue saturation + 504s.

I've looped in Markus (runtime lead) and Priyom (SRE) to confirm. We'll share an action plan in the next 20 minutes.

Hannah Schmitt
Senior Account Manager, Redwood Inference
From: Markus Klein <markus.klein@redwood.com>
To: Hannah Schmitt <hannah.schmitt@redwood.com>, Priyom Das <priyom.das@redwood.com>
Cc: Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwood.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwood.com>
Date: 2027-05-09T08:40:44-07:00
Subject: Re: Intermittent 504s impacting API latency

Hannah / Priyom — investigating now. Observed: a sudden increase in prefix cache invalidations for customer namespace stratos-prod-01 starting ~07:36 PT. Invalidations triggered repeated full-KV cache reloads on worker pods, causing blocking I/O and longer token generation times. Batching windows expanded (we saw batch sizes drop then balloon unpredictably) leading to head-of-line blocking and queue timeouts.

Immediate mitigation I will try:
- Disable prefix-based KV invalidation for stratos-prod-01 (flip to conservative TTL mode)
- Temporarily reduce max batch size to lower queue occupancy
- Scale up 2x serving replicas in US-west for the affected model variant

I will run the TTL flip first (low-risk) and report back with metrics. If that doesn't help in 10min, we escalate and perform rolling pod restarts after draining.

Attachments: kv-cache-heap-snapshot.txt (small heap diff)

—Markus
Markus Klein
Principal Engineer, Serving Runtime
From: Priyom Das <priyom.das@redwood.com>
To: Markus Klein <markus.klein@redwood.com>, Hannah Schmitt <hannah.schmitt@redwood.com>
Cc: Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwood.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwood.com>
Date: 2027-05-09T08:50:12-07:00
Subject: Re: Intermittent 504s impacting API latency

Markus — I flipped stratos-prod-01 to conservative TTL mode at 07:52 PT and lowered max batch size from 128 -> 48 across the US-west pool at 07:54 PT. Also provisioned two standby replicas (scaled to +2).

Preliminary telemetry: error rate dropped from 28% 5xx/429 to ~8% within 6 minutes; p99 latency reduced from 3.9s to 1.1s. However, we're still seeing periodic 504 blips tied to a small set of request IDs (attached runbook and partial logs).

I recommend:
- Keep conservative TTL and reduced batch size for 2 hours while we observe
- Collect a full heap/profile and root-cause the invalidation pattern (likely a bad prefix-churn from Stratos' client SDK)

—Priyom
Priyom Das
SRE, Redwood Inference
From: Hannah Schmitt <hannah.schmitt@redwood.com>
To: Michael Price <michael.price@stratoshealth.com>
Cc: Emily Chen <emily.chen@stratoshealth.com>, Markus Klein <markus.klein@redwood.com>, Priyom Das <priyom.das@redwood.com>
Date: 2027-05-09T09:05:30-07:00
Subject: Re: Intermittent 504s impacting API latency

Michael / Emily — update: our team has applied a temporary mitigation (conservative KV TTL + lower batching) and scaled the US-west pool. You're seeing improvement in error rate and latency; we will maintain the change while we finish a root cause analysis.

Next steps for Stratos: can your SDK/ingestion team share any recent client rollout that might cause high prefix churn (e.g., per-request ad-hoc prefixes or metadata variants)? Also please avoid aggressive retry loops for 10 minutes while we stabilize — exponential backoff will help reduce the retry storm.

We will prepare an executive summary for your leadership with timeline, impact, and remediation plan. I'm also drafting a credit/SLR proposal to make this right.

Best,
Hannah Schmitt
Senior Account Manager, Redwood Inference
From: Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwood.com>
To: Hannah Schmitt <hannah.schmitt@redwood.com>, Markus Klein <markus.klein@redwood.com>, Priyom Das <priyom.das@redwood.com>
Cc: Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwood.com>, Michael Price <michael.price@stratoshealth.com>
Date: 2027-05-10T14:42:11-07:00
Subject: Re: URGENT: Stratos Health production queue thrash causing 504s — exec escalation

Folks — timely recap and exec-facing summary ready. See attached mitigation-runbook-v1.md and short incident timeline. Proposed customer-facing remediation and follow-up:

1) Postmortem within 72 hours with root cause (KV-cache invalidation algorithm mis-triggered by high cardinality prefixes).
2) Fix: add rate-limiter on prefix invalidations + safe debounce window in the runtime.
3) Product item: add per-customer invalidation throttles and observability for prefix churn.

Proposed customer goodwill: 20% credit on May usage or one month of Dedicated trial applied to Stratos' account (whichever they prefer).

Michael — I can join an exec call with your leadership tomorrow at 9:00 PT to walk through the timeline and the plan.

—Lauren
Lauren Bishop
Director, Customer Success, Redwood Inference

Date: 2027-05-09T08:12:03-07:00
Subject: Re: Intermittent 504s impacting API latency

Hannah —

We started seeing a spike in 504s and 429s around 07:40 PT this morning. Our product team reports timeouts for POST /v1/generate calls and downstream queues filling up. Token success rate dropped to ~72% for the last 30min. We're seeing lots of client retries which is amplifying the issue. Can you confirm if anything changed on your side? This is impacting our triage dashboard and several active users right now.

Attached is a screenshot of our dashboard and a short export of recent request IDs.

—Michael
Michael Price
VP Product, Stratos Health


From: Hannah Schmitt <hannah.schmitt@redwood.com>
To: Michael Price <michael.price@stratoshealth.com>
Cc: Markus Klein <markus.klein@redwood.com>, Priyom Das <priyom.das@redwood.com>
Date: 2027-05-09T08:28:17-07:00
Subject: Re: Intermittent 504s impacting API latency

Michael — thank you for the alert and the snapshots. I'm escalating internally now.

Quick notes: our global telemetry shows a localized latency spike on the US-west serving pool that correlates with heavy prefix-cache invalidations for Stratos' model variant. Initial hypothesis is a batching/KV-cache thrash that degraded tail latency and caused queue saturation + 504s.

I've looped in Markus (runtime lead) and Priyom (SRE) to confirm. We'll share an action plan in the next 20 minutes.

Hannah Schmitt
Senior Account Manager, Redwood Inference


From: Markus Klein <markus.klein@redwood.com>
To: Hannah Schmitt <hannah.schmitt@redwood.com>, Priyom Das <priyom.das@redwood.com>
Cc: Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwood.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwood.com>
Date: 2027-05-09T08:40:44-07:00
Subject: Re: Intermittent 504s impacting API latency

Hannah / Priyom — investigating now. Observed: a sudden increase in prefix cache invalidations for customer namespace stratos-prod-01 starting ~07:36 PT. Invalidations triggered repeated full-KV cache reloads on worker pods, causing blocking I/O and longer token generation times. Batching windows expanded (we saw batch sizes drop then balloon unpredictably) leading to head-of-line blocking and queue timeouts.

Immediate mitigation I will try:
- Disable prefix-based KV invalidation for stratos-prod-01 (flip to conservative TTL mode)
- Temporarily reduce max batch size to lower queue occupancy
- Scale up 2x serving replicas in US-west for the affected model variant

I will run the TTL flip first (low-risk) and report back with metrics. If that doesn't help in 10min, we escalate and perform rolling pod restarts after draining.

Attachments: kv-cache-heap-snapshot.txt (small heap diff)

—Markus
Markus Klein
Principal Engineer, Serving Runtime


From: Priyom Das <priyom.das@redwood.com>
To: Markus Klein <markus.klein@redwood.com>, Hannah Schmitt <hannah.schmitt@redwood.com>
Cc: Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwood.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwood.com>
Date: 2027-05-09T08:50:12-07:00
Subject: Re: Intermittent 504s impacting API latency

Markus — I flipped stratos-prod-01 to conservative TTL mode at 07:52 PT and lowered max batch size from 128 -> 48 across the US-west pool at 07:54 PT. Also provisioned two standby replicas (scaled to +2).

Preliminary telemetry: error rate dropped from 28% 5xx/429 to ~8% within 6 minutes; p99 latency reduced from 3.9s to 1.1s. However, we're still seeing periodic 504 blips tied to a small set of request IDs (attached runbook and partial logs).

I recommend:
- Keep conservative TTL and reduced batch size for 2 hours while we observe
- Collect a full heap/profile and root-cause the invalidation pattern (likely a bad prefix-churn from Stratos' client SDK)

—Priyom
Priyom Das
SRE, Redwood Inference


From: Hannah Schmitt <hannah.schmitt@redwood.com>
To: Michael Price <michael.price@stratoshealth.com>
Cc: Emily Chen <emily.chen@stratoshealth.com>, Markus Klein <markus.klein@redwood.com>, Priyom Das <priyom.das@redwood.com>
Date: 2027-05-09T09:05:30-07:00
Subject: Re: Intermittent 504s impacting API latency

Michael / Emily — update: our team has applied a temporary mitigation (conservative KV TTL + lower batching) and scaled the US-west pool. You're seeing improvement in error rate and latency; we will maintain the change while we finish a root cause analysis.

Next steps for Stratos: can your SDK/ingestion team share any recent client rollout that might cause high prefix churn (e.g., per-request ad-hoc prefixes or metadata variants)? Also please avoid aggressive retry loops for 10 minutes while we stabilize — exponential backoff will help reduce the retry storm.

We will prepare an executive summary for your leadership with timeline, impact, and remediation plan. I'm also drafting a credit/SLR proposal to make this right.

Best,
Hannah Schmitt
Senior Account Manager, Redwood Inference


From: Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwood.com>
To: Hannah Schmitt <hannah.schmitt@redwood.com>, Markus Klein <markus.klein@redwood.com>, Priyom Das <priyom.das@redwood.com>
Cc: Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwood.com>, Michael Price <michael.price@stratoshealth.com>
Date: 2027-05-10T14:42:11-07:00
Subject: Re: URGENT: Stratos Health production queue thrash causing 504s — exec escalation

Folks — timely recap and exec-facing summary ready. See attached mitigation-runbook-v1.md and short incident timeline. Proposed customer-facing remediation and follow-up:

1) Postmortem within 72 hours with root cause (KV-cache invalidation algorithm mis-triggered by high cardinality prefixes).
2) Fix: add rate-limiter on prefix invalidations + safe debounce window in the runtime.
3) Product item: add per-customer invalidation throttles and observability for prefix churn.

Proposed customer goodwill: 20% credit on May usage or one month of Dedicated trial applied to Stratos' account (whichever they prefer).

Michael — I can join an exec call with your leadership tomorrow at 9:00 PT to walk through the timeline and the plan.

—Lauren
Lauren Bishop
Director, Customer Success, Redwood Inference

source.subject: Alteon pilot — request: temporary uptime threshold and commercial concession
messages:
From: Michael Yuan <michael.yuan@alteon.com>
To: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>
Cc: Hannah Schmitt <hannah.schmitt@redwood.com>, procurement@alteon.com
Date: Mon, 3 May 2027 10:15:00 -0700
Subject: Pilot request: adjusted uptime target + one-time credit

Hi Laura (and Hannah),

Thanks for the demo last week — team was impressed with the throughput. We’re preparing an internal procurement packet for a 3-month pilot of Redwood’s hosted API. Two asks came up from procurement and security that I want to surface early:

1) Uptime: For the pilot we need the uptime target relaxed to 99.5% (monthly) rather than the 99.9% in standard terms. This is a temporary tolerance while we validate ingestion patterns.
2) Commercial concession: Procurement is asking for a one-time service credit or discount to offset onboarding risk. Ballpark request is $10k or equivalent early-bird pricing on the first paid term.

I attached the procurement spec (procurement_spec.pdf). Can you confirm whether Redwood can accommodate a temporary SLA threshold and a one-time commercial concession for a pilot? If so, we’ll prep internal approval and sign as soon as legal has the amended clause.

Thanks — Michael
Michael Yuan
Head of AI Platform, Alteon Systems
From: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>
To: Michael Yuan <michael.yuan@alteon.com>
Cc: Hannah Schmitt <hannah.schmitt@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 3 May 2027 15:05:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Pilot request: adjusted uptime target + one-time credit

Michael —

Appreciate the clarity and the procurement spec. Short answer: we can consider a temporary uptime tolerance for a limited pilot window and a one-time commercial concession, but we need Deal Desk + finance + an exec signoff for non-standard SLA language or credit amounts.

Proposed next steps from our side:
- I’ll draft an SLA amendment that sets pilot uptime to 99.5% for a 90-day pilot and defines proportional service credit calculation (cap at one month’s invoiced value). Draft will include telemetry obligations and the data retention limits you requested. (see attached sla-amendment-draft.docx)
- We need a short mutual action plan: pilot start date, success criteria, and whether you’ll commit to a 12-month reserved capacity at pilot completion (that affects commercial concessions).
- I’ll escalate to Hannah, Marissa, Sean and Ben for approval on the credit amount.

Can you confirm the pilot start target and whether Alteon is willing to discuss a 12-month commitment if the pilot is successful?

Best,
Laura
Laura Bennett
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
From: Hannah Schmitt <hannah.schmitt@redwood.com>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 4 May 2027 09:45:00 -0700
Subject: Escalation: Alteon pilot — approval needed for temporary uptime & commercial concession

Team —

Looping you in on Alteon’s pilot request (thread below). Highlights:
- Customer asks for 99.5% uptime for a 90-day pilot instead of our standard 99.9%.
- They’ve requested a one-time concession ~ $10k to offset onboarding risk.
- Potential upside: if pilot goes well, they may convert to a 12-month reserved commitment.

Recommendation (risk-balanced):
1) Approve a temporary SLA threshold of 99.5% for exactly 90 days. Include clear telemetry and a go/no-go review at day 75.
2) Offer a one-time service credit of $7,500 (applied once against first paid invoice) rather than the full $10k request.
3) Add a commercial incentive: if they sign a 12-month reserved contract within 30 days of pilot end, extend a 20% discount on first renewal term.
4) Add a hard cap on cumulative credits for this account (no more than one one-time credit per contract year).

Financial impact: preliminary estimate in attached financial-impact.xlsx — the one-time credit is immaterial for a mid-size reserved commitment but reduces churn risk.

Please respond with Approve / Approve with changes / Decline by end of day tomorrow so we can get legal and Deal Desk to finalize the SLA amendment and DocuSign.

Thanks — Hannah
Hannah Schmitt
Head of Sales Operations, Redwood Inference
From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Hannah Schmitt <hannah.schmitt@redwood.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 4 May 2027 12:10:00 -0700
Subject: RE: Escalation: Alteon pilot — approval needed for temporary uptime & commercial concession

Hannah —

I’m comfortable with the recommendation with the following commercial guardrails:
- Approve one-time credit of $7,500 applied to the first paid invoice.
- Require a convertible commitment clause: if they do not sign at least a 12-month reserved agreement within 60 days of pilot completion, Redwood reserves the right to revoke the renewal discount.
- Add a cumulative-credit cap: total credits against the account cannot exceed 10% of ARR tied to any subsequent committed term.

Please mirror these conditions in the SLA amendment. I’ve attached a short summary for Deal Desk (credit_summary.pdf).

Ben
Ben Carter
Director, Revenue Finance
From: Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwood.com>
To: Michael Yuan <michael.yuan@alteon.com>
Cc: Hannah Schmitt <hannah.schmitt@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, procurement@alteon.com
Date: Wed, 5 May 2027 08:30:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Pilot request: adjusted uptime target + one-time credit

Hi Michael —

Thanks for the clear ask and for working with Laura on the details. We’re prepared to proceed with the following, subject to the SLA amendment and legal review:

- Temporary uptime threshold: 99.5% (monthly) for a 90-day pilot window.
- One-time service credit: $7,500 applied to the first paid invoice.
- Conditional renewal incentive: 20% discount on the first renewal term if Alteon signs a 12-month reserved capacity agreement within 60 days of pilot completion.
- Limits: cumulative credits capped per Ben’s note; telemetry and an agreed go/no-go review at day 75.

Laura will send a DocuSign for the SLA amendment once legal integrates the convertible commitment language and the credit cap. Please confirm pilot start target (earliest you can begin ingestion) and we’ll aim to have the amendment routed for signature within 48 hours.

Best regards,
Sean Gallagher
VP, Commercial and Partnerships

1) Uptime: For the pilot we need the uptime target relaxed to 99.5% (monthly) rather than the 99.9% in standard terms. This is a temporary tolerance while we validate ingestion patterns.
2) Commercial concession: Procurement is asking for a one-time service credit or discount to offset onboarding risk. Ballpark request is $10k or equivalent early-bird pricing on the first paid term.

I attached the procurement spec (procurement_spec.pdf). Can you confirm whether Redwood can accommodate a temporary SLA threshold and a one-time commercial concession for a pilot? If so, we’ll prep internal approval and sign as soon as legal has the amended clause.

Thanks — Michael
Michael Yuan
Head of AI Platform, Alteon Systems


From: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>
To: Michael Yuan <michael.yuan@alteon.com>
Cc: Hannah Schmitt <hannah.schmitt@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 3 May 2027 15:05:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Pilot request: adjusted uptime target + one-time credit

Michael —

Appreciate the clarity and the procurement spec. Short answer: we can consider a temporary uptime tolerance for a limited pilot window and a one-time commercial concession, but we need Deal Desk + finance + an exec signoff for non-standard SLA language or credit amounts.

Proposed next steps from our side:
- I’ll draft an SLA amendment that sets pilot uptime to 99.5% for a 90-day pilot and defines proportional service credit calculation (cap at one month’s invoiced value). Draft will include telemetry obligations and the data retention limits you requested. (see attached sla-amendment-draft.docx)
- We need a short mutual action plan: pilot start date, success criteria, and whether you’ll commit to a 12-month reserved capacity at pilot completion (that affects commercial concessions).
- I’ll escalate to Hannah, Marissa, Sean and Ben for approval on the credit amount.

Can you confirm the pilot start target and whether Alteon is willing to discuss a 12-month commitment if the pilot is successful?

Best,
Laura
Laura Bennett
Account Executive, Redwood Inference


From: Hannah Schmitt <hannah.schmitt@redwood.com>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 4 May 2027 09:45:00 -0700
Subject: Escalation: Alteon pilot — approval needed for temporary uptime & commercial concession

Team —

Looping you in on Alteon’s pilot request (thread below). Highlights:
- Customer asks for 99.5% uptime for a 90-day pilot instead of our standard 99.9%.
- They’ve requested a one-time concession ~ $10k to offset onboarding risk.
- Potential upside: if pilot goes well, they may convert to a 12-month reserved commitment.

Recommendation (risk-balanced):
1) Approve a temporary SLA threshold of 99.5% for exactly 90 days. Include clear telemetry and a go/no-go review at day 75.
2) Offer a one-time service credit of $7,500 (applied once against first paid invoice) rather than the full $10k request.
3) Add a commercial incentive: if they sign a 12-month reserved contract within 30 days of pilot end, extend a 20% discount on first renewal term.
4) Add a hard cap on cumulative credits for this account (no more than one one-time credit per contract year).

Financial impact: preliminary estimate in attached financial-impact.xlsx — the one-time credit is immaterial for a mid-size reserved commitment but reduces churn risk.

Please respond with Approve / Approve with changes / Decline by end of day tomorrow so we can get legal and Deal Desk to finalize the SLA amendment and DocuSign.

Thanks — Hannah
Hannah Schmitt
Head of Sales Operations, Redwood Inference


From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Hannah Schmitt <hannah.schmitt@redwood.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 4 May 2027 12:10:00 -0700
Subject: RE: Escalation: Alteon pilot — approval needed for temporary uptime & commercial concession

Hannah —

I’m comfortable with the recommendation with the following commercial guardrails:
- Approve one-time credit of $7,500 applied to the first paid invoice.
- Require a convertible commitment clause: if they do not sign at least a 12-month reserved agreement within 60 days of pilot completion, Redwood reserves the right to revoke the renewal discount.
- Add a cumulative-credit cap: total credits against the account cannot exceed 10% of ARR tied to any subsequent committed term.

Please mirror these conditions in the SLA amendment. I’ve attached a short summary for Deal Desk (credit_summary.pdf).

Ben
Ben Carter
Director, Revenue Finance


From: Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwood.com>
To: Michael Yuan <michael.yuan@alteon.com>
Cc: Hannah Schmitt <hannah.schmitt@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, procurement@alteon.com
Date: Wed, 5 May 2027 08:30:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Pilot request: adjusted uptime target + one-time credit

Hi Michael —

Thanks for the clear ask and for working with Laura on the details. We’re prepared to proceed with the following, subject to the SLA amendment and legal review:

- Temporary uptime threshold: 99.5% (monthly) for a 90-day pilot window.
- One-time service credit: $7,500 applied to the first paid invoice.
- Conditional renewal incentive: 20% discount on the first renewal term if Alteon signs a 12-month reserved capacity agreement within 60 days of pilot completion.
- Limits: cumulative credits capped per Ben’s note; telemetry and an agreed go/no-go review at day 75.

Laura will send a DocuSign for the SLA amendment once legal integrates the convertible commitment language and the credit cap. Please confirm pilot start target (earliest you can begin ingestion) and we’ll aim to have the amendment routed for signature within 48 hours.

Best regards,
Sean Gallagher
VP, Commercial and Partnerships

>
> Thanks for the demo last week — team was impressed with the throughput. We’re preparing an internal procurement packet for a 3-month pilot of Redwood’s hosted API. Two asks came up from procurement and security that I want to surface early:
>
> 1) Uptime: For the pilot we need the uptime target relaxed to 99.5% (monthly) rather than the 99.9% in standard terms. This is a temporary tolerance while we validate ingestion patterns.
> 2) Commercial concession: Procurement is asking for a one-time service credit or discount to offset onboarding risk. Ballpark request is $10k or equivalent early-bird pricing on the first paid term.
>
> I attached the procurement spec (procurement_spec.pdf). Can you confirm whether Redwood can accommodate a temporary SLA threshold and a one-time commercial concession for a pilot? If so, we’ll prep internal approval and sign as soon as legal has the amended clause.

source.subject: Post-call: prompt pack request + recommended model targets for evaluation
messages:
From: Danielle Price <danielle@foresightlabs.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai>
Cc: Ravi Shah <ravi.shah@foresightlabs.com>, Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwood.ai>
Date: Thu, 22 Apr 2027 15:07:00 -0700
Subject: Fwd: Quick follow-up + sample prompts from today's call

Thanks again for the demo today — appreciate the concise walkthrough. Attaching a small set of customer prompts we captured during research (foresight_sample_prompts.csv). High level follow-up items for us:

- We're primarily evaluating model fit for: customer support summarization, policy classification (safety flags), and a short answer bot for internal docs.
- Our rough SLA target: P95 latency under 400ms for single-turn short answers; for streaming summaries we care about tail latency and throughput for batch processing.
- Data sensitivity: some prompts include PII-like strings (emails, order IDs). We need guidance on redaction and how Redwood suggests collecting examples safely.

Could you share recommended model targets for an evaluation (balanced cost vs quality), a link to any relevant benchmark pages, and a short list of the exact prompt/edge-case examples you want from us?

Also looping procurement on this thread (procurement@foresightlabs.com) — they asked for a checklist for security/compliance artifacts we should expect during POC.

Attachments: foresight_sample_prompts.csv (CSV, 42 rows)

Best,
Danielle
Director of Product
Foresight Labs

From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai>
To: Danielle Price <danielle@foresightlabs.com>
Cc: Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwood.ai>, Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.ai>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwood.ai>
Date: Thu, 22 Apr 2027 16:12:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Fwd: Quick follow-up + sample prompts from today's call

Danielle — thanks for sending these over and for the clear priorities. Quick recap + next steps from our side: 

Summary of what we recommend for your POC evaluation:

1) Model targets (recommended starting set):
   - redwood-13b-mixq (balanced quality/cost) — good baseline for summarization and short answers
   - redwood-3b-q4 (cost-optimized) — run on representative high-volume routes for throughput checks
   - meta-70b-std (high-quality reference) — use small sample slices for fidelity checks only (not production throughput)

2) Benchmarks and links:
   - Redwood benchmark dashboard (throughput/latency): https://console.redwood.ai/benchmarks/throughput/bench-2027-Q1
   - Example eval harness we recommend (prompt-based): https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1ForesightPromptPack

3) Prompt / edge-case collection guidance we need from you:
   - ~200 representative prompts per use case (support summary, policy classification, short answer). Split by typical/edge-case/intent-ambiguous examples.
   - Redaction: replace PII tokens with <REDACTED:EMAIL> / <REDACTED:ORDER_ID> and include an additional column with a boolean 'contains_pii' flag.
   - Annotate desired output for a ~25% sample so we can run automatic regression checks.

4) Quality/regression concerns to watch for during the POC:
   - Instruction-following drift after few-shot prompts — we'll run a 'control prompt' suite weekly to detect regressions.
   - Hallucination risk on internal doc short answers — include golden responses for a subset of 100 question-answer pairs.

5) Logistics & timeline:
   - If you can deliver the prompt pack and redaction flags by Tue 04/27, we can load into our eval harness and provide a preliminary model comparison by Fri 04/30.
   - Procurement checklist (initial): SOC2 report, data retention policy, schema for logs. I'll attach a short prep checklist.

Attachment: ava_prep_checklist.pdf (one-page POC intake checklist)

I'm cc'ing Aditya and Monica — Aditya will add the redaction guideline template; Monica will coordinate the eval harness run once we receive the pack.

Would you prefer the prompt pack uploaded to the Drive folder you shared, or would you like us to create a secure upload link?

Best,
Ava Chen
Principal Solutions Engineer | Redwood Inference

From: Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwood.ai>
To: Danielle Price <danielle@foresightlabs.com>, Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai>
Cc: Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.ai>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwood.ai>
Date: Thu, 22 Apr 2027 17:05:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Post-call: prompt pack request + recommended model targets for evaluation

Quick redaction and labeling guidance from my side — attaching a minimal template (redaction_guidelines_v1.md). A few practical notes: 

- For PII that is structural (order IDs, invoice numbers), replace with <REDACTED:ID_TYPE> and keep a separate column 'redaction_token' with the original token hashed (SHA256) if you need traceability for debugging.
- For free-text user messages that contain names/emails, replace only the exact token, do not change sentence structure; include a separate 'masking_reason' column when redaction affects context (e.g., "customer mentions doctor name").
- Edge-case examples we particularly need: ambiguous queries that require contextual follow-up, multi-turn snippets where the answer depends on prior messages, and examples with nested quoted text.

Attachments: redaction_guidelines_v1.md (Markdown, 3 sections)

I'll be available tomorrow morning PT to run through the redaction template with your data team if helpful.

—Aditya
Aditya Rao
Solutions Architect | Redwood Inference

From: Danielle Price <danielle@foresightlabs.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai>
Cc: Ravi Shah <ravi.shah@foresightlabs.com>, procurement@foresightlabs.com, Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwood.ai>, Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.ai>
Date: Thu, 22 Apr 2027 18:22:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Post-call: prompt pack request + recommended model targets for evaluation

Thanks — that is super helpful. We'll: 

- Put together the prompt pack in the Drive folder you linked and flag rows with PII using the 'contains_pii' column. Expect a first-draft upload by Mon 04/26 EOD.
- Include 150 control prompts + 100 annotated golden responses for regression checks.
- Procurement will drop SOC2 and basic SSO/KMS questions into the Drive folder as well.

One ask from procurement: can Redwood provide an explicit list of the artifacts you will need for an initial security review (exact file names/formats)? That will speed things up on our side.

Proposed mutual action plan (high level):
- 04/26: Prompt pack + initial artifacts uploaded to Drive
- 04/27: Redwood runs eval harness and returns model comparison + notes on redaction issues
- 04/30: Call to review results and pick a POC model to load into a dedicated capacity window

I'll follow up with the procurement contact on the exact artifact list. Looking forward to the eval.

Best,
Danielle
Director of Product
Foresight Labs

On Thu, Apr 22, 2027 at 16:12, Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai> wrote:
> Danielle — thanks for sending these over and for the clear priorities. Quick recap + next steps from our side: ...
> 
> Attachment: ava_prep_checklist.pdf
> 
> Best,
> Ava

source.subject: Synapse Health — pricing & volume check (post-demo)
messages:
From: Jordan Blake <jordan@redwood.com>
To: Alex Morgan <alex.morgan@synapsehealth.com>
Cc: Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwood.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwood.com>
Date: 2027-04-18T09:12:00-07:00
Subject: Synapse Health — pricing & volume check (post-demo)

Hi Alex,

Great to meet the Synapse team earlier — thanks for the demo and for walking us through the clinical workflow. As promised, I wanted to capture the follow-ups on pricing/volume so we can get a rough invoice and a simple unit-econ model to share with your finance team.

Quick recap from our call (please correct me if I missed anything):
- Primary workload: triage assistant for inbound clinical questions (chat generation) + occasional embeddings for document search.
- Expected pilot audience: ~250 clinicians, ~1.5M token/day peak during pilot spikes (approx 45M tokens/month).
- Average prompt+completion length: 600 tokens for chat (prompt 200 / completion 400).
- Embeddings: ~100k calls/month at 6KB average input length.
- Target budget window for pilot: $40k–$80k over first 3 months (team indicated desire to stay nearer the low end).
- Security/compliance: will evaluate Private deployment later; pilot can run in hosted with contractual controls.

Ask from our side to produce a useful estimate:
1) Can you confirm expected monthly token run-rate (low/medium/high) and whether volumes are steady or bursty?
2) Any hard budget targets in $/1M tokens you want us to aim for (helps to shape model assumptions)?
3) Will there be a requirement for guaranteed latency SLA during pilot?

I attached a simple modeling workbook (synapse_token_modeling.xlsx) with three scenarios (Conservative / Expected / Aggressive). I filled the Expected row using the numbers above — if you can drop in your actuals in the green column the sheet will auto-populate per-month cost and a projected annualized $/1M tokens.

Next steps I propose:
- You reply with confirmed monthly ranges and any budget constraints (1–2 bullets).
- We will return a short invoice-style estimate + one-pager explaining key levers (batching, KV cache, quantization) by Thursday (Apr 21).
- Optional: 30-minute pricing walkthrough with our finance lead (Neha) to align assumptions.

Thanks again — happy to hop on another quick call if that helps.

Best,
Jordan Blake

Alex / Jordan — thanks for the data. Attached is a concise invoice-style estimate and an explainer (redwood_pricing_outline.pdf). Summary below: 

Invoice estimate — pilot (30 days):
- Pilot period tokens (Expected): 45M tokens
- Unit price (hosted, standard SLAs): $95 / 1M tokens
- Pilot subtotal: $4,275 (tokens) + $2,500 flat for reserved support and onboarding = $6,775

Invoice estimate — committed 12-month option (annualized):
- Annualized tokens (expected x12): 540M tokens
- Committed unit price (12mo, 20% off list): $76 / 1M tokens
- Annual token charge: $41,040
- Monthly equivalent: $3,420 + optional Dedicated setup (one-time) if they require lower latency/high SLOs

Notes on levers (short):
- Batching & KV cache: Improving cache hit rate from 30% to 60% reduces effective token compute by ~18% on chat workloads.
- Model variants: Routing embeddings to a cheaper 8-bit quantized embedding model can cut embedding spend by ~60%.
- Commitment discounts: Committing to 12 months and a minimum throughput band reduces list unit price; we show a 20% illustrative discount here.

Attachments: invoice-style PDF + the modeling workbook with cells locked for the customer. Please review and let me know if you want different discounting tiers or a pilot cushion. If acceptable, Jordan — we can ship the PDF and a short statement of work for the pilot.

Cheers,
Neha Kapoor
Senior Pricing Manager
Redwood Inference

source.subject: FinRetain: elevated 5xx/429 + timeouts — peering / NAT handoff investigation
messages:
From: Sarah Brooks <sarah@finretain.com>
To: kimberly.park@redwood.ai
Cc: ops@finretain.com
Date: 2027-04-18T09:12:24-07:00
Subject: Spike in 5xx/429 from Redwood API — started ~08:50 PT

Kimberly,

We’re seeing a sharp increase in 5xx and 429 responses on our production checkout flows starting around 08:50 PT. Error rate climbed to ~12% for token-generation endpoints and users are seeing audible latency/timeouts.

Quick details:
- Affects region: us-west-2 -> Redwood dedicated pool FR-9231
- Symptoms: repeated client retries, some sessions report persistent 504s and client-side backoff saturation
- First observed: 2027-04-18 08:50 PT
- We've temporarily scaled our client-side backoff but it’s only reduced overall throughput, not error rate

Can you confirm if anything changed on the Redwood side (peering, NAT, or firewall rules)? We need an ETA on diagnosis — this is blocking checkout flow for high-value customers.

Attaching a short capture and our synthetic-scan times.

—Sarah
Sarah Brooks
Head of Reliability, FinRetain
attachments: packet-capture.pcap (pcap), synth-times.csv (csv)
From: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.ai>
To: Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwood.ai>, Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwood.ai>, Vivek Kulkarni <vivek.kulkarni@redwood.ai>
Cc: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.ai>, Sarah Brooks <sarah@finretain.com>
Date: 2027-04-18T09:22:10-07:00
Subject: Re: Spike in 5xx/429 from Redwood API — started ~08:50 PT

Thanks Sarah — sorry you’re hit with this. I’m pulling in networking and on-call infra. Initial asks:
- Sean / Rishi: can you confirm whether there were any peering or NAT capacity changes to the FR-9231 dedicated pool between 07:30–09:00 PT?
- Vivek: please pull NAT port usage and gateway metrics for that pool (last 6 hours) and upload to the Grafana link below.
- I’ll open an incident and keep Sarah updated with timelines.

We’ll coordinate over email until we have a call if needed.

Kimberly Park
Director, Customer Success — Redwood Inference
Redwood Inference | kimberly.park@redwood.ai

---
Attachment: incident-note.txt (stub)
From: Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwood.ai>
To: kimberly.park@redwood.ai, Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwood.ai>, Vivek Kulkarni <vivek.kulkarni@redwood.ai>
Cc: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.ai>, Sarah Brooks <sarah@finretain.com>
Date: 2027-04-18T10:03:41-07:00
Subject: Re: Spike in 5xx/429 from Redwood API — started ~08:50 PT

Quick update from runtime/network side — preliminary findings below (work in progress):

What we see:
- Backend request chains show elevated upstream 5xx and consistent TCP RSTs between our peering egress node and FinRetain’s ingress (trace attached).
- NAT port allocation is near exhaustion on the dedicated egress gateway for FR-9231. NAT ephemeral ports used jumped from ~12k to ~62k starting at 08:45 PT.
- Some flows are being re-homed mid-session due to peering path flaps; this appears to trigger request retries and creates amplification of active connections (retry storm).

Immediate actions taken:
- Increased ephemeral port pool on the gateway (hotfix) — will monitor.
- Bumped connection tracking timeouts for short-lived sessions to reduce port hold time.
- Triggered a controlled reroute to a warm standby peering path for the FR-9231 pool to see if errors drop.

Attachments:
- vpc-trace.png (hogging paths highlighted)
- nat-port-usage.csv (6h sample)

Next steps:
- If errors drop after the reroute we’ll schedule a teardown/repair of the peering session for root-cause analysis (likely asymmetric routing + port exhaustion).
- If errors persist we’ll escalate to network infra for packet captures (already requested).

—Sean
Sean Gallagher
SRE Lead, Serving Runtime — Redwood Inference

> On Apr 18, 2027, at 09:22, Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.ai> wrote:
>
> Thanks Sarah — sorry you’re hit with this. I’m pulling in networking and on-call infra.
From: Sarah Brooks <sarah@finretain.com>
To: kimberly.park@redwood.ai, sean.gallagher@redwood.ai
Cc: ops@finretain.com
Date: 2027-04-18T11:18:56-07:00
Subject: Re: Spike in 5xx/429 from Redwood API — started ~08:50 PT

Thank you for the quick triage. A couple of asks from our side:
- Please confirm whether the hotfix (ephemeral port increase / reroute) is expected to be permanent or a short-term mitigation. We need to know if we should expect more customer-facing interruptions.
- If this is a peering-level asymmetry, will FinRetain be involved in a joint post-mortem? We’ll need packet captures and a timeline (we have legal/regulatory reporting to consider).

We’ve seen an initial reduction after your reroute but still ~2.5% 5xx across some pods. If you need us to open anything on our side (support ticket with our cloud provider) we can do that — tell us what to include.

—Sarah

> On Apr 18, 2027, at 10:03, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwood.ai> wrote:
>
> Quick update from runtime/network side — preliminary findings below (work in progress):
From: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.ai>
To: Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwood.ai>, Vivek Kulkarni <vivek.kulkarni@redwood.ai>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwood.ai>
Cc: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.ai>, Sarah Brooks <sarah@finretain.com>
Bcc: laura.bennett@redwood.ai
Date: 2027-04-18T16:05:02-07:00
Subject: Re: Spike in 5xx/429 from Redwood API — started ~08:50 PT

Folks — escalation note for leadership & request for exec coordination with FinRetain:

Summary: persistent elevated 5xx/429 was caused by a combination of NAT ephemeral port exhaustion on the dedicated egress gateway for the FR-9231 pool and intermittent peering path flaps that re-homed active sessions. The combined effect created retry amplification and short-lived connection churn.

Customer impact: FinRetain checkout throughput degraded; customer synthetic monitors and real traffic hit transient 504s and 429s. FinRetain reports sensitive compliance obligations for incident timelines.

Asks from leadership:
- Approve a customer goodwill concession (1 month of usage credits for FR-9231) pending completed post-mortem.
- Authorize collaboration with FinRetain networking team and their cloud provider for joint packet-capture/peering repair (legal sign-off already requested by FinRetain).
- Fast-track a permanent NAT scaling/config change for Dedicated pools (engineering + infra roadmap).

Status and next steps:
- Sean: continue monitoring; full pcaps uploaded to the incident ticket and shared via the Grafana link.
- Rishi/Vivek: write a short RCA and target a post-mortem doc in 72 hours.
- Kimberly: I will coordinate exec outreach and send a short summary to FinRetain leadership.

I’ll hold a 30-minute sync if needed — propose 16:30 PT. Feedback welcome.

Kimberly Park
Director, Customer Success — Redwood Inference
Phone: +1 (415) 555-0147

---
Attachments referenced: vpc-trace.png, nat-port-usage.csv, packet-capture.pcap

Cc: ops@finretain.com
Date: 2027-04-18T09:12:24-07:00
Subject: Spike in 5xx/429 from Redwood API — started ~08:50 PT

Kimberly,

We’re seeing a sharp increase in 5xx and 429 responses on our production checkout flows starting around 08:50 PT. Error rate climbed to ~12% for token-generation endpoints and users are seeing audible latency/timeouts.

Quick details:
- Affects region: us-west-2 -> Redwood dedicated pool FR-9231
- Symptoms: repeated client retries, some sessions report persistent 504s and client-side backoff saturation
- First observed: 2027-04-18 08:50 PT
- We've temporarily scaled our client-side backoff but it’s only reduced overall throughput, not error rate

Can you confirm if anything changed on the Redwood side (peering, NAT, or firewall rules)? We need an ETA on diagnosis — this is blocking checkout flow for high-value customers.

Attaching a short capture and our synthetic-scan times.

—Sarah
Sarah Brooks
Head of Reliability, FinRetain
attachments: packet-capture.pcap (pcap), synth-times.csv (csv)

From: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.ai>
To: Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwood.ai>, Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwood.ai>, Vivek Kulkarni <vivek.kulkarni@redwood.ai>
Cc: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.ai>, Sarah Brooks <sarah@finretain.com>
Date: 2027-04-18T09:22:10-07:00
Subject: Re: Spike in 5xx/429 from Redwood API — started ~08:50 PT

Thanks Sarah — sorry you’re hit with this. I’m pulling in networking and on-call infra. Initial asks:
- Sean / Rishi: can you confirm whether there were any peering or NAT capacity changes to the FR-9231 dedicated pool between 07:30–09:00 PT?
- Vivek: please pull NAT port usage and gateway metrics for that pool (last 6 hours) and upload to the Grafana link below.
- I’ll open an incident and keep Sarah updated with timelines.

We’ll coordinate over email until we have a call if needed.

Kimberly Park
Director, Customer Success — Redwood Inference
Redwood Inference | kimberly.park@redwood.ai

---
Attachment: incident-note.txt (stub)


From: Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwood.ai>
To: kimberly.park@redwood.ai, Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwood.ai>, Vivek Kulkarni <vivek.kulkarni@redwood.ai>
Cc: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.ai>, Sarah Brooks <sarah@finretain.com>
Date: 2027-04-18T10:03:41-07:00
Subject: Re: Spike in 5xx/429 from Redwood API — started ~08:50 PT

Quick update from runtime/network side — preliminary findings below (work in progress):

What we see:
- Backend request chains show elevated upstream 5xx and consistent TCP RSTs between our peering egress node and FinRetain’s ingress (trace attached).
- NAT port allocation is near exhaustion on the dedicated egress gateway for FR-9231. NAT ephemeral ports used jumped from ~12k to ~62k starting at 08:45 PT.
- Some flows are being re-homed mid-session due to peering path flaps; this appears to trigger request retries and creates amplification of active connections (retry storm).

Immediate actions taken:
- Increased ephemeral port pool on the gateway (hotfix) — will monitor.
- Bumped connection tracking timeouts for short-lived sessions to reduce port hold time.
- Triggered a controlled reroute to a warm standby peering path for the FR-9231 pool to see if errors drop.

Attachments:
- vpc-trace.png (hogging paths highlighted)
- nat-port-usage.csv (6h sample)

Next steps:
- If errors drop after the reroute we’ll schedule a teardown/repair of the peering session for root-cause analysis (likely asymmetric routing + port exhaustion).
- If errors persist we’ll escalate to network infra for packet captures (already requested).

—Sean
Sean Gallagher
SRE Lead, Serving Runtime — Redwood Inference

> On Apr 18, 2027, at 09:22, Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.ai> wrote:
>
> Thanks Sarah — sorry you’re hit with this. I’m pulling in networking and on-call infra.


From: Sarah Brooks <sarah@finretain.com>
To: kimberly.park@redwood.ai, sean.gallagher@redwood.ai
Cc: ops@finretain.com
Date: 2027-04-18T11:18:56-07:00
Subject: Re: Spike in 5xx/429 from Redwood API — started ~08:50 PT

Thank you for the quick triage. A couple of asks from our side:
- Please confirm whether the hotfix (ephemeral port increase / reroute) is expected to be permanent or a short-term mitigation. We need to know if we should expect more customer-facing interruptions.
- If this is a peering-level asymmetry, will FinRetain be involved in a joint post-mortem? We’ll need packet captures and a timeline (we have legal/regulatory reporting to consider).

We’ve seen an initial reduction after your reroute but still ~2.5% 5xx across some pods. If you need us to open anything on our side (support ticket with our cloud provider) we can do that — tell us what to include.

—Sarah

> On Apr 18, 2027, at 10:03, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwood.ai> wrote:
>
> Quick update from runtime/network side — preliminary findings below (work in progress):


From: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.ai>
To: Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwood.ai>, Vivek Kulkarni <vivek.kulkarni@redwood.ai>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwood.ai>
Cc: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.ai>, Sarah Brooks <sarah@finretain.com>
Bcc: laura.bennett@redwood.ai
Date: 2027-04-18T16:05:02-07:00
Subject: Re: Spike in 5xx/429 from Redwood API — started ~08:50 PT

Folks — escalation note for leadership & request for exec coordination with FinRetain:

Summary: persistent elevated 5xx/429 was caused by a combination of NAT ephemeral port exhaustion on the dedicated egress gateway for the FR-9231 pool and intermittent peering path flaps that re-homed active sessions. The combined effect created retry amplification and short-lived connection churn.

Customer impact: FinRetain checkout throughput degraded; customer synthetic monitors and real traffic hit transient 504s and 429s. FinRetain reports sensitive compliance obligations for incident timelines.

Asks from leadership:
- Approve a customer goodwill concession (1 month of usage credits for FR-9231) pending completed post-mortem.
- Authorize collaboration with FinRetain networking team and their cloud provider for joint packet-capture/peering repair (legal sign-off already requested by FinRetain).
- Fast-track a permanent NAT scaling/config change for Dedicated pools (engineering + infra roadmap).

Status and next steps:
- Sean: continue monitoring; full pcaps uploaded to the incident ticket and shared via the Grafana link.
- Rishi/Vivek: write a short RCA and target a post-mortem doc in 72 hours.
- Kimberly: I will coordinate exec outreach and send a short summary to FinRetain leadership.

I’ll hold a 30-minute sync if needed — propose 16:30 PT. Feedback welcome.

Kimberly Park
Director, Customer Success — Redwood Inference
Phone: +1 (415) 555-0147

---
Attachments referenced: vpc-trace.png, nat-port-usage.csv, packet-capture.pcap
