source.subject: Service surge impact & commercial compensation (Delphi Systems)
messages:
From: Elena Rossi <elena.rossi@delphi-sys.com>
To: Jasmine Liu <jasmine.liu@redwood.ai>, Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.ai>
Cc: Marco Giordani <marco.giordani@delphi-sys.com>
Date: Sat, 10 Apr 2027 08:12:00 -0400
Subject: Unexpected throughput drop + elevated 5xxs (Apr 9-10)

Hi Jasmine / Monica,

Quick note from ops: we observed a sustained throughput regression and a spike in 5xx errors during a heavy job window last night (2027-04-09 22:30 - 2027-04-10 01:15 UTC). Latency P95 increased from ~120ms to ~1.2s for a subset of routes; error rate (5xx) peaked at 3.6% for 30 minutes. This affected our batch inference pipelines and caused a missed SLA for two production routes.

I've attached a short extract of our telemetry (delphi_sla_metrics.csv). Procurement has asked we open a commercial conversation about credits and any contract language changes required to avoid surprises.

Can we get an initial estimate of the credit calculation methodology and a timeline for remediation? We need to brief our CFO and procurement by EOD Tuesday.

Thanks,
Elena
Head of Platform Reliability
Delphi Systems

Attachments: delphi_sla_metrics.csv (CSV, 42 KB)

From: Jasmine Liu <jasmine.liu@redwood.ai>
To: Elena Rossi <elena.rossi@delphi-sys.com>
Cc: Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.ai>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwood.ai>
Date: Sat, 10 Apr 2027 09:05:00 -0400
Subject: Re: Unexpected throughput drop + elevated 5xxs (Apr 9-10)

Elena — thanks for the heads up and the data. I'm really sorry you hit that window.

Immediate next steps from our side:
- Monica is running a root-cause triage now and will follow up with the mitigation timeline.
- I'll loop in Sean on commercial/credits so procurement has a single point of contact.
- Can you confirm the two routes you mentioned (route IDs or endpoints) + the impacted time range you want us to use for a credit calc?

We will share an initial credit proposal by EOD Monday and set up an executive sync for Tuesday if that works for your team.

-Jasmine
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
Email: jasmine.liu@redwood.ai | +1 415-555-0162

From: Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.ai>
To: Elena Rossi <elena.rossi@delphi-sys.com>, Jasmine Liu <jasmine.liu@redwood.ai>
Cc: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.ai>
Date: Sat, 10 Apr 2027 11:40:00 -0400
Subject: Re: Unexpected throughput drop + elevated 5xxs (Apr 9-10)

Elena — I pulled the handler traces and host-level telemetry. Preliminary findings:
- A config churn at ~22:20 UTC caused an uneven request distribution that increased queueing on a small subset of nodes.
- Kernel-level backpressure + a GC spike on a background transformer task worsened latency during the window.
- We have a mitigation path (pinning the routing policy + temporary additional burst capacity) that should prevent recurrence.

For commercial: attached is a short spreadsheet with a proposed credit calculation (proposed_credit_table.xlsx). It's conservative: we compute credits based on total affected token consumption for the window and a flat per-token refund model aligned to your current plan.

I'll put a technical write-up on the exec call. Karthik, can you review the commercial phrasing for any legal/contract implications?

-Monica
Sr. Solutions Engineer, Redwood Inference

Attachments: proposed_credit_table.xlsx (XLSX, 18 KB)

From: Marco Giordani <marco.giordani@delphi-sys.com>
To: Jasmine Liu <jasmine.liu@redwood.ai>, Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.ai>
Cc: Elena Rossi <elena.rossi@delphi-sys.com>, Delphi Systems Procurement <procure@delphi-sys.com>
Date: Mon, 12 Apr 2027 09:22:00 +0200
Subject: Re: Unexpected throughput drop + elevated 5xxs (Apr 9-10)

Thanks for the quick responses. Procurement would like: 
1) A clear credit figure and the assumptions behind it;
2) A short contract amendment that records the incident, the agreed credit, and changes to the SLOs/response commitments (if any);
3) DocuSign-ready language for our legal team.

We're prepared to accept a credit that covers direct invoice impact for the affected routes; however we need the amendment to be explicit about when credits are applied to invoices and how that shows up in billing exports.

Can you get us a draft amendment and a single credit number (not a range) before the exec call? Procurement prefers to sign amendments during the call if everything is in order.

Regards,
Marco
VP, Cloud Ops — Delphi Systems

From: Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwood.ai>
To: Marco Giordani <marco.giordani@delphi-sys.com>, Elena Rossi <elena.rossi@delphi-sys.com>
Cc: Jasmine Liu <jasmine.liu@redwood.ai>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.ai>
Date: Tue, 13 Apr 2027 14:30:00 -0400
Subject: Re: Unexpected throughput drop + elevated 5xxs (Apr 9-10)

Marco / Elena — appreciate the clarity. We've reviewed Monica's worksheet and, after accounting for token consumption, redundancy, and the contractual SLOs, Redwood proposes a one-time commercial credit of EUR 14,750 to be applied to Delphi's next invoice.

Attached is a draft amendment that:
- Documents the incident window and agreed credit;
- Clarifies credit application timing (applied to the next invoice; itemized on billing exports);
- Adds a one-off operational improvement commitment (routing pin + burst capacity window) for 60 days.

If that number works for you we can send the amendment via DocuSign during the exec call. We're happy to accept any small editorial tweaks from your legal team.

Sean Gallagher
Head of Commercial, Redwood Inference

Attachments: redwood_amendment_draft_v1.pdf (PDF, 26 KB)

From: Elena Rossi <elena.rossi@delphi-sys.com>
To: Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwood.ai>, Jasmine Liu <jasmine.liu@redwood.ai>
Cc: Marco Giordani <marco.giordani@delphi-sys.com>, Delphi Systems Procurement <procure@delphi-sys.com>
Date: Sun, 18 Apr 2027 16:05:00 +0200
Subject: Re: Unexpected throughput drop + elevated 5xxs (Apr 9-10)

Sean — thank you. Delphi accepts the one-time credit of EUR 14,750 conditional on the amendment being signed by procurement on the call. Procurement suggests a 30-minute slot on Tuesday 2027-04-20 at 09:00 UTC; legal will join for a quick review.

Before the call, procurement will need:
- The DocuSign link for the amendment;
- A brief note on how the credit will appear in billing exports (line-item code and invoice field).

We appreciate the operational fixes Monica proposed — please make sure the routing pin and burst window are in place before the call.

Elena
Head of Platform Reliability
Delphi Systems

On Tue, Apr 13, 2027 at 14:30, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwood.ai> wrote:
> Marco / Elena — appreciate the clarity. We've reviewed Monica's worksheet and, after accounting for token consumption, redundancy, and the contractual SLOs, Redwood proposes a one-time commercial credit of EUR 14,750 to be applied to Delphi's next invoice.
>
> Attached is a draft amendment that:
> - Documents the incident window and agreed credit;
> - Clarifies credit application timing (applied to the next invoice; itemized on billing exports);
> - Adds a one-off operational improvement commitment (routing pin + burst capacity window) for 60 days.
>
> If that number works for you we can send the amendment via DocuSign during the exec call. We're happy to accept any small editorial tweaks from your legal team.
>
> Sean Gallagher
> Head of Commercial, Redwood Inference
>
> Attachments: redwood_amendment_draft_v1.pdf (PDF, 26 KB)

Sean — thank you. Delphi accepts the one-time credit of EUR 14,750 conditional on the amendment being signed by procurement on the call. Procurement suggests a 30-minute slot on Tuesday 2027-04-20 at 09:00 UTC; legal will join for a quick review.

Before the call, procurement will need:
- The DocuSign link for the amendment;
- A brief note on how the credit will appear in billing exports (line-item code and invoice field).

We appreciate the operational fixes Monica proposed — please make sure the routing pin and burst window are in place before the call.

Elena

Delphi Systems

text:
From: Elena Rossi <elena.rossi@delphi-sys.com>
To: Jasmine Liu <jasmine.liu@redwood.ai>, Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.ai>
Cc: Marco Giordani <marco.giordani@delphi-sys.com>
Date: Sat, 10 Apr 2027 08:12:00 -0400
Subject: Unexpected throughput drop + elevated 5xxs (Apr 9-10)

Hi Jasmine / Monica,

Quick note from ops: we observed a sustained throughput regression and a spike in 5xx errors during a heavy job window last night (2027-04-09 22:30 - 2027-04-10 01:15 UTC). Latency P95 increased from ~120ms to ~1.2s for a subset of routes; error rate (5xx) peaked at 3.6% for 30 minutes. This affected our batch inference pipelines and caused a missed SLA for two production routes.

I've attached a short extract of our telemetry (delphi_sla_metrics.csv). Procurement has asked we open a commercial conversation about credits and any contract language changes required to avoid surprises.

Can we get an initial estimate of the credit calculation methodology and a timeline for remediation? We need to brief our CFO and procurement by EOD Tuesday.

Thanks,
Elena
Head of Platform Reliability
Delphi Systems

Attachments: delphi_sla_metrics.csv (CSV, 42 KB)

From: Jasmine Liu <jasmine.liu@redwood.ai>
To: Elena Rossi <elena.rossi@delphi-sys.com>
Cc: Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.ai>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwood.ai>
Date: Sat, 10 Apr 2027 09:05:00 -0400
Subject: Re: Unexpected throughput drop + elevated 5xxs (Apr 9-10)

Elena — thanks for the heads up and the data. I'm really sorry you hit that window.

Immediate next steps from our side:
- Monica is running a root-cause triage now and will follow up with the mitigation timeline.
- I'll loop in Sean on commercial/credits so procurement has a single point of contact.
- Can you confirm the two routes you mentioned (route IDs or endpoints) + the impacted time range you want us to use for a credit calc?

We will share an initial credit proposal by EOD Monday and set up an executive sync for Tuesday if that works for your team.

-Jasmine
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
Email: jasmine.liu@redwood.ai | +1 415-555-0162



From: Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.ai>
To: Elena Rossi <elena.rossi@delphi-sys.com>, Jasmine Liu <jasmine.liu@redwood.ai>
Cc: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.ai>
Date: Sat, 10 Apr 2027 11:40:00 -0400
Subject: Re: Unexpected throughput drop + elevated 5xxs (Apr 9-10)

Elena — I pulled the handler traces and host-level telemetry. Preliminary findings:
- A config churn at ~22:20 UTC caused an uneven request distribution that increased queueing on a small subset of nodes.
- Kernel-level backpressure + a GC spike on a background transformer task worsened latency during the window.
- We have a mitigation path (pinning the routing policy + temporary additional burst capacity) that should prevent recurrence.

For commercial: attached is a short spreadsheet with a proposed credit calculation (proposed_credit_table.xlsx). It's conservative: we compute credits based on total affected token consumption for the window and a flat per-token refund model aligned to your current plan.

I'll put a technical write-up on the exec call. Karthik, can you review the commercial phrasing for any legal/contract implications?

-Monica
Sr. Solutions Engineer, Redwood Inference

Attachments: proposed_credit_table.xlsx (XLSX, 18 KB)

From: Marco Giordani <marco.giordani@delphi-sys.com>
To: Jasmine Liu <jasmine.liu@redwood.ai>, Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.ai>
Cc: Elena Rossi <elena.rossi@delphi-sys.com>, Delphi Systems Procurement <procure@delphi-sys.com>
Date: Mon, 12 Apr 2027 09:22:00 +0200
Subject: Re: Unexpected throughput drop + elevated 5xxs (Apr 9-10)

Thanks for the quick responses. Procurement would like: 
1) A clear credit figure and the assumptions behind it;
2) A short contract amendment that records the incident, the agreed credit, and changes to the SLOs/response commitments (if any);
3) DocuSign-ready language for our legal team.

We're prepared to accept a credit that covers direct invoice impact for the affected routes; however we need the amendment to be explicit about when credits are applied to invoices and how that shows up in billing exports.

Can you get us a draft amendment and a single credit number (not a range) before the exec call? Procurement prefers to sign amendments during the call if everything is in order.

Regards,
Marco
VP, Cloud Ops — Delphi Systems



From: Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwood.ai>
To: Marco Giordani <marco.giordani@delphi-sys.com>, Elena Rossi <elena.rossi@delphi-sys.com>
Cc: Jasmine Liu <jasmine.liu@redwood.ai>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.ai>
Date: Tue, 13 Apr 2027 14:30:00 -0400
Subject: Re: Unexpected throughput drop + elevated 5xxs (Apr 9-10)

Marco / Elena — appreciate the clarity. We've reviewed Monica's worksheet and, after accounting for token consumption, redundancy, and the contractual SLOs, Redwood proposes a one-time commercial credit of EUR 14,750 to be applied to Delphi's next invoice.

Attached is a draft amendment that:
- Documents the incident window and agreed credit;
- Clarifies credit application timing (applied to the next invoice; itemized on billing exports);
- Adds a one-off operational improvement commitment (routing pin + burst capacity window) for 60 days.

If that number works for you we can send the amendment via DocuSign during the exec call. We're happy to accept any small editorial tweaks from your legal team.

Sean Gallagher
Head of Commercial, Redwood Inference

Attachments: redwood_amendment_draft_v1.pdf (PDF, 26 KB)

source.subject: Exec: Persistent chunkless streams causing production timeouts - Aria Solutions
messages:
From: Aria Support <support@aria-solutions.com>
To: Irene Choi <irene_choi@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 17 Apr 2027 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Realtime responses stopping mid-stream (multiple customers)

Hi Irene,

We started seeing streaming responses cut off after ~10–14s this morning across several of our end users. The UI shows the socket still open but no further chunks arrive — the transcript is truncated and we get a 200 with partial content. This started after the deploy yesterday (we're using the hosted API). Error rate spikes at 4xx/5xx correlate with high concurrent sessions.

Example: request ids attached (aria_request_ids.csv) and a brief pcap we captured. Can you tell us if anything changed on your side (timeouts, proxies, model variants)? This is affecting our workspace collaboration feature; customers are already reaching out.

Thanks,
Lina Park
Platform Ops — Aria Solutions
attachments: aria_request_ids.csv, pcap_summary.txt
From: Irene Choi <irene_choi@redwood.com>
To: Lina Park <lina@aria-solutions.com>
Cc: Monica Patel <monica_patel@redwood.com>, Markus Klein <markus_klein@redwood.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean_gallagher@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 17 Apr 2027 09:34:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Realtime responses stopping mid-stream (multiple customers)

Hi Lina — thanks for the quick heads-up and for attaching the IDs. I'm escalating this now.

Quick ask so we can triage faster: can you share the Hub request ids (looks like you did, thanks), and any timestamps for when the UI reported the end-of-stream? We need one end-to-end example to follow in logs.

Monica/Markus/Sean — looping you in for triage. Initial notes: 1) this looks like chunked transfer / websocket output silence rather than outright request failures; 2) Aria is seeing it across multiple users which suggests an infra-level timeout or routing mismatch; 3) impact: multiple enterprise customers in this account (deal ARIA-4532).

Proposed immediate steps:
- Monica: confirm any rate-limit or quota hits on Aria's API key.
- Markus: check ALB/edge and backend proxy idle/send timeouts and any 502/504 patterns.
- Sean: prep a short customer update and offer a temporary credit if we find platform fault.

I'll coordinate exec escalation if this looks broad.

Best,
Irene Choi
Director, Strategic Accounts
Redwood Inference
From: Monica Patel <monica_patel@redwood.com>
To: Irene Choi <irene_choi@redwood.com>, Markus Klein <markus_klein@redwood.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean_gallagher@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 17 Apr 2027 10:05:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Realtime responses stopping mid-stream (multiple customers)

I dug into the API key and usage for Aria (key: aria-prod-01). No quota ceiling hit over the last 72h; TPS increased from 45->72 during our peak window but well within committed throughput. However, error-rate for generation endpoints jumped from 0.8% -> 4.9% between 08:50 and 09:20. Most errors are timeouts/front-end 502 backends and a bunch of partial 200s with truncated chunk sequences.

I attached a tar with collector logs (aria_stream_logs.tar.gz) including request ids. Quick observations:
- Multiple requests show the backend returning chunks then an abrupt stop with no final chunk. The ALB logs show the connection remained open for a while after the last upstream write.
- We see spikes in connection resets from edge proxies at the same time window.

Next: Markus — can you confirm ALB/edge config and recent deploys to the realtime path?

-Monica
Customer Success Manager, Redwood Inference
From: Markus Klein <markus_klein@redwood.com>
To: Monica Patel <monica_patel@redwood.com>, Irene Choi <irene_choi@redwood.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean_gallagher@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 17 Apr 2027 12:10:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Realtime responses stopping mid-stream (multiple customers)

Triage summary (quick):
- Root cause (likely): idle/send timeout mismatch between AWS ALB (default 60s) and our internal frontend NGINX/uvicorn proxy stack which has proxy_send_timeout=15s for kept-alive shards. When a model emits intermittent longer pauses (token generation gaps due to backend batching or KV cache warmups), the proxy can stop forwarding chunks while ALB keeps the TCP connection open — resulting in client sockets remaining open with no new data (exactly what Aria sees).

Evidence:
- Attached ALB snapshot (alb-config-snapshot.txt) shows idle_timeout: 60.
- Sample log (excerpt):
  [2027-04-17T08:56:23Z] upstream_write: wrote 2048 bytes
  [2027-04-17T08:56:35Z] upstream_write: wrote 256 bytes
  [2027-04-17T08:56:50Z] proxy_send_timeout reached, closing upstream stream
  [2027-04-17T08:56:50Z] client connection still open (no downstream close)

Recommended immediate mitigations (to reduce customer impact):
1) Increase ALB idle_timeout to 120s (Customers with long-generation patterns benefit).
2) Update NGINX proxy_send_timeout and proxy_buffering off for realtime routes to 90s and add keepalive probes to detect true connection death.
3) Short-term routing policy: for Aria, route heavy-concurrency sessions to a lower-latency model variant (redwood-small-rt) that emits more consistent token flow while we stabilize.
4) Deploy a streaming heartbeat patch (server-side chunk: "
") to emit a minimal keepalive every 8s until the next tokens arrive (backward compatible).

Rollback/monitor plan: if 1–2 cause regression, revert ALB change and toggle routing policy. We'll watch p95/95 error rate in STREAM-7894 and send an update within 2 hours.

I'll push a config change to our staging ALB now and then to prod if greenlighted.

-Markus
SRE, Redwood Inference
From: Irene Choi <irene_choi@redwood.com>
To: Sean Gallagher <sean_gallagher@redwood.com>, Monica Patel <monica_patel@redwood.com>, Markus Klein <markus_klein@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 17 Apr 2027 14:48:00 -07:00
Subject: Exec: Persistent chunkless streams causing production timeouts - Aria Solutions

Folks — because this is affecting multiple enterprise seats at Aria and they've already escalated to their VP of Product, I'm escalating for exec visibility and ask for authorization on immediate mitigations. Impact summary for leadership:
- Impact: 7% of active sessions for Aria experienced truncated streams in the 09:00–09:30 window; error-rate peaked at 4.9% (from baseline 0.8%). Several customers reported degraded UX and one major account staged a rollback.
- Business risk: Aria is a mid-market strategic account (ARR ~$360k, active POC with expansion). If unresolved, risk of churn/credit and pipeline delay on expansion (deal ARIA-4532).

Asks for execs:
1) Approve the ALB idle_timeout increase to 120s and the NGINX proxy_send_timeout adjustment as an emergency config change (I approve as owner of the escalation path, but need SRE signoff to deploy).
2) Approve a one-off goodwill credit (10% monthly usage credit for April) if we confirm platform fault in post-mortem.
3) Authorize customer-facing comms: short ETA and root-cause update within 2 hours.

Markus — proceed with the staged config change on your go. Monica/Sean — draft the external message; I'm happy to send exec-level note to Aria's leadership.

Irene Choi
Director, Strategic Accounts | Redwood Inference
Direct: +1 415 555 0123
irene_choi@redwood.com

1) Increase ALB idle_timeout to 120s (Customers with long-generation patterns benefit).
2) Update NGINX proxy_send_timeout and proxy_buffering off for realtime routes to 90s and add keepalive probes to detect true connection death.
3) Short-term routing policy: for Aria, route heavy-concurrency sessions to a lower-latency model variant (redwood-small-rt) that emits more consistent token flow while we stabilize.
4) Deploy a streaming heartbeat patch (server-side chunk: "
") to emit a minimal keepalive every 8s until the next tokens arrive (backward compatible).

Rollback/monitor plan: if 1–2 cause regression, revert ALB change and toggle routing policy. We'll watch p95/95 error rate in STREAM-7894 and send an update within 2 hours.

I'll push a config change to our staging ALB now and then to prod if greenlighted.

-Markus
SRE, Redwood Inference


From: Irene Choi <irene_choi@redwood.com>
To: Sean Gallagher <sean_gallagher@redwood.com>, Monica Patel <monica_patel@redwood.com>, Markus Klein <markus_klein@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 17 Apr 2027 14:48:00 -07:00
Subject: Exec: Persistent chunkless streams causing production timeouts - Aria Solutions

Folks — because this is affecting multiple enterprise seats at Aria and they've already escalated to their VP of Product, I'm escalating for exec visibility and ask for authorization on immediate mitigations. Impact summary for leadership:
- Impact: 7% of active sessions for Aria experienced truncated streams in the 09:00–09:30 window; error-rate peaked at 4.9% (from baseline 0.8%). Several customers reported degraded UX and one major account staged a rollback.
- Business risk: Aria is a mid-market strategic account (ARR ~$360k, active POC with expansion). If unresolved, risk of churn/credit and pipeline delay on expansion (deal ARIA-4532).

Asks for execs:
1) Approve the ALB idle_timeout increase to 120s and the NGINX proxy_send_timeout adjustment as an emergency config change (I approve as owner of the escalation path, but need SRE signoff to deploy).
2) Approve a one-off goodwill credit (10% monthly usage credit for April) if we confirm platform fault in post-mortem.
3) Authorize customer-facing comms: short ETA and root-cause update within 2 hours.

Markus — proceed with the staged config change on your go. Monica/Sean — draft the external message; I'm happy to send exec-level note to Aria's leadership.

Irene Choi
Director, Strategic Accounts | Redwood Inference
Direct: +1 415 555 0123
irene_choi@redwood.com

source.subject: Action: small-multiples latency & spend tiles on Console home
source.snippet: Request to build compact small-multiples tiles on Console home showing per-tenant latency percentiles and spend attribution; ask for data requirements, infra impact, and timeline
messages:
From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>, Priyom Das <priyom.das@redwood.ai>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwood.ai>, Dylan Brooks <dylan.brooks@redwood.ai>
Cc: Mike Rhodes <mike.rhodes@acme.com>
Date: Mon, 12 Apr 2027 09:14:00 -0700
Subject: Action: small-multiples latency & spend tiles on Console home

Team —

Following the convo with Acme (Mike copied) and a couple of other enterprise prospects, we have a clear ask: customers want an at-a-glance view on Console home that surfaces both latency and spend signals per tenant (or billing tag). The idea is compact "small-multiples" tiles — one row per tenant, showing p50/p95 latency sparkline and a spend bar for the selected window (default: 7d). Key goals:

- Detect tenant regressions quickly (spike in p95)
- Correlate latency shifts with spend changes (e.g., sudden burst due to traffic or pricing anomaly)
- Provide quick drill paths: click a tenant -> per-route breakdown, recent errors, and last 24h traces

I attached a quick mock (console-home-tiles-mock.png). High-level product asks:
- Tiles limited to top-20 tenants by spend, with a search to pin any tenant
- 7d default window, 1h aggregation for sparkline; ability to switch to 24h/30d
- Spend attribution: show tokens billed and estimated $ (using current price mapping)
- Toggle to show p50/p95 or both

Questions for engineering/analytics:
1) What rollups do we need (granularity, retention) to support 1h/1m views?
2) Can we derive spend quickly from existing billing events or do we need a new aggregation job?
3) Any perf concerns for a Console home query that may show 20 tenants concurrently?

Requesting: a short feasibility note + rough ETA for an MVP (top-10 tenants, p95 + spend, 7d) by EOD Wednesday 4/14.

Thanks,
Ava Chen
Product Manager, Console
Redwood Inference

Attachment: console-home-tiles-mock.png (image/png)

From: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai>
Cc: Priyom Das <priyom.das@redwood.ai>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwood.ai>, Dylan Brooks <dylan.brooks@redwood.ai>, Mike Rhodes <mike.rhodes@acme.com>
Date: Tue, 13 Apr 2027 10:02:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Action: small-multiples latency & spend tiles on Console home

Ava —

Love this — a few clarifying items from CS/field perspective:

- For "tenant" we should default to the billing tenant id that Finance uses (the same tag we surface in invoice exports). If we misalign on tag naming we will confuse accounts.
- Customers will want a link from the tile directly to the current Mutual Action Plan (MAP) or open ticket. Could we have an optional 1-line footnote area for a linked MAP/PS ticket?
- Regarding spend: many customers have committed discounts and multiple price tiers. At minimum we should show raw billed units + estimated list-price $ so sales/customer success have a consistent baseline.

Cc'ing Mike — Mike, would you prefer list-price display or your contracted rate on the tile?

On the engineering asks: I'll defer to Priyom and Dylan, but note that customers expect near-real-time for latency (<=5m) and daily accuracy for spend. If 1m rollups are expensive, we can show p95 as a gateway alert and keep the sparkline at 1h.

- Marissa

On Mon, Apr 12, 2027 at 09:14 AM Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai> wrote:
> Team —
> 
> Following the convo with Acme (Mike copied) and a couple of other enterprise prospects, we have a clear ask: customers want an at-a-glance view on Console home that surfaces both latency and spend signals per tenant (or billing tag). The idea is compact "small-multiples" tiles — one row per tenant, showing p50/p95 latency sparkline and a spend bar for the selected window (default: 7d). Key goals:
> 
> - Detect tenant regressions quickly (spike in p95)
> - Correlate latency shifts with spend changes (e.g., sudden burst due to traffic or pricing anomaly)
> - Provide quick drill paths: click a tenant -> per-route breakdown, recent errors, and last 24h traces
> 
> I attached a quick mock (console-home-tiles-mock.png). High-level product asks:
> - Tiles limited to top-20 tenants by spend, with a search to pin any tenant
> - 7d default window, 1h aggregation for sparkline; ability to switch to 24h/30d
> - Spend attribution: show tokens billed and estimated $ (using current price mapping)
> - Toggle to show p50/p95 or both
> 
> Questions for engineering/analytics:
> 1) What rollups do we need (granularity, retention) to support 1h/1m views?
> 2) Can we derive spend quickly from existing billing events or do we need a new aggregation job?
> 3) Any perf concerns for a Console home query that may show 20 tenants concurrently?
> 
> Requesting: a short feasibility note + rough ETA for an MVP (top-10 tenants, p95 + spend, 7d) by EOD Wednesday 4/14.
> 
> Thanks,
> Ava Chen
> Product Manager, Console
> Redwood Inference

From: Priyom Das <priyom.das@redwood.ai>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>
Cc: Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwood.ai>, Dylan Brooks <dylan.brooks@redwood.ai>
Date: Wed, 14 Apr 2027 08:45:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Action: small-multiples latency & spend tiles on Console home

Quick feasibility note below. Short answer: doable but requires a small pipeline + schema additions.

Data assumptions and needs:
- Latency: we currently ingest per-request telemetry with a 1s timestamp; we have hourly and daily rollups but no retained 1m rollups for tenant-level p95 historically. To support 1h sparkline and a 5m near-real-time alert we can:
  - Materialize 5m and 1h rollups keyed by tenant_id + route_id in a new rollup table. 30d retention for 5m, 90d for 1h should be sufficient for Console.
- Spend: billing events exist as line items in billing_events (per-invoice). To show recent spend in near-real-time we need a streaming aggregator that groups billing events by tenant and applies the pricing map. If we show list-price units + estimated list-price $ we can avoid contract-rate lookups at render time.

Suggested MVP scope:
- Build 1h rollups for top-20 tenants (weekly job to recompute top-20) + realtime windowed aggregator to keep a 5m p95 for only top-10 tenants.
- Expose an API endpoint /api/console/tiles/tenants that returns cached JSON per-home-view (recompute cache every 1m for top tenants).

Effort rough estimate:
- Rollup pipelines (ETL + infra): 2 engineers x 2 weeks
- API + cache: 1 engineer x 1 week
- Frontend tiles + drill UX: 1 FE engineer x 1 week (MVP limited visuals)
- Backfill scripts and testing: 1 engineer x 4 days

Storage/cost impact: expect additional storage for 5m rollups ~50GB/month for global tenant set; limiting to top-20 reduces that by ~10x. Also small incremental compute for streaming aggregator.

I attached a sample query that would power the API (cost-latency-rollup-proposal.sql).

Priyom
Senior Data Engineer, Platform Metrics
Redwood Inference

On Tue, Apr 13, 2027 at 10:02 AM Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai> wrote:
> Ava —
> 
> Love this — a few clarifying items from CS/field perspective:
> 
> - For "tenant" we should default to the billing tenant id that Finance uses (the same tag we surface in invoice exports). If we misalign on tag naming we will confuse accounts.
> - Customers will want a link from the tile directly to the current Mutual Action Plan (MAP) or open ticket. Could we have an optional 1-line footnote area for a linked MAP/PS ticket?
> - Regarding spend: many customers have committed discounts and multiple price tiers. At minimum we should show raw billed units + estimated list-price $ so sales/customer success have a consistent baseline.
> 
> Cc'ing Mike — Mike, would you prefer list-price display or your contracted rate on the tile?
> 
> On the engineering asks: I'll defer to Priyom and Dylan, but note that customers expect near-real-time for latency (<=5m) and daily accuracy for spend. If 1m rollups are expensive, we can show p95 as a gateway alert and keep the sparkline at 1h.
> 
> - Marissa

From: Dylan Brooks <dylan.brooks@redwood.ai>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai>, Priyom Das <priyom.das@redwood.ai>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwood.ai>
Date: Thu, 15 Apr 2027 11:20:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Action: small-multiples latency & spend tiles on Console home

Putting on my infra hat — a couple of notes and a recommended constrained MVP: 

- Live compute: computing 5m p95 for *all* tenants at 1m cadence is expensive. We should scope an MVP to top-10 tenants and provide a soft-fail for "others" (show last computed value + link to per-tenant page for on-demand compute).
- Cache strategy: use a read-through cache with 1m TTL for top tenants; fall back to on-demand worker for pinned tenants. This will keep Console home QPS affordable.
- Backfill: if we want historical sparklines beyond the cache window we need a backfill job; estimate 3 days effort to backfill 90d for top-20 using the offline rollups.

MVP timeline (assuming Priyom's estimates):
- Week 1: ETL + small rollup infra + API scaffolding
- Week 2: FE tiles + API integration + cache tuning
- Week 3: testing, backfill and polish, docs

If everyone is aligned I can spin up the cache and allocate infra quota for a 2-week sprint.

- Dylan

On Wed, Apr 14, 2027 at 08:45 AM Priyom Das <priyom.das@redwood.ai> wrote:
> Quick feasibility note below. Short answer: doable but requires a small pipeline + schema additions.
> 
> Data assumptions and needs:
> - Latency: we currently ingest per-request telemetry with a 1s timestamp; we have hourly and daily rollups but no retained 1m rollups for tenant-level p95 historically. To support 1h sparkline and a 5m near-real-time alert we can:
>   - Materialize 5m and 1h rollups keyed by tenant_id + route_id in a new rollup table. 30d retention for 5m, 90d for 1h should be sufficient for Console.
> - Spend: billing events exist as line items in billing_events (per-invoice). To show recent spend in near-real-time we need a streaming aggregator that groups billing events by tenant and applies the pricing map. If we show list-price units + estimated list-price $ we can avoid contract-rate lookups at render time.

From: Priyom Das <priyom.das@redwood.ai>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>
Cc: Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwood.ai>, Dylan Brooks <dylan.brooks@redwood.ai>
Date: Wed, 14 Apr 2027 08:45:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Action: small-multiples latency & spend tiles on Console home

Quick feasibility note below. Short answer: doable but requires a small pipeline + schema additions.

Data assumptions and needs:
- Latency: we currently ingest per-request telemetry with a 1s timestamp; we have hourly and daily rollups but no retained 1m rollups for tenant-level p95 historically. To support 1h sparkline and a 5m near-real-time alert we can:
  - Materialize 5m and 1h rollups keyed by tenant_id + route_id in a new rollup table. 30d retention for 5m, 90d for 1h should be sufficient for Console.
- Spend: billing events exist as line items in billing_events (per-invoice). To show recent spend in near-real-time we need a streaming aggregator that groups billing events by tenant and applies the pricing map. If we show list-price units + estimated list-price $ we can avoid contract-rate lookups at render time.

Suggested MVP scope:
- Build 1h rollups for top-20 tenants (weekly job to recompute top-20) + realtime windowed aggregator to keep a 5m p95 for only top-10 tenants.
- Expose an API endpoint /api/console/tiles/tenants that returns cached JSON per-home-view (recompute cache every 1m for top tenants).

Effort rough estimate:
- Rollup pipelines (ETL + infra): 2 engineers x 2 weeks
- API + cache: 1 engineer x 1 week
- Frontend tiles + drill UX: 1 FE engineer x 1 week (MVP limited visuals)
- Backfill scripts and testing: 1 engineer x 4 days

Storage/cost impact: expect additional storage for 5m rollups ~50GB/month for global tenant set; limiting to top-20 reduces that by ~10x. Also small incremental compute for streaming aggregator.

I attached a sample query that would power the API (cost-latency-rollup-proposal.sql).

Priyom
Senior Data Engineer, Platform Metrics
Redwood Inference

On Tue, Apr 13, 2027 at 10:02 AM Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai> wrote:
> Ava —
> 
> Love this — a few clarifying items from CS/field perspective:
> 
> - For \"tenant\" we should default to the billing tenant id that Finance uses (the same tag we surface in invoice exports). If we misalign on tag naming we will confuse accounts.
> - Customers will want a link from the tile directly to the current Mutual Action Plan (MAP) or open ticket. Could we have an optional 1-line footnote area for a linked MAP/PS ticket?
> - Regarding spend: many customers have committed discounts and multiple price tiers. At minimum we should show raw billed units + estimated list-price $ so sales/customer success have a consistent baseline.
> 
> Cc'ing Mike — Mike, would you prefer list-price display or your contracted rate on the tile?
> 
> On the engineering asks: I'll defer to Priyom and Dylan, but note that customers expect near-real-time for latency (<=5m) and daily accuracy for spend. If 1m rollups are expensive, we can show p95 as a gateway alert and keep the sparkline at 1h.
> 
> - Marissa


From: Dylan Brooks <dylan.brooks@redwood.ai>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai>, Priyom Das <priyom.das@redwood.ai>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwood.ai>
Date: Thu, 15 Apr 2027 11:20:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Action: small-multiples latency & spend tiles on Console home

Putting on my infra hat — a couple of notes and a recommended constrained MVP: 

- Live compute: computing 5m p95 for *all* tenants at 1m cadence is expensive. We should scope an MVP to top-10 tenants and provide a soft-fail for "others" (show last computed value + link to per-tenant page for on-demand compute).
- Cache strategy: use a read-through cache with 1m TTL for top tenants; fall back to on-demand worker for pinned tenants. This will keep Console home QPS affordable.
- Backfill: if we want historical sparklines beyond the cache window we need a backfill job; estimate 3 days effort to backfill 90d for top-20 using the offline rollups.

MVP timeline (assuming Priyom's estimates):
- Week 1: ETL + small rollup infra + API scaffolding
- Week 2: FE tiles + API integration + cache tuning
- Week 3: testing, backfill and polish, docs

If everyone is aligned I can spin up the cache and allocate infra quota for a 2-week sprint.

- Dylan

On Wed, Apr 14, 2027 at 08:45 AM Priyom Das <priyom.das@redwood.ai> wrote:
> Quick feasibility note below. Short answer: doable but requires a small pipeline + schema additions.
> 
> Data assumptions and needs:
> - Latency: we currently ingest per-request telemetry with a 1s timestamp; we have hourly and daily rollups but no retained 1m rollups for tenant-level p95 historically. To support 1h sparkline and a 5m near-real-time alert we can:
>   - Materialize 5m and 1h rollups keyed by tenant_id + route_id in a new rollup table. 30d retention for 5m, 90d for 1h should be sufficient for Console.
> - Spend: billing events exist as line items in billing_events (per-invoice). To show recent spend in near-real-time we need a streaming aggregator that groups billing events by tenant and applies the pricing map. If we show list-price units + estimated list-price $ we can avoid contract-rate lookups at render time.

source.subject: Platform fee allocation and tiered support options — request for breakdown
messages:
From: Jordan Kim <jordan.kim@acmehealth.com>
To: Aditya Rao <aditya_rao@redwood.ai>
Cc: Priya Singh <priya.singh@acmehealth.com>
Date: Mon, 12 Apr 2027 15:22:00 -0700
Subject: Clarify platform fee amortization + support tiers for VPC

Hi Aditya,

Thanks again for yesterday's demo — the team was impressed with the latency numbers and the routing options. Before we move to formal SOW language, procurement needs a clearer breakdown of how Redwood treats the platform fee vs. licensing and support. Specifically:

- Is the platform fee a one-time upfront license, an annual charge, or amortized monthly?
- For on-prem/VPC installs, do you offer a per-seat vs. per-token meter? Which is recommended for our usage profile (50k inference requests/day, 8k monthly active users)?
- Support tiers: can you confirm response times for P1/P2, and whether 24x7 on-call is included at the Standard vs. Premium support level?
- Any standard migration credits or one-time onboarding discounts for 2-year committed deals?

I attached our RFP excerpt and our SOC2 snapshot. If you can send a short SKU/line-item example we can drop into procurement for budget approval, that would help us move fast.

Best,
Jordan
Procurement Manager, Acme Health

Attachments: ACME_RFP_v2.pdf, SOC2-Attestation.pdf

From: Aditya Rao <aditya_rao@redwood.ai>
To: Jordan Kim <jordan.kim@acmehealth.com>
Cc: Priya Singh <priya.singh@acmehealth.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.ai>, Sean Gallagher <sean_gallagher@redwood.ai>
Date: Tue, 13 Apr 2027 09:14:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Clarify platform fee amortization + support tiers for VPC

Jordan — thanks for the clear list. Quick answers and an attached example line-item table below. TL;DR: platform fee is an annual subscription that can be amortized monthly; licensing for VPC is primarily capacity-committed (GPU hours) with optional seat-based admin licenses.

Short answers: 
- Platform fee: annual, invoiced up-front or spread monthly on invoice (we can accommodate customer accounting preferences). For a 2-year commitment we typically offer to credit 25% of year-1 platform fee pro-rated into year-2 (applied as a one-time migration credit).
- Licensing: we offer (A) committed capacity (preferred for predictable throughput) priced as vGPU-hour blocks, or (B) metered seat + token mix for variable workloads. For your profile we recommend committed capacity with a lightweight token meter for burst (saves ~18–25% vs pure metered).
- Support tiers: Standard = business hours with 4-hr P1 SLAs and next-business-day P2; Premium = 24x7 on-call with 1-hr P1 and 4-hr P2. Premium also includes quarterly architecture reviews and 24/7 escalation channel.
- Migration/onboarding credits: Standard practice is a one-time onboarding credit (15% of year-1 platform fee) tied to a 24-month commitment; we can move to 20% for deals > $1M ARR.

Attached is an example pricing breakdown (line items + sample invoice phrasing). Highlights:
- Platform Fee (Annual): $150,000
- Committed Capacity (vGPU): $0.95 / vGPU-hour (24/7 reserved) — estimated for your baseline: $360,000 / year
- Admin Seats (optional): $120 / seat / month (10 seats assumed)
- Premium Support (optional): $45,000 / year
- One-time migration credit: 15% of Platform Fee ($22,500) applied on first invoice for 24-month commit

If you want, Marissa can put together an alternative that replaces part of the committed capacity with a lower-commit burst pool to reduce upfront cost. Sean, can you confirm whether the proposed migration credit sits inside our standard discount band?

Best,
Aditya
Account Executive, Redwood Inference

Attachment: Redwood_LineItem_Example.xlsx

From: Jordan Kim <jordan.kim@acmehealth.com>
To: Aditya Rao <aditya_rao@redwood.ai>
Cc: Priya Singh <priya.singh@acmehealth.com>
Date: Wed, 14 Apr 2027 10:05:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Clarify platform fee amortization + support tiers for VPC

Aditya — this is helpful, thanks. A few follow-ups from procurement and security: 

- Can you put the migration credit and the Premium support cost into a single side-by-side comparison (A = committed capacity + Standard support + 15% credit; B = committed capacity + Premium support + 10% credit)? Procurement needs the dollar impact by year.
- SOC2 doc looks good; can you confirm whether audit logs for VPC are retained for 13 months by default or if we need an add-on? If add-on, cost please.
- We also need a short justification note for finance describing why committed capacity yields lower unit cost than metered.

If you can get a draft SOW and the side-by-side by EOD Thursday our legal will begin redline.

Regards,
Jordan

Quoted text:
> On Tue, 13 Apr 2027 at 09:14, Aditya Rao <aditya_rao@redwood.ai> wrote:
> > Quick answers and an attached example line-item table below...

From: Aditya Rao <aditya_rao@redwood.ai>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.ai>, Sean Gallagher <sean_gallagher@redwood.ai>, Laura Bennett <laura_bennett@redwood.ai>
Date: Thu, 15 Apr 2027 11:03:00 -0700
Subject: Fwd: Platform fee allocation and tiered support options — request for breakdown

Folks — forwarding Acme's follow-ups. I drafted the side-by-side below and flagged questions we need answers to from finance/legal. Please reply with approvals or edits: 

Proposal summary (numbers illustrative; final to come in SOW):
- Option A (Standard):
  - Year-1 Platform Fee: $150,000 (annual, invoice up-front)
  - Committed Capacity estimate: $360,000 / year
  - Standard Support: included in Platform Fee (4-hr P1)
  - Migration credit: 15% of platform fee ($22,500) applied on invoice 1
  - Year-1 Net: $487,500
- Option B (Premium):
  - Year-1 Platform Fee: $150,000
  - Committed Capacity: $360,000 / year
  - Premium Support add-on: $45,000 / year
  - Migration credit: 10% of platform fee ($15,000) applied on invoice 1
  - Year-1 Net: $540,000

Notes / action items:
- Sean: confirm whether we can extend migration credit to 20% if Acme signs 36 months or crosses $1.2M ARR threshold. If yes, I'll include a conditional line in the SOW.
- Marissa: please prepare a short finance justification paragraph on committed capacity vs. metered pricing (2–3 bullets) for Jordan's team.
- Laura: legal — confirm that 13-month audit log retention is standard for our VPC offering; if it's an add-on, estimate cost to propose.

If we greenlight, I’ll deliver a draft SOW + one-page commercial summary to Jordan by EOD Thursday.

Thanks,
Aditya
Account Executive, Redwood Inference

---- Forwarded message included above ----

source.subject: Command Center: final OKR slate for owner endorsement
messages:
From: Hannah Schmitt <hannah@redwood.com>
To: Kimberly Park <kimberly@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean@redwood.com>, Irene Choi <irene@redwood.com>
Cc: Emily Rivera <emily@acme-analytics.com>, Rajesh Malhotra <rajesh@nimbus.ai>
Date: Wed, 14 Apr 2027 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Command Center: final OKR slate for owner endorsement

Team —

Thanks for the prep yesterday. Attached is Q2 slate v3 with the owner assignments and the measurement rubrics populated (Q2_2027_OKR_slate_v3.pdf). This is the command-center view we’ll present in the QBR; before we lock it I need a quick owners' affirmation and any redlines by EOD today.

Top asks:
- Owners: please confirm you can commit to the measurement bar and the primary milestone dates. If not, flag which line item (sheet + row) and propose an alternate.
- Kimberly: we need an adjusted risk mitigation note on the Dedicated capacity cadence—one short paragraph.
- Marissa: call out any dependencies on Sales Ops for enablement assets; if you need procurement support, say so.
- Sean/Irene: two bullets on "fallback/resilience" strategy for model routing (we’ll include in the QBR slide).

I’ve put a 30m owners rollcall in your calendars (owners_rollcall.ics) for 4/15 10:00 PDT to confirm final sign-off. If anyone cannot make it, reply here with a time slot or sign-off in-line.

Link to working folder: https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1a2b3cQ2_OKR_Slates
HubSpot note: HUBSPOT-REC-98341

Thanks —
Hannah
Hannah Schmitt
VP, Product & GTM Operations
Redwood Inference
From: Kimberly Park <kimberly@redwood.com>
To: Hannah Schmitt <hannah@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean@redwood.com>, Irene Choi <irene@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 14 Apr 2027 10:01:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Command Center: final OKR slate for owner endorsement

Hannah —

Thanks, saw the slate. Quick notes:
- I can own the Dedicated cadence risk paragraph; draft below (feel free to paste into the doc):
  "Dedicated capacity cadence: commit to T-7 day spike window for seasonal load. If we anticipate sustained >60% peak utilization, trigger procurement fast-path; fallback to quantized model variant for best-effort SLA. Owning: Infra PM."
- Measurement bars look good overall; I flagged two rows where we should change success criteria from "50% latency improvement" to "median latency < 120ms" (I left comments in the PDF).

I can make the owners rollcall on 4/15.

—Kimberly
Kimberly Park
Head of Platform Product
Redwood Inference
From: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>

source.subject: Follow-up: Redwood demo & vendor setup for NetScale Health
messages:
From: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
To: Olivia Chen <olivia@netscalehealth.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 05 Apr 2027 16:12:00 -0700
Subject: Redwood demo recap & next steps (NetScale Health)

Hi Olivia —

Thanks again for taking time today. Quick recap from our call and recommended next steps so we have a shared checklist: 

- What we demoed: Redwood hosted API (low-friction start), Dedicated capacity options for predictable throughput, and the Console for routing/rollouts.
- Key fit: you mentioned strict latency targets for triage workflows and a 3-6 month pilot scope for 50-100k queries/month.
- Security asks: SOC2 + vendor onboarding (W-9 + ACH for payment) were called out by procurement.

Recommended next steps:
1) Send W-9/ACH to your procurement contact so they can start vendor setup (I can send the PDF).
2) Confirm procurement contact & ideal timeline for vendor portal invite (Ops needs vendor ID to enable test keys).
3) If helpful, Marissa can schedule a short technical onboarding to confirm token limits and batching options.

If priority shifted or timeline changed, just reply with a line and I’ll adjust.

I attached my annotated demo notes for your reference (highlights on latency vs batch tradeoffs).

Thanks — looking forward to next steps.

Best,
Ben Carter
Enterprise Account Executive
Redwood Inference
ben@redwood.com | (415) 555-0192

Attachments: demo-annotated-notes.pdf (document stub)
From: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
To: Olivia Chen <olivia@netscalehealth.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 08 Apr 2027 09:24:00 -0700
Subject: Quick check-in on vendor setup & timeline

Hi Olivia —

Just wanted to bump this in case procurement's queue changed. Any update on who on your team owns the vendor intake? If you prefer, I can route W-9/ACH to procurement@netscalehealth.com directly.

Also, appreciate any signal on priority — are you still targeting a pilot kickoff in May? If the window slipped, happy to push scheduling.

Thanks,
Ben

> Hi Olivia —
> 
> Thanks again for taking time today. Quick recap from our call and recommended next steps so we have a shared checklist:

> - Key fit: you mentioned strict latency targets for triage workflows and a 3-6 month pilot scope for 50-100k queries/month.
> - Security asks: SOC2 + vendor onboarding (W-9 + ACH for payment) were called out by procurement.
> 
> Recommended next steps:
> 1) Send W-9/ACH to your procurement contact so they can start vendor setup (I can send the PDF).
> 2) Confirm procurement contact & ideal timeline for vendor portal invite (Ops needs vendor ID to enable test keys).
> 3) If helpful, Marissa can schedule a short technical onboarding to confirm token limits and batching options.
> 
> If priority shifted or timeline changed, just reply with a line and I’ll adjust.
> 
> Best,
> Ben
From: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
To: procurement@netscalehealth.com, Olivia Chen <olivia@netscalehealth.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 12 Apr 2027 11:03:00 -0700
Subject: Vendor paperwork + request for portal invite (NetScale Health)

Hi procurement team / Olivia —

Following up after our discovery: procurement flagged W-9 + ACH as the primary gating items. I’ve attached both a completed W-9 and our ACH form to this thread to speed things along. Can you confirm receipt and whether you need any additional fields?

Attachments: NetScale_W9.pdf (W-9 stub), NetScale_ACH.pdf (ACH form stub)

Two quick asks to unblock onboarding: 
- Can you confirm when vendor portal invites are usually issued? We need vendor ID to add test keys and verify billing.
- If NetScale needs a sample invoice or Redwood bank details to complete vendor enrollment, I attached a short sample invoice at the link below.

DocuSign link for the Master Services Agreement (optional): https://www.docusign.net/envelope/ABC1234
HubSpot deal: https://app.hubspot.com/contacts/12345/deal/ND-2027-0412-001

If there’s a preferred procurement contact I should route vendor docs to directly, please let me know and I’ll send immediately.

Thanks,
Ben Carter
Enterprise Account Executive
Redwood Inference
ben@redwood.com | (415) 555-0192

On Thu, Apr 8, 2027 at 9:24 AM Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com> wrote:
> Hi Olivia —
> 
> Just wanted to bump this in case procurement's queue changed. Any update on who on your team owns the vendor intake? If you prefer, I can route W-9/ACH to procurement@netscalehealth.com directly.
> 
> Also, appreciate any signal on priority — are you still targeting a pilot kickoff in May? If the window slipped, happy to push scheduling.
> 
> Thanks,
> Ben
From: procurement@netscalehealth.com <procurement@netscalehealth.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>, Olivia Chen <olivia@netscalehealth.com>
Date: Wed, 14 Apr 2027 10:05:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Vendor paperwork + request for portal invite (NetScale Health)

Hi Ben / Olivia —

Thanks for the attachments — we received the W-9 and ACH form. Finance has routed the packet to Vendor Ops for verification. Two items from our side: 

1) Vendor Ops needs a sample invoice that includes Redwood's billing contact and bank details so they can validate ACH metadata. Can you upload a short sample invoice (PDF) to this thread?
2) Portal invites are handled by Vendor Ops; they are currently backlogged but have scheduled NetScale for issuance on 2027-04-16. Once the vendor profile is active you will receive an auto-generated invite to the portal.

For timing: assuming the vendor profile is accepted on 4/16, Vendor Ops will enable test API keys within 24 hours. ACH payments require a completed bank validation step after vendor activation — our team will share the next form.

Appreciate the quick turnaround. If you can attach the sample invoice here, I’ll mark this as complete on our end and loop Ops to expedite.

Best,
NetScale Procurement Team
procurement@netscalehealth.com

source.subject: Leadership recap: Novacare sponsor alignment, procurement ladder & critical ops blockers
messages:
From: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwoodai.com>
To: Alicia Moreno <alicia.moreno@novacare.com>, Carlos Vega <carlos.vega@novacare.com>
Cc: Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodai.com>
Date: Mon, 12 Apr 2027 15:05:00 -0700
Subject: Post-call recap & leadership highlights (next steps)

Alicia / Carlos —

Thanks again for the time today. Quick recap from our side so leadership at Novacare and ours are aligned on outcomes, risk, and the procurement path. Goal here is to make the sponsor/ops checklist explicit so your execs can see the sequence and we can avoid surprises during procurement.

Key pain points you confirmed today:
- Legacy inference cost + spiky patient-loads causing tiered GPU overprovisioning (Q1-2 peak weeks).
- Regulatory constraints for PHI handling and auditability (retention and key management).
- Need for predictable throughput for scheduled billing cycles and weekend batch jobs.

Business outcomes Novacare is chasing:
1) Reduce per-call inference cost by ~35% on average while preserving tail latency under 250ms for triage flows.
2) Provide auditable access logs and a KMS-backed key rotation policy for production data.
3) Pilot with 3 workloads (triage, summarization, search rerank) over a 60-day pilot and validate cost/SLA metrics.

Procurement / decision ladder (recommended):
- Week 0: SOC2 / security evidence review (we can send redacted evidence + architecture diagram).
- Week 1: Pilot SOW and pilot capacity sizing (dedicated pool or hosted with VPC).
- Week 2–4: Pilot run and weekly checkpoints; ops runbook and SLO definitions.
- Week 6–8: Procurement/contract signature and reserved capacity start (if committing).

Escalation / blockers we flagged in the call:
- Legal: potential dataplane residency clause for PHI — will need a Novacare legal handshake on KMS/HSM approach.
- Ops: internal firewalls and network proxy that may require extra setup windows for private deployment.
- Budget cadence: procurement cycles close late May — this suggests we prioritize a short pilot this quarter.

Attachments: SOC2_summary.pdf (redacted summary) and a draft procurement_timeline_draft.xlsx — both attached.

Next steps I propose (clear actions):
1) We send SOC2 summary + architecture diagram (today).
2) Novacare to confirm procurement lead and legal contact (you mentioned procurement@novacare.com) — Alicia to confirm.
3) If procurement agrees, we will draft a 60-day pilot SOW and an ops runbook template for your infra team.

Happy to set a 30-min follow-up with your procurement + legal + ops next week if that helps move things.

Thanks,

Kimberly Park
Senior Account Executive | Redwood Inference
kimberly.park@redwoodai.com | (415) 555-0182

Attachments: SOC2_summary.pdf (redacted summary); procurement_timeline_draft.xlsx
From: Alicia Moreno <alicia.moreno@novacare.com>
To: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwoodai.com>
Cc: Carlos Vega <carlos.vega@novacare.com>, Novacare Procurement <procurement@novacare.com>
Date: Mon, 12 Apr 2027 17:12:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Post-call recap & leadership highlights (next steps)

Thanks Kimberly — this is exactly the kind of summary our execs wanted to see. A couple quick clarifications and asks from our side:

1) Procurement / Legal: Please include the Novacare procurement alias on future threads (copied here). Our procurement lead (Erin Sato) will pick up next steps for contracting; she wants SOC2 evidence and a short data flow diagram emphasizing PHI boundaries.
2) Timeline: We like the idea of a 60-day pilot but need a firm estimate for committed vs. burst capacity cost lines for the pilot so execs can model the budget. Can you include expected cost per 100k tokens and an estimate for a 2-week peak that doubles traffic?
3) Ops blocker: We have an internal proxy that enforces outbound TLS inspection — we will need an ops workshop to validate private deployment connectivity.

I'll loop in Procurement and our Legal once I have the SOC2 summary and a short network runbook.

Alicia
VP Product Operations | Novacare
alicia.moreno@novacare.com | +1 617-555-3420

On Mon, Apr 12, 2027 at 15:05, Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwoodai.com> wrote:
> Alicia / Carlos —
> 
> Thanks again for the time today. Quick recap from our side so leadership at Novacare and ours are aligned on outcomes, risk, and the procurement path. Goal here is to make the sponsor/ops checklist explicit so your execs can see the sequence and we can avoid surprises during procurement.
> 
> [trimmed for thread]
From: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwoodai.com>
To: Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwoodai.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodai.com>
Date: Mon, 12 Apr 2027 18:02:00 -0700
Subject: Fwd: Post-call recap & leadership highlights (Novacare) — legal/ops asks

Team — forwarding Alicia's note and procurement CC. Short internal ask: can you validate legal flags and propose a redline timeline we can share with Erin (procurement)? Also: we need a concrete cost delta for pilot sizing (committed vs. burst).

Quick context: Novacare wants PHI-safe setup, KMS-backed keys, SOC2 evidence, and a connectivity workshop for their TLS inspection proxy. They asked for cost modeling that includes a 2-week peak that doubles traffic — call that a 2x tail.

Attachments included for your review: SOC2_summary.pdf (redacted), procurement_timeline_draft.xlsx

--- Forwarded message ---
From: Alicia Moreno <alicia.moreno@novacare.com>
Date: Mon, 12 Apr 2027 17:12:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Post-call recap & leadership highlights (next steps)
To: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwoodai.com>, Carlos Vega <carlos.vega@novacare.com>
Cc: Novacare Procurement <procurement@novacare.com>

> Thanks Kimberly — this is exactly the kind of summary our execs wanted to see. A couple quick clarifications and asks from our side:
> 
> 1) Procurement / Legal: Please include the Novacare procurement alias on future threads (copied here). Our procurement lead (Erin Sato) will pick up next steps for contracting; she wants SOC2 evidence and a short data flow diagram emphasizing PHI boundaries.
> 2) Timeline: We like the idea of a 60-day pilot but need a firm estimate for committed vs. burst capacity cost lines for the pilot so execs can model the budget. Can you include expected cost per 100k tokens and an estimate for a 2-week peak that doubles traffic?
> 3) Ops blocker: We have an internal proxy that enforces outbound TLS inspection — we will need an ops workshop to validate private deployment connectivity.

Kim — do you want me to include Erin or should we run the next note through legal first?

Thanks,
Alicia
From: Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwoodai.com>
To: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwoodai.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodai.com>, Alicia Moreno <alicia.moreno@novacare.com>
Date: Tue, 13 Apr 2027 08:12:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Fwd: Post-call recap & leadership highlights (Novacare) — legal/ops asks

Kimberly / Sean / Alicia —

Thanks for the clear summary. Legal flags and suggested path:

1) Data residency / PHI: We can offer a VPC-private deployment with customer-managed KMS; we will need a short MOU line in the SOW about key custody and HSM interfaces. I suggest the SOW include a 45-day evidence window for key rotation proofs.
2) SOC2 evidence: attached is a redacted SOC2_summary.pdf — I reviewed and this is safe for procurement review. For deeper validation, legal will request the SOC2 auditor letter under NDA.
3) Contract cadence: recommend the following gating items before signature:
   - Pilot SOW signed (scope + success criteria)
   - Network workshop scheduled and completed (ops validation)
   - Legal checklist (PHI, indemnity thresholds, KMS wording) accepted by both counsel
4) Procurement timeline: I drafted a redline timeline (legal_flags_and_redline_timeline.docx). Proposed turnaround: 10 business days from SOW to signature if procurement provides standard PO and we agree on KMS/indemnity language. If procurement requires custom legal terms, add 2–3 weeks.

Escalation note: If Novacare requires an on-premise air-gapped deployment (very unlikely), this will materially shift cost and timeline — flag to executive sponsors immediately.

Action items:
- Kimberly: Share the SOC2_summary.pdf with Erin Sato (procurement) and propose two slots for a 45–60 min network workshop.
- Sean: Can you pull a committed vs burst pilot cost model by EOD today? We need an estimate for baseline (committed) and a 2x peak scenario.
- Alicia / Procurement: If you can confirm Erin's email, we'll loop her into the SOW thread.

Thanks,
Aditya Rao
Senior Counsel | Redwood Inference
aditya.rao@redwoodai.com

Attachments: legal_flags_and_redline_timeline.docx
From: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwoodai.com>
To: Alicia Moreno <alicia.moreno@novacare.com>, Novacare Procurement <procurement@novacare.com>
Cc: Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodai.com>, Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwoodai.com>
Date: Tue, 13 Apr 2027 09:02:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Leadership recap: Novacare sponsor alignment, procurement ladder & critical ops blockers

Alicia / Erin / Carlos —

Thanks — attaching the redacted SOC2 summary and the legal redline timeline Aditya drafted. Sean and I will follow up with the pilot cost model this afternoon. Proposed next step: 45-minute network workshop on Thu 15 Apr at 10:00am PT (proposed) — please confirm Erin's availability or suggest two alternate slots.

To be explicit on the ask from Procurement when Erin steps in:
- Review SOC2_summary and confirm whether Novacare needs the auditor letter under NDA.
- Confirm procurement PO cadence and preferred SOW approval workflow (electronic signature vs. signed PDF).
- Flag any high-priority legal terms now (indemnity caps, data residency phrases) so we can include in the SOW draft.

If procurement confirms, we will:
1) Send a concise SOW draft with measurable success criteria for the 60-day pilot.
2) Schedule the network workshop and ops runbook review.
3) Provide committed vs. burst pilot cost lines (Sean).

Appreciate the quick turnaround here — Kim

Kimberly Park
Senior Account Executive | Redwood Inference
kimberly.park@redwoodai.com | (415) 555-0182

Attachments: SOC2_summary.pdf (redacted); legal_flags_and_redline_timeline.docx

Cc: Carlos Vega <carlos.vega@novacare.com>, Novacare Procurement <procurement@novacare.com>
Date: Mon, 12 Apr 2027 17:12:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Post-call recap & leadership highlights (next steps)

Thanks Kimberly — this is exactly the kind of summary our execs wanted to see. A couple quick clarifications and asks from our side:

1) Procurement / Legal: Please include the Novacare procurement alias on future threads (copied here). Our procurement lead (Erin Sato) will pick up next steps for contracting; she wants SOC2 evidence and a short data flow diagram emphasizing PHI boundaries.
2) Timeline: We like the idea of a 60-day pilot but need a firm estimate for committed vs. burst capacity cost lines for the pilot so execs can model the budget. Can you include expected cost per 100k tokens and an estimate for a 2-week peak that doubles traffic?
3) Ops blocker: We have an internal proxy that enforces outbound TLS inspection — we will need an ops workshop to validate private deployment connectivity.

I'll loop in Procurement and our Legal once I have the SOC2 summary and a short network runbook.

Alicia
VP Product Operations | Novacare
alicia.moreno@novacare.com | +1 617-555-3420

On Mon, Apr 12, 2027 at 15:05, Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwoodai.com> wrote:
> Alicia / Carlos —
> 
> Thanks again for the time today. Quick recap from our side so leadership at Novacare and ours are aligned on outcomes, risk, and the procurement path. Goal here is to make the sponsor/ops checklist explicit so your execs can see the sequence and we can avoid surprises during procurement.
> 
> [trimmed for thread]

From: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwoodai.com>
To: Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwoodai.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodai.com>
Date: Mon, 12 Apr 2027 18:02:00 -0700
Subject: Fwd: Post-call recap & leadership highlights (Novacare) — legal/ops asks

Team — forwarding Alicia's note and procurement CC. Short internal ask: can you validate legal flags and propose a redline timeline we can share with Erin (procurement)? Also: we need a concrete cost delta for pilot sizing (committed vs. burst).

Quick context: Novacare wants PHI-safe setup, KMS-backed keys, SOC2 evidence, and a connectivity workshop for their TLS inspection proxy. They asked for cost modeling that includes a 2-week peak that doubles traffic — call that a 2x tail.

Attachments included for your review: SOC2_summary.pdf (redacted), procurement_timeline_draft.xlsx

--- Forwarded message ---
From: Alicia Moreno <alicia.moreno@novacare.com>
Date: Mon, 12 Apr 2027 17:12:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Post-call recap & leadership highlights (next steps)
To: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwoodai.com>, Carlos Vega <carlos.vega@novacare.com>
Cc: Novacare Procurement <procurement@novacare.com>
