> 
> 1) Procurement / Legal: Please include the Novacare procurement alias on future threads (copied here). Our procurement lead (Erin Sato) will pick up next steps for contracting; she wants SOC2 evidence and a short data flow diagram emphasizing PHI boundaries.
> 2) Timeline: We like the idea of a 60-day pilot but need a firm estimate for committed vs. burst capacity cost lines for the pilot so execs can model the budget. Can you include expected cost per 100k tokens and an estimate for a 2-week peak that doubles traffic?
> 3) Ops blocker: We have an internal proxy that enforces outbound TLS inspection — we will need an ops workshop to validate private deployment connectivity.

Kim — do you want me to include Erin or should we run the next note through legal first?

Thanks,
Alicia


From: Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwoodai.com>
To: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwoodai.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodai.com>, Alicia Moreno <alicia.moreno@novacare.com>
Date: Tue, 13 Apr 2027 08:12:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Fwd: Post-call recap & leadership highlights (Novacare) — legal/ops asks

Kimberly / Sean / Alicia —

Thanks for the clear summary. Legal flags and suggested path:

1) Data residency / PHI: We can offer a VPC-private deployment with customer-managed KMS; we will need a short MOU line in the SOW about key custody and HSM interfaces. I suggest the SOW include a 45-day evidence window for key rotation proofs.
2) SOC2 evidence: attached is a redacted SOC2_summary.pdf — I reviewed and this is safe for procurement review. For deeper validation, legal will request the SOC2 auditor letter under NDA.
3) Contract cadence: recommend the following gating items before signature:
   - Pilot SOW signed (scope + success criteria)
   - Network workshop scheduled and completed (ops validation)
   - Legal checklist (PHI, indemnity thresholds, KMS wording) accepted by both counsel
4) Procurement timeline: I drafted a redline timeline (legal_flags_and_redline_timeline.docx). Proposed turnaround: 10 business days from SOW to signature if procurement provides standard PO and we agree on KMS/indemnity language. If procurement requires custom legal terms, add 2–3 weeks.

Escalation note: If Novacare requires an on-premise air-gapped deployment (very unlikely), this will materially shift cost and timeline — flag to executive sponsors immediately.

Action items:
- Kimberly: Share the SOC2_summary.pdf with Erin Sato (procurement) and propose two slots for a 45–60 min network workshop.
- Sean: Can you pull a committed vs burst pilot cost model by EOD today? We need an estimate for baseline (committed) and a 2x peak scenario.
- Alicia / Procurement: If you can confirm Erin's email, we'll loop her into the SOW thread.

Thanks,
Aditya Rao
Senior Counsel | Redwood Inference
aditya.rao@redwoodai.com

Attachments: legal_flags_and_redline_timeline.docx


From: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwoodai.com>
To: Alicia Moreno <alicia.moreno@novacare.com>, Novacare Procurement <procurement@novacare.com>
Cc: Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodai.com>, Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwoodai.com>
Date: Tue, 13 Apr 2027 09:02:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Leadership recap: Novacare sponsor alignment, procurement ladder & critical ops blockers

Alicia / Erin / Carlos —

Thanks — attaching the redacted SOC2 summary and the legal redline timeline Aditya drafted. Sean and I will follow up with the pilot cost model this afternoon. Proposed next step: 45-minute network workshop on Thu 15 Apr at 10:00am PT (proposed) — please confirm Erin's availability or suggest two alternate slots.

To be explicit on the ask from Procurement when Erin steps in:
- Review SOC2_summary and confirm whether Novacare needs the auditor letter under NDA.
- Confirm procurement PO cadence and preferred SOW approval workflow (electronic signature vs. signed PDF).
- Flag any high-priority legal terms now (indemnity caps, data residency phrases) so we can include in the SOW draft.

If procurement confirms, we will:
1) Send a concise SOW draft with measurable success criteria for the 60-day pilot.
2) Schedule the network workshop and ops runbook review.
3) Provide committed vs. burst pilot cost lines (Sean).

Appreciate the quick turnaround here — Kim

Kimberly Park
Senior Account Executive | Redwood Inference
kimberly.park@redwoodai.com | (415) 555-0182

Attachments: SOC2_summary.pdf (redacted); legal_flags_and_redline_timeline.docx

source.subject: Vanguard renewal: capacity commitment and throughput plan
messages:
From: Hannah Schmitt <hannah.schmitt@redwood.ai>
To: Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwood.ai>, Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.ai>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.ai>
Date: Thu, 1 Apr 2027 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Vanguard renewal: capacity planning and recommended commit

Team —

Vanguard's renewal is up next month and procurement has signaled they want a clear throughput commitment. I pulled the usage file (attached) and ran a quick runway:

- Current reserved pool: 16 x A100-40GB (region us-west-2).
- Baseline (last 30d avg): 320 RPS for short completions (avg ~350 tokens / request).
- Trailing 3-month token growth: ~28% MoM (driven by new persona features).
- Projection: conservative scenario = +45% RPS over 12 months (~464 RPS), aggressive = +90% (~608 RPS).

Sizing assumptions (from infra sims): an optimized A100 instance in our runtime sustains ~22k tokens/s under the current model config at p50, with headroom considerations dropping to ~15k tokens/s at p95 when accounting for KV cache inefficiencies and bursts.

Quick calc: at 350 tokens/request:
- 16 GPUs * 15k tokens/s = 240k tokens/s capacity -> ~685 RPS at p95 headroom.
- Conservative demand (464 RPS) -> ~162k tokens/s required.
- Aggressive demand (608 RPS) -> ~212.8k tokens/s required.

Recommendation (for renewal conversation):
1) Commit to 28 GPUs (add 12) reserved A100s to create a 1.4x buffer above aggressive forecast. This yields ~420k tokens/s raw capacity (p95 headroom ~300k tokens/s), cover bursts and regional failover.
2) Offer a two-tier SLO: 99.9% availability on reserved throughput (tokens/sec baseline) + 20% burst pool at on-demand pricing.
3) Price: propose a blended rate for the 12 additional GPUs at a 12% discount vs list for a 12-month term; include a 90-day ramp credit if usage climbs slower than forecast.

Attachments: vanguard_usage_q1_2027.csv, proposed_commit_matrix_Q2-2027.xlsx

Please review — Sean, can you draft the external ask with two commitment options (conservative: +8 GPUs; recommended: +12 GPUs w/ burst), and include the SLO language? Karthik, can you sanity-check the per-GPU throughput numbers and add any constraints around long-context requests?

Hannah
Director, Enterprise Sales
Redwood Inference
From: Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwood.ai>
To: Hannah Schmitt <hannah.schmitt@redwood.ai>, Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.ai>
Cc: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.ai>
Date: Fri, 2 Apr 2027 10:03:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Vanguard renewal: capacity planning and recommended commit

Thanks Hannah — good summary. I like offering two clear options to make procurement's decision easier. Draft external options below; please flag edits.

Option A (Conservative): +8 GPUs (total 24)
- Expected p95 capacity ~180k tokens/s -> covers conservative demand + 10% buffer.
- 12-month term. No ramp credit.
- Pricing: list rate for incremental units.

Option B (Recommended): +12 GPUs (total 28)
- Expected p95 capacity ~300k tokens/s -> covers aggressive demand with room for bursts/failover.
- 12-month term. 12% discount on incremental units. 90-day ramp credit if utilization < 60% of projected by month 3.
- Add 20% shared burst pool (on-demand) for traffic spikes.

SLO: 99.9% throughput availability (measured as tokens/sec per region averaged daily). Failure modes and fallback: automatic routing to on-demand pool and cross-region failover to us-east-1 in case reserved pool saturates.

I'll send this to Alex with both options and ask for a quick sizing call. Monica — can you attach the updated matrix and the high-level pricing bullets?

Sean
Sr. Account Exec
Redwood Inference
From: Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwood.ai>
To: Alex Rivers <alex.rivers@vanguardht.com>
Cc: Samantha Jensen <samantha.jensen@vanguardht.com>, procurement@vanguardht.com, Hannah Schmitt <hannah.schmitt@redwood.ai>, Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.ai>
Date: Mon, 5 Apr 2027 08:30:00 -0700
Subject: Vanguard renewal options and throughput commitment (two proposals attached)

Alex —

Great working with you on the renewal timeline. Per our conversation, attached are two commitment options that map to the usage scenarios we discussed. TL;DR: the recommended path (Option B) is designed to avoid a forced uplift mid-term and includes a burst pool to handle feature-driven spikes.

Attachments: proposed_commit_matrix_Q2-2027.xlsx, throughput_modeling_notes_ky_2027.pdf

Key asks:
- Which option aligns with your budget/procurement constraints?
- Do you need the SLO to be tokens/sec or sustained RPS — we can express either, but tokens/sec maps directly to cost and KV cache headroom.
- Can we book a 30-minute sizing call this week to lock the final commit? Here’s my calendar: https://cal.me/sean-gallagher/30

Thanks,
Sean
From: Alex Rivers <alex.rivers@vanguardht.com>
To: Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwood.ai>, Samantha Jensen <samantha.jensen@vanguardht.com>, procurement@vanguardht.com
Cc: Hannah Schmitt <hannah.schmitt@redwood.ai>
Date: Wed, 7 Apr 2027 11:05:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Vanguard renewal options and throughput commitment (two proposals attached)

Sean,

Thanks for the clear options. A few clarifying questions before we book time with procurement:

1) Can you convert the tokens/sec SLO to an equivalent p95 latency at our workload? Our PMs care about user-perceived latency rather than tokens.
2) How does the burst pool billing trigger — instant overage vs. sustained over X minutes?
3) We have two heavy long-context workflows (avg 2k tokens) that the engineering team flagged as a concern — will those materially reduce overall p95 capacity?

Procurement: cc’d — we prefer a single committed figure for the purchase order but want the ability to scale faster if adoption accelerates.

Alex
Head of Platform
Vanguard HealthTech
From: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.ai>
To: Alex Rivers <alex.rivers@vanguardht.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwood.ai>
Cc: Hannah Schmitt <hannah.schmitt@redwood.ai>, Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.ai>
Date: Thu, 8 Apr 2027 09:08:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Vanguard renewal options and throughput commitment (technical note)

Alex / team —

Quick technical note to answer the long-context question and latency mapping:

- Short-context baseline (avg 350 tokens): our internal tests for the current model + runtime show ~18k tokens/s per A100 at p50 and ~12k tokens/s per A100 at p95 under production batching and KV cache behavior. That maps to ~34ms p50 token latency and ~55-75ms p95 token latency; end-to-end p95 for a 350-token request (generation only) is ~200-260ms.
- Long-context workloads (avg 2k tokens) are amortized differently: higher memory pressure increases KV cache misses and reduces throughput to roughly 6-7k tokens/s per A100 at p95. For sizing, treat each long-context request as ~3.5x the token-cost of a short request.

Burst billing: we propose a billing window of 10 minutes where sustained consumption above reserved throughput triggers the burst pool rate. Short spikes (<10s) are absorbed by our automatic request shaping and prefix/KV caching — they should not immediately incur burst billing.

If you want a more granular sim, I can run your two representative traces against our test harness and return a per-route GPU equivalent number.

Karthik
Senior Infra Engineer, Serving Runtime
Redwood Inference
From: Hannah Schmitt <hannah.schmitt@redwood.ai>
To: Alex Rivers <alex.rivers@vanguardht.com>, procurement@vanguardht.com
Cc: Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwood.ai>, Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.ai>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.ai>
Date: Mon, 12 Apr 2027 16:40:00 -0700
Subject: RE: Vanguard renewal options and throughput commitment (final terms)

Alex / Procurement —

Thanks for the notes and for surfacing the long-context workflows. Based on Karthik's guidance and Hannah's original runway, we've revised the proposal to a single recommended commit to simplify procurement:

- Commit: 28 x A100-40GB reserved GPUs (us-west-2) for 12 months.
- Throughput SLO: 99.9% tokens/sec availability measured daily (we will provide the daily throughput metric dashboard).
- Burst policy: 20% shared burst pool billed on sustained consumption >10 minutes. Short spikes will be absorbed.
- Pricing: incremental units at 12% discount vs list; 90-day ramp credit if utilization <60% of projected.
- Implementation notes: we will run a pre-renewal acceptance test (two representative traces) and finalize the DocuSign by EOD Friday once acceptance metrics are agreed.

Attachments: proposed_commit_matrix_Q2-2027.xlsx, throughput_modeling_notes_ky_2027.pdf

DocuSign: https://app.docusign.com/link/vg-commit-2027
Drive folder for evidence: https://drive.redwood.ai/drive/folders/1a2b3c-vanguard-renewal

Alex — can you confirm procurement can accept a single PO for 28 units and whether we should mark the order as a fixed-term commitment or an auto-renew with 30-day cancellation?

Hannah
Director, Enterprise Sales
Redwood Inference

Date: Fri, 2 Apr 2027 10:03:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Vanguard renewal: capacity planning and recommended commit

Thanks Hannah — good summary. I like offering two clear options to make procurement's decision easier. Draft external options below; please flag edits.

Option A (Conservative): +8 GPUs (total 24)
- Expected p95 capacity ~180k tokens/s -> covers conservative demand + 10% buffer.
- 12-month term. No ramp credit.
- Pricing: list rate for incremental units.

Option B (Recommended): +12 GPUs (total 28)
- Expected p95 capacity ~300k tokens/s -> covers aggressive demand with room for bursts/failover.
- 12-month term. 12% discount on incremental units. 90-day ramp credit if utilization < 60% of projected by month 3.
- Add 20% shared burst pool (on-demand) for traffic spikes.

SLO: 99.9% throughput availability (measured as tokens/sec per region averaged daily). Failure modes and fallback: automatic routing to on-demand pool and cross-region failover to us-east-1 in case reserved pool saturates.

I'll send this to Alex with both options and ask for a quick sizing call. Monica — can you attach the updated matrix and the high-level pricing bullets?

Sean
Sr. Account Exec
Redwood Inference


From: Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwood.ai>
To: Alex Rivers <alex.rivers@vanguardht.com>
Cc: Samantha Jensen <samantha.jensen@vanguardht.com>, procurement@vanguardht.com, Hannah Schmitt <hannah.schmitt@redwood.ai>, Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.ai>
Date: Mon, 5 Apr 2027 08:30:00 -0700
Subject: Vanguard renewal options and throughput commitment (two proposals attached)

Alex —

Great working with you on the renewal timeline. Per our conversation, attached are two commitment options that map to the usage scenarios we discussed. TL;DR: the recommended path (Option B) is designed to avoid a forced uplift mid-term and includes a burst pool to handle feature-driven spikes.

Attachments: proposed_commit_matrix_Q2-2027.xlsx, throughput_modeling_notes_ky_2027.pdf

Key asks:
- Which option aligns with your budget/procurement constraints?
- Do you need the SLO to be tokens/sec or sustained RPS — we can express either, but tokens/sec maps directly to cost and KV cache headroom.
- Can we book a 30-minute sizing call this week to lock the final commit? Here’s my calendar: https://cal.me/sean-gallagher/30

Thanks,
Sean


From: Alex Rivers <alex.rivers@vanguardht.com>
To: Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwood.ai>, Samantha Jensen <samantha.jensen@vanguardht.com>, procurement@vanguardht.com
Cc: Hannah Schmitt <hannah.schmitt@redwood.ai>
Date: Wed, 7 Apr 2027 11:05:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Vanguard renewal options and throughput commitment (two proposals attached)

Sean,

Thanks for the clear options. A few clarifying questions before we book time with procurement:

1) Can you convert the tokens/sec SLO to an equivalent p95 latency at our workload? Our PMs care about user-perceived latency rather than tokens.
2) How does the burst pool billing trigger — instant overage vs. sustained over X minutes?
3) We have two heavy long-context workflows (avg 2k tokens) that the engineering team flagged as a concern — will those materially reduce overall p95 capacity?

Procurement: cc’d — we prefer a single committed figure for the purchase order but want the ability to scale faster if adoption accelerates.

Alex
Head of Platform
Vanguard HealthTech


From: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.ai>
To: Alex Rivers <alex.rivers@vanguardht.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwood.ai>
Cc: Hannah Schmitt <hannah.schmitt@redwood.ai>, Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.ai>
Date: Thu, 8 Apr 2027 09:08:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Vanguard renewal options and throughput commitment (technical note)

Alex / team —

Quick technical note to answer the long-context question and latency mapping:

- Short-context baseline (avg 350 tokens): our internal tests for the current model + runtime show ~18k tokens/s per A100 at p50 and ~12k tokens/s per A100 at p95 under production batching and KV cache behavior. That maps to ~34ms p50 token latency and ~55-75ms p95 token latency; end-to-end p95 for a 350-token request (generation only) is ~200-260ms.
- Long-context workloads (avg 2k tokens) are amortized differently: higher memory pressure increases KV cache misses and reduces throughput to roughly 6-7k tokens/s per A100 at p95. For sizing, treat each long-context request as ~3.5x the token-cost of a short request.

Burst billing: we propose a billing window of 10 minutes where sustained consumption above reserved throughput triggers the burst pool rate. Short spikes (<10s) are absorbed by our automatic request shaping and prefix/KV caching — they should not immediately incur burst billing.

If you want a more granular sim, I can run your two representative traces against our test harness and return a per-route GPU equivalent number.

Karthik
Senior Infra Engineer, Serving Runtime
Redwood Inference


From: Hannah Schmitt <hannah.schmitt@redwood.ai>
To: Alex Rivers <alex.rivers@vanguardht.com>, procurement@vanguardht.com
Cc: Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwood.ai>, Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.ai>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.ai>
Date: Mon, 12 Apr 2027 16:40:00 -0700
Subject: RE: Vanguard renewal options and throughput commitment (final terms)

Alex / Procurement —

Thanks for the notes and for surfacing the long-context workflows. Based on Karthik's guidance and Hannah's original runway, we've revised the proposal to a single recommended commit to simplify procurement:

- Commit: 28 x A100-40GB reserved GPUs (us-west-2) for 12 months.
- Throughput SLO: 99.9% tokens/sec availability measured daily (we will provide the daily throughput metric dashboard).
- Burst policy: 20% shared burst pool billed on sustained consumption >10 minutes. Short spikes will be absorbed.
- Pricing: incremental units at 12% discount vs list; 90-day ramp credit if utilization <60% of projected.
- Implementation notes: we will run a pre-renewal acceptance test (two representative traces) and finalize the DocuSign by EOD Friday once acceptance metrics are agreed.

Attachments: proposed_commit_matrix_Q2-2027.xlsx, throughput_modeling_notes_ky_2027.pdf

DocuSign: https://app.docusign.com/link/vg-commit-2027
Drive folder for evidence: https://drive.redwood.ai/drive/folders/1a2b3c-vanguard-renewal

Alex — can you confirm procurement can accept a single PO for 28 units and whether we should mark the order as a fixed-term commitment or an auto-renew with 30-day cancellation?

Hannah
Director, Enterprise Sales
Redwood Inference

source.subject: Prod impact: post-rollout quantized model causing 5xx/429 surge
messages:
From: Timothy Reynolds <tim.reynolds@finlytics.com>
To: Kimberly Park <kimberly_park@redwood.ai>
Cc: support@finlytics.com
Date: Thu, 8 Apr 2027 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Production timeouts since model v2.3-q4 rollout

Hi Kimberly / Redwood team,

We started seeing elevated 5xx/429 rates and a large number of client timeouts starting around 03:30 PDT after your rollout on 4/6. Error rate jumped dramatically and several production jobs have been retried multiple times causing a backlog. Sample incident: our payment reconciliation pipeline timed out repeatedly (lots of 504s) and our retry logic caused a retry storm that impacted downstream systems.

Quick facts from our side:
- 5xx rate (API calls to Redwood) rose from ~0.12% baseline to ~8.7% within the hour
- P95 latency increased from ~120ms to ~860ms for the affected endpoint
- We saw both 429 (rate limit / throttling) and 504 (gateway timeout) responses

We believe this correlates with the quantized model variant you rolled (v2.3-q4-int8) and the related kernel change (nvfuser-v2). Can you confirm whether Redwood sees the same spike and what your immediate mitigation is? This is now on our executives' radar.

Attaching a zip of our client-side logs and a short timeline. Please treat as high priority.

Thanks,
Tim
Timothy Reynolds
VP of Engineering, FinLytics
---
Attachments: finlytics-request-logs-20270408.zip (log bundle)

From: Kimberly Park <kimberly_park@redwood.ai>
To: Timothy Reynolds <tim.reynolds@finlytics.com>
Cc: Aditya Rao <aditya_rao@redwood.ai>, Laura Bennett <laura_bennett@redwood.ai>, Sean Gallagher <sean_gallagher@redwood.ai>
Date: Thu, 8 Apr 2027 09:28:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Production timeouts since model v2.3-q4 rollout

Tim — thank you for the report and the logs. We see the same signal in our monitoring and PagerDuty incident PDR-12458 is active. Apologies for the customer impact. We're mobilizing an internal triage and will keep you updated with an ETA for a mitigation plan within 60 minutes.

Immediate actions we're taking:
- Engaging infra and model-serving teams (cc'd).
- Collecting perf/trace slices for the affected route and model variant.
- Preparing an emergency rollback to the last known-good model (v2.2) and disabling the new quantization profile for that route if needed.

Could you confirm whether you are on Redwood Hosted (API key) or Dedicated? Also whether you have any pinned model versions in your config for this route.

—Kimberly
Kimberly Park
Head of Sales, Redwood Inference
Redwood Inference
Phone: +1 (415) 555-0124
---
Attachment: rollback-checklist.md (small checklist for customer-facing rollback notes)

From: Laura Bennett <laura_bennett@redwood.ai>
To: Kimberly Park <kimberly_park@redwood.ai>, Aditya Rao <aditya_rao@redwood.ai>
Cc: Sean Gallagher <sean_gallagher@redwood.ai>, Marcus Lin <marcus_lin@redwood.ai>
Date: Thu, 8 Apr 2027 09:43:00 -07:00
Subject: RE: Production timeouts since model v2.3-q4 rollout — initial triage

Folks — quick summary of what we see from the serving side (v2.3-q4-int8 / nvfuser-v2 rollout):

Observations: 
- The quantized path (int8, q4-profile) exhibits a ~7–12% increase in CPU-side kernel latency for short sequences (<=64), which is amplified by continuous-batching and leads to head-of-line blocking.
- Measured 5xx/429 spike aligns exactly with the canary ramp window (03:18–03:41 PDT).
- Metric highlights (global for the affected pool):
  - 5xx rate: baseline 0.12% -> observed 8.7% at peak
  - 429 rate: baseline ~0.03% -> 3.4%
  - p95 latency (tokens/sec path): 120ms -> 860ms
  - Avg memory pressure per GPU thread rose ~18% leading to occasional kernel timeouts in nvfuser-v2 when under high batch concurrency
- Error signature in logs: repeated "kv_cache_miss -> recompute" patterns followed by long tail RPC timeouts and then 504 at the gateway.

Preliminary root cause hypothesis: an interaction between the new nvfuser-v2 kernel scheduling and the int8 quantization micro-kernels increases scheduling jitter under continuous batching. This produces head-of-line blocking that cascades into client retries and throttling. The behavior is model-variant specific (doesn't reproduce on v2.2 / float16).

Immediate recommendation (safest fastest rollback path):
1) Rollback the affected pool to model v2.2 (float16) for the FinLytics customer and any other routes using the v2.3-q4 variant.
2) Disable the q4-int8 profile globally for 24–48 hrs while we reproduce and capture targeted perf traces.
3) Increase short-term concurrency slots for the pool (scale up + temporary per-pod buffer) to reduce tail latencies during rollback window.

I can prepare a hot rollback playbook and run the rollback with infra — will need a 7 minute maintenance window to shift traffic and validate.

—Laura
Laura Bennett
Senior Software Engineer, Serving Runtime
Redwood Inference
---
Attachment: quant-rollout-metrics-20270409.png (perf graph)

From: Aditya Rao <aditya_rao@redwood.ai>
To: Kimberly Park <kimberly_park@redwood.ai>, Laura Bennett <laura_bennett@redwood.ai>
Cc: Sean Gallagher <sean_gallagher@redwood.ai>, Marcus Lin <marcus_lin@redwood.ai>
Date: Thu, 8 Apr 2027 10:02:00 -07:00
Subject: RE: Production timeouts — infra mitigation options

On infra side: we corroborate the memory pressure and scheduling jitter Laura called out. Two pragmatic mitigations while we prepare rollback:
- Short-term: increase pool capacity by +30% in us-west to absorb retry bursts (spin up warm Dedicated nodes, will take ~9 minutes).
- Middleware: apply a conservative client-side throttling policy for FinLytics route (leaky-bucket cap) to avoid further retry storms until rollback completes. We can push a routing override at edge for that customer specifically.
- We should NOT change global rate limits without coordination (risk amplifying other customers' latency).

If the customer agrees to a brief maintenance window, I'll coordinate capacity add and routing override.

—Aditya
Aditya Rao
Director, Infrastructure
Redwood Inference
---
Related: PagerDuty PDR-12458, Jira SUP-3421

From: Kimberly Park <kimberly_park@redwood.ai>
To: Timothy Reynolds <tim.reynolds@finlytics.com>
Cc: Aditya Rao <aditya_rao@redwood.ai>, Laura Bennett <laura_bennett@redwood.ai>, Sean Gallagher <sean_gallagher@redwood.ai>
Date: Thu, 8 Apr 2027 10:16:00 -07:00
Subject: Action plan and rollback ETA (FinLytics) — high priority

Tim — internal triage complete. Proposed plan to restore normalcy:

1) Immediate mitigation (now): apply an edge routing override to limit concurrency for FinLytics route (conservative throttling) and spin up +30% warm capacity in us-west. Estimated time: 10 minutes.
2) Hot rollback (10:30 PDT): rollback the FinLytics-serving pool from v2.3-q4-int8 (nvfuser-v2) -> v2.2-fp16. This is a traffic switch to the previous model variant, no code changes. Estimated maintenance window: 7 minutes.
3) Post-rollback validation (10:30–10:40 PDT): run synthetic and canary traffic validation, confirm 5xx drop to baseline and p95 latency return to expected.

We will run rollback at 10:30 PDT unless you request otherwise. Please confirm you are ok with the plan and we will proceed. I know this is urgent — we will also prep a postmortem once the immediate incident is closed.

—Kimberly
Kimberly Park
Head of Sales, Redwood Inference
Redwood Inference
Phone: +1 (415) 555-0124

From: Timothy Reynolds <tim.reynolds@finlytics.com>
To: Kimberly Park <kimberly_park@redwood.ai>
Cc: support@finlytics.com
Date: Thu, 8 Apr 2027 10:21:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Action plan and rollback ETA (FinLytics) — high priority

Thanks — please proceed with the rollback at 10:30 PDT. We will pause any client-side heavy-batch jobs during the window. Appreciate the quick coordination. Our execs (CTO + Head of Ops) are standing by for confirmation once the smoke clears.

—Tim
Timothy Reynolds
VP of Engineering, FinLytics

---
On Thu, 8 Apr 2027 at 10:16, Kimberly Park <kimberly_park@redwood.ai> wrote:
> Tim — internal triage complete. Proposed plan to restore normalcy:
> 
> 1) Immediate mitigation (now): apply an edge routing override to limit concurrency for FinLytics route (conservative throttling) and spin up +30% warm capacity in us-west. Estimated time: 10 minutes.
> 2) Hot rollback (10:30 PDT): rollback the FinLytics-serving pool from v2.3-q4-int8 (nvfuser-v2) -> v2.2-fp16. This is a traffic switch to the previous model variant, no code changes. Estimated maintenance window: 7 minutes.
> 3) Post-rollback validation (10:30–10:40 PDT): run synthetic and canary traffic validation, confirm 5xx drop to baseline and p95 latency return to expected.
> 
> We will run rollback at 10:30 PDT unless you request otherwise. Please confirm you are ok with the plan and we will proceed. I know this is urgent — we will also prep a postmortem once the immediate incident is closed.
> 
> —Kimberly

Folks — quick summary of what we see from the serving side (v2.3-q4-int8 / nvfuser-v2 rollout):

Observations: 
- The quantized path (int8, q4-profile) exhibits a ~7–12% increase in CPU-side kernel latency for short sequences (<=64), which is amplified by continuous-batching and leads to head-of-line blocking.
- Measured 5xx/429 spike aligns exactly with the canary ramp window (03:18–03:41 PDT).
- Metric highlights (global for the affected pool):
  - 5xx rate: baseline 0.12% -> observed 8.7% at peak
  - 429 rate: baseline ~0.03% -> 3.4%
  - p95 latency (tokens/sec path): 120ms -> 860ms
  - Avg memory pressure per GPU thread rose ~18% leading to occasional kernel timeouts in nvfuser-v2 when under high batch concurrency
- Error signature in logs: repeated \"kv_cache_miss -> recompute\" patterns followed by long tail RPC timeouts and then 504 at the gateway.

Preliminary root cause hypothesis: an interaction between the new nvfuser-v2 kernel scheduling and the int8 quantization micro-kernels increases scheduling jitter under continuous batching. This produces head-of-line blocking that cascades into client retries and throttling. The behavior is model-variant specific (doesn't reproduce on v2.2 / float16).

Immediate recommendation (safest fastest rollback path):
1) Rollback the affected pool to model v2.2 (float16) for the FinLytics customer and any other routes using the v2.3-q4 variant.
2) Disable the q4-int8 profile globally for 24–48 hrs while we reproduce and capture targeted perf traces.
3) Increase short-term concurrency slots for the pool (scale up + temporary per-pod buffer) to reduce tail latencies during rollback window.

I can prepare a hot rollback playbook and run the rollback with infra — will need a 7 minute maintenance window to shift traffic and validate.

—Laura
Laura Bennett
Senior Software Engineer, Serving Runtime
Redwood Inference
---
Attachment: quant-rollout-metrics-20270409.png (perf graph)

From: Aditya Rao <aditya_rao@redwood.ai>
To: Kimberly Park <kimberly_park@redwood.ai>, Laura Bennett <laura_bennett@redwood.ai>
Cc: Sean Gallagher <sean_gallagher@redwood.ai>, Marcus Lin <marcus_lin@redwood.ai>
Date: Thu, 8 Apr 2027 10:02:00 -07:00
Subject: RE: Production timeouts — infra mitigation options

On infra side: we corroborate the memory pressure and scheduling jitter Laura called out. Two pragmatic mitigations while we prepare rollback:
- Short-term: increase pool capacity by +30% in us-west to absorb retry bursts (spin up warm Dedicated nodes, will take ~9 minutes).
- Middleware: apply a conservative client-side throttling policy for FinLytics route (leaky-bucket cap) to avoid further retry storms until rollback completes. We can push a routing override at edge for that customer specifically.
- We should NOT change global rate limits without coordination (risk amplifying other customers' latency).

If the customer agrees to a brief maintenance window, I'll coordinate capacity add and routing override.

—Aditya
Aditya Rao
Director, Infrastructure
Redwood Inference
---
Related: PagerDuty PDR-12458, Jira SUP-3421

From: Kimberly Park <kimberly_park@redwood.ai>
To: Timothy Reynolds <tim.reynolds@finlytics.com>
Cc: Aditya Rao <aditya_rao@redwood.ai>, Laura Bennett <laura_bennett@redwood.ai>, Sean Gallagher <sean_gallagher@redwood.ai>
Date: Thu, 8 Apr 2027 10:16:00 -07:00
Subject: Action plan and rollback ETA (FinLytics) — high priority

Tim — internal triage complete. Proposed plan to restore normalcy:

1) Immediate mitigation (now): apply an edge routing override to limit concurrency for FinLytics route (conservative throttling) and spin up +30% warm capacity in us-west. Estimated time: 10 minutes.
2) Hot rollback (10:30 PDT): rollback the FinLytics-serving pool from v2.3-q4-int8 (nvfuser-v2) -> v2.2-fp16. This is a traffic switch to the previous model variant, no code changes. Estimated maintenance window: 7 minutes.
3) Post-rollback validation (10:30–10:40 PDT): run synthetic and canary traffic validation, confirm 5xx drop to baseline and p95 latency return to expected.

We will run rollback at 10:30 PDT unless you request otherwise. Please confirm you are ok with the plan and we will proceed. I know this is urgent — we will also prep a postmortem once the immediate incident is closed.

—Kimberly
Kimberly Park
Head of Sales, Redwood Inference
Redwood Inference
Phone: +1 (415) 555-0124

From: Timothy Reynolds <tim.reynolds@finlytics.com>
To: Kimberly Park <kimberly_park@redwood.ai>
Cc: support@finlytics.com
Date: Thu, 8 Apr 2027 10:21:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Action plan and rollback ETA (FinLytics) — high priority

Thanks — please proceed with the rollback at 10:30 PDT. We will pause any client-side heavy-batch jobs during the window. Appreciate the quick coordination. Our execs (CTO + Head of Ops) are standing by for confirmation once the smoke clears.

—Tim
Timothy Reynolds
VP of Engineering, FinLytics

---
On Thu, 8 Apr 2027 at 10:16, Kimberly Park <kimberly_park@redwood.ai> wrote:
> Tim — internal triage complete. Proposed plan to restore normalcy:
> 
> 1) Immediate mitigation (now): apply an edge routing override to limit concurrency for FinLytics route (conservative throttling) and spin up +30% warm capacity in us-west. Estimated time: 10 minutes.
> 2) Hot rollback (10:30 PDT): rollback the FinLytics-serving pool from v2.3-q4-int8 (nvfuser-v2) -> v2.2-fp16. This is a traffic switch to the previous model variant, no code changes. Estimated maintenance window: 7 minutes.
> 3) Post-rollback validation (10:30–10:40 PDT): run synthetic and canary traffic validation, confirm 5xx drop to baseline and p95 latency return to expected.
>

> 
> —Kimberly

source.subject: Request: Move ClearPath technical demo — finance blackout week
messages:
From: Chloe Martin <chloe.martin@redwoodinfer.com>
To: Lucia Ramos <lucia.ramos@clearpath.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinfer.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinfer.com>
Date: 2027-03-29T09:12:00-07:00
Subject: ClearPath technical demo — proposed windows + agenda

Hi Lucia / Taylor,

Thanks again for the time on the discovery call last week — excited to run a tailored technical demo for the ClearPath infra team. Per our discussion, attached: a one-page agenda (demo-agenda-v2.docx) and the draft MSA we discussed so procurement can start an initial review (clearpath-MSA-draft.pdf).

Proposed slots (Pacific):
- Tue Apr 5 — 10:00–11:15 (deep technical walkthrough + Q&A)
- Wed Apr 6 — 13:00–14:15 (deep technical walkthrough + Q&A)
- Thu Apr 7 — 09:00–09:45 (30-min exec preview)

If procurement wants to be looped in earlier, please share a contact and we’ll forward the MSA and a short procurement checklist.

A couple of asks from our side:
- Which environment (prod/test) would you like us to demonstrate against?
- Any specific latency/throughput targets we should validate in the demo?

Thanks — flexible on timing if you need alternative windows.

Best,
Chloe Martin
Solutions Engineer, Redwood Inference
chloe.martin@redwoodinfer.com
+1 (415) 555-0192

Attachments: demo-agenda-v2.docx (docx), clearpath-MSA-draft.pdf (pdf)

From: Lucia Ramos <lucia.ramos@clearpath.com>
To: Chloe Martin <chloe.martin@redwoodinfer.com>
Cc: Taylor Forman <taylor@clearpath.com>, procurement@clearpath.com
Date: 2027-03-30T11:22:00-07:00
Subject: Re: ClearPath technical demo — proposed windows + agenda

Hi Chloe,

Appreciate the materials and the clear options. Unfortunately, Finance just announced a quarter-end blackout week for any vendor briefings — the CFO will be on a roadshow and procurement is locking down approvals until the FY budget reset (we're entering a short spend freeze). That affects the week of Apr 5–9.

Can we push the full technical walk-through to the week of Apr 19–23? We could do a 20–30 minute exec preview during the blackout week if you can accommodate a short slot, but any procurement or PO conversations will need to wait.

Also tagging procurement@clearpath.com so they have the MSA; they may provide redline guidance once the freeze lifts.

Thanks for understanding — hate to delay but this is a hard calendar freeze tied to closing out FY commitments.

Best,
Lucia Ramos
Head of Infrastructure, ClearPath Logistics
lucia.ramos@clearpath.com
+1 (312) 555-0123

From: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinfer.com>
To: Chloe Martin <chloe.martin@redwoodinfer.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinfer.com>
Date: 2027-03-30T14:05:00-07:00
Subject: Re: ClearPath — finance blackout; prep options

Chloe / Sean,

Noted — this is the classic quarter-end spend gate. Two practical options to keep momentum:

1) Offer a 20-min exec preview during the blackout (no procurement conversation) — focus on high-level outcomes and ROI to keep exec buy-in.
2) Record a short technical walkthrough and share artifacts now; schedule the full hands-on demo for Apr 19–23 when procurement and PO discussions are available.

If we do exec-preview, I can prepare a 2-slide ROI summary and a short live demo script. Also suggest we pre-fill a procurement checklist for procurement@clearpath.com so when the freeze lifts it's one less blocker.

I can cover the ROI slides — Chloe, can you do a 20-min slot on Apr 6 at 09:00 PT if Lucia wants the preview? If not, we push to Apr 20 for the full session.

-Marissa
Senior Director, Solutions
Marissa Cole | Redwood Inference

From: Chloe Martin <chloe.martin@redwoodinfer.com>
To: Lucia Ramos <lucia.ramos@clearpath.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinfer.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinfer.com>, procurement@clearpath.com
Date: 2027-03-31T10:16:00-07:00
Subject: Re: ClearPath technical demo — proposed windows + agenda

Hi Lucia — thanks for the heads up and for looping procurement. Totally understand the blackout.

We're happy to be flexible:
- Exec preview: Tue Apr 6, 09:00–09:30 PT (20–25 minutes) — Chloe (me) + Marissa (ROI) + Sean (brief Q&A)
- Full technical deep-dive: Tue Apr 20, 10:00–11:30 PT (or Wed Apr 21, 13:00–14:30 PT) — include live load testing if you want us to run any sample traffic (we'll need pre-config details).

If you prefer, I can also record a 10–12 minute technical walkthrough and share it before the blackout. That often helps procurement and engineering see the fit while approvals are paused.

Sharing the HubSpot deal link here so procurement can reference: https://app.hubspot.com/contacts/1234567/deal/CLP-2049

Let me know which of the above you prefer and if procurement wants an editable MSA redline — I can send DocuSign when the freeze lifts.

Regards,
Chloe
Solutions Engineer, Redwood Inference

Attachments referenced: demo-agenda-v2.docx, clearpath-MSA-draft.pdf
DocuSign prep link: https://www.docusign.net/CLP-2049/exec-signature

From: Lucia Ramos <lucia.ramos@clearpath.com>
To: Chloe Martin <chloe.martin@redwoodinfer.com>
Cc: Taylor Forman <taylor@clearpath.com>, procurement@clearpath.com
Date: 2027-03-31T16:05:00-07:00
Subject: Re: ClearPath technical demo — proposed windows + agenda

Chloe,

Thanks — appreciate the flexibility. Let's do the short exec preview on Apr 6 at 09:00 PT to keep the exec sponsors engaged. Please send the 2-slide ROI deck and the short recorded walkthrough beforehand (we'll circulate to procurement).

For the deep technical session, block Apr 20 10:00–11:30 PT; procurement will reach out after the FY reset with any MSA redlines.

Procurement contact: procurement@clearpath.com (they've received the draft MSA). Taylor / procurement — once the freeze lifts, can you confirm a PO timeline?

Speak soon — Lucia

---

> 
> Appreciate the materials and the clear options. Unfortunately, Finance just announced a quarter-end blackout week for any vendor briefings — the CFO will be on a roadshow and procurement is locking down approvals until the FY budget reset (we're entering a short spend freeze). That affects the week of Apr 5–9.
>

source.subject: SOC 2 Type II coverage request — AtlasPay procurement
messages:
From: Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwood.ai>
To: Lauren Bell <lauren.bell@atlaspay.com>
Date: 2027-03-28T09:12:00-07:00
Subject: SOC 2 Type II coverage request — AtlasPay procurement

Hi Lauren,

Thanks for running the procurement intake. Attached is a one-page summary of our most recent SOC 2 Type II posture (summary attached). We do have a Type II examination in progress and expect the final report to land in mid-April, but I understand AtlasPay's procurement cutoff is tight.

Can you confirm the exact audit period AtlasPay needs covered and any format preference for an interim/coverage communication (PDF letter, signed attestation, or a bridge-style commitment)? Also please confirm your absolute deadline — you mentioned end of month at the demo but I want to be sure.

If helpful, I can loop in our security and legal immediately. We want to make this as fast as possible to avoid blocking the deal.

Thanks,
Alex Martinez
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
alex.martinez@redwood.ai
(415) 555-0183

Attachments: 2027_SOC2_TypeII_summary.pdf (summary, 2 pages)

From: Lauren Bell <lauren.bell@atlaspay.com>
To: Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwood.ai>
Cc: procurement@atlaspay.com
Date: 2027-03-28T10:03:00-07:00

source.subject: MAP: BrightGate evaluation & budget guidance / pricing options
messages:
From: Evan Mills <evan.mills@brightgate.com>
To: Connor O'Brien <connor@redwood.com>
Cc: Procurement Team <procurement@brightgate.com>, BrightGate Legal <legal@brightgate.com>
Date: Fri, 19 Mar 2027 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: MAP: BrightGate evaluation & budget guidance

Connor — thanks for the demo yesterday. The execs want a clear MAP and a practical pricing pathway so we can finalize our Q2 budget ask. Two quick facts up front:

- Target commit: $150,000 ARR (ideally 12-month commit, subject to exec sign-off).
- Need evaluation credits to scope a 6-week POC (approx. 10k token/day usage expected).

Can Redwood put together:
1) A short MAP with milestones for the 6-week evaluation (owners, success criteria, dates).
2) Two pricing scenarios: (A) Pilot + credits then conversion to committed (B) Upfront commit with deeper discount. Please include how credits will be applied and any timing constraints.

Also: BrightGate's procurement will run an internal security questionnaire next week — we need a contact on your side for SOC2 evidence and retention policy.

Attaching our RFP and the internal MAP outline we use. Happy to jump on a 30-min sync if that speeds things up.

Thanks,
Evan Mills
Dir. of Platform Procurement
BrightGate Inc.
--- Attachments: BrightGate_RFP.pdf (application/pdf), BrightGate_MAP_v1.pdf (application/pdf)

From: Connor O'Brien <connor@redwood.com>
To: Evan Mills <evan.mills@brightgate.com>
Cc: Neha Kapoor <neha@redwood.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>, Aisha Rahman <aisha@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 19 Mar 2027 11:03:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: MAP: BrightGate evaluation & budget guidance

Evan — great framing, thanks. I've pulled the right internal owners and sketched two concrete paths below. I've attached a preliminary quote workbook so procurement can see line-item math.

Summary (two scenarios):

1) Pilot-first (lower near-term cash):
- 6-week POC with 40% evaluation credits (applied as a credit against first invoice).
- POC success criteria: 1) end-to-end latency under 200ms p99 for target flows, 2) model quality metrics hitting agreed prompt set baselines, 3) integration points validated in pre-prod.
- Expected conversion: commit to 12-month reserved capacity at T0 + 30 days after POC if success criteria met.
- Pricing: standard list discounted 15% + credit.
- Owner: Connor O'Brien (AM) for commercial; Marissa Cole for technical validation; Marissa -> provides evaluation checklists.

2) Commit-upfront (discount for revenue certainty):
- 12-month commit at $150k ARR with a 25% one-time credit applied within the first invoice cycle (reduces cash in month 1).
- Requires legal & pricing committee approval for discounts >20%.
- Expected timeline: contract signature within 2–3 weeks, SOC2 evidence provided within 5 business days after LOI.
- Owner: Sean Gallagher (Sales Director) for discount sign-off; Neha Kapoor (Finance) for billing/credit structure.

Action items (owners & due dates):
- Connor: finalize formal MAP doc and deliver to BrightGate by Mon 2027-03-22. (ACTION: provide milestones, owners, and success metrics)
- Marissa: supply evaluation checklist + runbook for POC by Mon 2027-03-22. (ACTION: technical runbook)
- Neha: prepare internal pricing memo and required approvals for 25% discount scenario by Wed 2027-03-24. (ACTION: finance memo + approval path)
- Sean: confirm pricing committee availability for expedited review (target Fri 2027-03-26). (ACTION: schedule approval slot)
- Aisha: SOC2 evidence lead — collect and share artifacts for procurement by Tue 2027-03-23. (ACTION: artifacts + contact sheet)

Notes / Constraints:
- Discounts over 20% require VP-level approval and a short justification memo (financial impact + strategic rationale).
- Credits applied to pilot cannot be reclaimed after conversion; they are one-time invoice-level credits.

Next step proposal: 30-minute sync Tuesday 2027-03-23 10:00 AM PT to review MAP and pick a pricing path. Connor will send an invite once Evan confirms.

Attachments: proposed_quote_BG_v1.xlsx (spreadsheet)

Thanks — Connor
Connor O'Brien
Account Executive | Redwood Inference
Office: +1 415 555 0124
---
On Fri, Mar 19, 2027 at 9:12 AM Evan Mills <evan.mills@brightgate.com> wrote:
> Connor — thanks for the demo yesterday. The execs want a clear MAP and a practical pricing pathway so we can finalize our Q2 budget ask...

From: Neha Kapoor <neha@redwood.com>
To: Evan Mills <evan.mills@brightgate.com>, Connor O'Brien <connor@redwood.com>
Cc: Sean Gallagher <sean@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>, Aisha Rahman <aisha@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 23 Mar 2027 16:05:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: MAP: BrightGate evaluation & budget guidance

Evan / team — quick finance note after reviewing Connor's options. We can support a blended approach: a 20% evaluation credit to cover the POC plus a 20% committed discount for a $150k ARR 12-month contract. To go to the 25% discount in Connor's second scenario, we'll need:
- A 1-page pricing justification (Connor: include strategic rationale + ARR runway).
- VP pricing committee approval (Sean: please flag availability).
- Legal to confirm standard T&Cs for credits applied to the first invoice (Marissa: loop legal if redlines expected).

If BrightGate prefers pilot-first, we can bill the first month net-30 with the credit applied on invoice 1 and convert the remaining term once success criteria are met.

I'll draft the internal memo once Connor confirms which scenario BrightGate will prefer. Happy to join the Tuesday sync.

Neha Kapoor
Finance Program Manager | Redwood Inference
---
On Fri, Mar 19, 2027 at 11:03 AM Connor O'Brien <connor@redwood.com> wrote:
> Evan — great framing, thanks. I've pulled the right internal owners and sketched two concrete paths below...

text:
From: Evan Mills <evan.mills@brightgate.com>
To: Connor O'Brien <connor@redwood.com>
Cc: Procurement Team <procurement@brightgate.com>, BrightGate Legal <legal@brightgate.com>
Date: Fri, 19 Mar 2027 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: MAP: BrightGate evaluation & budget guidance

Connor — thanks for the demo yesterday. The execs want a clear MAP and a practical pricing pathway so we can finalize our Q2 budget ask. Two quick facts up front:

- Target commit: $150,000 ARR (ideally 12-month commit, subject to exec sign-off).
- Need evaluation credits to scope a 6-week POC (approx. 10k token/day usage expected).

Can Redwood put together:
1) A short MAP with milestones for the 6-week evaluation (owners, success criteria, dates).
2) Two pricing scenarios: (A) Pilot + credits then conversion to committed (B) Upfront commit with deeper discount. Please include how credits will be applied and any timing constraints.

Also: BrightGate's procurement will run an internal security questionnaire next week — we need a contact on your side for SOC2 evidence and retention policy.

Attaching our RFP and the internal MAP outline we use. Happy to jump on a 30-min sync if that speeds things up.

Thanks,
Evan Mills
Dir. of Platform Procurement
BrightGate Inc.
--- Attachments: BrightGate_RFP.pdf (application/pdf), BrightGate_MAP_v1.pdf (application/pdf)

From: Connor O'Brien <connor@redwood.com>
To: Evan Mills <evan.mills@brightgate.com>
Cc: Neha Kapoor <neha@redwood.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>, Aisha Rahman <aisha@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 19 Mar 2027 11:03:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: MAP: BrightGate evaluation & budget guidance

Evan — great framing, thanks. I've pulled the right internal owners and sketched two concrete paths below. I've attached a preliminary quote workbook so procurement can see line-item math.

Summary (two scenarios):

1) Pilot-first (lower near-term cash):
- 6-week POC with 40% evaluation credits (applied as a credit against first invoice).
- POC success criteria: 1) end-to-end latency under 200ms p99 for target flows, 2) model quality metrics hitting agreed prompt set baselines, 3) integration points validated in pre-prod.
- Expected conversion: commit to 12-month reserved capacity at T0 + 30 days after POC if success criteria met.
- Pricing: standard list discounted 15% + credit.
- Owner: Connor O'Brien (AM) for commercial; Marissa Cole for technical validation; Marissa -> provides evaluation checklists.

2) Commit-upfront (discount for revenue certainty):
- 12-month commit at $150k ARR with a 25% one-time credit applied within the first invoice cycle (reduces cash in month 1).
- Requires legal & pricing committee approval for discounts >20%.
- Expected timeline: contract signature within 2–3 weeks, SOC2 evidence provided within 5 business days after LOI.
- Owner: Sean Gallagher (Sales Director) for discount sign-off; Neha Kapoor (Finance) for billing/credit structure.

Action items (owners & due dates):
- Connor: finalize formal MAP doc and deliver to BrightGate by Mon 2027-03-22. (ACTION: provide milestones, owners, and success metrics)
- Marissa: supply evaluation checklist + runbook for POC by Mon 2027-03-22. (ACTION: technical runbook)
- Neha: prepare internal pricing memo and required approvals for 25% discount scenario by Wed 2027-03-24. (ACTION: finance memo + approval path)
- Sean: confirm pricing committee availability for expedited review (target Fri 2027-03-26). (ACTION: schedule approval slot)
- Aisha: SOC2 evidence lead — collect and share artifacts for procurement by Tue 2027-03-23. (ACTION: artifacts + contact sheet)

Notes / Constraints:
- Discounts over 20% require VP-level approval and a short justification memo (financial impact + strategic rationale).
- Credits applied to pilot cannot be reclaimed after conversion; they are one-time invoice-level credits.

Next step proposal: 30-minute sync Tuesday 2027-03-23 10:00 AM PT to review MAP and pick a pricing path. Connor will send an invite once Evan confirms.

Attachments: proposed_quote_BG_v1.xlsx (spreadsheet)

Thanks — Connor
Connor O'Brien
Account Executive | Redwood Inference
Office: +1 415 555 0124

messages:
From: Sara Patel <sara.patel@novahealth.com>
To: Irene Choi <irene.choi@redwood.ai>
Cc: Tom Bradley <t.bradley@novahc.com>
Date: Sat, 20 Mar 2027 09:15:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Production latency spikes — SLA hits

Hi Irene,

Quick note from our on-call: we've observed repeated P95 token latencies crossing our agreed SLA during two peak windows yesterday and again this morning. Impact is customer-facing (search autocomplete). We flagged incident INC-3291 with your support portal; attached is a short CSV extract of the telemetry windows.

This is on the critical path for our Mar 31 evaluation close — if we can't show sustained P95 under 400ms for the next three days, our legal/compliance needs a formal exception request and exec sign-off. Can you confirm Redwood's plan to close the gap and who on your side will own the remediation and communication?

Thanks —
Sara
Sara Patel
Director, Product Operations
NovaHealth

Attachment: sla_telemetry_20270321.csv

From: Irene Choi <irene.choi@redwood.ai>
To: Sara Patel <sara.patel@novahealth.com>
Cc: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.ai>, Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.ai>
Date: Mon, 22 Mar 2027 08:40:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Production latency spikes — SLA hits

Sara —

Appreciate the heads-up and the sample telemetry. I’m escalating this to our SRE and Exec Sponsor looped below. High level: we agree this is a priority and have a MAP-style plan to both mitigate immediately and validate stability before your evaluation close. Brief summary and ask below.

Immediate mitigation (next 24h):
- Marcus Lin (SRE) will roll a targeted kernel configuration and enable prefix-KV cache for the two affected routes by 10:30 PT.
- We will throttle large-batch background jobs to preserve capacity for real-time inference windows.
- Monica will run a load replay against staging with matched seq-length distribution to validate the change.

Owner and exec escalation:
- Exec sponsor on our side: Sean Gallagher (VP Customer Success). He is aware and will coordinate updates to your execs if an SLA exception becomes necessary.

Requested from NovaHealth:
- Confirm two representative request IDs from the attached CSV (Tom can point to the exact traces).
- Allow us a 36-hour window to validate the fixes and return a runbook + stabilized telemetry. If we miss the stabilization objective, we will prepare a formal SLA exception request with proposed credits and remediation timeline.

I’ll follow with a short MAP checkpoint note and owner-level action log after Marcus posts the rollout.

Regards,
Irene Choi
Director, Sales Engineering — Redwood Inference

From: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.ai>
To: Irene Choi <irene.choi@redwood.ai>, Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.ai>, Sara Patel <sara.patel@novahealth.com>
Cc: Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwood.ai>
Date: Mon, 22 Mar 2027 11:05:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Production latency spikes — SLA hits

Team —

Rolled out the initial mitigation at 10:20 PT: kernel tweak + prefix-KV cache enabled for routes /api/v1/autocomplete and /api/v1/suggest. Throttled batch-job cluster for 1h windows around 09:00 and 12:00 PT — we will keep that schedule until nightly traffic settles.

Preliminary observations (first 90m): P95 dropped from ~620ms to ~380–420ms depending on sequence length. Attached are the initial run results and the staging replay plan. Next steps:
1) Today by 16:00 PT: full 4-hour production validation with synthetic replay at 80% of peak (owner: Marcus).
2) Tomorrow 09:00 PT: deploy a second iteration that enables CQ compression for KV cache (owner: Monica).
3) By 2027-03-24 17:00 PT: deliver a stabilization report and proposed MAP amendment if needed (owner: Irene).

If we fail to hold P95 < 400ms for the test window, we will prepare a formal SLA exception package (credit calc + remediation milestones) and route to Sean for exec approval.

Attachments: support_runbook_notes.txt

—Marcus
Marcus Lin
Sr. SRE — Redwood Inference

From: Tom Bradley <t.bradley@novahc.com>
To: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.ai>, Sara Patel <sara.patel@novahealth.com>
Cc: Irene Choi <irene.choi@redwood.ai>
Date: Mon, 22 Mar 2027 13:00:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Production latency spikes — SLA hits

Thanks Marcus — that initial delta looks promising. I confirmed the two representative request IDs: req_137922 and req_137945. We'll stand by for the 16:00 validation. If you need deeper traces, our team can open a temporary read-only view into our observability stack for your SREs.

Tom Bradley
Head of Platform Engineering
NovaHealth

From: Irene Choi <irene.choi@redwood.ai>
To: Sara Patel <sara.patel@novahealth.com>, Tom Bradley <t.bradley@novahc.com>
Cc: Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwood.ai>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.ai>, Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.ai>
Date: Mon, 22 Mar 2027 16:30:00 -0700
Subject: SLA gap bridging plan — weekly MAP checkpoint & exec sponsor note

Team — quick checkpoint and exec note for NovaHealth leadership: 

Summary (final for today):
- Mitigation rolled at 10:20 PT. Early production validation shows P95 back in the 380–420ms band; still a bit of variance tied to long-tail sequences.
- 16:00 PT extended validation completed: 3 of 4 windows met the <400ms target; one window at 415ms during a short burst tied to a vendor-cached lookup.

Action items and owners (MAP-style action log):
- A1: Full 4-hour stability replay and telemetry export (owner: Marcus Lin) — due 2027-03-22T23:59:00-07:00
- A2: Enable CQ compression for KV cache and monitor memory impact (owner: Monica Patel) — due 2027-03-23T09:00:00-07:00
- A3: Prepare formal SLA exception package (if stabilization fails) including credit calc & remediation milestones (owner: Irene Choi; reviewer: Sean Gallagher) — due 2027-03-24T17:00:00-07:00
- A4: Customer trace access (NovaHealth) to be provided for deeper correlation (owner: Tom Bradley) — requested complete by 2027-03-22T18:00:00-07:00

Escalation posture:
- If two consecutive 4-hour validations miss P95 < 400ms, Sean will convene execs and authorize either (a) temporary SLA exception with partial credit or (b) priority engineering assistance (reserved capacity allocation) for 72 hours.

Next checkpoint: 2027-03-23 10:00 PT — short sync (15m) to review A1 and A2 progress. I will circulate meeting notes and update the MAP draft (map_draft_v3.pdf) after that sync.

Thanks for the quick collaboration — we’re treating this as highest priority until validated.

—
Irene Choi
Director, Sales Engineering — Redwood Inference

Attachment: map_draft_v3.pdf

Next checkpoint: 2027-03-23 10:00 PT — short sync (15m) to review A1 and A2 progress. I will circulate meeting notes and update the MAP draft (map_draft_v3.pdf) after that sync.

Thanks for the quick collaboration — we’re treating this as highest priority until validated.

—
Irene Choi
Director, Sales Engineering — Redwood Inference

Attachment: map_draft_v3.pdf

messages:
From: Emily Park <emily.park@cartistry.ai>
To: support@redwood.ai; Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.ai>
Cc: ops@cartistry.ai
Date: 2027-03-07T08:47:15Z
Subject: sudden p95/p99 jumps across hosted API (started ~03:10 UTC)

Hi team,

We started seeing a sharp tail-latency regression around 03:10 UTC this morning. Our dashboard for Cartistry production (US) shows p95 increase from ~120ms to ~420ms and p99 hitting ~1.8s. Error rate also ticked up slightly (0.6% -> 1.2%). This is impacting checkout autocomplete in bursts — conversion is down and CS is getting complaints.

Sample request IDs: rq_9b12c, rq_9b1ee, rq_9b26f (I attached one trace from our side). We suspect something on your side because our upstream infrastructure (CDN + app servers) shows normal latency.

Can you confirm whether there were any kernel/prefix cache invalidations, KV cache thrash, or rollout activity in hosted during that window? We need an ETA on recovery and an interim mitigation — even a short-term fallback would help while you investigate.

Thanks,
Emily
Emily Park
Head of Engineering, Cartistry
From: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.ai>
To: Emily Park <emily.park@cartistry.ai>
Cc: Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>
Date: 2027-03-07T09:03:02Z
Subject: Re: sudden p95/p99 jumps across hosted API (started ~03:10 UTC)

Hi Emily — thanks for the quick signal and sample IDs, really helpful.

Monica (CS) and Marissa (SRE) are on it; I’ve opened INF-2345 and attached your trace to the ticket. Initial ask from our side: do you have any recent changes to request payloads or traffic shape we should know about? We'll pull traces, routing logs, and cache metrics for the 02:50–03:40 UTC window and report back within 60 minutes.

We understand the impact and will prioritize — I’ll keep you copied on updates.

— Karthik
Karthik Iyer
Director, Customer Reliability
Redwood Inference
From: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>
To: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.ai>, Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.ai>
Cc: Emily Park <emily.park@cartistry.ai>
Date: 2027-03-07T09:38:44Z
Subject: initial findings — KV prefix eviction spike at ~03:12 UTC

Summary (initial):
- We observed a coordinated KV prefix eviction event affecting the US-hosted Redis-KV tier at ~03:12:05 UTC. Evictions were concentrated on prefixes associated with Cartistry’s high-volume model route.
- Before the event, prefix hit rate ~92%; during the spike hit rate dropped to ~14% for ~4 minutes.
- Correlated consequences: p95 rose to ~430ms and p99 to ~1.8s; tail CPU on the serving pool increased due to repeated KV lookups and cache fills.
- Root cause hypothesis: an autoscaler restart combined with an aggressive memory eviction policy that pruned cold/prefetched prefixes while workloads surged (small burst at 03:11). This created a cold-cache wave with heavy synchronous KV refills.

Actions taken (impacts):
- Immediately increased prefix retention timeout for affected pools (temporary override) — this reduced miss amplification within ~3 minutes.
- Enabled conservative warmup policy on the route (slower but prevents mass eviction).
- Applied a short-lived traffic-steer to a warm pool variant for requests tagged as high-throughput to reduce downstream tail.

Attachments: stack traces + example span timeline (cartistry_p99_traces_20270307.zip).

Next steps:
1) Follow-up deep analysis on eviction rationale (eviction logs + memory pressure timeline).
2) Propose longer-term fix: change eviction threshold and add async background pre-warms for common prefixes.
3) Run a controlled canary today for the new warmup/ejection parameters.

We’ll update Emily once we have the full postmortem.

—Marissa
Marissa Cole
Principal SRE, Serving Runtime
Redwood Inference
From: Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwood.ai>
To: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>
Cc: Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwood.ai>, Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.ai>, Emily Park <emily.park@cartistry.ai>
Date: 2027-03-08T14:11:03Z
Subject: RE: Cartistry outage impact & mitigation — executive note

Looping in leadership to ensure we handle customer comms and contractual concerns.

Karthik/Marissa: please confirm by EOD the timeline for the postmortem and the customer-facing summary (root cause + remediation + credits if required). We need to be explicit about whether this was an isolated KV prefix eviction misconfiguration or indicative of broader autoscaler instability.

Sean: advise on a succinct customer note and credit bucket (if any). Monica: please coordinate SLO impact numbers for the last 7 days and any immediate retention credits.

I want one clear owner for the internal postmortem and an owner for the customer-facing writeup.

—Rishi
Rishi Malhotra
VP, Infrastructure
Redwood Inference
From: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.ai>
To: Emily Park <emily.park@cartistry.ai>
Cc: Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwood.ai>, Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwood.ai>
Date: 2027-03-10T17:22:02Z
Subject: Update: mitigation complete and next steps (INF-2345)

Hi Emily — quick status update and next steps from our side. Thanks again for the signal and patience while we investigated.

What happened: a memory-pressure driven eviction wave in the US-hosted KV tier pruned several prefetched prefixes for Cartistry’s primary route at ~03:12 UTC on 2027-03-07. That eviction, amplified by a small traffic burst and an autoscaler restart, created a cold-cache wave that increased synchronous KV refills and produced the p95/p99 spikes you observed.

What we did (already in prod):
- Temporarily increased prefix retention and tuned eviction thresholds for affected pools.
- Enabled conservative background prefix warmup for the route and added short-term traffic steering to warm pools while refill occurred.
- Deployed a patch to the autoscaler to avoid rapid restarts under transient memory pressure (canaryed in US yesterday).

Customer impact & credits: Monica will follow up with the exact SLO impact numbers; Sean and I will propose a credit in line with our credit policy for the affected window.

Next steps (timeline):
- Postmortem draft (internal) due 2027-03-11.
- Customer-facing summary + proposed credits due 2027-03-12.
- Longer-term fix (auto warmup + eviction threshold changes) scheduled to land in canary by 2027-03-18.

For visibility, INF-2345 will track all artifacts — traces, eviction logs, and the postmortem (link: https://redwood.atlassian.net/browse/INF-2345). I attached a short SLO impact summary.

If you need an interim hotfix (e.g., a longer retention window), we can enable that for a short period while the long-term change is validated.

Regards,
Karthik
Karthik Iyer
Director, Customer Reliability
Redwood Inference
Attachments: slo_impact_summary_20270310.pdf

source.subject: Clarify commit curve vs. volume blend for per-token tiers
messages:
From: Priya Rao <priya@clearlinehealth.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 5 Mar 2027 09:15:00 -0700
Subject: Questions on your per-token tiers

Ben — thanks for the proposal you shared earlier. The procurement team has a couple of open items before we can move to legal:

1) We want to understand whether the discounts on your per-token ladder are marginal (only applied to tokens in each band) or blended (the lower blended effective rate applied to all consumption once a breakpoint is crossed). Which pricing approach did you assume in your scenarios?

2) Related: how do true-ups work if our monthly usage spikes above a committed monthly baseline but annual commit is on track? Any mid-quarter reconciliations or invoice credits?

3) Can you provide a short worked example (with numbers) for both marginal and blended for our proposed commit curve? That will help our finance team model cashflows.

Attaching our standard PO template for reference. Can we get a one-pager that shows both calculations by EOD Monday?

Thanks —
Priya Rao
Head of Procurement
Clearline Health

(Attachment: Clearline_PO_Template.pdf)

From: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
To: Priya Rao <priya@clearlinehealth.com>
Cc: Lauren Bishop <lauren@redwood.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 5 Mar 2027 10:37:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Questions on your per-token tiers

Priya — thanks for the clear questions. I put together two short scenarios and attached a simple spreadsheet (Proposed_Pricing_Scenarios.xlsx) that walks the math. High level answers first so your team can move quickly: 

- We can support both billing conventions. Historically most of our enterprise customers prefer marginal for visibility into unit economics, but we offer blended if you prefer simpler cash modeling.
- True-up: our standard contract uses monthly metering with an annual true-up. If monthly usage exceeds the committed monthly baseline, we prorate at the applicable marginal band for that month and reflect any overages on the next invoice; at year-end we true-up against the committed annual volume and apply credits/charges per the agreement's true-up table. We can add a mid-quarter true-up clause (optional) if you want a predictable reconciliation cadence.

Worked examples (see sheet 'Worked Examples'):
- Marginal: Commit 600M tokens/yr (50M/mo). Breakpoints: 0-10M @ $0.00008, next 40M @ $0.00006, 50M+ @ $0.00005. If in June you use 80M tokens, you pay 10M*$0.00008 + 40M*$0.00006 + 30M*$0.00005 = $8 + $24 + $15 = $47 for June.
- Blended: Using same breakpoints, once you cross the 50M/mo breakpoint (i.e., 50M committed trigger), blended rate might be, e.g., $0.000055 on all 80M => $4.4. Blended gives a lower-looking per-token amount but increases cost unpredictability on lower months.

Operational notes from our side: batching and caching behavior still applies (billed tokens measured after prefix cache deductions). Any quantization/accelerator discounts are additive and applied post-break calculation per the SOW.

I'll sync with Lauren and Sean on the contract language we'll propose for the true-up cadence and the exact invoice mechanics. Call to discuss Monday morning?

Best,
Ben Carter
Enterprise Lead, Strategic Accounts
Redwood Inference

(Attachment: Proposed_Pricing_Scenarios.xlsx)

From: Lauren Bishop <lauren@redwood.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 5 Mar 2027 11:05:00 -0700
Subject: Fwd: Re: Questions on your per-token tiers

Looping ops and billing here — a few practical constraints to flag for the contract draft: 

- If Clearline wants blended rates, legal will need explicit language on when the blended rate is triggered (month-level crossing vs. rolling 3-mo average). We typically see disputes with month-level blenders because seasonality can create surprising credits/charges. Recommend a 3-month rolling trigger if they care about smoothing.

- For marginal true-ups, we need to confirm what token definition they use (prompt+completion vs. completion-only for embedding/semantic calls). That matters for the band math.

- SLA crediting: if they're asking for a committed latency SLA tied to Dedicated routing, make sure we separate compute-tier credits from billing true-ups. Sean — can you check if there's any regional tax handling for their HQ?

I'll draft language for both options and include an optional mid-quarter reconciliation clause as Ben suggested.

-Lauren
Director, Product Partnerships
Redwood Inference

[Quoted text]
> From: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
> Date: Fri, 5 Mar 2027 10:37:00 -0700
> Subject: Re: Questions on your per-token tiers
>
> Priya — thanks for the clear questions...

From: Priya Rao <priya@clearlinehealth.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
Cc: Clearline Health Procurement <procurement@clearlinehealth.com>
Date: Fri, 5 Mar 2027 13:02:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Questions on your per-token tiers

Thanks, Ben and Lauren — that helps. A couple quick follow-ups from procurement: 

- Can you show the blended example using a 3-month rolling average trigger (as Lauren suggested)?
- We often have month-to-month spikes due to seasonal campaigns; would Redwood consider a soft cap where we get a one-time smoothing credit for a single month spike per quarter (no more than 2 per year)? If so, how would that show on the invoice?

If you can add those two lines to the spreadsheet and return, we can move to legal review. Also proposing a 30-minute call next Tue to finalize — does 10am PT work?

Best,
Priya

(Attachment: none)

From: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
To: Priya Rao <priya@clearlinehealth.com>
Cc: Lauren Bishop <lauren@redwood.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 5 Mar 2027 17:22:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Questions on your per-token tiers — next steps

Priya — appreciate the quick turn. I've updated the spreadsheet: new tab '3mo_blend_trigger' shows a blended-rate calculation that only takes effect if the trailing 3-month usage average crosses the breakpoint. Example included where months = [40M, 120M, 80M] => trailing avg = 80M, which triggers the blended band and applies blended price across the trailing months per the optional smoothing clause.

Regarding the soft cap: we can include a Quarterly Spike Smoothing credit: one-month spike per quarter (up to 50% above committed monthly baseline) eligible for a credit equal to the delta calculated at the marginal rate vs. blended (so you get the smaller charge of the two for that month). We'll mark that as optional in the SOW so finance can opt in. On the invoice it will show as an adjustment line item referencing the month and the credit ID.

Notes and next steps:
- I created a HubSpot deal with the pricing scenarios + one-pager (link below).
- Proposed next call: Tue 2027-03-09 10:00 AM PT. I'll send a calendar invite after you confirm.
- If legal needs specific clause language, Lauren will push a tracked-change SOW draft to the Drive link.

Links: https://app.hubspot.com/deal/54321 | Drive: https://drive.google.com/open?id=1Xk_ExampleLink

Thanks — talk Tuesday?
