title: Clarify commit curve vs. volume blend for per-token tiers
preview:
From: Priya Rao <priya@clearlinehealth.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 5 Mar 2027 09:15:00 -0700
Subject: Questions on your per-token tiers

Ben — thanks for the proposal you shared earlier. The procurement team has a couple of open items before we can move to legal:

1) We want to understand whether the discounts on your per-token ladder are marginal (only applied to tokens in each band) or blended (the lower blended effective rate applied to all consumption once a breakpoint is crossed). Which pricing approach did you assume in your scenarios?

2) Related: how do true-ups work if our monthly usage spikes above a committed monthly baseline but annual commit is on track? Any mid-quarter reconciliations or invoice credits?

3) Can you provide a short worked example (with numbers) for both marginal and blended for our proposed commit curve? That will help our finance team model cashflows.

Attaching our standard PO template for reference. Can we get a one-pager that shows both calculations by EOD Monday?

Thanks —
Priya Rao
Head of Procurement
Clearline Health

(Attachment: Clearline_PO_Template.pdf)

From: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
To: Priya Rao <priya@clearlinehealth.com>
Cc: Lauren Bishop <lauren@redwood.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 5 Mar 2027 10:37:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Questions on your per-token tiers

Priya — thanks for the clear questions. I put together two short scenarios and attached a simple spreadsheet (Proposed_Pricing_Scenarios.xlsx) that walks the math. High level answers first so your team can move quickly: 

- We can support both billing conventions. Historically most of our enterprise customers prefer marginal for visibility into unit economics, but we offer blended if you prefer simpler cash modeling.
- True-up: our standard contract uses monthly metering with an annual true-up. If monthly usage exceeds the committed monthly baseline, we prorate at the applicable marginal band for that month and reflect any overages on the next invoice; at year-end we true-up against the committed annual volume and apply credits/charges per the agreement's true-up table. We can add a mid-quarter true-up clause (optional) if you want a predictable reconciliation cadence.

Worked examples (see sheet 'Worked Examples'):
- Marginal: Commit 600M tokens/yr (50M/mo). Breakpoints: 0-10M @ $0.00008, next 40M @ $0.00006, 50M+ @ $0.00005. If in June you use 80M tokens, you pay 10M*$0.00008 + 40M*$0.00006 + 30M*$0.00005 = $8 + $24 + $15 = $47 for June.
- Blended: Using same breakpoints, once you cross the 50M/mo breakpoint (i.e., 50M committed trigger), blended rate might be, e.g., $0.000055 on all 80M => $4.4. Blended gives a lower-looking per-token amount but increases cost unpredictability on lower months.

Operational notes from our side: batching and caching behavior still applies (billed tokens measured after prefix cache deductions). Any quantization/accelerator discounts are additive and applied post-break calculation per the SOW.

I'll sync with Lauren and Sean on the contract language we'll propose for the true-up cadence and the exact invoice mechanics. Call to discuss Monday morning?

Best,
Ben Carter
Enterprise Lead, Strategic Accounts
Redwood Inference

(Attachment: Proposed_Pricing_Scenarios.xlsx)

From: Lauren Bishop <lauren@redwood.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 5 Mar 2027 11:05:00 -0700
Subject: Fwd: Re: Questions on your per-token tiers

Looping ops and billing here — a few practical constraints to flag for the contract draft: 

- If Clearline wants blended rates, legal will need explicit language on when the blended rate is triggered (month-level crossing vs. rolling 3-mo average). We typically see disputes with month-level blenders because seasonality can create surprising credits/charges. Recommend a 3-month rolling trigger if they care about smoothing.

- For marginal true-ups, we need to confirm what token definition they use (prompt+completion vs. completion-only for embedding/semantic calls). That matters for the band math.

- SLA crediting: if they're asking for a committed latency SLA tied to Dedicated routing, make sure we separate compute-tier credits from billing true-ups. Sean — can you check if there's any regional tax handling for their HQ?

I'll draft language for both options and include an optional mid-quarter reconciliation clause as Ben suggested.

-Lauren
Director, Product Partnerships
Redwood Inference

[Quoted text]
> From: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
> Date: Fri, 5 Mar 2027 10:37:00 -0700
> Subject: Re: Questions on your per-token tiers
>
> Priya — thanks for the clear questions...

From: Priya Rao <priya@clearlinehealth.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
Cc: Clearline Health Procurement <procurement@clearlinehealth.com>
Date: Fri, 5 Mar 2027 13:02:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Questions on your per-token tiers

Thanks, Ben and Lauren — that helps. A couple quick follow-ups from procurement: 

- Can you show the blended example using a 3-month rolling average trigger (as Lauren suggested)?
- We often have month-to-month spikes due to seasonal campaigns; would Redwood consider a soft cap where we get a one-time smoothing credit for a single month spike per quarter (no more than 2 per year)? If so, how would that show on the invoice?

If you can add those two lines to the spreadsheet and return, we can move to legal review. Also proposing a 30-minute call next Tue to finalize — does 10am PT work?

Best,
Priya

(Attachment: none)

From: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
To: Priya Rao <priya@clearlinehealth.com>
Cc: Lauren Bishop <lauren@redwood.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 5 Mar 2027 17:22:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Questions on your per-token tiers — next steps

Priya — appreciate the quick turn. I've updated the spreadsheet: new tab '3mo_blend_trigger' shows a blended-rate calculation that only takes effect if the trailing 3-month usage average crosses the breakpoint. Example included where months = [40M, 120M, 80M] => trailing avg = 80M, which triggers the blended band and applies blended price across the trailing months per the optional smoothing clause.

Regarding the soft cap: we can include a Quarterly Spike Smoothing credit: one-month spike per quarter (up to 50% above committed monthly baseline) eligible for a credit equal to the delta calculated at the marginal rate vs. blended (so you get the smaller charge of the two for that month). We'll mark that as optional in the SOW so finance can opt in. On the invoice it will show as an adjustment line item referencing the month and the credit ID.

Notes and next steps:
- I created a HubSpot deal with the pricing scenarios + one-pager (link below).
- Proposed next call: Tue 2027-03-09 10:00 AM PT. I'll send a calendar invite after you confirm.
- If legal needs specific clause language, Lauren will push a tracked-change SOW draft to the Drive link.

Links: https://app.hubspot.com/deal/54321 | Drive: https://drive.google.com/open?id=1Xk_ExampleLink

Thanks — talk Tuesday?

Best,
Ben Carter
Enterprise Lead, Strategic Accounts
Redwood Inference

source.subject: Urgent: Geo-traffic shift request — Ridgeway production latency and 5xx spike
messages:
From: Jessica White <jessica.white@ridgeway-analytics.com>
To: Alex Martinez <alex_martinez@redwo od.com>
Cc: sre-oncall@ridgeway.com
Date: Fri, 12 Feb 2027 09:13:00 -0800
Subject: Production latency and 5xx spike — need geo shift

Alex —

We're seeing a sudden 6-8x latency increase for critical inference routes and a rising 5xx rate beginning ~08:45 PST. Error rates are concentrated in us-west-2, AZ b. Throughput is fine but token latencies are killing our SLAs and clients are opening tickets. Can Redwood initiate an emergency geo routing shift to us-east-1 (read traffic at minimum) while Ridgeway SRE collects logs?

Quick details:
- Impact: 30% of requests seeing >2s p50, 8% 5xx (gateway timeouts)
- Seen across multiple clients, started 08:45 PST
- Oncall has checked our infra; looks like Redwood-backed endpoints are returning 502s and timeouts

We need a runbook-driven mitigation and an ETA. Please escalate to engineering and exec if needed.

Attaching: none (we can upload logs on the call).

Jessica White
Head of Platform Ops | Ridgeway Analytics

From: Alex Martinez <alex_martinez@redwood.com>
To: Jessica White <jessica.white@ridgeway-analytics.com>
Cc: Rishi Malhotra <rishi_malhotra@redwood.com>, Evelyn Hart <evelyn_hart@redwood.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean_gallagher@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 12 Feb 2027 09:22:00 -08:00
Subject: Re: Production latency and 5xx spike — need geo shift

Jessica — thanks for the heads up and sorry for the disruption. I'm pulling the on-call infra and our routing team in now. Can you confirm whether clients are session-pinned (no sticky routing) for the impacted endpoints and whether any customer-specific routing policies are in place?

We propose an immediate, partial traffic diversion to us-east-1 for non-sticky read-only inference routes while we stabilize us-west-2. I'll loop Rishi for a quick diagnosis and proposed commands.

Standby for a call invite (10m).

Alex
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
Attachments: redwood_runbook_failover.pdf (runbook excerpt)

From: Alex Martinez <alex_martinez@redwood.com>
To: Rishi Malhotra <rishi_malhotra@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.com>, Evelyn Hart <evelyn_hart@redwood.com>
Cc: Sean Gallagher <sean_gallagher@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 12 Feb 2027 09:30:00 -08:00
Subject: FYI / Triage: Ridgeway production — propose geo shift to us-east-1

Team —
Ridgeway just reported a large latency/5xx spike affecting us-west-2 (AZ b). They asked for an emergency geo shift for critical inference routes. I'm on a quick call with customer.

Immediate asks:
- Rishi: quick triage (KV cache, node saturation, GPU queueing) and proposed mitigations with exact CLI/console steps.
- Evelyn: prepare a rollback plan and check our cross-region capacity (us-east-1 burst availability).
- Marissa: I'm going to need a short exec note and signoff on any credit/comp if this extends past 4 hours.

Attached runbook (excerpt) — follow the "traffic diversion" section; we should prefer prefix-based routing to minimize cache misses.

On the call now.

Alex
(On behalf of Ridgeway escalation)

From: Rishi Malhotra <rishi_malhotra@redwood.com>
To: Alex Martinez <alex_martinez@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.com>, Evelyn Hart <evelyn_hart@redwood.com>
Cc: Sean Gallagher <sean_gallagher@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 12 Feb 2027 10:05:00 -08:00
Subject: Re: FYI / Triage: Ridgeway production — propose geo shift to us-east-1

Quick findings from our initial probes (10:00–10:03 PST):
- us-west-2 fleet nodes show elevated GPU queuing and intermittent gRPC timeouts to the KV cache layer. Token generation latency spikes align with cache miss storms between 08:40–09:05 PST.
- No obvious host CPU/memory OOMs; autoscaler did not fire because node-local sched pressure prevented new pods from accepting traffic.

Immediate mitigation (low-risk) I recommend now:
1) Prefix-based diversion: shift 30–40% of non-sticky traffic for route set /api/v1/infer/read/* to us-east-1 via the routing policy UI or our routing-api: POST /routing/shift {"from":"us-west-2","to":"us-east-1","percent":35,"routes":["/api/v1/infer/read/*"]}
2) Evict aged KV entries: run kvctl flush --prefix temp-cache-20270212 on affected clusters (this reduces cache-miss amplification)
3) Increase worker concurrency on us-west-2 nodes by +2 to drain queues (risk: temporary increased latency at tail)

I attached a short debug dump I asked Ridgeway to upload. Actions are safe to run now; expected customer p50 improvement within 5–8 minutes and drop in 5xx from 8% to ~1.5% as load shifts.

Attachments: ridgeway_debug_packets_0212.txt (extract), traffic-shift_proposal.pdf

-Rishi
Principal Infra Engineer, Redwood Inference

From: Alex Martinez <alex_martinez@redwood.com>
To: Jessica White <jessica.white@ridgeway-analytics.com>, sre-oncall@ridgeway.com
Cc: Rishi Malhotra <rishi_malhotra@redwood.com>, Evelyn Hart <evelyn_hart@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 12 Feb 2027 12:10:00 -08:00
Subject: Re: Production latency and 5xx spike — Ridgeway mitigation in flight

Jessica / Ridgeway SRE —

We executed a 35% prefix diversion for /api/v1/infer/read/* to us-east-1 at 11:53 PST and evicted aged KV entries as per Rishi's recommendation. Early telemetry:
- p50 dropped from ~1.8s to ~380ms for diverted traffic
- 5xx rate dropped from 8% to 1.7% fleet-wide
- us-west-2 still experiencing higher tail latency for sticky write routes; we did not divert write/sensitive routes.

We're continuing to monitor and will keep the diversion in place until we finish a controlled drain and KV warmup on us-west-2. Please flag if you observe any client regressions (auth, session consistency).

Thanks for the quick coordination.

Alex

On Fri, 12 Feb 2027 at 10:05, Rishi Malhotra <rishi_malhotra@redwood.com> wrote:
> Quick findings from our initial probes (10:00–10:03 PST):
> - us-west-2 fleet nodes show elevated GPU queuing and intermittent gRPC timeouts to the KV cache layer. Token generation latency spikes align with cache miss storms between 08:40–09:05 PST.
> - No obvious host CPU/memory OOMs; autoscaler did not fire because node-local sched pressure prevented new pods from accepting traffic.
>
> Immediate mitigation (low-risk) I recommend now:
> 1) Prefix-based diversion: shift 30–40% of non-sticky traffic for route set /api/v1/infer/read/* to us-east-1 via the routing policy UI or our routing-api: POST /routing/shift {"from":"us-west-2","to":"us-east-1","percent":35,"routes":["/api/v1/infer/read/*"]}
> 2) Evict aged KV entries: run kvctl flush --prefix temp-cache-20270212 on affected clusters (this reduces cache-miss amplification)
> 3) Increase worker concurrency on us-west-2 nodes by +2 to drain queues (risk: temporary increased latency at tail)

From: Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.com>
To: Jessica White <jessica.white@ridgeway-analytics.com>, Alex Martinez <alex_martinez@redwood.com>
Cc: Rishi Malhotra <rishi_malhotra@redwood.com>, Evelyn Hart <evelyn_hart@redwood.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean_gallagher@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 12 Feb 2027 18:45:00 -08:00
Subject: Ridgeway outage: summary, next steps, and follow-up

Jessica / Ridgeway leadership —

Thanks again for your patience and for working through the mitigation with our team today. Below is a concise summary and next steps: 

Summary:
- Incident window: 2027-02-12 08:40–12:00 PST (mitigation began 11:53 PST)
- Root cause (initial): KV cache miss amplification combined with node-level scheduling pressure in us-west-2 AZ b leading to GPU queueing and gRPC timeouts.
- Mitigation performed: 35% prefix traffic diversion to us-east-1 for read-only inference routes, KV eviction, temporary concurrency increase.
- Current status: customer traffic stable; total 5xx reduced to <2% and p50 restored for diverted traffic.

Immediate next steps:
1) Joint postmortem: we propose a 60-minute bridge on Monday 2027-02-15 to review full traces, autotuning, and routing policy thresholds. Link: https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1A2b3Cpostmortem
2) Action items (assigned): Rishi to run kernel-level profiling and propose autoscaler threshold updates (JIRA SUP-4872). Evelyn to validate cross-region capacity and update our burst pool config. Alex to own customer comms and credit proposal.
3) Credit: we will issue a goodwill credit for the customer SLA window; Alex to follow up with details (HubSpot: https://app.hubspot.com/contacts/12345/deal/8271).

We will draft the full postmortem and share before the Monday call. If you need anything sooner, mark the bridge or ping me directly.

Regards,
Marissa Cole
Head of Customer Reliability, Redwood Inference
Attachments: traffic-shift_proposal.pdf (internal remediation notes)

From: Alex Martinez <alex_martinez@redwood.com>
To: Jessica White <jessica.white@ridgeway-analytics.com>, sre-oncall@ridgeway.com
Cc: Rishi Malhotra <rishi_malhotra@redwood.com>, Evelyn Hart <evelyn_hart@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 12 Feb 2027 12:10:00 -08:00
Subject: Re: Production latency and 5xx spike — Ridgeway mitigation in flight

Jessica / Ridgeway SRE —

We executed a 35% prefix diversion for /api/v1/infer/read/* to us-east-1 at 11:53 PST and evicted aged KV entries as per Rishi's recommendation. Early telemetry:
- p50 dropped from ~1.8s to ~380ms for diverted traffic
- 5xx rate dropped from 8% to 1.7% fleet-wide
- us-west-2 still experiencing higher tail latency for sticky write routes; we did not divert write/sensitive routes.

We're continuing to monitor and will keep the diversion in place until we finish a controlled drain and KV warmup on us-west-2. Please flag if you observe any client regressions (auth, session consistency).

Thanks for the quick coordination.

Alex

On Fri, 12 Feb 2027 at 10:05, Rishi Malhotra <rishi_malhotra@redwood.com> wrote:
> Quick findings from our initial probes (10:00–10:03 PST):
> - us-west-2 fleet nodes show elevated GPU queuing and intermittent gRPC timeouts to the KV cache layer. Token generation latency spikes align with cache miss storms between 08:40–09:05 PST.
> - No obvious host CPU/memory OOMs; autoscaler did not fire because node-local sched pressure prevented new pods from accepting traffic.
>
> Immediate mitigation (low-risk) I recommend now:
> 1) Prefix-based diversion: shift 30–40% of non-sticky traffic for route set /api/v1/infer/read/* to us-east-1 via the routing policy UI or our routing-api: POST /routing/shift {\"from\":\"us-west-2\",\"to\":\"us-east-1\",\"percent\":35,\"routes\":[\"/api/v1/infer/read/*\"]}
> 2) Evict aged KV entries: run kvctl flush --prefix temp-cache-20270212 on affected clusters (this reduces cache-miss amplification)
> 3) Increase worker concurrency on us-west-2 nodes by +2 to drain queues (risk: temporary increased latency at tail)



From: Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.com>
To: Jessica White <jessica.white@ridgeway-analytics.com>, Alex Martinez <alex_martinez@redwood.com>
Cc: Rishi Malhotra <rishi_malhotra@redwood.com>, Evelyn Hart <evelyn_hart@redwood.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean_gallagher@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 12 Feb 2027 18:45:00 -08:00
Subject: Ridgeway outage: summary, next steps, and follow-up

Jessica / Ridgeway leadership —

Thanks again for your patience and for working through the mitigation with our team today. Below is a concise summary and next steps: 

Summary:
- Incident window: 2027-02-12 08:40–12:00 PST (mitigation began 11:53 PST)
- Root cause (initial): KV cache miss amplification combined with node-level scheduling pressure in us-west-2 AZ b leading to GPU queueing and gRPC timeouts.
- Mitigation performed: 35% prefix traffic diversion to us-east-1 for read-only inference routes, KV eviction, temporary concurrency increase.
- Current status: customer traffic stable; total 5xx reduced to <2% and p50 restored for diverted traffic.

Immediate next steps:
1) Joint postmortem: we propose a 60-minute bridge on Monday 2027-02-15 to review full traces, autotuning, and routing policy thresholds. Link: https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1A2b3Cpostmortem
2) Action items (assigned): Rishi to run kernel-level profiling and propose autoscaler threshold updates (JIRA SUP-4872). Evelyn to validate cross-region capacity and update our burst pool config. Alex to own customer comms and credit proposal.
3) Credit: we will issue a goodwill credit for the customer SLA window; Alex to follow up with details (HubSpot: https://app.hubspot.com/contacts/12345/deal/8271).

We will draft the full postmortem and share before the Monday call. If you need anything sooner, mark the bridge or ping me directly.

Regards,
Marissa Cole
Head of Customer Reliability, Redwood Inference
Attachments: traffic-shift_proposal.pdf (internal remediation notes)
text:
From: Jessica White <jessica.white@ridgeway-analytics.com>
To: Alex Martinez <alex_martinez@redwo od.com>
Cc: sre-oncall@ridgeway.com
Date: Fri, 12 Feb 2027 09:13:00 -0800
Subject: Production latency and 5xx spike — need geo shift

Alex —

We're seeing a sudden 6-8x latency increase for critical inference routes and a rising 5xx rate beginning ~08:45 PST. Error rates are concentrated in us-west-2, AZ b. Throughput is fine but token latencies are killing our SLAs and clients are opening tickets. Can Redwood initiate an emergency geo routing shift to us-east-1 (read traffic at minimum) while Ridgeway SRE collects logs?

Quick details:
- Impact: 30% of requests seeing >2s p50, 8% 5xx (gateway timeouts)
- Seen across multiple clients, started 08:45 PST
- Oncall has checked our infra; looks like Redwood-backed endpoints are returning 502s and timeouts

We need a runbook-driven mitigation and an ETA. Please escalate to engineering and exec if needed.

Attaching: none (we can upload logs on the call).

Jessica White
Head of Platform Ops | Ridgeway Analytics


From: Alex Martinez <alex_martinez@redwood.com>
To: Jessica White <jessica.white@ridgeway-analytics.com>
Cc: Rishi Malhotra <rishi_malhotra@redwood.com>, Evelyn Hart <evelyn_hart@redwood.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean_gallagher@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 12 Feb 2027 09:22:00 -08:00
Subject: Re: Production latency and 5xx spike — need geo shift

Jessica — thanks for the heads up and sorry for the disruption. I'm pulling the on-call infra and our routing team in now. Can you confirm whether clients are session-pinned (no sticky routing) for the impacted endpoints and whether any customer-specific routing policies are in place?

We propose an immediate, partial traffic diversion to us-east-1 for non-sticky read-only inference routes while we stabilize us-west-2. I'll loop Rishi for a quick diagnosis and proposed commands.

Standby for a call invite (10m).

Alex
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
Attachments: redwood_runbook_failover.pdf (runbook excerpt)


From: Alex Martinez <alex_martinez@redwood.com>
To: Rishi Malhotra <rishi_malhotra@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.com>, Evelyn Hart <evelyn_hart@redwood.com>
Cc: Sean Gallagher <sean_gallagher@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 12 Feb 2027 09:30:00 -08:00
Subject: FYI / Triage: Ridgeway production — propose geo shift to us-east-1

Team —
Ridgeway just reported a large latency/5xx spike affecting us-west-2 (AZ b). They asked for an emergency geo shift for critical inference routes. I'm on a quick call with customer.

Immediate asks:
- Rishi: quick triage (KV cache, node saturation, GPU queueing) and proposed mitigations with exact CLI/console steps.
- Evelyn: prepare a rollback plan and check our cross-region capacity (us-east-1 burst availability).
- Marissa: I'm going to need a short exec note and signoff on any credit/comp if this extends past 4 hours.

Attached runbook (excerpt) — follow the \"traffic diversion\" section; we should prefer prefix-based routing to minimize cache misses.

On the call now.

Alex
(On behalf of Ridgeway escalation)


From: Rishi Malhotra <rishi_malhotra@redwood.com>
To: Alex Martinez <alex_martinez@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.com>, Evelyn Hart <evelyn_hart@redwood.com>
Cc: Sean Gallagher <sean_gallagher@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 12 Feb 2027 10:05:00 -08:00
Subject: Re: FYI / Triage: Ridgeway production — propose geo shift to us-east-1

Quick findings from our initial probes (10:00–10:03 PST):
- us-west-2 fleet nodes show elevated GPU queuing and intermittent gRPC timeouts to the KV cache layer. Token generation latency spikes align with cache miss storms between 08:40–09:05 PST.
- No obvious host CPU/memory OOMs; autoscaler did not fire because node-local sched pressure prevented new pods from accepting traffic.

Immediate mitigation (low-risk) I recommend now:
1) Prefix-based diversion: shift 30–40% of non-sticky traffic for route set /api/v1/infer/read/* to us-east-1 via the routing policy UI or our routing-api: POST /routing/shift {\"from\":\"us-west-2\",\"to\":\"us-east-1\",\"percent\":35,\"routes\":[\"/api/v1/infer/read/*\"]}
2) Evict aged KV entries: run kvctl flush --prefix temp-cache-20270212 on affected clusters (this reduces cache-miss amplification)
3) Increase worker concurrency on us-west-2 nodes by +2 to drain queues (risk: temporary increased latency at tail)

I attached a short debug dump I asked Ridgeway to upload. Actions are safe to run now; expected customer p50 improvement within 5–8 minutes and drop in 5xx from 8% to ~1.5% as load shifts.

Attachments: ridgeway_debug_packets_0212.txt (extract), traffic-shift_proposal.pdf

-Rishi
Principal Infra Engineer, Redwood Inference


From: Alex Martinez <alex_martinez@redwood.com>
To: Jessica White <jessica.white@ridgeway-analytics.com>, sre-oncall@ridgeway.com
Cc: Rishi Malhotra <rishi_malhotra@redwood.com>, Evelyn Hart <evelyn_hart@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 12 Feb 2027 12:10:00 -08:00
Subject: Re: Production latency and 5xx spike — Ridgeway mitigation in flight

We executed a 35% prefix diversion for /api/v1/infer/read/* to us-east-1 at 11:53 PST and evicted aged KV entries as per Rishi's recommendation. Early telemetry:

- 5xx rate dropped from 8% to 1.7% fleet-wide
- us-west-2 still experiencing higher tail latency for sticky write routes; we did not divert write/sensitive routes.

We're continuing to monitor and will keep the diversion in place until we finish a controlled drain and KV warmup on us-west-2. Please flag if you observe any client regressions (auth, session consistency).

Thanks for the quick coordination.

Alex

messages:
From: Alex Dunn <alex.dunn@finera.com>
To: Jonas Weber <jonas.weber@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
Cc: Priya Shah <priya.shah@finera.com>
Date: Wed, 03 Feb 2027 09:12:00 -0800
Subject: Re: MAP kickoff -> milestone alignment

Jonas / Marissa,

Thanks for the kickoff yesterday. As discussed, the execs want a short weekly one-page status that calls out: current milestone, acceptance criteria progress, open blockers, owners, and next week target. Can you send the first snapshot before EOD Friday (your time)?

We have two critical acceptance items for the POC: throughput >= 120 reqs/min at p95 token latency <= 250ms, and data handling proof-of-concept for VPC routing + audit logs. Those are the gating criteria for the internal success review.

Also attaching the Finera internal checklist we shared with procurement — I pulled the items that map to Redwood deliverables.

Appreciate a quick confirmation on owners so I can escalate any procurement questions to Priya.

Best,
Alex
Alex Dunn
Head of Platform Integrations, Finera
[attachment: finera_poc_checklist.pdf (pdf)]

From: Jonas Weber <jonas.weber@redwood.com>
To: Alex Dunn <alex.dunn@finera.com>, Priya Shah <priya.shah@finera.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 05 Feb 2027 16:28:43 -0800
Subject: MAP playbook: POC milestone runway & acceptance checklist

Alex / Priya —

Attached is our first weekly MAP snapshot (redwood_map_v2.xlsx). High level: we are on track to hit the milestone runway but there are two engineering blockers that could impact the acceptance criteria date if they persist. Summary below and full owner-by-owner actions in the spreadsheet.

Milestone (current): Full trial ingress + KV cache validation (Week 2 of 4)
Acceptance criteria status:
- Throughput: baseline tests show ~135 reqs/min under synthetic traffic but p95 tail varies with long sequences; overall green for baseline, amber for long-tail token scenarios.
- VPC & audit logs: VPC peering established in staging; audit log ingestion proving out with retention policy flagged by FinOps (need KMS artifact).

Open blockers (owners):
1) Long-tail token p95 spikes when sequence length > 1024 — owner: Karthik Iyer (engineering). Action: kernel scheduling tweak and controlled re-quantization test. ETA: 4 business days.
2) KMS policy for audit log encryption — owner: Marissa Cole (security/programs). Action: provide KMS connection guide and short-term workaround with encrypted S3 bucket. ETA: 3 business days.

Next steps / asks for Finera:
- Please confirm whether the Finera acceptance review can accept a conditional pass for throughput if long-tail p95 is mitigated by a scheduled config change (we will provide before/after test artifacts).
- Share the procurement timeline for the KMS/keys if you expect any procurement-level hold.

Attachments: redwood_map_v2.xlsx (single-sheet MAP playbook), POC_acceptance_criteria_v1.pdf (annotated acceptance checklist).

--
Jonas Weber
Director, Solutions Engineering | Redwood Inference
[jonas.weber@redwood.com]

From: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
To: Alex Dunn <alex.dunn@finera.com>, Jonas Weber <jonas.weber@redwood.com>
Cc: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 08 Feb 2027 09:03:10 -0800
Subject: Re: MAP playbook: POC milestone runway & acceptance checklist

Thanks Jonas — quick clarifications from the product & programs side:

- Acceptance criterion for throughput: can we make the gating explicit in the MAP as two paths? 1) hard pass: p95 <= 250ms across realistic seq distribution; 2) conditional pass: baseline throughput met + documented mitigation plan for long-tail with clear regression tests. Finera, please confirm which path you prefer.

- For audit logs/KMS: provisional approach is to use our customer-managed KMS with a scoped key and provide log export to Finera's secure bucket. Marissa will run a short-run playbook with Priya on Wed (invite pending).

Action items I pulled into the spreadsheet (see tab: Owners & Actions):
- Karthik: perf kernel tests + test plan (ETA 2027-02-09)
- Marissa: KMS runbook + short-term encrypted-bucket plan (ETA 2027-02-10)
- Jonas: prepare test artifacts + canary config to demonstrate p95 improvement (ETA 2027-02-11)

Marissa Cole
Senior PM, Product Programs | Redwood Inference
[attachment: POC_acceptance_criteria_v1.pdf (pdf)]

From: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Jonas Weber <jonas.weber@redwood.com>
Cc: Alex Dunn <alex.dunn@finera.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 09 Feb 2027 11:22:05 -0800
Subject: Re: MAP playbook: POC milestone runway & acceptance checklist

On Mon, Feb 8, 2027 at 09:03 AM Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com> wrote:
> Thanks Jonas — quick clarifications from the product & programs side:
>
> - Acceptance criterion for throughput: can we make the gating explicit in the MAP as two paths? ...

Update from engineering: I reproduced the p95 tail issue locally with long sequences and identified two mitigations: 1) tighter KV cache warmup (reduces tail in our runs) and 2) a small quantization fallback for < 8-bit kernels for particular transformer layers. Plan: run A/B canary on staging cluster by EOD Thursday and share before/after results. If successful, this should move the long-tail item from amber -> green without infra changes.

I will attach the perf logs and the test plan to the Drive folder. If the canary fails, ETA for a safe rollback and an infra change is ~7 business days.

Karthik
Staff Engineer, Inference Runtime | Redwood Inference
[attachment: perf_logs_20270209.tar.gz (log archive)]

From: Alex Dunn <alex.dunn@finera.com>
To: Jonas Weber <jonas.weber@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
Cc: Priya Shah <priya.shah@finera.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 10 Feb 2027 15:04:00 -0800
Subject: Re: MAP playbook: POC milestone runway & acceptance checklist

Thanks for the detailed snapshot and clearly assigned owners. That format works well for the Finera review board. We will accept a conditional pass for throughput provided you deliver the canary before Friday and we get the before/after artifacts.

Priya — can you confirm procurement for the KMS key? If we see any delay here we will use the encrypted-bucket interim approach (per Marissa).

Proposed next check-in: Friday 2027-02-12 10:00 AM PT (30 min) — please confirm. I also pasted a quick DocuSign link for the trial terms addendum.

DocuSign: https://docusign.com/Finera-RW-trial-addendum/ABC123

Best,
Alex
Head of Platform Integrations, Finera
[inline: acknowledged]

From: Jonas Weber <jonas.weber@redwood.com>
To: Alex Dunn <alex.dunn@finera.com>, Priya Shah <priya.shah@finera.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 05 Feb 2027 16:28:43 -0800
Subject: MAP playbook: POC milestone runway & acceptance checklist

Alex / Priya —

Attached is our first weekly MAP snapshot (redwood_map_v2.xlsx). High level: we are on track to hit the milestone runway but there are two engineering blockers that could impact the acceptance criteria date if they persist. Summary below and full owner-by-owner actions in the spreadsheet.

Milestone (current): Full trial ingress + KV cache validation (Week 2 of 4)
Acceptance criteria status:
- Throughput: baseline tests show ~135 reqs/min under synthetic traffic but p95 tail varies with long sequences; overall green for baseline, amber for long-tail token scenarios.
- VPC & audit logs: VPC peering established in staging; audit log ingestion proving out with retention policy flagged by FinOps (need KMS artifact).

Open blockers (owners):
1) Long-tail token p95 spikes when sequence length > 1024 — owner: Karthik Iyer (engineering). Action: kernel scheduling tweak and controlled re-quantization test. ETA: 4 business days.
2) KMS policy for audit log encryption — owner: Marissa Cole (security/programs). Action: provide KMS connection guide and short-term workaround with encrypted S3 bucket. ETA: 3 business days.

Next steps / asks for Finera:
- Please confirm whether the Finera acceptance review can accept a conditional pass for throughput if long-tail p95 is mitigated by a scheduled config change (we will provide before/after test artifacts).
- Share the procurement timeline for the KMS/keys if you expect any procurement-level hold.

Attachments: redwood_map_v2.xlsx (single-sheet MAP playbook), POC_acceptance_criteria_v1.pdf (annotated acceptance checklist).

--
Jonas Weber
Director, Solutions Engineering | Redwood Inference
[jonas.weber@redwood.com]

From: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
To: Alex Dunn <alex.dunn@finera.com>, Jonas Weber <jonas.weber@redwood.com>
Cc: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 08 Feb 2027 09:03:10 -0800
Subject: Re: MAP playbook: POC milestone runway & acceptance checklist

Thanks Jonas — quick clarifications from the product & programs side:

- Acceptance criterion for throughput: can we make the gating explicit in the MAP as two paths? 1) hard pass: p95 <= 250ms across realistic seq distribution; 2) conditional pass: baseline throughput met + documented mitigation plan for long-tail with clear regression tests. Finera, please confirm which path you prefer.

- For audit logs/KMS: provisional approach is to use our customer-managed KMS with a scoped key and provide log export to Finera's secure bucket. Marissa will run a short-run playbook with Priya on Wed (invite pending).

Action items I pulled into the spreadsheet (see tab: Owners & Actions):
- Karthik: perf kernel tests + test plan (ETA 2027-02-09)
- Marissa: KMS runbook + short-term encrypted-bucket plan (ETA 2027-02-10)
- Jonas: prepare test artifacts + canary config to demonstrate p95 improvement (ETA 2027-02-11)

Marissa Cole
Senior PM, Product Programs | Redwood Inference
[attachment: POC_acceptance_criteria_v1.pdf (pdf)]

From: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Jonas Weber <jonas.weber@redwood.com>
Cc: Alex Dunn <alex.dunn@finera.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 09 Feb 2027 11:22:05 -0800
Subject: Re: MAP playbook: POC milestone runway & acceptance checklist

On Mon, Feb 8, 2027 at 09:03 AM Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com> wrote:
> Thanks Jonas — quick clarifications from the product & programs side:
>
> - Acceptance criterion for throughput: can we make the gating explicit in the MAP as two paths? ...

Update from engineering: I reproduced the p95 tail issue locally with long sequences and identified two mitigations: 1) tighter KV cache warmup (reduces tail in our runs) and 2) a small quantization fallback for < 8-bit kernels for particular transformer layers. Plan: run A/B canary on staging cluster by EOD Thursday and share before/after results. If successful, this should move the long-tail item from amber -> green without infra changes.

I will attach the perf logs and the test plan to the Drive folder. If the canary fails, ETA for a safe rollback and an infra change is ~7 business days.

Karthik
Staff Engineer, Inference Runtime | Redwood Inference
[attachment: perf_logs_20270209.tar.gz (log archive)]

From: Alex Dunn <alex.dunn@finera.com>
To: Jonas Weber <jonas.weber@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
Cc: Priya Shah <priya.shah@finera.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 10 Feb 2027 15:04:00 -0800
Subject: Re: MAP playbook: POC milestone runway & acceptance checklist

Thanks for the detailed snapshot and clearly assigned owners. That format works well for the Finera review board. We will accept a conditional pass for throughput provided you deliver the canary before Friday and we get the before/after artifacts.

Priya — can you confirm procurement for the KMS key? If we see any delay here we will use the encrypted-bucket interim approach (per Marissa).

Proposed next check-in: Friday 2027-02-12 10:00 AM PT (30 min) — please confirm. I also pasted a quick DocuSign link for the trial terms addendum.

DocuSign: https://docusign.com/Finera-RW-trial-addendum/ABC123

Best,
Alex
Head of Platform Integrations, Finera
[inline: acknowledged]

source.subject: Targeted thread removal request: MARIGOLD-2334
messages:
From: Lena Hoffman <lena.hoffman@marigoldtx.com>
To: Irene Choi <irene.choi@redwood.com>
Cc: Legal Ops <legal.ops@marigoldtx.com>
Date: Sat, 12 Dec 2026 09:18:00 -0800
Subject: Request: remove one conversation from logs (support case MG-2334)

Hi Irene,

We need Redwood's assistance to remove a single conversation thread from our tenant logs. The thread in question is the support exchange between our internal user (sarah.jones@marigoldtx.com) and a Redwood agent on 2026-11-18 that contains PII we did not intend to share. Our internal compliance team has opened case MG-2334 and is requesting a targeted removal (only that thread) from both the activity/audit logs and the short-term telemetry used for model quality.

Can you confirm Redwood's process and what evidence you require from Marigold to authorize that deletion? We can provide a signed authorization and any required redaction manifest. This is time-sensitive for our audit window.

Thanks,
Lena
Lena Hoffman
Head of IT Compliance
Marigold Therapeutics


---
This message begins the request thread.
From: Irene Choi <irene.choi@redwood.com>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwood.com>
Cc: Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 14 Dec 2026 08:12:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Request: remove one conversation from logs (support case MG-2334)

Marissa / Rishi —

Looping you in on Marigold's request (case MG-2334). High level: they want a surgical deletion of one specific conversation thread from our tenant telemetry and activity logs. They will provide a signed authorization; legal wants a short manifest from us describing which records will be removed and confirmation we preserve other audit trails.

Questions for ops/legal:
- Do we require a DocuSign with the customer's letterhead + specific RID?
- Can we simply redact the thread from user-facing audit logs while retaining an internal trace (sealed) for incidents?
- What is the expected timeline if we accept? SLAs typically say 5 business days for targeted log deletions.

I'll coordinate with CS for the authorization template and with infra to scope exactly which indices/partitions will be affected. Rishi — will you own the customer comms (ask for signed DocuSign) and include the Drive link for the authorization? Sean — please get an estimated ETA from infra for a safe excise that doesn't affect backups needed for unrelated investigations.

Thanks,
Irene
Director, Customer Security Operations
Redwood Inference

On Sat, Dec 12, 2026 at 09:18 AM Lena Hoffman <lena.hoffman@marigoldtx.com> wrote:
> Hi Irene,
> 
> We need Redwood's assistance to remove a single conversation thread from our tenant logs...
From: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
To: Irene Choi <irene.choi@redwood.com>, Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwood.com>
Cc: Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 14 Dec 2026 10:03:00 -08:00
Subject: Re: Request: remove one conversation from logs (support case MG-2334)

Irene / team —

Thanks for the heads up. From a compliance/customer success perspective here's a pragmatic plan:

1) Customer provides a signed removal authorization (we have a lightweight template I can attach).
2) We produce a removal manifest listing the exact IDs/timestamps/indices to be excised and a statement of what remains accessible.
3) Legal reviews and signs off; ops schedules a maintenance window for the excision (we run it in a contained job and snapshot affected indices beforehand).
4) We confirm to the customer with proof-of-removal (job ID + checksum of the post-run manifest).

Attachments: support_manifest.pdf (example manifest), removal_auth_template.docx (internal).

I'll take point on drafting the manifest once we have the signed doc. Rishi — can you request the signed DocuSign from Marigold and ask them to include the original thread ID and the exact message timestamps?

Marissa
Sr. Manager, Customer Success
Redwood Inference

On Mon, Dec 14, 2026 at 08:12 AM Irene Choi <irene.choi@redwood.com> wrote:
> Marissa / Rishi —
> 
> Looping you in on Marigold's request (case MG-2334)...
From: Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwood.com>
To: Lena Hoffman <lena.hoffman@marigoldtx.com>
Cc: Irene Choi <irene.choi@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 15 Dec 2026 09:07:00 -08:00
Subject: Re: Request: remove one conversation from logs (support case MG-2334)

Hi Lena —

Thanks for raising this. Quick note on next steps so we're aligned:

1) Please review, sign, and return the authorization we uploaded to the shared Drive folder: https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1a2b3c-MARIGOLD-MG2334
2) In the DocuSign please include: the original thread timestamp (2026-11-18T14:22:00Z), the internal Marigold user (sarah.jones@marigoldtx.com), and an explicit request to remove the thread from activity logs and short-term telemetry only.
3) Once we receive a completed DocuSign, we will schedule the deletion job and provide a job ID and proof-of-removal manifest.

We attached a copy of our support manifest for reference (support_manifest.pdf). If your legal team prefers, we can accept their letter on company letterhead instead of DocuSign but we need the signature and explicit identifiers.

Best,
Rishi
Account Executive, Redwood Inference

> On Mon, Dec 14, 2026 at 10:03 AM Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com> wrote:
> > Irene / team —
> > 
> > Thanks for the heads up. From a compliance/customer success perspective here's a pragmatic plan...
From: Lena Hoffman <lena.hoffman@marigoldtx.com>
To: Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwood.com>
Cc: Legal Ops <legal.ops@marigoldtx.com>, Irene Choi <irene.choi@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 15 Dec 2026 14:23:00 -08:00
Subject: Re: Request: remove one conversation from logs (support case MG-2334)

Rishi / team —

Attached is the signed DocuSign (marigold_removal_auth_docusign.pdf) and a short confirmation from Legal Ops. We included the timestamp and the user's address as requested. Please proceed with scheduling the deletion job and share the job ID + proof-of-removal manifest when complete.

Thank you for the quick turnaround — this helps with our compliance deadline.

Regards,
Lena

Marigold Therapeutics

source.subject: Re: Execution routing and signatory authority for Acme DPA
messages:
From: Janet Smith <janet.smith@acmecorp.com>
To: Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwood.com>
Cc: Legal Team <legal@acmecorp.com>
Date: Thu, 10 Dec 2026 09:12:00 -0800
Subject: Acme DPA redlines and signers

Evelyn — attaching our consolidated redlines (v3) and the signatory matrix. We need to get this executed this week if possible so Q1 integrations can proceed.

Key points:
- We redlined the EU SCC clause and added a subprocessors annex (see pp. 4-5).
- Signatory authority: Acme requires an Officer + Legal Countersign for enterprise DPAs.
- Preferred e-sign: DocuSign (Acme has an enterprise account).

Attachments: acme_dpa_redlines_v3.docx (document)

Janet Smith
Senior Counsel | Acme Corp
janet.smith@acmecorp.com
From: Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwood.com>
To: Janet Smith <janet.smith@acmecorp.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 10 Dec 2026 10:02:00 -08:00
Subject: Re: Acme DPA redlines and signers

Thanks, Janet — received and reviewing. High level: the subprocessors annex looks OK but Sean will confirm the practical implications for our telemetry exports.

On routing/execution logistics — proposed DocuSign flow we want to use:
1) Redwood signer: VP Legal (Evelyn Hart) — Signer 1
2) Acme signer: Officer (Janet) — Signer 2
3) Acme Legal: Countersign (legal@acmecorp.com) — Signer 3 (final)

A few asks from our side so we can build the envelope: 
- Can Acme confirm the email address for the Officer signer (will set recipientId=2 and require ID check if possible).
- We prefer the envelope routing order (sequential) so signatures and the certificate of completion are clean for audit.
- Include the subprocessors annex as a separate PDF attachment page in the envelope (for visibility).

I attached a light routing matrix (esig_routing_matrix.xlsx) that maps Redwood roles to envelope recipient roles. Once we have the Officer email we can create the envelope and send an initial test.

-Evelyn
Evelyn Hart | Senior Contracts Manager | Redwood Inference
From: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
To: Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwood.com>
Cc: Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwood.com>, Janet Smith <janet.smith@acmecorp.com>
Date: Thu, 10 Dec 2026 11:18:00 -08:00

To: Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwood.com>
Cc: Legal Team <legal@acmecorp.com>
Date: Thu, 10 Dec 2026 09:12:00 -0800
Subject: Acme DPA redlines and signers

Evelyn — attaching our consolidated redlines (v3) and the signatory matrix. We need to get this executed this week if possible so Q1 integrations can proceed.

Key points:
- We redlined the EU SCC clause and added a subprocessors annex (see pp. 4-5).
- Signatory authority: Acme requires an Officer + Legal Countersign for enterprise DPAs.
- Preferred e-sign: DocuSign (Acme has an enterprise account).

Attachments: acme_dpa_redlines_v3.docx (document)

Janet Smith
Senior Counsel | Acme Corp
janet.smith@acmecorp.com

I attached a light routing matrix (esig_routing_matrix.xlsx) that maps Redwood roles to envelope recipient roles. Once we have the Officer email we can create the envelope and send an initial test.

source.subject: Intro product tour — 30m windows & stakeholder options
messages:
From: Diego Fernandez <diego.fernandez@redwoodinference.com>
To: Sofia Park <sofia.park@bridgewell.io>, Ethan Shaw <ethan.shaw@bridgewell.io>
Cc: Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Sat, 12 Dec 2026 14:02:00 -0500
Subject: Intro product tour — 30m windows & stakeholder options

Hi Sofia / Ethan —

Thanks again for today’s discovery — really helpful to understand your MRP ingest constraints and the batch inference cadence you mentioned. Quick recap of the things we agreed on:

- Primary success metric: reduce per-request latency below 300ms for the invoicing pipeline.
- Security priorities: VPC-only deployment for PHI, SOC2 evidence required during procurement.
- Key stakeholders for the next call: Sofia (Analytics lead), Ethan (Platform/infra), and a potential observer from your procurement team.

Next step: 30-minute intro product tour (high-level demo + brief Q&A). To save back-and-forth, here are three concrete options — all times in ET, with approximate UTC equivalents: 

Option A — Tue Dec 15, 10:00–10:30 ET (15:00 UTC) — good if Ethan wants the morning slot
Option B — Wed Dec 16, 13:00–13:30 ET (18:00 UTC) — best overlap for your London office if needed
Option C — Thu Dec 17, 16:00–16:30 ET (21:00 UTC) — flexible for late-day schedules

Please tell me which option works and who you want on the invite (we’ll include a short agenda and the attachment 'redwood-demo-agenda-v1.docx'). If none of these work, send me two 30-minute windows that do.

Attachments: bridgewell-mrp-reqs.pdf (shared for reference)

I’ll have Monica (solutions engineer) and Sean (customer reliability) on standby in case there are infra/security questions.

Cheers,
Diego
Account Executive | Redwood Inference
 diego.fernandez@redwoodinference.com | +1 (415) 555-0132

From: Sofia Park <sofia.park@bridgewell.io>
To: Diego Fernandez <diego.fernandez@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Ethan Shaw <ethan.shaw@bridgewell.io>
Date: Sat, 12 Dec 2026 18:11:00 -05:00
Subject: Re: Intro product tour — 30m windows & stakeholder options

Diego —

Thanks for the clear recap and the options. Option B (Wed Dec 16, 13:00 ET) works best for our team. I’ll invite our procurement lead, Laura Kim, as an observer — she’s mostly there to understand timelines and SOC2 evidence requests.

Quick ask before the demo: can you include a 5–7 min walkthrough of how Redwood handles dual-region failover and the audit log retention settings? That’s high on procurement’s checklist.

I’ll accept the calendar invite once you send it.

Best,
Sofia Park
Director, Analytics | Bridgewell Health
sofia.park@bridgewell.io | +1 (212) 555-2099

On Sat, Dec 12, 2026 at 2:02 PM Diego Fernandez <diego.fernandez@redwoodinference.com> wrote:
> Hi Sofia / Ethan —
>

> - Primary success metric: reduce per-request latency below 300ms for the invoicing pipeline.
> - Security priorities: VPC-only deployment for PHI, SOC2 evidence required during procurement.
> - Key stakeholders for the next call: Sofia (Analytics lead), Ethan (Platform/infra), and a potential observer from your procurement team.
> 
> Next step: 30-minute intro product tour (high-level demo + brief Q&A). To save back-and-forth, here are three concrete options — all times in ET, with approximate UTC equivalents: 
> 
> Option A — Tue Dec 15, 10:00–10:30 ET (15:00 UTC) — good if Ethan wants the morning slot
> Option B — Wed Dec 16, 13:00–13:30 ET (18:00 UTC) — best overlap for your London office if needed
> Option C — Thu Dec 17, 16:00–16:30 ET (21:00 UTC) — flexible for late-day schedules
> 
> Please tell me which option works and who you want on the invite (we’ll include a short agenda and the attachment 'redwood-demo-agenda-v1.docx'). If none of these work, send me two 30-minute windows that do.
> 
> Attachments: bridgewell-mrp-reqs.pdf (shared for reference)
> 
> I’ll have Monica (solutions engineer) and Sean (customer reliability) on standby in case there are infra/security questions.
> 
> Cheers,
> Diego
> Account Executive | Redwood Inference
> diego.fernandez@redwoodinference.com | +1 (415) 555-0132

From: Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwoodinference.com>
To: Diego Fernandez <diego.fernandez@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Sun, 13 Dec 2026 08:05:00 -05:00
Subject: Re: Intro product tour — 30m windows & stakeholder options

Diego —

Option B is good on our side. I’ll prepare a 6-minute segment covering dual-region failover (architecture diagram + failback behavior) and a quick pointer to where audit logs are surfaced in the Console. I’ve attached a lightweight slide (redwood-demo-agenda-v1.docx) with the proposed time split.

Also: do we want to surface the SOC2 report during the demo or save it for procurement follow-up? My recommendation is to mention it quickly and say we’ll share the formal report via your procurement contact afterwards — keeps the demo tight.

—Monica
Solutions Engineer | Redwood Inference
monica.patel@redwoodinference.com
Attachment: redwood-demo-agenda-v1.docx (2 slides, 48 KB)

From: Diego Fernandez <diego.fernandez@redwoodinference.com>
To: Sofia Park <sofia.park@bridgewell.io>, Ethan Shaw <ethan.shaw@bridgewell.io>, Laura Kim <laura.kim@bridgewell.io>
Cc: Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwoodinference.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Sun, 13 Dec 2026 09:15:00 -05:00
Subject: Confirmed — Wed Dec 16, 13:00–13:30 ET (Bridgewell intro product tour)

Hi all —

Thanks — calendar invite sent for Wed Dec 16, 13:00–13:30 ET. Agenda in the invite includes: 7m overview, 6m dual-region + audit logs (Monica), 10m live demo (happy path + quantization/latency plan), 7m Q&A.

Laura — welcome, we’ll briefly cover procurement timelines and I’ll follow up after the call with SOC2 evidence and our standard procurement checklist.

Attachments: bridgewell-mrp-reqs.pdf (reference), redwood-demo-agenda-v1.docx

See you Wednesday —
Diego
Account Executive | Redwood Inference
 diego.fernandez@redwoodinference.com | +1 (415) 555-0132

source.subject: Penetration test report - conditional sharing, watermarking & viewer proposal
messages:
From: Jill Turner <security@acme-payments.com>
To: Sean Gallagher <sean_gallagher@redwood.com>
Cc: legal@acme-payments.com
Date: Fri, 4 Dec 2026 09:08:00 -0500
Subject: Request for penetration test deliverables ahead of SOC2 audit

Hi Sean,

Thanks for taking this up. Acme Payments is finalizing our SOC 2 evidence package for an audit scheduled the week of Jan 11. Our auditor has requested the most recent penetration test report and an executive summary that we can include in the binder.

Before we proceed, our legal team needs confirmation on what level of detail you can share with a third-party auditor and what (if any) redactions or protections are required under Redwood's vendor agreements and your pen-test contract with RedShore. In particular, we need to know:

- Can you provide a full exec summary that includes risk classifications and remediation status?
- Are there any PII, customer identifiers, or sensitive hostnames that must be redacted?
- Will Redwood or the pen-test vendor require an NDA amendment or viewing agreement?

If possible, we'd prefer: (1) a short annotated executive summary for auditors, and (2) a way to view supporting evidence (screenshots, logs) in a time-limited, auditable viewer rather than receiving full raw artifacts.

Please advise on the preferred workflow and any docs we should sign.

Best,
Jill Turner
Head of Security Operations
Acme Payments

Attachments: none

From: Sean Gallagher <sean_gallagher@redwood.com>
To: Jill Turner <security@acme-payments.com>
Cc: Tom Ellis <pen-tests@redshoresec.com>, Laura Bennett <laura_bennett@redwood.com>, Kimberly Park <kimberly_park@redwood.com>
Date: Sun, 6 Dec 2026 10:34:00 -0500
Subject: Re: Request for penetration test deliverables ahead of SOC2 audit

Jill —

Thanks for the clear ask and the audit timeline. We can support a controlled share that meets auditor needs while protecting sensitive collateral and our vendor obligations. Proposed deliverables and controls:

1) Annotated executive summary (attached) — a 2-3 page PDF containing: high-level findings, CVSS-style severity, remediation status (fixed/in-progress/accepted), and per-finding risk note. This is safe to include in an audit binder.

2) Evidence package access — instead of shipping raw artifacts, we can provide: (a) a temporary secure viewer session (7 days by default) hosted on Redwood with per-user watermarking and download logging, and (b) an Evidence manifest (CSV) that lists artifact names and timestamps.

3) Redaction boundaries — our baseline: redact internal customer identifiers, external customer transaction IDs, and full internal IP ranges. We will not redact technical details required to understand a finding (vulnerability type, affected component), but screenshots that include customer data will be blurred.

4) NDA/viewer agreement — because the pen test was conducted under contract with RedShoreSec, we need either (A) a standard Redwood mutual NDA addendum that includes secure-viewer terms and audit access, or (B) an agreement from Acme to treat the viewer output as internal use only and not to re-share raw artifacts. We can send a short addendum for quick signature.

Attached is a draft exec summary (Redwood_PT_ExecSummary_v1.pdf) and an Evidence_package_manifest.csv listing the artifacts we propose to make available via the viewer. If that looks acceptable, Tom (pen-test vendor) and our legal/security teams can finalize the viewer configuration and watermarking text.

Open to one quick call this week to finalize the redaction checklist and signer for the addendum.

Thanks,
Sean Gallagher
Director, Trust & Security
Redwood Inference
Attachments: Redwood_PT_ExecSummary_v1.pdf (PDF, 1.2MB), Evidence_package_manifest.csv (CSV, 2.3KB)

From: Tom Ellis <pen-tests@redshoresec.com>
To: Sean Gallagher <sean_gallagher@redwood.com>, Jill Turner <security@acme-payments.com>
Date: Sun, 6 Dec 2026 15:03:00 -0500
Subject: Re: Request for penetration test deliverables ahead of SOC2 audit

Thanks Sean and Jill — quick clarifications from RedShore's side:

- Contractual constraints: Our engagement terms require that raw evidence (full logs, unredacted screenshots containing 3rd-party customer data) are not distributed beyond the client without RedShore approval. We can, however, provide sanitized artifacts or a certified evidence bundle that omits customer-identifying columns.

- Viewer controls: We can support per-document watermarking (user, company, timestamp) and a viewer that prevents bulk export. A 7-day window is fine; we recommend issuing unique one-time links per auditor contact to preserve non-repudiation.

- Redaction suggestions: We agree with Redwood on redacting external customer IDs and internal hostnames that are PII-like. For audit purposes, we recommend including a short attest statement in the exec summary describing what was redacted and why (e.g., "Certain identifiers redacted to protect third-party customer privacy; full artifacts available under RedShore-approved viewing conditions").

If you want, I can prepare a brief vendor attestation PDF we can include in the binder stating that a full evidence review was performed and the viewer session is verifiable.

Best,
Tom Ellis
Principal Consultant
RedShore Security Services

Attachments: none

From: Sean Gallagher <sean_gallagher@redwood.com>
To: Laura Bennett <laura_bennett@redwood.com>, Kimberly Park <kimberly_park@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 7 Dec 2026 14:26:00 -0500
Subject: Fwd: Request for penetration test deliverables ahead of SOC2 audit

Team — forwarding the thread with Acme (Jill) and RedShore (Tom). Action items I propose before we send anything to Acme: 

- Laura: confirm legal signatory and prepare a one-page addendum (mutual NDA + viewer terms) that allows a 7-day watermarked viewer and prohibits re-sharing. Target: review copy by EOD Tue.
- Kimberly: prepare the exec summary (finalize wording of redaction attestation paragraph) and sanitize the Evidence_package_manifest.csv to remove any customer-specific filenames. I attached my draft; please edit.
- Security ops: ensure the secure viewer instance will log IP and user-agent for each access and produce an audit log export for Acme's auditor.

I will coordinate with RedShore to produce the vendor attestation PDF Tom offered and confirm the one-time link mechanics. We should be able to deliver the exec summary and viewer access to Acme within 48 hours once legal approves the addendum.

Sean Gallagher
Director, Trust & Security
Redwood Inference

> >
> > 1) Annotated executive summary (attached) — a 2-3 page PDF containing: high-level findings, CVSS-style severity, remediation status (fixed/in-progress/accepted), and per-finding risk note. This is safe to include in an audit binder.
> >
> > 2) Evidence package access — instead of shipping raw artifacts, we can provide: (a) a temporary secure viewer session (7 days by default) hosted on Redwood with per-user watermarking and download logging, and (b) an Evidence manifest (CSV) that lists artifact names and timestamps.
> >
> > 3) Redaction boundaries — our baseline: redact internal customer identifiers, external customer transaction IDs, and full internal IP ranges. We will not redact technical details required to understand a finding (vulnerability type, affected component), but screenshots that include customer data will be blurred.
> >
> > 4) NDA/viewer agreement — because the pen test was conducted under contract with RedShoreSec, we need either (A) a standard Redwood mutual NDA addendum that includes secure-viewer terms and audit access, or (B) an agreement from Acme to treat the viewer output as internal use only and not to re-share raw artifacts. We can send a short addendum for quick signature.
> >
> > Attached is a draft exec summary (Redwood_PT_ExecSummary_v1.pdf) and an Evidence_package_manifest.csv listing the artifacts we propose to make available via the viewer. If that looks acceptable, Tom (pen-test vendor) and our legal/security teams can finalize the viewer configuration and watermarking text.
> >

Attached is a draft exec summary (Redwood_PT_ExecSummary_v1.pdf) and an Evidence_package_manifest.csv listing the artifacts we propose to make available via the viewer. If that looks acceptable, Tom (pen-test vendor) and our legal/security teams can finalize the viewer configuration and watermarking text.

Open to one quick call this week to finalize the redaction checklist and signer for the addendum.

Thanks,
Sean Gallagher
Director, Trust & Security
Redwood Inference
Attachments: Redwood_PT_ExecSummary_v1.pdf (PDF, 1.2MB), Evidence_package_manifest.csv (CSV, 2.3KB)


From: Tom Ellis <pen-tests@redshoresec.com>
To: Sean Gallagher <sean_gallagher@redwood.com>, Jill Turner <security@acme-payments.com>
Date: Sun, 6 Dec 2026 15:03:00 -0500
Subject: Re: Request for penetration test deliverables ahead of SOC2 audit

Thanks Sean and Jill — quick clarifications from RedShore's side:

- Contractual constraints: Our engagement terms require that raw evidence (full logs, unredacted screenshots containing 3rd-party customer data) are not distributed beyond the client without RedShore approval. We can, however, provide sanitized artifacts or a certified evidence bundle that omits customer-identifying columns.

- Viewer controls: We can support per-document watermarking (user, company, timestamp) and a viewer that prevents bulk export. A 7-day window is fine; we recommend issuing unique one-time links per auditor contact to preserve non-repudiation.

- Redaction suggestions: We agree with Redwood on redacting external customer IDs and internal hostnames that are PII-like. For audit purposes, we recommend including a short attest statement in the exec summary describing what was redacted and why (e.g., "Certain identifiers redacted to protect third-party customer privacy; full artifacts available under RedShore-approved viewing conditions").

If you want, I can prepare a brief vendor attestation PDF we can include in the binder stating that a full evidence review was performed and the viewer session is verifiable.

Best,
Tom Ellis
Principal Consultant
RedShore Security Services

Attachments: none


From: Sean Gallagher <sean_gallagher@redwood.com>
To: Laura Bennett <laura_bennett@redwood.com>, Kimberly Park <kimberly_park@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 7 Dec 2026 14:26:00 -0500
Subject: Fwd: Request for penetration test deliverables ahead of SOC2 audit

Team — forwarding the thread with Acme (Jill) and RedShore (Tom). Action items I propose before we send anything to Acme: 

- Laura: confirm legal signatory and prepare a one-page addendum (mutual NDA + viewer terms) that allows a 7-day watermarked viewer and prohibits re-sharing. Target: review copy by EOD Tue.
- Kimberly: prepare the exec summary (finalize wording of redaction attestation paragraph) and sanitize the Evidence_package_manifest.csv to remove any customer-specific filenames. I attached my draft; please edit.
- Security ops: ensure the secure viewer instance will log IP and user-agent for each access and produce an audit log export for Acme's auditor.

I will coordinate with RedShore to produce the vendor attestation PDF Tom offered and confirm the one-time link mechanics. We should be able to deliver the exec summary and viewer access to Acme within 48 hours once legal approves the addendum.

Sean Gallagher
Director, Trust & Security
Redwood Inference

Quoted text:

> > Jill —
> >
> > Thanks for the clear ask and the audit timeline. We can support a controlled share that meets auditor needs while protecting sensitive collateral and our vendor obligations. Proposed deliverables and controls:
> >
> > 1) Annotated executive summary (attached) — a 2-3 page PDF containing: high-level findings, CVSS-style severity, remediation status (fixed/in-progress/accepted), and per-finding risk note. This is safe to include in an audit binder.
> >
> > 2) Evidence package access — instead of shipping raw artifacts, we can provide: (a) a temporary secure viewer session (7 days by default) hosted on Redwood with per-user watermarking and download logging, and (b) an Evidence manifest (CSV) that lists artifact names and timestamps.
> >
> > 3) Redaction boundaries — our baseline: redact internal customer identifiers, external customer transaction IDs, and full internal IP ranges. We will not redact technical details required to understand a finding (vulnerability type, affected component), but screenshots that include customer data will be blurred.
> >
> > 4) NDA/viewer agreement — because the pen test was conducted under contract with RedShoreSec, we need either (A) a standard Redwood mutual NDA addendum that includes secure-viewer terms and audit access, or (B) an agreement from Acme to treat the viewer output as internal use only and not to re-share raw artifacts. We can send a short addendum for quick signature.

source.subject: Request: token-level usage export and sample traces for invoice dispute
messages:
From: Lindsey Park <lindsey.park@acme-bio.com>
To: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwoodinference.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Acme Bio Finance <payments@acme-bio.com>
Date: Sun, 29 Nov 2026 09:12:00 -0800
Subject: Unexpected overage on Nov invoice — request for token-level export

Hi Jordan / Marissa,

We received invoice #2026-11-23 and are seeing a ~68% jump in billed tokens compared to our internal estimates for the same window. The attached invoice is here (invoice_20261123.pdf). Before we open a formal billing dispute, could you provide a token-level export and a couple of sample request traces for Nov 21–23?

Specifically, we'd like:
- CSV export of request_id, timestamp (UTC), model, prompt_tokens, completion_tokens, total_tokens for Nov 21–23 (UTC).
- 10 sample full traces (prompt + response lengths) around peak hours (UTC 16:00–20:00) so we can reconcile with our app logs.

We suspect either double-counting from a client-side retry pattern or a change in tokenization when we switched to the 8k model on Nov 20. Please confirm ETA — we need this for our month-end close.

Thanks,
Lindsey Park
Head of Finance, Acme Bio
Lindsey.Park@acme-bio.com
+1 (415) 555-0142

Attachment: invoice_20261123.pdf (pdf)

From: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwoodinference.com>
To: Lindsey Park <lindsey.park@acme-bio.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Mon, 30 Nov 2026 10:03:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Unexpected overage on Nov invoice — request for token-level export

Hi Lindsey — thanks for flagging this and for sending the invoice. I've looped in Marcus (finance) and Sean (your AE) to help prioritize. We're happy to pull the CSV export and sample traces.

Quick notes so we're aligned:
- We bill on total_tokens (prompt + completion) per request_id.
- Retries that result in multiple accepted requests may be billed separately unless you used the Idempotency key header; we'll check for duplicates in our logs.
- The model switch on Nov 20 could change token counts if tokenization differs between variants.

I'll produce an initial export for Nov 21–23 (UTC) and 10 sample traces by EOD Dec 2. If you'd like faster turnaround, we can push a priority export — just confirm.

Jordan Blake
Director, Revenue Ops | Redwood Inference
jordan.blake@redwoodinference.com
Attachment: usage_export_202611_preview.csv (csv stub)

From: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com>
To: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwoodinference.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Lindsey Park <lindsey.park@acme-bio.com>
Date: Wed, 02 Dec 2026 14:18:00 -08:00
Subject: Re: Unexpected overage on Nov invoice — request for token-level export

Jordan / team — I pulled the full per-request export and a small set of traces and uploaded them to the shared Drive link (see below). Initial findings:

- There are ~1.9k request_id duplicates across Nov 21–23 where the same user request returned different request_ids due to client retries without an idempotency key. Each attempt was accepted by the runtime and therefore billed.
- ~12% of total_tokens uplift looks correlated with a model variant change (the 8k context variant uses a slightly different tokenizer).

Recommendation for Acme: we can provide a one-time courtesy credit for clearly duplicate retries where timestamps are within 5s and payloads match exactly. For the tokenizer delta, we can provide a CSV mapping of token counts per request so you can reconcile your internal metrics.

Marcus Lin
Finance Manager | Redwood Inference
marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com
Attachment: token_sample_trace.txt (text)
Related Drive: https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1ExampleIdAcmeBilling

From: Lindsey Park <lindsey.park@acme-bio.com>
To: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwoodinference.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Fri, 05 Dec 2026 16:03:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Unexpected overage on Nov invoice — request for token-level export

Thanks Jordan / Marcus — received the export and sample traces. The duplicate-retry analysis matches our internal request ids; appreciate the quick turn. A couple of follow-ups:

- Please confirm the exact credit amount you propose for the duplicate attempts (we counted ~1,920 billed duplicates).
- For the tokenizer differences, can the CSV include both \"redwood_tokens\" and \"acme_estimated_tokens\" columns so our reconciliation is line-by-line?
- We also need confirmation whether VAT/tax was applied to the credit (we are in the US but our entity is VAT-registered for EU deployments).

If we can get a written credit estimate and the updated CSV by Tue Dec 8, we can close the dispute on our side.

Best,
Lindsey

On Thu, Dec 2, 2026 at 14:18:00 -0800 Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com> wrote:
> Jordan / team — I pulled the full per-request export and a small set of traces and uploaded them to the shared Drive link (see below). Initial findings:
>
> - There are ~1.9k request_id duplicates across Nov 21–23 where the same user request returned different request_ids due to client retries without an idempotency key. Each attempt was accepted by the runtime and therefore billed.
> - ~12% of total_tokens uplift looks correlated with a model variant change (the 8k context variant uses a slightly different tokenizer).
>
> Recommendation for Acme: we can provide a one-time courtesy credit for clearly duplicate retries where timestamps are within 5s and payloads match exactly. For the tokenizer delta, we can provide a CSV mapping of token counts per request so you can reconcile your internal metrics.
>
> Marcus Lin
> Finance Manager | Redwood Inference
> marus.lin@redwoodinference.com
> Attachment: token_sample_trace.txt (text)

source.subject: Next steps & vendor setup - BrightLeaf POC
messages:
From: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.ai>
To: Priya Desai <priya.desai@brightleafhealth.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>
Date: Fri, 20 Nov 2026 15:22:00 -0800
Subject: Next steps & vendor setup - BrightLeaf POC

Hi Priya —

Great to meet you and Tom on today's call. Quick recap and proposed next steps so we stay aligned: 

- Scope: 2-week POC to validate inference latency & cost for BrightLeaf's summarization pipeline (live patient notes).
- Success criteria: 95th %ile token latency < 350ms, end-to-end throughput of 25 qps, and < 15% delta in token quality vs. baseline.
- Redwood deliverables: sandbox endpoint, sample prompts + evaluation harness, cost estimate and vendor enrollment for production-ready access.

Action items we discussed: 
1) You to loop in procurement/finance so we can complete vendor setup (W-9 + ACH) — they mentioned a portal invite is required.
2) We will send an updated quote and docs (attached).
3) Schedule POC kickoff once vendor account is provisioned.

If procurement prefers, we can work directly with your vendor team — please share the contact name/email and any portal link we should use. Marissa (cc'd) will handle the vendor onboarding once we have the W-9/ACH details.

Attachments: redwood-proposal-v2.pdf (PDF, 48KB)

Thanks again, and I can be flexible on timing next week if you want to lock the kickoff.

— Jordan Blake
Account Executive | Redwood Inference
jordan.blake@redwood.ai | +1 (415) 555-0132
http://redwood.ai
From: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.ai>
To: Priya Desai <priya.desai@brightleafhealth.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>
Date: Wed, 25 Nov 2026 09:07:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Next steps & vendor setup - BrightLeaf POC

Hi Priya —

Just bumping this in case it got buried. We haven't received the vendor portal invite or a procurement contact yet. Is vendor setup still a go for this month, or should we shift the kickoff timing?

If it helps, Marissa can take care of the portal invite and vendor enrollment paperwork directly — happy to introduce her to whoever in finance owns onboarding.

(Quoted for context)
On Fri, Nov 20, 2026 at 3:22 PM Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.ai> wrote:
> Hi Priya —
> 
> Great to meet you and Tom on today's call. Quick recap and proposed next steps so we stay aligned: ...
> 
> Attachments: redwood-proposal-v2.pdf

— Jordan
From: Priya Desai <priya.desai@brightleafhealth.com>
To: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.ai>
Cc: Mark Rivers <mark.rivers@brightleafhealth.com>
Date: Mon, 30 Nov 2026 09:03:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Next steps & vendor setup - BrightLeaf POC

Jordan —

Thanks for the follow-up and apologies for the delay. Procurement picked this up but asked us to route vendor onboarding through Finance. I've looped in our finance lead Mark Rivers (cc).

Mark — can you confirm the vendor portal link or whether BrightLeaf uses an internal spreadsheet process? Also want to confirm we need a W-9 and ACH form from Redwood.

We remain interested in the POC, but timeline has moved a bit on our side (end of year priorities). If vendor setup can be completed by mid-December, we should still be able to kick off before the new year.

Thanks,
Priya
Senior Product Manager, BrightLeaf Health
priya.desai@brightleafhealth.com

(Forwarded) On Wed, Nov 25, 2026 at 9:07 AM Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.ai> wrote:
> Just bumping this in case it got buried. ...
From: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.ai>
To: Mark Rivers <mark.rivers@brightleafhealth.com>, Priya Desai <priya.desai@brightleafhealth.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwood.ai>
Date: Thu, 03 Dec 2026 10:15:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Next steps & vendor setup - BrightLeaf POC

Hi Mark (and Priya) —

Thanks for the quick turnaround. Looping in Marissa from Sales Ops and Sean from Legal to speed vendor enrollment: 

Marissa — can you take point on the onboarding flow for BrightLeaf? Per Priya, it sounds like W-9 + ACH details are required. I'm attaching our W-9 and a short vendor setup checklist.

Mark — two options below — whichever is easiest for your finance team: 
1) Portal invite: we can send a vendor portal invite to mark.rivers@brightleafhealth.com — portal link and steps are here: https://redwood.app/vendor-portal/invite/RID-2026-0987
2) DocuSign enrollment: we can send the enrollment packet via DocuSign if you prefer (https://www.docusign.com/en/prepare/rid-2026-0987-enrollment).
