source.subject: Alleged missing answers in batched responses — investigation & closure
source.messages:
From: Alex Johnson <alex.johnson@aasparrow.com>
To: Irene Choi <irene.choi@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 30 Nov 2026 09:12:00 -0800
Subject: Missing answers in batched responses

Hi Irene,

We saw an intermittent issue today where a 10-item batched completion returned 8 completed items and 2 items with empty 'choices' arrays (no error). I've attached a screenshot and our request/response log excerpt (api-request-log-20261130.txt). The request id is batch_req_20261130_09:02_aa; timestamps in our system match the call at 09:02:37 PST.

This caused a partial failure in our processing pipeline. Can you confirm whether anything on Redwood's side could have dropped two items in a batch? We haven't seen this before and it's blocking a customer demo.

Thanks,
Alex
Alex Johnson
Platform Engineer, AA Sparrow
alex.johnson@aasparrow.com

--- Attachment: screenshot-missing-answer-20261130.png (image/png)
--- Attachment: api-request-log-20261130.txt (text/plain)

---
From: Irene Choi <irene.choi@redwood.com>
To: Alex Johnson <alex.johnson@aasparrow.com>
Cc: Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 30 Nov 2026 10:05:12 -0800
Subject: Re: Missing answers in batched responses

Hi Alex — thanks for the report and the attachments. I’ve looped in Sean and Marissa from Support/Platform so we can triage quickly.

Initial plan:
- Sean will try to reproduce against the same model + sequence length and inspect Redwood runtime logs for batch_req_20261130_09:02_aa.
- If we can’t reproduce, we’ll ask for a minimal curl/postman sample that reproduces the issue and the full X-Request-Id header from any failing response.

We’ll aim to come back within a few hours. Apologies for the interruption to your demo prep.

Best,
Irene
Irene Choi
Account Manager, Redwood Inference
irene.choi@redwood.com

---
From: Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwood.com>
To: Irene Choi <irene.choi@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Alex Johnson <alex.johnson@aasparrow.com>
Date: Mon, 01 Dec 2026 13:22:43 -0800
Subject: Re: Missing answers in batched responses — triage update

Team / Alex,

I dug into runtime logs for the time window you provided and ran local reproductions against the same model and batch size (10). Summary: we could not reproduce missing 'choices' entries on the server side. Observations:

- The Redwood serving runtime shows a successful batching pass for batch_req_20261130_09:02_aa; all sub-requests completed with non-empty token outputs in our logs. The server-side API logs show 10 positive completions and no internal errors.
- The lines in the api-request-log you sent that look like empty responses correspond to a client-side timeout and subsequent retry logic in your pipeline that recorded an empty placeholder when the client aborted waiting for the streamed response. See excerpt below (redacted):
  "client_received_timeout": true
  "client_retry_placeholder": true

- We also checked the KV cache and no eviction or prefix drop is visible for the call. Kernel/quantization telemetry normal.

Next steps I recommend for immediate validation:
1) Re-run the call with streaming disabled (or ensure stream consumer waits for full response). Example curl:
   curl -X POST https://api.redwood.com/v1/batch_complete \n     -H "Authorization: Bearer <key>" \n     -H "Content-Type: application/json" \n     -d '{"batch": [...], "stream": false}'

2) Include the X-Request-Id header from your client logs if you still see empty items, and send the raw server response if possible.
3) If you want we can run a side-by-side test with our engineering account hitting the same model/region while you trigger a request.

I’ve attached a short trimmed server log verifying per-item completions. Let me know if you want us to run a coordinated test window.

Thanks,
Sean Gallagher
Support Engineer, Redwood Inference
sean.gallagher@redwood.com

--- Attachment: internal-log-excerpt-20261130.txt (text/plain)

> On Dec 1, 2026, at 14:02 PST, Alex Johnson <alex.johnson@aasparrow.com> wrote:
>
> Thanks Sean — we ran a controlled test following your curl sample and observed the same behavior in our staging environment when our consumer timed out after 5s. When we increased the client timeout to 20s and disabled early-abort retry logic, all 10 items returned correctly.
>
> It looks like the issue is on our side (aggressive timeout + retry). Apologies for the false alarm — very helpful to have the logs validated by your team.
>
> Could you please send a brief note we can include in our internal postmortem stating that Redwood's logs showed no internal errors and that all sub-requests completed server-side? Also, confirm the request id we provided did not show missing items server-side.
>
> Thanks again,
> Alex

---
From: Irene Choi <irene.choi@redwood.com>
To: Alex Johnson <alex.johnson@aasparrow.com>
Cc: Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 02 Dec 2026 15:47:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Missing answers in batched responses — closure and summary

Hi Alex — glad the coordinated test helped surface the client timeout behavior. Below is a brief statement you can paste into your postmortem: 

"Redwood Inference investigated API call batch_req_20261130_09:02_aa. Server-side telemetry and runtime logs show 10 completed sub-requests with non-empty outputs; no internal errors, kernel faults, or batch drop events were observed. The partial results reported by the customer were attributable to an aggressive client-side timeout and retry that recorded placeholders when the client aborted waiting for the full streamed response. Redwood recommends verifying client-side stream consumption and increasing consumer timeout or implementing safe retry/backoff for batched calls.

If AA Sparrow would like Redwood to participate in a live reproduction during a narrow window, we can coordinate with SEs to run simultaneous calls.
"

We’ve attached the internal log excerpt used during triage and linked the support ticket for your records. Let us know if you need anything else.

Best,
Irene
Irene Choi
Account Manager, Redwood Inference
irene.choi@redwood.com

--- Attachment: internal-log-excerpt-20261130.txt (text/plain)
--- Link: https://redwood.zendesk.com/tickets/987654

---
From: Irene Choi <irene.choi@redwood.com>
To: Alex Johnson <alex.johnson@aasparrow.com>
Cc: Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 02 Dec 2026 15:47:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Missing answers in batched responses — closure and summary

Hi Alex — glad the coordinated test helped surface the client timeout behavior. Below is a brief statement you can paste into your postmortem: 

"Redwood Inference investigated API call batch_req_20261130_09:02_aa. Server-side telemetry and runtime logs show 10 completed sub-requests with non-empty outputs; no internal errors, kernel faults, or batch drop events were observed. The partial results reported by the customer were attributable to an aggressive client-side timeout and retry that recorded placeholders when the client aborted waiting for the full streamed response. Redwood recommends verifying client-side stream consumption and increasing consumer timeout or implementing safe retry/backoff for batched calls.

If AA Sparrow would like Redwood to participate in a live reproduction during a narrow window, we can coordinate with SEs to run simultaneous calls.
"

We’ve attached the internal log excerpt used during triage and linked the support ticket for your records. Let us know if you need anything else.

Best,
Irene
Irene Choi
Account Manager, Redwood Inference
irene.choi@redwood.com

--- Attachment: internal-log-excerpt-20261130.txt (text/plain)
--- Link: https://redwood.zendesk.com/tickets/987654

messages:
From: Camila Reyes <camila@redwood.com>
To: Claire Nguyen <claire@acmehealth.com>
Cc: Rishi Malhotra <rishi@redwood.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 30 Nov 2026 14:02:00 -0800
Subject: Next steps — hosting choice & procurement readiness for Acme Health

Hi Claire — thanks again for the call earlier. Quick recap and proposed next steps so we stay aligned on procurement requirements and final hosting selection.

Recap (from our discovery)
- Primary need: secure, predictable inference for patient-facing summarization workflows (P0).
- Data classification: PHI-equivalent; must remain in VPC or dedicated tenancy.
- Timeline: procurement kickoff this month, contract signed by end of Q1 2027 if scope is stable.

Procurement/Commercial options we discussed
1) Redwood Private (VPC/on-prem): preferred for strict network controls and KMS integration; typically enterprise license + support.
2) Redwood Dedicated (reserved GPU capacity): good fit if they want predictable throughput without on-prem ops; supports commit-based pricing.
3) Redwood Hosted (on-demand): fastest to get started; good for pilots but less ideal for PHI unless we use private tenancy.

Simple asks for your procurement team (if you can confirm):
- Preferred vendor path: PO + vendor onboarding OR credit card + self-serve?
- Is Acme required to go through a marketplace listing (CloudMarketplace) for invoices/terms?
- Who owns redlines: Acme legal or third-party procurement? If you can share a preferred contract template that helps.

Pricing logistics to align before procurement kickoff
- If procurement prefers PO + revenue recognition via SAP/ERP, we should propose Dedicated with a 12-mo commit to simplify billing.
- If card/on-demand is required, we can pilot on Hosted for 60–90 days; recommend scoped commit post-pilot to drop unit price.

Attachments: I attached our latest SOC 2 summary and a draft Master Agreement + SOW template for your legal team to scan.

Proposed next steps
- Claire/procurement: confirm vendor route (PO vs card) and whether marketplace is required by Wed, Dec 3.
- Redwood: provide a 12-month Dedicated commit pricing and a Hosted pilot quote by Fri, Dec 6.
- Schedule a 30-min procurement sync next week to walk through onboarding and evidence requirements.

Thanks — happy to jump on a call with your procurement and IT security.

Best,
Camila Reyes
Account Executive | Redwood Inference
camila@redwood.com | (415) 555-0192

Attachments: SOC2_Type2_v3.pdf; Redwood_Draft_MA_Acme.docx; SOW-Acme-template.pdf

From: Claire Nguyen <claire@acmehealth.com>
To: Camila Reyes <camila@redwood.com>
Cc: Acme Health Procurement <procure@acmehealth.com>
Date: Tue, 01 Dec 2026 09:10:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Next steps — hosting choice & procurement readiness for Acme Health

Camila — thanks for the clear recap. A few clarifications from procurement and IT: 

1) Procurement path: Acme prefers a PO process (we route vendors through our SAP system). Card payments are only for one-off purchases under $5k, so not an option for production.
2) Marketplace: we prefer using CloudMarketplace if the vendor listing supports our standard terms; otherwise Procurement will run a direct vendor onboarding (takes ~3–4 weeks).
3) Redlines: Legal will own initial redlines but Procurement will coordinate T&Cs and signature. We will need a copy of your SOC 2 Type II and a brief summary of data residency controls for our security review.

Action items on our side:
- I'll confirm whether CloudMarketplace is mandatory (I have a note into ops team) by EOD Thu, Dec 3.
- Procurement will prepare initial vendor onboarding packet; expect a packet request with our vendor form and W-9 early next week.

One question: on Dedicated commits, do you offer quarterly invoicing via PO or only annual invoicing? If quarterly is available it would help our internal budget cadence.

Thanks,
Claire
Claire Nguyen
Senior Procurement Partner | Acme Health
claire@acmehealth.com

-----Original Message-----
On Mon, Nov 30, 2026 at 2:02 PM Camila wrote: > (see above)

From: Camila Reyes <camila@redwood.com>
To: Claire Nguyen <claire@acmehealth.com>
Cc: Rishi Malhotra <rishi@redwood.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean@redwood.com>, Acme Health Procurement <procure@acmehealth.com>
Date: Tue, 01 Dec 2026 10:25:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Next steps — hosting choice & procurement readiness for Acme Health

Claire — great, thanks. Short answers and next steps below.

1) Quarterly invoicing for Dedicated commits: yes, we support quarterly billing against a PO for committed capacity (we can do quarterly, semi-annual, or annual invoicing depending on your ERP requirements). I'll include that explicitly in the pricing sheet.

2) Marketplace: if CloudMarketplace is OK with your legal team we can list Acme as a marketplace customer and route invoices that way; otherwise direct vendor onboarding is typically straightforward and we can help by pre-filling Redwood's vendor profile.

3) Evidence: attached SOC 2 summary; Sean I copied IT/security so we can provide network control notes and KMS integration details if needed.

Request from our side to finalize the quote:
- Please confirm whether Acme requires the Dedicated environment (recommended for PHI) or if a private tenancy in Hosted would suffice for security controls. If Dedicated, what expected steady-state token throughput (tokens/sec or concurrent requests) should we size for?
- Any procurement-specific PO template or fields we should include (PO # format, billing address, SAP vendor ID)?

I'll aim to get both a Dedicated 12-mo commit quote with quarterly invoicing and a Hosted pilot T&Cs estimate to you by Fri, Dec 6. Attaching the draft MA and SOW for legal intake.

Best,
Camila Reyes
Account Executive | Redwood Inference
camila@redwood.com | (415) 555-0192

Attachments: SOC2_Type2_v3.pdf; Redwood_Draft_MA_Acme.docx; SOW-Acme-template.pdf

On Tue, 01 Dec 2026 at 09:10 AM Claire Nguyen <claire@acmehealth.com> wrote: > (see above)

To: Rishi Malhotra <rishi@redwood.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 01 Dec 2026 10:30:00 -0800
Subject: Internal: Acme procurement path and pricing ask

Rishi/Sean — quick internal note (keeping external recipients on previous message). Highlights: 
- Procurement confirmed PO path; marketplace may be optional but Claire will confirm by Dec 3.
- They need SOC2 and data residency notes (attached).
- Ask: build a Dedicated 12-mo commit quote with quarterly invoicing + Hosted pilot quote. Include optional KMS/HSM add-on pricing and migration SOW.
- Risk: if they insist on marketplace-only billing, we may need to coordinate with CloudMarketplace team for listing timelines (could add 3–4 weeks).

I'll draft the quote and send preliminary numbers by EOD Fri the 6th. Sean — can you prepare a short note on expected concurrency and token throughput assumptions for a 3x producer baseline?

Thanks,
Camila

source.subject: Project governance checklist & kickoff commitments (Brightline)
messages:
From: Alex Mercer <alex@brightline.com>
To: Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.com>, Monica Patel <monica@redwood.com>
Cc: Brightline Onboarding <onboarding@brightline.com>
Date: Sun, 29 Nov 2026 09:02:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Project governance checklist & kickoff commitments (Brightline)

Hi Karthik / Monica,

Thanks again for the demo last week — team was impressed with the latency/cost tradeoffs you showed. We want to lock down a short mutual governance checklist before kickoff so we can align internal stakeholders. Key asks from our side:

- Confirmation of roles (primary POC for ops + escalation lead)
- Initial milestone dates for API test window, pilot go-live, and SLA review
- Any required networking or security artifacts we need to open as part of prep

I attached a draft SOW the procurement team requested and a security worksheet — please confirm if these look like the right level of detail. Ideally we get a one-page governance checklist and a quick cadence for the first 90 days.

Can you propose owners and dates by EOD tomorrow? We have an exec sync Thurs and would like to show a plan.

Thanks,
Alex Mercer
Director, Product Integrations
Brightline Logistics

Attachments: Implementation_SOW_v1.pdf (pdf), network-security-checklist.xlsx (spreadsheet)

From: Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.com>
To: Alex Mercer <alex@brightline.com>
Cc: Monica Patel <monica@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean@redwood.com>
Date: Sun, 29 Nov 2026 11:18:00 -08:00
Subject: Re: Project governance checklist & kickoff commitments (Brightline)

Alex — appreciate the clear asks and the SOW. I pulled together a short governance checklist and an initial milestone calendar (see summary below). I also uploaded a shared note to the Brightline folder and linked it here: https://drive.redwood.com/teams/brightline/rdw-4521-governance-note

High-level owners (proposed):
- Technical Integration Lead (API / network): Monica Patel (monica@redwood.com)
- Customer Success / Launch PM: Marissa Cole (marissa@redwood.com)
- Escalation / SLA owner: Sean Gallagher (sean@redwood.com)
- Sales / commercial contact: Karthik Iyer (karthik@redwood.com)

Proposed milestone dates (initial):
- API test window opens (sandbox): 2026-12-06 to 2026-12-13
- Pilot go-live (limited traffic): 2027-01-10
- Production SLA review & baseline metrics: 2027-02-07

Network / security asks (from our side):
- Temporary CIDR allowlist for sandbox endpoints (we'll provide IPs)
- TLS cert validation and client IP expectations (see attached checklist)
- SOC2 evidence request window (we can ship logs + configuration by 2027-01-03)

Next steps I propose:
1) Monica to confirm feasibility on the sandbox window and IP allowlist by 12/01.
2) Marissa to own weekly 30-min standups starting 12/08 (invite to follow).
3) Sean to confirm SLA baseline metrics we'll use for the Feb review by 12/15.

Alex — if this aligns, I'll send a one-page governance checklist today and Circulate the kickoff invite.

Best,
Karthik Iyer
Senior Account Executive | Redwood Inference
karthik@redwood.com

On Sun, Nov 29, 2026 at 09:02 AM Alex Mercer <alex@brightline.com> wrote:
> Hi Karthik / Monica,
>
> Thanks again for the demo last week — team was impressed with the latency/cost tradeoffs you showed...

From: Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.com>
To: Monica Patel <monica@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 30 Nov 2026 08:04:00 -08:00
Subject: Internal: Brightline kickoff owners & quick commitments

Monica / Marissa / Sean — forwarding Alex's asks and the attached SOW. Copying the short plan I shared with them. Need your confirmation on owners and any red flags by EOB today so I can finalize the governance one-pager and send the kickoff invite.

Quick assignment (please reply inline):
- Monica: Confirm sandbox IP allowlist timing (we proposed 12/06) and whether you need anything from Brightline infra prior to that (VPN creds, network change windows).
- Marissa: Confirm you'll run weekly launch standups (30 min), starting 12/08. Also propose a draft agenda.
- Sean: Provide the SLA baseline metrics you plan to use for the production review (latency p50/p95, error budget, traffic thresholds) and whether you want any additional monitoring hooks instrumented during pilot.

Risk notes:
- If the sandbox window slips more than 3 business days we should shift the pilot go-live by a full week.
- Procurement has flagged a short DocuSign turnaround — Marissa, please coordinate with legal if we need any SOW edits.

Thanks — I know this is tight but we should be able to get the one-pager in front of their execs on Thursday.

— Karthik

From: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>
To: Alex Mercer <alex@brightline.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.com>
Cc: Monica Patel <monica@redwood.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 30 Nov 2026 11:15:00 -08:00
Subject: Re: Project governance checklist & kickoff commitments (Brightline)

Alex / team — thanks for sending the SOW. Quick confirmations from our side:

- I will run the weekly 30-min kickoff standups starting 12/08; draft agenda below.
- Monica confirmed sandbox IP allowlist for 12/06 (she'll send the CIDR block and any VPN notes by EOD).
- Sean attached the SLA baseline doc (summary lines below) — he'll route the monitoring hook request to infra.

Draft standup agenda (12/08):
1) 5m — Status check (what's blocked)
2) 10m — Sandbox progress & network prep
3) 10m — Integration items (API keys, auth, sample traffic)
4) 5m — Risks / next steps / action owners

SLA baseline summary (from Sean):
- Latency targets: p50 < 80ms, p95 < 300ms for pilot traffic
- Error budget: 0.5% over rolling 30 days
- Monitoring hooks: token-level tracing + route-level cost breakdown during pilot

Final note: I've uploaded the one-page governance checklist and a cleaned SOW to the Brightline folder and added a DocuSign request for the SOW (link below). If Alex confirms the dates, I'll mark the kickoff invite and send out the DocuSign.

DocuSign (SOW): https://docusign.com/rdw/4521
Drive: https://drive.redwood.com/teams/brightline/rdw-4521-governance-note

Thanks,
Marissa Cole
Customer Success Manager | Redwood Inference
marissa@redwood.com

Attachments: (see folder)

From: Alex Mercer <alex@brightline.com>
To: Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.com>, Monica Patel <monica@redwood.com>
Cc: Brightline Onboarding <onboarding@brightline.com>
Date: Sun, 29 Nov 2026 09:02:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Project governance checklist & kickoff commitments (Brightline)

Hi Karthik / Monica,

Thanks again for the demo last week — team was impressed with the latency/cost tradeoffs you showed. We want to lock down a short mutual governance checklist before kickoff so we can align internal stakeholders. Key asks from our side:

- Confirmation of roles (primary POC for ops + escalation lead)
- Initial milestone dates for API test window, pilot go-live, and SLA review
- Any required networking or security artifacts we need to open as part of prep

I attached a draft SOW the procurement team requested and a security worksheet — please confirm if these look like the right level of detail. Ideally we get a one-page governance checklist and a quick cadence for the first 90 days.

Can you propose owners and dates by EOD tomorrow? We have an exec sync Thurs and would like to show a plan.

Thanks,
Alex Mercer
Director, Product Integrations
Brightline Logistics

Attachments: Implementation_SOW_v1.pdf (pdf), network-security-checklist.xlsx (spreadsheet)

From: Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.com>
To: Alex Mercer <alex@brightline.com>
Cc: Monica Patel <monica@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean@redwood.com>
Date: Sun, 29 Nov 2026 11:18:00 -08:00
Subject: Re: Project governance checklist & kickoff commitments (Brightline)

Alex — appreciate the clear asks and the SOW. I pulled together a short governance checklist and an initial milestone calendar (see summary below). I also uploaded a shared note to the Brightline folder and linked it here: https://drive.redwood.com/teams/brightline/rdw-4521-governance-note

High-level owners (proposed):
- Technical Integration Lead (API / network): Monica Patel (monica@redwood.com)
- Customer Success / Launch PM: Marissa Cole (marissa@redwood.com)
- Escalation / SLA owner: Sean Gallagher (sean@redwood.com)
- Sales / commercial contact: Karthik Iyer (karthik@redwood.com)

Proposed milestone dates (initial):
- API test window opens (sandbox): 2026-12-06 to 2026-12-13
- Pilot go-live (limited traffic): 2027-01-10
- Production SLA review & baseline metrics: 2027-02-07

Network / security asks (from our side):
- Temporary CIDR allowlist for sandbox endpoints (we'll provide IPs)
- TLS cert validation and client IP expectations (see attached checklist)
- SOC2 evidence request window (we can ship logs + configuration by 2027-01-03)

Next steps I propose:
1) Monica to confirm feasibility on the sandbox window and IP allowlist by 12/01.
2) Marissa to own weekly 30-min standups starting 12/08 (invite to follow).
3) Sean to confirm SLA baseline metrics we'll use for the Feb review by 12/15.

Alex — if this aligns, I'll send a one-page governance checklist today and Circulate the kickoff invite.

Best,
Karthik Iyer
Senior Account Executive | Redwood Inference
karthik@redwood.com

On Sun, Nov 29, 2026 at 09:02 AM Alex Mercer <alex@brightline.com> wrote:
> Hi Karthik / Monica,
>
> Thanks again for the demo last week — team was impressed with the latency/cost tradeoffs you showed...


From: Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.com>
To: Monica Patel <monica@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 30 Nov 2026 08:04:00 -08:00
Subject: Internal: Brightline kickoff owners & quick commitments

Monica / Marissa / Sean — forwarding Alex's asks and the attached SOW. Copying the short plan I shared with them. Need your confirmation on owners and any red flags by EOB today so I can finalize the governance one-pager and send the kickoff invite.

Quick assignment (please reply inline):
- Monica: Confirm sandbox IP allowlist timing (we proposed 12/06) and whether you need anything from Brightline infra prior to that (VPN creds, network change windows).
- Marissa: Confirm you'll run weekly launch standups (30 min), starting 12/08. Also propose a draft agenda.
- Sean: Provide the SLA baseline metrics you plan to use for the production review (latency p50/p95, error budget, traffic thresholds) and whether you want any additional monitoring hooks instrumented during pilot.

Risk notes:
- If the sandbox window slips more than 3 business days we should shift the pilot go-live by a full week.
- Procurement has flagged a short DocuSign turnaround — Marissa, please coordinate with legal if we need any SOW edits.

Thanks — I know this is tight but we should be able to get the one-pager in front of their execs on Thursday.

— Karthik

From: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>
To: Alex Mercer <alex@brightline.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.com>
Cc: Monica Patel <monica@redwood.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 30 Nov 2026 11:15:00 -08:00
Subject: Re: Project governance checklist & kickoff commitments (Brightline)

Alex / team — thanks for sending the SOW. Quick confirmations from our side:

- I will run the weekly 30-min kickoff standups starting 12/08; draft agenda below.
- Monica confirmed sandbox IP allowlist for 12/06 (she'll send the CIDR block and any VPN notes by EOD).
- Sean attached the SLA baseline doc (summary lines below) — he'll route the monitoring hook request to infra.

Draft standup agenda (12/08):
1) 5m — Status check (what's blocked)
2) 10m — Sandbox progress & network prep
3) 10m — Integration items (API keys, auth, sample traffic)
4) 5m — Risks / next steps / action owners

SLA baseline summary (from Sean):
- Latency targets: p50 < 80ms, p95 < 300ms for pilot traffic
- Error budget: 0.5% over rolling 30 days
- Monitoring hooks: token-level tracing + route-level cost breakdown during pilot

Final note: I've uploaded the one-page governance checklist and a cleaned SOW to the Brightline folder and added a DocuSign request for the SOW (link below). If Alex confirms the dates, I'll mark the kickoff invite and send out the DocuSign.

DocuSign (SOW): https://docusign.com/rdw/4521
Drive: https://drive.redwood.com/teams/brightline/rdw-4521-governance-note

Thanks,
Marissa Cole
Customer Success Manager | Redwood Inference
marissa@redwood.com

Attachments: (see folder)

source.subject: Execution landing queue: MAP checklist, signature timing, and e-sign schedule
messages:
From: Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwood.ai>
To: Claire Benson <claire.benson@orionretail.com>
Cc: Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwood.ai>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwood.ai>
Date: Wed, 25 Nov 2026 09:04:00 -0800
Subject: Execution landing queue: proposed MAP items + target signature week

Hi Claire —

Thanks again for the time yesterday. Per our mutual action plan discussion, I put together a concise checklist so we can lock the delivery window and the start-of-service date. Attached: Redwood_MAP_Orion_v3.xlsx and SOW_Orion_Redwood_v1.3.pdf. Quick summary below and asks.

Key milestones I propose:
- Legal review complete: target by Tue, Nov 30
- Procurement countersign/PO: target by Thu, Dec 2
- eSign window (DocuSign): Fri, Dec 3 - Mon, Dec 6
- Start date (preferred): Mon, Dec 13

Immediate asks for your team:
1) Confirm signers and whether procurement will issue a PO or you prefer DocuSign countersign only.
2) Confirm SOC2 evidence holder — we attached a one-page SOC2_summary; let us know if your legal needs the full report.
3) Any blackout dates for your SSO/KMS onboarding the week of Dec 6-10.

If the week of Dec 13 is aggressive for your ops team, we can shift to Dec 20. Lauren and Sean are copied to coordinate parallel tracks (legal, security docs, ops prep).

Thanks — sending the MAP as an editable workbook so we can check off items live.

Best,
Aditya Rao
Director, Strategic Accounts
Redwood Inference
attachments: Redwood_MAP_Orion_v3.xlsx (workbook), SOW_Orion_Redwood_v1.3.pdf (pdf)

From: Claire Benson <claire.benson@orionretail.com>
To: Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwood.ai>
Cc: Kyle Morgan <kyle.morgan@orionretail.com>, procurement@orionretail.com
Date: Thu, 26 Nov 2026 11:12:00 -0500
Subject: Re: Execution landing queue: proposed MAP items + target signature week

Aditya —

Appreciate the clear steps and the MAP file. A few notes from our side after initial legal and procurement review:

- Procurement prefers a PO to a pure eSign route; procurement@orionretail.com is looped. They asked for a 2% net-30 vs. eSign-only credit.
- Legal wants the full SOC2 Type II report before countersign; SOC2_summary is helpful but they flagged 3 controls that require more context (KMS policies, data retention, incident notification SLA). Can you share the full report or a redacted bundle?
- Ops is fine with the Dec 13 start if we can confirm SSO integration windows by Dec 6. Otherwise we need to move to Dec 20.

We can target PO issuance on Dec 2 if the SOC2 docs are delivered by Nov 30 and legal can clear by then.

On signatures: Claire Benson (SVP, Product) will sign; procurement needs the PO to attach.

Claire
Claire Benson
SVP, Product
Orion Retail

On Wed, Nov 25, 2026 at 09:04 AM Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwood.ai> wrote:
> Hi Claire —
> 
> Thanks again for the time yesterday. Per our mutual action plan discussion...

From: Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwood.ai>
To: Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwood.ai>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwood.ai>
Cc: Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.ai>
Date: Fri, 27 Nov 2026 08:45:00 -0800
Subject: RE: Execution landing queue: proposed MAP items + target signature week (internal)

Team —

Notes/action items for internal track: we need to get SOC2 deliverables and a short change-log for the SOW finalized. Tasks and owners below:

- SOC2 full bundle (redacted as needed): Monica to pull and package; target Monday Nov 30 EOD.
- SOW change-log (we agreed to tweak the onboarding section + SLA ramp week): Aditya to update and circulate v1.3 -> v1.4 by Nov 29 noon.
- Pricing fallback note: if procurement insists on PO + 2% net-30, finance wants visibility before countersign. Sean to prep margin impact note.
- DocuSign envelope creation: Lauren will prepare DocuSign template and set a 7-day expiration window once envelope is sent.

We should also create a simple internal readiness checklist to share with Claire that tracks SSO/KMS owners, network allowlist, and a contact for go-live day. I drafted that into the MAP tab "Execution-ready checklist" — please review.

If we can deliver SOC2 bundle by Nov 30 and SOW v1.4 by Nov 29 noon, this keeps the Dec 2 PO target intact and the Dec 13 start feasible. Otherwise plan for Dec 20.

-LB
Lauren Bishop
Sr. Manager, Account Success (internal ops)

On Thu, Nov 26, 2026 at 11:12 AM Claire Benson <claire.benson@orionretail.com> wrote:
> Aditya —
> 
> Appreciate the clear steps and the MAP file. A few notes from our side...

From: Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwood.ai>
To: Claire Benson <claire.benson@orionretail.com>, Kyle Morgan <kyle.morgan@orionretail.com>, procurement@orionretail.com
Cc: Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwood.ai>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwood.ai>, Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.ai>
Date: Mon, 29 Nov 2026 16:21:00 -0800
Subject: Finalized landing queue + DocuSign envelope (links + next steps)

Claire / Kyle / Procurement team —

Per our internal checklist and your notes, we completed the updates and attached the SOC2 bundle (redacted) and SOW v1.4. Two quick updates to the plan:

1) SOC2 bundle uploaded to the Drive link below and also attached (SOC2_summary.pdf + redacted bundle). Please confirm receipt so legal can re-review.
2) DocuSign envelope has been created and scheduled: https://docusign.com/1234-ORION-ESIGN (envelope expires Dec 10). If procurement prefers PO + countersign, please confirm and let us know the PO number once available; we'll attach it to the envelope and pause the eSign flow until PO is in place.

Updated milestone cadence (final):
- SOC2 confirmation to legal: Nov 30
- SOW v1.4 final: Nov 29 (attached)
- Procurement PO issuance: target Dec 2 (or confirm eSign by Dec 3)
- DocuSign execution window: Dec 3 - Dec 10 (envelope active)
- Target operational start: Dec 13 (contingent on signed SOW + completed SSO/KMS checks)

Actions for your team: please reply with either (A) PO number by Dec 2, or (B) explicit approval to proceed with DocuSign-only countersign. If we don't hear back by EOD Dec 2 we'll assume you want the DocuSign path and will proceed to request signature from Claire on Dec 3.

Thanks — happy to hop on a 20-minute call if that helps unblock procurement/legal.

Best,
Aditya Rao
Director, Strategic Accounts
Redwood Inference
attachments: SOW_Orion_Redwood_v1.4.pdf (pdf), SOC2_summary.pdf (pdf), redacted_SOC2_bundle.zip (archive)

Date: Thu, 26 Nov 2026 11:12:00 -0500
Subject: Re: Execution landing queue: proposed MAP items + target signature week

Aditya —

Appreciate the clear steps and the MAP file. A few notes from our side after initial legal and procurement review:

- Procurement prefers a PO to a pure eSign route; procurement@orionretail.com is looped. They asked for a 2% net-30 vs. eSign-only credit.
- Legal wants the full SOC2 Type II report before countersign; SOC2_summary is helpful but they flagged 3 controls that require more context (KMS policies, data retention, incident notification SLA). Can you share the full report or a redacted bundle?
- Ops is fine with the Dec 13 start if we can confirm SSO integration windows by Dec 6. Otherwise we need to move to Dec 20.

We can target PO issuance on Dec 2 if the SOC2 docs are delivered by Nov 30 and legal can clear by then.

On signatures: Claire Benson (SVP, Product) will sign; procurement needs the PO to attach.

Claire
Claire Benson
SVP, Product
Orion Retail

Cc: Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.ai>
Date: Fri, 27 Nov 2026 08:45:00 -0800
Subject: RE: Execution landing queue: proposed MAP items + target signature week (internal)

Team —

Notes/action items for internal track: we need to get SOC2 deliverables and a short change-log for the SOW finalized. Tasks and owners below:

- SOC2 full bundle (redacted as needed): Monica to pull and package; target Monday Nov 30 EOD.
- SOW change-log (we agreed to tweak the onboarding section + SLA ramp week): Aditya to update and circulate v1.3 -> v1.4 by Nov 29 noon.
- Pricing fallback note: if procurement insists on PO + 2% net-30, finance wants visibility before countersign. Sean to prep margin impact note.
- DocuSign envelope creation: Lauren will prepare DocuSign template and set a 7-day expiration window once envelope is sent.

We should also create a simple internal readiness checklist to share with Claire that tracks SSO/KMS owners, network allowlist, and a contact for go-live day. I drafted that into the MAP tab \"Execution-ready checklist\" — please review.

If we can deliver SOC2 bundle by Nov 30 and SOW v1.4 by Nov 29 noon, this keeps the Dec 2 PO target intact and the Dec 13 start feasible. Otherwise plan for Dec 20.

-LB
Lauren Bishop
Sr. Manager, Account Success (internal ops)

Per our internal checklist and your notes, we completed the updates and attached the SOC2 bundle (redacted) and SOW v1.4. Two quick updates to the plan:

1) SOC2 bundle uploaded to the Drive link below and also attached (SOC2_summary.pdf + redacted bundle). Please confirm receipt so legal can re-review.
2) DocuSign envelope has been created and scheduled: https://docusign.com/1234-ORION-ESIGN (envelope expires Dec 10). If procurement prefers PO + countersign, please confirm and let us know the PO number once available; we'll attach it to the envelope and pause the eSign flow until PO is in place.

- SOC2 confirmation to legal: Nov 30
- SOW v1.4 final: Nov 29 (attached)
- Procurement PO issuance: target Dec 2 (or confirm eSign by Dec 3)
- DocuSign execution window: Dec 3 - Dec 10 (envelope active)
- Target operational start: Dec 13 (contingent on signed SOW + completed SSO/KMS checks)

Actions for your team: please reply with either (A) PO number by Dec 2, or (B) explicit approval to proceed with DocuSign-only countersign. If we don't hear back by EOD Dec 2 we'll assume you want the DocuSign path and will proceed to request signature from Claire on Dec 3.

Thanks — happy to hop on a 20-minute call if that helps unblock procurement/legal.

source.subject: Cornerstone: Missing Tax ID on Resale Cert — need EIN confirmation and ship-to mapping
messages:
From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>
To: Alicia Moore <accounts@cornerstonehw.com>
Cc: Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Fri, 20 Nov 2026 09:12:00 -0800
Subject: Resale certificate — additional info needed to clear invoice #INV-1192

Hi Alicia,

Thanks for sending over the resale certificate for Cornerstone. I took a look with Finance and we noticed the document is missing the taxpayer identification (EIN) and the legal business name on page 1 doesn't exactly match the ship-to entity on the invoice. Because state acceptance varies, we need a copy of the certificate that includes BOTH: the EIN and an explicit mapping that shows which EIN/legal entity covers the specific ship-to address(es) you use for orders with Redwood.

Can you:
- Attach a corrected resale certificate that includes the EIN (or a state-specific blanket that lists EIN), or
- Provide a short confirmation email that the attached certificate covers invoices shipped to 123 Industrial Way, Springfield, IL (our ship-to on INV-1192).

If you prefer, you can send a stamped copy or PDF and we can upload it to the portal. Once we have that we can clear the invoice hold and release payment processing.

Thanks — I know this is a bit of paperwork. Happy to jump on a quick call if it helps.

Best,
Ben Carter
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwoodinference.com
Attachment: INV-1192-invoice.pdf (invoice stub)
From: Alicia Moore <accounts@cornerstonehw.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Eric Hanley <eric.hanley@cornerstonehw.com>
Date: Mon, 23 Nov 2026 11:48:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Resale certificate — additional info needed to clear invoice #INV-1192

Hi Ben —

Thanks for flagging. Apologies — our usual template omits the EIN on renewals unless specifically requested. Attached is the most recent resale cert we have on file; I also exported a short EIN confirmation from our accounting system. Please note the legal name on the cert is "Cornerstone Hardware LLC" while some of our ship-to addresses are under the DBA "Cornerstone Supply" — that might be the mismatch you saw.

We can either:
1) Send a consolidated, signed certificate that lists both "Cornerstone Hardware LLC (DBA Cornerstone Supply)" and the EIN, or
2) Provide a line-item mapping that ties each ship-to address to the primary legal entity with the EIN.

Which option would speed the release? If you need a signed/stamped PDF I can go get it tomorrow.

Best,
Alicia Moore
Accounts Payable, Cornerstone Hardware LLC
accounts@cornerstonehw.com
Attachments: Cornerstone-Resale-Cert-2026-11-23.pdf, Cornerstone-EIN-Confirmation-2026-11-23.txt
From: Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa.ortiz@redwoodinference.com>
To: Alicia Moore <accounts@cornerstonehw.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, 24 Nov 2026 14:05:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Resale certificate — additional info needed to clear invoice #INV-1192

Hi Alicia —

Thanks for the quick reply and the attachments. To keep the invoice moving from our side, Finance prefers the consolidated approach (option 1) where the certificate explicitly lists the EIN and any DBAs covered. That reduces back-and-forth during state validation. If you can provide a single PDF with:

- Full legal name + DBA in parentheses,
- EIN clearly printed, and
- A short statement (one sentence) that the certificate applies to all shipments to the addresses listed on invoices with Cornerstone order numbers —

...then we can validate internally and mark INV-1192 as cleared.

If it helps, I attached a quick sample text we can copy into the cert before you sign:

"This certificate applies to all sales shipped to the ship-to addresses provided by Cornerstone Hardware LLC (DBA Cornerstone Supply). EIN: XX-XXXXXXX."

Once you send the signed PDF we will upload it to our tax documentation portal and release payment. If you need Redwood's vendor portal link for document upload, let me know and I will send it.

Thanks —
Vanessa Ortiz
Senior Manager, Revenue Operations
vanessa.ortiz@redwoodinference.com
Attachment: sample-evidence-text.txt
From: Alicia Moore <accounts@cornerstonehw.com>
To: Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa.ortiz@redwoodinference.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Eric Hanley <eric.hanley@cornerstonehw.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Wed, 25 Nov 2026 15:03:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Resale certificate — additional info needed to clear invoice #INV-1192

Hi Vanessa / Ben —

Attached is the signed consolidated certificate. We updated the legal name line to include the DBA and added the EIN as requested. Per your sample sentence, we included: "This certificate applies to all sales shipped to the ship-to addresses provided by Cornerstone Hardware LLC (DBA Cornerstone Supply). EIN: 12-3456789."

We also included a one-line mapping at the end that ties the Springfield IL ship-to (123 Industrial Way) to the primary legal entity. Please confirm when the invoice is released.

Appreciate the quick turnaround — let us know if you need anything else for compliance.

Regards,
Alicia Moore
Accounts Payable, Cornerstone Hardware LLC
accounts@cornerstonehw.com
Attachments: Cornerstone-Resale-Cert-2026-11-24.pdf, Cornerstone-EIN-Confirmation-2026-11-24.txt

--- Forwarded message ---
From: Alicia Moore <accounts@cornerstonehw.com>
To: ben.carter@redwoodinference.com
Date: Wed, 25 Nov 2026 15:02:00 -0800
Subject: Signed consolidated cert

(Attached signed PDF)

source.subject: Pilot budget alignment — unit economics & short-term credit ask
messages:
From: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.inference>
To: Emily Zhang <emily@horizonhealth.ai>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.inference>
Date: Wed, 18 Nov 2026 15:02:00 -0800
Subject: Pilot budget alignment — unit economics & short-term credit ask

Hi Emily — great to connect earlier. Quick recap from our end and a concrete ask so your procurement and engineering teams can align: 

Summary of what we heard on the call:
- Pilot scope: ingest + realtime summarization for patient intake (avg request 1.8k tokens, 95th pct 5k).
- Expected concurrency: 6–12 RPS at peak daytime hours.
- Desired pilot length: 6 weeks, with a strong chance to expand to production if latency and accuracy meet SLAs.

Cost/volume hypothesis (ballpark):
- If you run ~10k requests/day at an average 1.8k tokens/request -> ~18M tokens/day -> ~126M tokens/week.
- Redwood target unit econ for this workload (inferred model mix): $32 / 1M tokens (mixed quantized model + caching).
- That maps to ~$4,032/week (~$16k/month) nominal inference cost at 126M tokens/week.

Ask and recommendation:
1) For a low-friction pilot, would Horizon accept a $15k credit top-up to cover the first 6 weeks of heavy experimentation? That would give you ~468M tokens of runway at the $32/1M assumption.
2) If your procurement needs a different framing, we can convert the ask to a 90-day trial with spend cap and usage reporting.

Next steps I propose:
- You confirm if the token-volume assumptions above look reasonable to your eng team. If off, please send rough daily request/avg token numbers.
- If procurement wants a formal SOW/credit letter, I’ll pull Marissa in to draft it.

Attached: pilot_scope.pdf (short scope), token_projection.xlsx (workload sensitivity).

Thanks — happy to hop on a quick 20-min follow-up tomorrow if helpful.

—
Ben Carter
Enterprise AE | Redwood Inference
ben@redwood.inference | +1 (415) 555-0191
From: Emily Zhang <emily@horizonhealth.ai>
To: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.inference>
Date: Thu, 19 Nov 2026 09:10:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Pilot budget alignment — unit economics & short-term credit ask

Thanks Ben — helpful recap. A couple clarifications from our side:
- The 10k requests/day was an overestimate on peak. Our current expected test volume is closer to 3–5k/day (mainly office hours), but we will spike during clinician labeling sessions.
- Average tokens/request is ~1.6k (we do additional prompt engineering to keep context tight).

So revised estimate: ~8M–20M tokens/day during peaks. Still want to proceed with the pilot but procurement will need a clear cap and an expiry on any credit offer.

Procurement asks:
- Can credits be scoped to pilot only and expire after 60 days of activation?
- Can you provide an estimated $/1M token for the model configuration you recommended (including quantization and caching) so we can present numbers to finance?

If you can send a short cost note and the credit terms, I’ll route to procurement. Thanks — Emily

Emily Zhang
Sr. Product Manager, Clinical AI | Horizon Health
emily@horizonhealth.ai | (650) 555-2234
From: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.inference>
To: Emily Zhang <emily@horizonhealth.ai>
Cc: Sean Gallagher <sean@redwood.inference>, Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.inference>
Date: Mon, 22 Nov 2026 11:34:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Pilot budget alignment — unit economics & short-term credit ask

Emily — thanks for the clarifications, that helps a lot. Short cost note for procurement (feel free to copy into their ticket):

Estimated unit economics (for your workload, conservatively):
- Typical model mix: quantized Llama-3-small variant for embedding/context + Redwood-optimized local reranker.
- Effective price we expect to surface for this pilot: $38 / 1M tokens (this includes conservative allowance for spikes and no long-term commit).

Revised run-rate scenarios (at $38 / 1M):
- 3k req/day * 1.6k tokens = ~4.8M tokens/day -> ~144M tokens/month -> ~$5,472/month.
- 5k req/day * 1.6k tokens = ~8M tokens/day -> ~240M tokens/month -> ~$9,120/month.
- Peak-spike buffer (short experiments): recommend 300k–500k token/day reserve.

Credit ask: Redwood can offer a one-time pilot credit of $12,000 (applies to inference spend only), expiring 60 days after activation. At $38/1M that maps to ~315M tokens of runway and should cover 4–6 weeks of normal testing for the volumes you described.

Marissa, Sean — can you approve a $12k pilot credit for Horizon Health (deal HZ-1172)? Procurement wants the expiry and spend-only-to-inference clause included in the credit letter. I’ve attached the draft SOW/credit note and a token projection sheet.

Thanks — Ben

On Thu, 19 Nov 2026 at 09:10, Emily Zhang <emily@horizonhealth.ai> wrote:
> Thanks Ben — helpful recap. A couple clarifications from our side: ...

—
Ben Carter
Enterprise AE | Redwood Inference
ben@redwood.inference
From: Sean Gallagher <sean@redwood.inference>
To: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.inference>, Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.inference>
Date: Tue, 23 Nov 2026 09:14:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Pilot budget alignment — unit economics & short-term credit ask

Ben / Marissa — approved.

- Approval: one-time pilot credit of $12,000 to Horizon Health (deal HZ-1172).
- Terms: credit applies to inference usage only, expires 60 days from date of activation, no rollover, Redwood provides weekly usage report to Horizon.
- Ops: Ben, please set a hard spend cap in the tenant to prevent overages; Marissa, please draft the credit letter and include the expiry/spend-scope language.

Sean Gallagher
VP, Sales Operations | Redwood Inference
sean@redwood.inference

On Mon, 22 Nov 2026 at 11:34, Ben Carter <ben@redwood.inference> wrote:
> Emily — thanks for the clarifications, that helps a lot. Short cost note for procurement (feel free to copy into their ticket):...

- If you run ~10k requests/day at an average 1.8k tokens/request -> ~18M tokens/day -> ~126M tokens/week.
- Redwood target unit econ for this workload (inferred model mix): $32 / 1M tokens (mixed quantized model + caching).
- That maps to ~$4,032/week (~$16k/month) nominal inference cost at 126M tokens/week.

Ask and recommendation:
1) For a low-friction pilot, would Horizon accept a $15k credit top-up to cover the first 6 weeks of heavy experimentation? That would give you ~468M tokens of runway at the $32/1M assumption.
2) If your procurement needs a different framing, we can convert the ask to a 90-day trial with spend cap and usage reporting.

Next steps I propose:
- You confirm if the token-volume assumptions above look reasonable to your eng team. If off, please send rough daily request/avg token numbers.
- If procurement wants a formal SOW/credit letter, I’ll pull Marissa in to draft it.

Attached: pilot_scope.pdf (short scope), token_projection.xlsx (workload sensitivity).

Thanks — happy to hop on a quick 20-min follow-up tomorrow if helpful.

—
Ben Carter
Enterprise AE | Redwood Inference
ben@redwood.inference | +1 (415) 555-0191


From: Emily Zhang <emily@horizonhealth.ai>
To: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.inference>
Date: Thu, 19 Nov 2026 09:10:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Pilot budget alignment — unit economics & short-term credit ask

Thanks Ben — helpful recap. A couple clarifications from our side:
- The 10k requests/day was an overestimate on peak. Our current expected test volume is closer to 3–5k/day (mainly office hours), but we will spike during clinician labeling sessions.
- Average tokens/request is ~1.6k (we do additional prompt engineering to keep context tight).

So revised estimate: ~8M–20M tokens/day during peaks. Still want to proceed with the pilot but procurement will need a clear cap and an expiry on any credit offer.

Procurement asks:
- Can credits be scoped to pilot only and expire after 60 days of activation?
- Can you provide an estimated $/1M token for the model configuration you recommended (including quantization and caching) so we can present numbers to finance?

If you can send a short cost note and the credit terms, I’ll route to procurement. Thanks — Emily

Emily Zhang
Sr. Product Manager, Clinical AI | Horizon Health
emily@horizonhealth.ai | (650) 555-2234


From: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.inference>
To: Emily Zhang <emily@horizonhealth.ai>
Cc: Sean Gallagher <sean@redwood.inference>, Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.inference>
Date: Mon, 22 Nov 2026 11:34:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Pilot budget alignment — unit economics & short-term credit ask

Emily — thanks for the clarifications, that helps a lot. Short cost note for procurement (feel free to copy into their ticket):

Estimated unit economics (for your workload, conservatively):
- Typical model mix: quantized Llama-3-small variant for embedding/context + Redwood-optimized local reranker.
- Effective price we expect to surface for this pilot: $38 / 1M tokens (this includes conservative allowance for spikes and no long-term commit).

Revised run-rate scenarios (at $38 / 1M):
- 3k req/day * 1.6k tokens = ~4.8M tokens/day -> ~144M tokens/month -> ~$5,472/month.
- 5k req/day * 1.6k tokens = ~8M tokens/day -> ~240M tokens/month -> ~$9,120/month.
- Peak-spike buffer (short experiments): recommend 300k–500k token/day reserve.

Credit ask: Redwood can offer a one-time pilot credit of $12,000 (applies to inference spend only), expiring 60 days after activation. At $38/1M that maps to ~315M tokens of runway and should cover 4–6 weeks of normal testing for the volumes you described.

Marissa, Sean — can you approve a $12k pilot credit for Horizon Health (deal HZ-1172)? Procurement wants the expiry and spend-only-to-inference clause included in the credit letter. I’ve attached the draft SOW/credit note and a token projection sheet.

Thanks — Ben

On Thu, 19 Nov 2026 at 09:10, Emily Zhang <emily@horizonhealth.ai> wrote:
> Thanks Ben — helpful recap. A couple clarifications from our side: ...

—
Ben Carter
Enterprise AE | Redwood Inference
ben@redwood.inference


From: Sean Gallagher <sean@redwood.inference>
To: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.inference>, Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.inference>
Date: Tue, 23 Nov 2026 09:14:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Pilot budget alignment — unit economics & short-term credit ask

Ben / Marissa — approved.

- Approval: one-time pilot credit of $12,000 to Horizon Health (deal HZ-1172).
- Terms: credit applies to inference usage only, expires 60 days from date of activation, no rollover, Redwood provides weekly usage report to Horizon.
- Ops: Ben, please set a hard spend cap in the tenant to prevent overages; Marissa, please draft the credit letter and include the expiry/spend-scope language.

Sean Gallagher
VP, Sales Operations | Redwood Inference
sean@redwood.inference

messages:
From: Elena Morales <elena.morales@vendorcorp.com>
To: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.com>
Cc: procurement@vendorcorp.com
Date: Fri, 20 Nov 2026 09:12:00 -0800
Subject: Documents for onboarding / request for PO steps

Hi Kimberly,

Thanks again for the walkthrough this morning. Per our conversation, I attached the current COI and W-9 so your procurement team can begin the vendor setup. A couple notes up front:

- Our insurer issued the COI with our standard limits; we can request the additional-insured endorsement if Redwood requires the carrier to sign an endorsement form rather than add us via a blanket endorsement.
- We maintain a short subvendor list (cloud infra & fulfillment partners) — included as a CSV.
- The MSA is in your DocuSign queue (link below) — we can finalize after the PO is queued.

Attachments: VendorCorp_COI.pdf (pdf), W9_VendorCorp.pdf (pdf), Vendor_Subvendors.csv (csv)

Can you confirm the exact endorsement wording Redwood needs and whether procurement requires a separate endorsement document or if the COI with a note is sufficient? Once we have that, we’ll request the endorsement from our broker.

Best,
Elena Morales
Head of Procurement Operations
VendorCorp Inc.
elena.morales@vendorcorp.com
+1 (415) 555-8123

DocuSign: https://app.docusign.com/VC-MSA-2026
From: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.com>
To: Elena Morales <elena.morales@vendorcorp.com>
Cc: Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 20 Nov 2026 12:05:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Documents for onboarding / request for PO steps

Elena — thanks for sending these over and for the quick turnaround. I confirmed receipt of the COI, W-9, and the subvendor list. A few procurement items to unblock activation and PO issuance:

1) Additional insured endorsement: Redwood needs an endorsement that names “Redwood Inference, its officers, directors, employees and agents” as additional insured on a primary and non-contributory basis. We typically accept either:
   - an Acord 25 with an attached endorsement PDF that includes the exact language above, or
   - a carrier-signed endorsement letter (preferred for large carriers).

2) Waiver of subrogation: If possible, a waiver in favor of Redwood is helpful for tech integrations — mark on the endorsement if your broker can provide it.

3) PO prerequisites: Procurement will open a conditional PO once we have: signed COI + endorsement acceptance, W-9, and DocuSign MSA in progress. Final PO issuance requires invoice routing info and a Redwood contact for billing (name + email).

4) Data handling note: Please confirm whether any subvendors will process customer data; if yes, we’ll need their SOC attestation or equivalent.

I’m looping Sean from Finance so he can confirm invoice routing and the billing contact to populate the PO. If procurement needs anything else I’ll route to Dev for contract intake.

Thanks — look forward to the endorsement document.

— Kimberly
Kimberly Park
Procurement Manager, Redwood Inference
kimberly.park@redwood.com
From: Elena Morales <elena.morales@vendorcorp.com>
To: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.com>
Cc: procurement@vendorcorp.com
Date: Sat, 21 Nov 2026 08:22:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Documents for onboarding / request for PO steps

Hi Kimberly —

Thanks for the clear checklist. I spoke with our broker and they can issue the endorsement with the language you requested. I attached the broker’s draft endorsement request form for Redwood to review — the carrier typically needs that filled in before issuing. We also included an updated COI with the policy effective dates and our W-9.

Quick answers to your questions:
- Waiver of subrogation: broker indicates our carrier will consider it; we requested it in the draft endorsement.
- Subvendor data processing: Two subvendors (CloudSpan and Fulfillio) may handle metadata for operations; I attached a CSV with their names and a short column indicating processing scope. We can supply SOC attestation PDF if Redwood requires it — not included in this packet.

Attachments: VendorCorp_Endorsement_Request.docx (docx), VendorCorp_COI.pdf (pdf), W9_VendorCorp.pdf (pdf), Vendor_Subvendors.csv (csv)

I’ll start the carrier request as soon as we get confirmation from your procurement team on the endorsement language and whether Redwood needs the endorsement to explicitly reference the PO number when issued.

Best regards,
Elena
From: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.com>
To: Dev Patel <dev.patel@redwood.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwood.com>
Date: Sun, 22 Nov 2026 09:30:00 -0800
Subject: Fwd: Documents for onboarding / request for PO steps

Dev / Sean — forwarding VendorCorp’s packet. Vendor can provide the endorsement if we confirm the standard additional-insured wording below. Can Procurement open a conditional PO (PROC-8721) and mark as conditional until we receive the carrier-signed endorsement and a final invoice contact? Once we have the endorsement we can flip to active and issue the first invoice.

Action items:
- Dev: please confirm whether integration with CloudSpan/Fulfillio requires any additional security attestation before activation (I think not, but confirm).
- Sean: can you confirm billing contact and whether Finance requires vendor bank details in the PO? If Finance needs bank details up front we’ll request a completed vendor banking form from VendorCorp.
- Procurement: please open ticket PROC-8721 and add the attachments from VendorCorp; tag me for the endorsement review.

Quoted message below.

> Hi Kimberly —
>
> Thanks for the clear checklist. I spoke with our broker and they can issue the endorsement with the language you requested. I attached the broker’s draft endorsement request form for Redwood to review — the carrier typically needs that filled in before issuing. We also included an updated COI with the policy effective dates and our W-9.
>
> Quick answers to your questions:
> - Waiver of subrogation: broker indicates our carrier will consider it; we requested it in the draft endorsement.
> - Subvendor data processing: Two subvendors (CloudSpan and Fulfillio) may handle metadata for operations; I attached a CSV with their names and a short column indicating processing scope. We can supply SOC attestation PDF if Redwood requires it — not included in this packet.
>
> Attachments: VendorCorp_Endorsement_Request.docx (docx), VendorCorp_COI.pdf (pdf), W9_VendorCorp.pdf (pdf), Vendor_Subvendors.csv (csv)
>
> I’ll start the carrier request as soon as we get confirmation from your procurement team on the endorsement language and whether Redwood needs the endorsement to explicitly reference the PO number when issued.
>
> Best regards,
> Elena

--

To: Dev Patel <dev.patel@redwood.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwood.com>
Date: Sun, 22 Nov 2026 09:30:00 -0800
Subject: Fwd: Documents for onboarding / request for PO steps

Dev / Sean — forwarding VendorCorp’s packet. Vendor can provide the endorsement if we confirm the standard additional-insured wording below. Can Procurement open a conditional PO (PROC-8721) and mark as conditional until we receive the carrier-signed endorsement and a final invoice contact? Once we have the endorsement we can flip to active and issue the first invoice.

Action items:
- Dev: please confirm whether integration with CloudSpan/Fulfillio requires any additional security attestation before activation (I think not, but confirm).
- Sean: can you confirm billing contact and whether Finance requires vendor bank details in the PO? If Finance needs bank details up front we’ll request a completed vendor banking form from VendorCorp.
- Procurement: please open ticket PROC-8721 and add the attachments from VendorCorp; tag me for the endorsement review.

Quoted message below.

On Sat, Nov 21, 2026 at 08:22 AM Elena Morales <elena.morales@vendorcorp.com> wrote:
> Hi Kimberly —
>
> Thanks for the clear checklist. I spoke with our broker and they can issue the endorsement with the language you requested. I attached the broker’s draft endorsement request form for Redwood to review — the carrier typically needs that filled in before issuing. We also included an updated COI with the policy effective dates and our W-9.
>
> Quick answers to your questions:
> - Waiver of subrogation: broker indicates our carrier will consider it; we requested it in the draft endorsement.
> - Subvendor data processing: Two subvendors (CloudSpan and Fulfillio) may handle metadata for operations; I attached a CSV with their names and a short column indicating processing scope. We can supply SOC attestation PDF if Redwood requires it — not included in this packet.
>
> Attachments: VendorCorp_Endorsement_Request.docx (docx), VendorCorp_COI.pdf (pdf), W9_VendorCorp.pdf (pdf), Vendor_Subvendors.csv (csv)
>
> I’ll start the carrier request as soon as we get confirmation from your procurement team on the endorsement language and whether Redwood needs the endorsement to explicitly reference the PO number when issued.
>
> Best regards,
> Elena

--
Kimberly Park

source.subject: SIG intake — consolidated access inventory & SAML/SCIM samples
source.body:
From: Tom Leary <tom.leary@gridsat.com>
To: Irene Choi <irene.choi@redwood.ai>
Date: Wed, 18 Nov 2026 09:12:00 -0800
Subject: SIG intake — SSO/SCIM/RBAC artifacts request

Hi Irene,

Thanks for taking this on. GridSat is running through the SIG intake for our next internal audit and we need a consolidated package of artifacts that prove account provisioning, role mappings, and at-rest SAML samples for user assertions. Specifically we need: 
- Access inventory (current users + roles) for tenant: gridsat-prod (CSV)
- A representative set of SAML assertion samples for 3 recent successful logins (sanitized)
- A SCIM delta representing the last 30 days (adds/updates/deletes)
- Role-to-permission mapping (RBAC matrix) and a short note on least-privilege enforcement (how service accounts vs user accounts are treated)

Target for submission: COB 2026-11-21. Let me know if any of those items need clarification or if you prefer a phased delivery.

Thanks,
Tom
Security Ops — GridSat
security@gridsat.com

---

From: Irene Choi <irene.choi@redwood.ai>
To: Tom Leary <tom.leary@gridsat.com>
Date: Wed, 18 Nov 2026 10:31:00 -0800
Subject: Re: SIG intake — SSO/SCIM/RBAC artifacts request

Tom,

Appreciate the clear list. We can deliver the package by the 21st. Quick clarifications so we provide exactly what you need: 
1) Access inventory format: CSV with columns (email, user_id, role, created_at, last_login, provisioning_source). Is that sufficient?
2) SAML samples: we will redact user-identifying attributes but keep NameID pseudonym and assertion timestamps—ok to provide as text files?
3) SCIM delta: do you need raw event records or the reconciled add/update/delete list? We typically export both; reconciled list is faster to review.

I'll assemble a consolidated bundle and share a Drive link and a HubSpot document. If GridSat needs signatures on an evidence receipt, include the DocuSign link and we will route to Legal.

Irene Choi
Security & Compliance Liaison, Redwood Inference

---

From: Irene Choi <irene.choi@redwood.ai>
To: Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwood.ai>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.ai>
Cc: (internal)
Date: Thu, 19 Nov 2026 08:10:00 -0800
Subject: ACTION: Pull artifacts for GridSat SIG intake (due 11/21)

Sean/Marcus — assigning items below so we can ship a single consolidated package to GridSat by COB 11/21. Please reply in-thread when your piece is uploaded to the shared Drive folder.

Sean: Can you pull the SAML assertion samples and scrub PII? Deliverables: 3 assertion files (saml-assertion-sample.txt) with header noting the request id, assertion timestamps, and NameID pseudonym. Redaction checklist: replace real email addresses with user+hash@example.com, remove raw IP addresses, keep assertion Audience and Issuer intact.

Marcus: Please pull the SCIM delta for tenant gridsat-prod covering 2026-10-22 to 2026-11-19 and produce both the raw event log (scim-events-20261119.json) and a reconciled add/update/delete CSV. Also export the role-to-permission matrix for all custom roles created on gridsat-prod this year.

Additional: I'll assemble the access inventory CSV and write a short (2-paragraph) note on least-privilege enforcement and service-account handling. We will reference the RBAC docs and include a link to the enforcement playbook.

Timeline: everything uploaded to Drive by 11/20 EOD so I can run a quick quality pass and deliver to GridSat on 11/21 morning.

Thanks — Irene

---

From: Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwood.ai>
To: Irene Choi <irene.choi@redwood.ai>
Cc: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.ai>
Date: Fri, 20 Nov 2026 09:05:00 -0800
Subject: Re: ACTION: Pull artifacts for GridSat SIG intake (due 11/21)

Irene — SAML samples are ready and redacted per checklist. Files uploaded to Drive: /GS-SIG-2026-11-21/saml-assertion-sample.txt (3 files concatenated). Notes included about redaction steps and assertion times.

Attachment stub: saml-assertion-sample.txt (text)

Will stand by for Marcus's SCIM exports.

— Sean

---

From: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.ai>
To: Irene Choi <irene.choi@redwood.ai>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwood.ai>
Date: Fri, 20 Nov 2026 11:23:00 -0800
Subject: Re: ACTION: Pull artifacts for GridSat SIG intake (due 11/21)

Uploaded: scim-delta-20261119.json and gridsat-role-matrix.csv to Drive folder. Reconciled add/update/delete CSV is in the folder as well. A couple of notes:
- The SCIM delta contains a batch of automated deprovisions from 2026-11-02 that were the result of an upstream HR sync; I flagged those rows in the reconciled CSV.
- Role matrix includes three custom roles with comments on the last grant date.

Attachment stubs: scim-delta-20261119.json (JSON), gridsat-role-matrix.csv (CSV)

— Marcus

---

From: Irene Choi <irene.choi@redwood.ai>
To: Tom Leary <tom.leary@gridsat.com>, GridSat Security <security@gridsat.com>
Cc: Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwood.ai>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.ai>
Date: Sun, 21 Nov 2026 14:05:00 -0800
Subject: SIG intake package — GridSat (gridsat-prod)

Tom / GridSat security team,

Attached is the consolidated evidence package for tenant gridsat-prod. Summary of deliverables included in the bundle: 
- redwood_access_matrix.csv — current user inventory and role assignments (columns: email, user_id, role, created_at, last_login, provisioning_source)
- saml-assertion-sample.txt — three sanitized SAML assertion samples with header comments on redaction and assertion times
- scim-delta-20261119.json — raw SCIM event stream for 2026-10-22 -> 2026-11-19
- gridsat-role-matrix.csv — role -> permission mapping and short notes on custom roles
- short-enforcement-note.txt — 2-paragraph explanation on least-privilege enforcement, separation of duties, and service-account treatment

A few callouts:
- We redacted user-identifying attributes in the SAML assertions (NameID replaced with stable pseudonym). If GridSat needs fully-identifiable traces for an internal investigation, we can provide a narrow exception export under a Data Access Agreement—please let me know.
- The SCIM delta includes HR-driven deprovisioning events which are highlighted in the reconciled CSV; these are legitimate automated removals and not indicative of an incident.

Drive link: https://drive.redwood.com/s/GS-SIG-2026-11-21
HubSpot record: https://app.hubspot.com/contacts/54321/company/99887

Attachment stubs: redwood_access_matrix.csv (CSV), saml-assertion-sample.txt (text), scim-delta-20261119.json (JSON), gridsat-role-matrix.csv (CSV), short-enforcement-note.txt (text)

Please confirm receipt. If you'd like, we can set up a 30-minute review call to walk through the artifacts and answer questions — I have availability tomorrow 11/22 between 09:00–11:00 PT.

Best,
Irene Choi
Security & Compliance Liaison, Redwood Inference
irene.choi@redwood.ai

source.subject: Integrated MAP & Legal Sequencing — Novagen evaluation timetable
messages:
From: Karen Mitchell <k.mitchell@novagen.com>
To: Jasmine Liu <jasmine.liu@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Evan Cho <e.cho@novagen.com>
Date: Tue, 10 Nov 2026 09:12:00 -0800
Subject: MAP & contract sequencing for our Novagen evaluation

Hi Jasmine,

Thanks again for the demo yesterday — the throughput numbers looked promising for our use case. Before we invest further time, procurement/legal asked for a one-page mutual action plan that lays out the dependencies between the MSA, DPA and order form so we can align internal reviewers and schedule a signature target.

High level ask: can you produce a MAP that includes (1) milestone dates for evaluation/POC, (2) legal review windows for MSA/DPA, (3) expected sign date for order form, and (4) which artifacts (SOC2, DPA text, data flow diagrams) you will provide and when? Our target is to decide by end of November if we can include Redwood in our Q1 vendor slate.

Appreciate a draft this week if possible.

Thanks,
Karen
Karen Mitchell
Director, Strategic Sourcing — Novagen Therapeutics
procurement@novagen.com

Attachments: none
From: Jasmine Liu <jasmine.liu@redwoodinference.com>
To: Karen Mitchell <k.mitchell@novagen.com>, Novagen Procurement <procurement@novagen.com>
Cc: Dev Patel <dev.patel@redwoodinference.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Wed, 11 Nov 2026 10:05:00 -0800
Subject: Re: MAP & contract sequencing for our Novagen evaluation

Karen — thanks for the clear ask. I put together a one-sheet MAP and an annotated timeline that maps the evaluation milestones to the legal/ procurement steps. High-level summary below and attachments included: Novagen-MAP-2026.xlsx (MAP + timeline) and Redwood-MSA-redlines-Novagen.pdf (our standard MSA with inline comments).

Key milestones (proposed):
- 11/11–11/18: Evaluation extension and focused test cases (Novagen technical team runs integration tests)
- 11/19–11/25: Legal review window — we’ll accept redlines to the MSA/DPA; Redwood commits to consolidated response by 11/29
- 11/30–12/03: Commercial review / PO issuance target (order form negotiated)
- 12/06: Signed order form target (subject to redline complexity)

Artifacts we will provide immediately: SOC2 Type II report, the DPA template, data flow diagram and a small security questionnaire. I attached the SOC2 report (SOC2-TypeII-Redwood-2025.pdf) and the MSA redlines.

Notes & risks: If legal redlines are extensive (more than 3 major clauses), the sign date will slip ~2–3 weeks. Also for regulated data scenarios we recommend Dedicated deployment which triggers additional legal review — flag if that’s the plan.

Next steps:
1) Can your team confirm whether the evaluation requires Dedicated or Hosted?
2) Please confirm procurement’s preferred sign target (end of Nov vs mid-Dec).

-Jasmine
Jasmine Liu
Senior Account Executive, Redwood Inference
+1 (415) 555-0147

Attachments: Novagen-MAP-2026.xlsx, Redwood-MSA-redlines-Novagen.pdf, SOC2-TypeII-Redwood-2025.pdf
From: Evan Cho <e.cho@novagen.com>
To: Jasmine Liu <jasmine.liu@redwoodinference.com>, Karen Mitchell <k.mitchell@novagen.com>
Cc: Novagen Procurement <procurement@novagen.com>
Date: Mon, 16 Nov 2026 08:18:00 -0800
Subject: Re: MAP & contract sequencing for our Novagen evaluation

Thanks Jasmine — quick answers from the technical side: the evaluation will start on Hosted, but if performance tests indicate we need isolation we will request Dedicated. That said, procurement wants the MSA/DPA agreed regardless of deployment choice to avoid duplicate legal cycles.

On procurement timing: procurement prefers a firm sign by 12/03 to make the January onboarding window. We can compress the legal review but need clear owners and a single consolidated redline set.

On another note — we need to see a completed data flow diagram with PII/PHI callouts for our security reviewers before they sign off. Can you confirm you’ll include that in the MAP artifacts?

—Evan
Evan Cho
Senior Integration Lead — Novagen Therapeutics

Quoted earlier message:
> On Wed, Nov 11, 2026 at 10:05 AM Jasmine Liu <jasmine.liu@redwoodinference.com> wrote:
> > Karen — thanks for the clear ask. I put together a one-sheet MAP and an annotated timeline...
From: Dev Patel <dev.patel@redwoodinference.com>
To: Novagen Procurement <procurement@novagen.com>, Karen Mitchell <k.mitchell@novagen.com>
Cc: Jasmine Liu <jasmine.liu@redwoodinference.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Fri, 20 Nov 2026 16:02:00 -0800

title: Integrated MAP & Legal Sequencing — Novagen evaluation timetable
preview:
From: Karen Mitchell <k.mitchell@novagen.com>
To: Jasmine Liu <jasmine.liu@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Evan Cho <e.cho@novagen.com>
Date: Tue, 10 Nov 2026 09:12:00 -0800
Subject: MAP & contract sequencing for our Novagen evaluation

Hi Jasmine,

Thanks again for the demo yesterday — the throughput numbers looked promising for our use case. Before we invest further time, procurement/legal asked for a one-page mutual action plan that lays out the dependencies between the MSA, DPA and order form so we can align internal reviewers and schedule a signature target.

High level ask: can you produce a MAP that includes (1) milestone dates for evaluation/POC, (2) legal review windows for MSA/DPA, (3) expected sign date for order form, and (4) which artifacts (SOC2, DPA text, data flow diagrams) you will provide and when? Our target is to decide by end of November if we can include Redwood in our Q1 vendor slate.

Appreciate a draft this week if possible.

Thanks,
Karen
Karen Mitchell
Director, Strategic Sourcing — Novagen Therapeutics
procurement@novagen.com

Attachments: none


From: Jasmine Liu <jasmine.liu@redwoodinference.com>
To: Karen Mitchell <k.mitchell@novagen.com>, Novagen Procurement <procurement@novagen.com>
Cc: Dev Patel <dev.patel@redwoodinference.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Wed, 11 Nov 2026 10:05:00 -0800
Subject: Re: MAP & contract sequencing for our Novagen evaluation

Karen — thanks for the clear ask. I put together a one-sheet MAP and an annotated timeline that maps the evaluation milestones to the legal/ procurement steps. High-level summary below and attachments included: Novagen-MAP-2026.xlsx (MAP + timeline) and Redwood-MSA-redlines-Novagen.pdf (our standard MSA with inline comments).

Key milestones (proposed):
- 11/11–11/18: Evaluation extension and focused test cases (Novagen technical team runs integration tests)
- 11/19–11/25: Legal review window — we’ll accept redlines to the MSA/DPA; Redwood commits to consolidated response by 11/29
- 11/30–12/03: Commercial review / PO issuance target (order form negotiated)
- 12/06: Signed order form target (subject to redline complexity)

Artifacts we will provide immediately: SOC2 Type II report, the DPA template, data flow diagram and a small security questionnaire. I attached the SOC2 report (SOC2-TypeII-Redwood-2025.pdf) and the MSA redlines.

Notes & risks: If legal redlines are extensive (more than 3 major clauses), the sign date will slip ~2–3 weeks. Also for regulated data scenarios we recommend Dedicated deployment which triggers additional legal review — flag if that’s the plan.

Next steps:
1) Can your team confirm whether the evaluation requires Dedicated or Hosted?
2) Please confirm procurement’s preferred sign target (end of Nov vs mid-Dec).

-Jasmine
Jasmine Liu
Senior Account Executive, Redwood Inference
+1 (415) 555-0147

Attachments: Novagen-MAP-2026.xlsx, Redwood-MSA-redlines-Novagen.pdf, SOC2-TypeII-Redwood-2025.pdf


From: Evan Cho <e.cho@novagen.com>
To: Jasmine Liu <jasmine.liu@redwoodinference.com>, Karen Mitchell <k.mitchell@novagen.com>
Cc: Novagen Procurement <procurement@novagen.com>
Date: Mon, 16 Nov 2026 08:18:00 -0800
Subject: Re: MAP & contract sequencing for our Novagen evaluation

Thanks Jasmine — quick answers from the technical side: the evaluation will start on Hosted, but if performance tests indicate we need isolation we will request Dedicated. That said, procurement wants the MSA/DPA agreed regardless of deployment choice to avoid duplicate legal cycles.

On procurement timing: procurement prefers a firm sign by 12/03 to make the January onboarding window. We can compress the legal review but need clear owners and a single consolidated redline set.

On another note — we need to see a completed data flow diagram with PII/PHI callouts for our security reviewers before they sign off. Can you confirm you’ll include that in the MAP artifacts?

—Evan
Evan Cho
