source.subject: Compliance Tooling Subscription Consolidation — vendor econ analysis
messages:
From: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>, Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwood.com>
Cc: Vanta Sales <sales@vanta.com>, Drata Account Exec <amy@drata.com>, TrustCenter Support <support@trustcenter.com>, Acme Analytics Procurement <procurement@acme-analytics.com>
Date: Wed, 18 Nov 2026 09:02:00 -0800
Subject: Compliance Tooling Subscription Consolidation — vendor econ analysis

Team,

Acme Analytics has asked for a consolidated recommendation on which compliance vendor to standardize on for the next 12–18 months. They want: SOC 2 readiness, continuous control monitoring, and API-driven evidence exports for our SSO/workflow integration. Procurement asked us to surface the total landed cost (subscription + onboarding + annual add-ons) and a short list of operational trade-offs (time to onboard, evidence automation, support SLA) across the main vendors (Vanta, Drata, TrustCenter).

Can you each review the attached quotes from Vanta and Drata and add any missing points I should flag? I’ll compile a single spreadsheet for Acme. Key ask: focus on cost-per-seat for 500 engineers + cost for continuous evidence collectors (SCIM, AWS/GCP connectors) and any mandatory professional services.

Deadline: I’ll send the consolidated recommendation to procurement by EOD Friday (11/20).

Thanks —
Jordan
Director, Platform Partnerships
Redwood Inference

Attachments: vanta_enterprise_quote_q4_2026.pdf, drata_pricing_tiers_2026.xlsx
From: Vanta Sales <sales@vanta.com>
To: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Acme Analytics Procurement <procurement@acme-analytics.com>
Date: Wed, 18 Nov 2026 14:35:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Compliance Tooling Subscription Consolidation — vendor econ analysis

Hi Jordan —

Thanks for the context. Attached is an updated enterprise quote that includes:
- 12-month commitment pricing for 500 seats (with 10% education discount)
- One-time onboarding fee (professional services) with an optional 3-week accelerated track
- Connector licensing for AWS/GCP, Okta SCIM, and GitHub actions evidence collectors

Quick clarifications:
- The onboarding fee includes two weeks of SOC 2 evidence mapping, but does not include remediation work.
- Our SLA for enterprise customers includes 99.9% availability and 4-hour P1 response via our enterprise support tier.
- API export limits are listed; for heavy exports we recommend the add-on export bundle.

If useful I can put you in touch with our solutions engineer to run a short demo ingest of a sample evidence set from Acme.

Best,
Samir Patel
Enterprise Account Executive, Vanta
Vanta Inc.

Attachment: vanta_enterprise_quote_q4_2026.pdf
From: Drata Account Exec <amy@drata.com>
To: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>, Acme Analytics Procurement <procurement@acme-analytics.com>
Date: Thu, 19 Nov 2026 10:10:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Compliance Tooling Subscription Consolidation — vendor econ analysis

Jordan —

Appreciate the head's up. I’ve attached our pricing tiers and an export of what continuous monitoring looks like for an organization with ~500 users. Highlights:
- Lower per-seat list price than Vanta at baseline, but professional services for complex connectors are an additional line item.
- Drata’s connector library is broad and we include several evidence collectors out of the box; custom API integration work is estimated separately.
- We do bulk license management (SCIM) without an extra fee, which reduces admin overhead.

We also can offer a 10% platform credit for 12-month prepay if you can commit to the enterprise tier by the end of the month.

Happy to schedule a 30-minute tech sync with our integration engineer — she can walk through the export cadence for evidence and show the CSV/API payloads.

Cheers,
Amy Lin
Account Executive, Drata

Attachment: drata_pricing_tiers_2026.xlsx
From: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>, Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwood.com>
Cc: Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwood.com>, Acme Analytics Procurement <procurement@acme-analytics.com>
Date: Fri, 20 Nov 2026 11:02:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Compliance Tooling Subscription Consolidation — vendor econ analysis

Team — quick draft summary attached (consolidated-vendor-econ-comparison.csv). Top-level observations:

- Cost: Drata shows ~12% lower TCO on list prices for 500 seats when excluding professional services. Vanta’s onboarding fee pushes their first-year TCO higher but their paid onboarding covers mapping and runbook creation which reduces internal PM hours.
- Automation: Drata includes more free collectors for out-of-the-box sources; Vanta has a stronger API export bundle (but as an add-on).
- Time-to-value: Vanta’s accelerated onboarding option shortens calendar time to evidence readiness by ~3 weeks if you pay for the 3-week track.
- Risk/Support: both vendors provide enterprise SLAs; Vanta highlights a 4-hour P1 response which Acme values for production controls.

I flagged three practical questions for procurement to nail down with Acme:
1) Are they willing to invest in a paid accelerated onboarding if it reduces internal remediation time?
2) Expected connector surface area (how many cloud projects/repos to wire up)?
3) Preference on prepay vs. monthly billing (Drata discount vs Vanta amortized PS).

I’ve attached the comparison CSV and included the quoted line items. Please add any corrections inline by EOD today and I’ll forward the recommendation to procurement and Acme.

Thanks —
Jordan
Director, Platform Partnerships
Redwood Inference

Attachment: consolidated-vendor-econ-comparison.csv

> On Thu, Nov 19, 2026 at 10:10 AM Drata Account Exec <amy@drata.com> wrote:
> 
> Jordan —
> 
> Appreciate the head's up. I’ve attached our pricing tiers and an export of what continuous monitoring looks like for an organization with ~500 users. Highlights:
> - Lower per-seat list price than Vanta at baseline, but professional services for complex connectors are an additional line item.
> - Drata’s connector library is broad and we include several evidence collectors out of the box; custom API integration work is estimated separately.
> - We do bulk license management (SCIM) without an extra fee, which reduces admin overhead.
> 
> Cheers,
> Amy Lin
> Account Executive, Drata
From: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
To: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>
Cc: Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwood.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwood.com>, Acme Analytics Procurement <procurement@acme-analytics.com>
Date: Fri, 20 Nov 2026 15:44:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Compliance Tooling Subscription Consolidation — vendor econ analysis

Jordan / team —

Thanks for the clear breakdown. A couple of follow-ups from procurement/legal:
- Legal wants the PS SOW language early (deliverables + acceptance criteria) so we can assess remediation scope exposure. Can someone from Vanta/Drata share a standard SOW template?
- Procurement prefers prepay for a 10% credit, but we need to verify cashflow with finance.

Next steps I’m proposing:
1) Jordan: finalize the consolidated spreadsheet and mark an explicit recommendation (Drata if cost + low PS needs; Vanta if ACME requests rapid ramp + embedded runbook deliverables).
2) Jordan: ask both vendors to send their standard SOW + sample runbook (if available) to legal@redwood.com and to Acme Procurement.
3) Marissa: I’ll coordinate finance visibility and confirm whether prepay is acceptable by Monday.

Marissa Cole
Sr. Manager, Sourcing & Procurement
Redwood Inference

> On Fri, Nov 20, 2026 at 11:02 AM Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com> wrote:
> Team — quick draft summary attached (consolidated-vendor-econ-comparison.csv). Top-level observations:
> 
> - Cost: Drata shows ~12% lower TCO on list prices for 500 seats when excluding professional services. Vanta’s onboarding fee pushes their first-year TCO higher but their paid onboarding covers mapping and runbook creation which reduces internal PM hours.
> 
> Thanks —
> Jordan

Jordan / team —

Thanks for the clear breakdown. A couple of follow-ups from procurement/legal:
- Legal wants the PS SOW language early (deliverables + acceptance criteria) so we can assess remediation scope exposure. Can someone from Vanta/Drata share a standard SOW template?
- Procurement prefers prepay for a 10% credit, but we need to verify cashflow with finance.

Marissa Cole

text:
From: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>, Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwood.com>
Cc: Vanta Sales <sales@vanta.com>, Drata Account Exec <amy@drata.com>, TrustCenter Support <support@trustcenter.com>, Acme Analytics Procurement <procurement@acme-analytics.com>
Date: Wed, 18 Nov 2026 09:02:00 -0800
Subject: Compliance Tooling Subscription Consolidation — vendor econ analysis

Team,

Acme Analytics has asked for a consolidated recommendation on which compliance vendor to standardize on for the next 12–18 months. They want: SOC 2 readiness, continuous control monitoring, and API-driven evidence exports for our SSO/workflow integration. Procurement asked us to surface the total landed cost (subscription + onboarding + annual add-ons) and a short list of operational trade-offs (time to onboard, evidence automation, support SLA) across the main vendors (Vanta, Drata, TrustCenter).

Can you each review the attached quotes from Vanta and Drata and add any missing points I should flag? I’ll compile a single spreadsheet for Acme. Key ask: focus on cost-per-seat for 500 engineers + cost for continuous evidence collectors (SCIM, AWS/GCP connectors) and any mandatory professional services.

Deadline: I’ll send the consolidated recommendation to procurement by EOD Friday (11/20).

Thanks —
Jordan
Director, Platform Partnerships
Redwood Inference

Attachments: vanta_enterprise_quote_q4_2026.pdf, drata_pricing_tiers_2026.xlsx


From: Vanta Sales <sales@vanta.com>
To: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Acme Analytics Procurement <procurement@acme-analytics.com>
Date: Wed, 18 Nov 2026 14:35:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Compliance Tooling Subscription Consolidation — vendor econ analysis

Hi Jordan —

Thanks for the context. Attached is an updated enterprise quote that includes:
- 12-month commitment pricing for 500 seats (with 10% education discount)
- One-time onboarding fee (professional services) with an optional 3-week accelerated track
- Connector licensing for AWS/GCP, Okta SCIM, and GitHub actions evidence collectors

Quick clarifications:
- The onboarding fee includes two weeks of SOC 2 evidence mapping, but does not include remediation work.
- Our SLA for enterprise customers includes 99.9% availability and 4-hour P1 response via our enterprise support tier.
- API export limits are listed; for heavy exports we recommend the add-on export bundle.

If useful I can put you in touch with our solutions engineer to run a short demo ingest of a sample evidence set from Acme.

Best,
Samir Patel
Enterprise Account Executive, Vanta
Vanta Inc.

Attachment: vanta_enterprise_quote_q4_2026.pdf


From: Drata Account Exec <amy@drata.com>
To: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>, Acme Analytics Procurement <procurement@acme-analytics.com>
Date: Thu, 19 Nov 2026 10:10:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Compliance Tooling Subscription Consolidation — vendor econ analysis

Jordan —

Appreciate the head's up. I’ve attached our pricing tiers and an export of what continuous monitoring looks like for an organization with ~500 users. Highlights:
- Lower per-seat list price than Vanta at baseline, but professional services for complex connectors are an additional line item.
- Drata’s connector library is broad and we include several evidence collectors out of the box; custom API integration work is estimated separately.
- We do bulk license management (SCIM) without an extra fee, which reduces admin overhead.

We also can offer a 10% platform credit for 12-month prepay if you can commit to the enterprise tier by the end of the month.

Happy to schedule a 30-minute tech sync with our integration engineer — she can walk through the export cadence for evidence and show the CSV/API payloads.

Cheers,
Amy Lin
Account Executive, Drata

Attachment: drata_pricing_tiers_2026.xlsx


From: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>, Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwood.com>
Cc: Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwood.com>, Acme Analytics Procurement <procurement@acme-analytics.com>
Date: Fri, 20 Nov 2026 11:02:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Compliance Tooling Subscription Consolidation — vendor econ analysis

Team — quick draft summary attached (consolidated-vendor-econ-comparison.csv). Top-level observations:

- Cost: Drata shows ~12% lower TCO on list prices for 500 seats when excluding professional services. Vanta’s onboarding fee pushes their first-year TCO higher but their paid onboarding covers mapping and runbook creation which reduces internal PM hours.
- Automation: Drata includes more free collectors for out-of-the-box sources; Vanta has a stronger API export bundle (but as an add-on).
- Time-to-value: Vanta’s accelerated onboarding option shortens calendar time to evidence readiness by ~3 weeks if you pay for the 3-week track.
- Risk/Support: both vendors provide enterprise SLAs; Vanta highlights a 4-hour P1 response which Acme values for production controls.

I flagged three practical questions for procurement to nail down with Acme:
1) Are they willing to invest in a paid accelerated onboarding if it reduces internal remediation time?
2) Expected connector surface area (how many cloud projects/repos to wire up)?
3) Preference on prepay vs. monthly billing (Drata discount vs Vanta amortized PS).

I’ve attached the comparison CSV and included the quoted line items. Please add any corrections inline by EOD today and I’ll forward the recommendation to procurement and Acme.

Thanks —
Jordan
Director, Platform Partnerships
Redwood Inference

Attachment: consolidated-vendor-econ-comparison.csv

source.subject: Production 5xx/429 spike & retry-flood — SLA credit discussion
messages:
From: Julian Park <julian@citrine.ai>
To: Evelyn Hart <evelyn_hart@redwood.ai>
Cc: Alice Navarro <alice@citrine.ai>
Date: Thu, 12 Nov 2026 09:12:00 -0800
Subject: High 5xx/429 spike + timeouts — production impact 11/12

Hi Evelyn,

We experienced a sustained spike in 5xx/429 responses and client timeouts between 09:06 and 09:40 PST this morning across our main inference route. Symptoms: client-side retry amplification, visible latency > 2s for baseline requests, and multiple downstream services reporting increased queueing. We saw multiple retry storms as clients attempted automated backoffs but concurrency limits exacerbated the problem.

I've attached a sample of client-side logs (citrine_client_logs_20261112.zip) and a brief impact spreadsheet (impact-summary.xlsx) — the latter shows ~18% of inference requests failed or timed out in the impacted window. This materially affected our customer flows and billing.

Please escalate — we need:
- immediate remediation and rollback plan
- a detailed timeline of what went wrong
- confirmation that redundant routing/regions weren't impacted
- proposed SLA credit for the impacted window (we believe ~30% is reasonable given impact)
- exec-level contact for updates

Julian
Julian Park
SRE, Citrine.ai

Attachments: citrine_client_logs_20261112.zip (logs), impact-summary.xlsx (calc)

From: Evelyn Hart <evelyn_hart@redwood.ai>
To: Julian Park <julian@citrine.ai>
Cc: Lauren Bishop <lauren_bishop@redwood.ai>, David Kim <david_kim@redwood.ai>
Date: Thu, 12 Nov 2026 09:20:00 -0800
Subject: Re: High 5xx/429 spike + timeouts — production impact 11/12

Julian — thank you for the heads-up and the attachments. We’re on it.

Short answer: we have a triage team looking at metrics and traces now (cc: David to lead the investigation). I’ll keep you updated every 30–60 minutes and loop in Sean (ops escalation) if we see a sustained SLA exposure.

Next steps we’re executing immediately:
- collect full server-side request traces and throttling logs
- temporarily increase concurrency caps for your account while we stabilize
- apply a protective client-side rate-limit recommendation (we’ll share specific values)

I’ll follow up with an initial post-mortem timeline as soon as we have root cause. Sorry for the disruption — we know how disruptive retry storms can be.

— Evelyn Hart
Head of Sales Engineering, Redwood Inference
Evelyn Hart | Redwood Inference

Attachment: (none yet)

From: David Kim <david_kim@redwood.ai>
To: Evelyn Hart <evelyn_hart@redwood.ai>, Lauren Bishop <lauren_bishop@redwood.ai>
Cc: Julian Park <julian@citrine.ai>
Date: Thu, 12 Nov 2026 09:33:00 -0800
Subject: Re: High 5xx/429 spike + timeouts — production impact 11/12

Julian / team — quick technical summary from initial triage: we observed an anomalous routing update at 09:04:42 PST that briefly removed an optimized pooling path for your tenant. That caused a subset of requests to be routed to a slower execution path plus a mis-graded autoscaler threshold that reduced available workers during the window. Client retries then amplified the backlog (retry flood).

Immediate mitigations applied by 09:18 PST:
- reinstated the pooling path and rolled back the routing change
- adjusted autoscaler thresholds and provisioned additional GPUs for the affected pool
- enabled ephemeral request-priority gating to drain backlog

We’re preparing a more detailed trace-by-trace timeline and will share the runbook we executed. Short-term recommendation for clients is an exponential backoff with jitter and a maximum retry cap of 3 for inference calls during high error rates.

I’m attaching the internal playbook excerpt we used: retry_flood_playbook.md (redacted).

— David
David Kim
Sr. Infrastructure Engineer, Redwood Inference
Attachment: retry_flood_playbook.md

From: Julian Park <julian@citrine.ai>
To: Evelyn Hart <evelyn_hart@redwood.ai>, David Kim <david_kim@redwood.ai>
Cc: Alice Navarro <alice@citrine.ai>
Date: Thu, 12 Nov 2026 10:01:00 -0800
Subject: Re: High 5xx/429 spike + timeouts — production impact 11/12

Thanks David — appreciate the quick triage. The mitigations reduced errors but impacted throughput for about 34 minutes total (09:06–09:40). We’ve pulled customer support tickets and will need an internal timeline to reconcile with our downstream SLAs.

On credits: given the customer impact and downstream costs, Citrine is requesting a formal SLA credit equal to 30% of our usage during the affected window. Please provide the server-side metrics and a formal credit proposal (amount and the billing invoice it will apply to). We’ll route the request internally once we have the documentation.

Also — can you confirm whether any historical data or cached embeddings were reprocessed during the incident? We want to ensure no duplicated inference billing occurred.

Julian

Attachments: impact-summary.xlsx

From: Evelyn Hart <evelyn_hart@redwood.ai>
To: Julian Park <julian@citrine.ai>
Cc: Lauren Bishop <lauren_bishop@redwood.ai>, Sean Gallagher <sean_gallagher@redwood.ai>
Date: Thu, 12 Nov 2026 11:45:00 -0800
Subject: Re: High 5xx/429 spike + timeouts — production impact 11/12

Julian — thanks for the clear ask and the example artifacts. We’ve reviewed the traces and David’s summary covers the technical sequence. Quick answers:
- No historical data reprocessing detected; billing logs show only live request attempts (we’ll attach the audit log slice).
- We can confirm the affected window as 09:06–09:40 PST.

On credits: as an immediate gesture of goodwill we can offer a 20% credit for usage during the impacted window applied to your next invoice. We need to finalize the exact amount with finance and legal — please allow 48 hours for a formal credit letter and invoice adjustment.

If that’s acceptable we’ll send the SLA claims form and the audit log extract. If Citrine prefers to escalate for a higher credit we can route to our commercial review (may take 3–5 business days).

Best,
Evelyn Hart
Head of Sales Engineering, Redwood Inference
Attachment: (will send SLA-claims-form.pdf & audit slice)

From: Lauren Bishop <lauren_bishop@redwood.ai>
To: Julian Park <julian@citrine.ai>
Cc: Evelyn Hart <evelyn_hart@redwood.ai>, Sean Gallagher <sean_gallagher@redwood.ai>
Date: Sat, 14 Nov 2026 14:05:00 -0800
Subject: Proposed credit and next steps — Citrine incident 11/12

Julian — following up with a formal proposal. Thank you for patience while we ran the audit.

Proposal:
- A 25% usage credit for the billing period covering 2026-11-12 09:06:00–09:40:00 PST applied to invoice INV-20261130-DEAL-7891.
- Delivery timeline: formal credit letter and invoice adjustment within 48 hours of your acceptance.
- Complimentary three months of Redwood Optimize (technical support and weekly optimization consults) as a one-time goodwill add-on to help mitigate future cost/perf risks.

We’re attaching the SLA claims form to formalize the request (SLA-claims-form.pdf) and the audit log slice. Please confirm acceptance and we’ll route the paperwork to Finance for processing. For transparency, I’ve cc’d Sean Gallagher (Revenue Ops) to ensure billing changes are executed promptly.

If you’d like a higher credit, we can escalate but it will require a commercial review.

Thanks,
Lauren Bishop
Director, Commercial Operations — Redwood Inference
Attachment: SLA-claims-form.pdf

From: Julian Park <julian@citrine.ai>
To: Lauren Bishop <lauren_bishop@redwood.ai>, Evelyn Hart <evelyn_hart@redwood.ai>
Cc: Alice Navarro <alice@citrine.ai>
Date: Tue, 17 Nov 2026 15:45:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Proposed credit and next steps — Citrine incident 11/12

Lauren / Evelyn — thank you. We accept the 25% credit and the Redwood Optimize offer. Please go ahead and route the credit letter and invoice adjustment. We’ll complete the SLA-claims-form and return it to you within 24 hours.

One small ask: could you include a short technical annex with the root cause summary and the steps taken to prevent recurrence? Our leadership wants that included in the vendor audit.

Appreciate the responsiveness.

— Julian Park
SRE, Citrine.ai
Attachment: completed-SLA-claims-form.pdf (to follow)

Date: Thu, 12 Nov 2026 09:33:00 -0800
Subject: Re: High 5xx/429 spike + timeouts — production impact 11/12

Julian / team — quick technical summary from initial triage: we observed an anomalous routing update at 09:04:42 PST that briefly removed an optimized pooling path for your tenant. That caused a subset of requests to be routed to a slower execution path plus a mis-graded autoscaler threshold that reduced available workers during the window. Client retries then amplified the backlog (retry flood).

Immediate mitigations applied by 09:18 PST:
- reinstated the pooling path and rolled back the routing change
- adjusted autoscaler thresholds and provisioned additional GPUs for the affected pool
- enabled ephemeral request-priority gating to drain backlog

We’re preparing a more detailed trace-by-trace timeline and will share the runbook we executed. Short-term recommendation for clients is an exponential backoff with jitter and a maximum retry cap of 3 for inference calls during high error rates.

I’m attaching the internal playbook excerpt we used: retry_flood_playbook.md (redacted).

— David
David Kim
Sr. Infrastructure Engineer, Redwood Inference
Attachment: retry_flood_playbook.md

From: Julian Park <julian@citrine.ai>
To: Evelyn Hart <evelyn_hart@redwood.ai>, David Kim <david_kim@redwood.ai>
Cc: Alice Navarro <alice@citrine.ai>
Date: Thu, 12 Nov 2026 10:01:00 -0800
Subject: Re: High 5xx/429 spike + timeouts — production impact 11/12

Thanks David — appreciate the quick triage. The mitigations reduced errors but impacted throughput for about 34 minutes total (09:06–09:40). We’ve pulled customer support tickets and will need an internal timeline to reconcile with our downstream SLAs.

On credits: given the customer impact and downstream costs, Citrine is requesting a formal SLA credit equal to 30% of our usage during the affected window. Please provide the server-side metrics and a formal credit proposal (amount and the billing invoice it will apply to). We’ll route the request internally once we have the documentation.

Also — can you confirm whether any historical data or cached embeddings were reprocessed during the incident? We want to ensure no duplicated inference billing occurred.

Julian

Attachments: impact-summary.xlsx

From: Evelyn Hart <evelyn_hart@redwood.ai>
To: Julian Park <julian@citrine.ai>
Cc: Lauren Bishop <lauren_bishop@redwood.ai>, Sean Gallagher <sean_gallagher@redwood.ai>
Date: Thu, 12 Nov 2026 11:45:00 -0800
Subject: Re: High 5xx/429 spike + timeouts — production impact 11/12

Julian — thanks for the clear ask and the example artifacts. We’ve reviewed the traces and David’s summary covers the technical sequence. Quick answers:
- No historical data reprocessing detected; billing logs show only live request attempts (we’ll attach the audit log slice).
- We can confirm the affected window as 09:06–09:40 PST.

On credits: as an immediate gesture of goodwill we can offer a 20% credit for usage during the impacted window applied to your next invoice. We need to finalize the exact amount with finance and legal — please allow 48 hours for a formal credit letter and invoice adjustment.

If that’s acceptable we’ll send the SLA claims form and the audit log extract. If Citrine prefers to escalate for a higher credit we can route to our commercial review (may take 3–5 business days).

Best,
Evelyn Hart
Head of Sales Engineering, Redwood Inference
Attachment: (will send SLA-claims-form.pdf & audit slice)

From: Lauren Bishop <lauren_bishop@redwood.ai>
To: Julian Park <julian@citrine.ai>
Cc: Evelyn Hart <evelyn_hart@redwood.ai>, Sean Gallagher <sean_gallagher@redwood.ai>
Date: Sat, 14 Nov 2026 14:05:00 -0800
Subject: Proposed credit and next steps — Citrine incident 11/12

Julian — following up with a formal proposal. Thank you for patience while we ran the audit.

Proposal:
- A 25% usage credit for the billing period covering 2026-11-12 09:06:00–09:40:00 PST applied to invoice INV-20261130-DEAL-7891.
- Delivery timeline: formal credit letter and invoice adjustment within 48 hours of your acceptance.
- Complimentary three months of Redwood Optimize (technical support and weekly optimization consults) as a one-time goodwill add-on to help mitigate future cost/perf risks.

We’re attaching the SLA claims form to formalize the request (SLA-claims-form.pdf) and the audit log slice. Please confirm acceptance and we’ll route the paperwork to Finance for processing. For transparency, I’ve cc’d Sean Gallagher (Revenue Ops) to ensure billing changes are executed promptly.

If you’d like a higher credit, we can escalate but it will require a commercial review.

Thanks,
Lauren Bishop
Director, Commercial Operations — Redwood Inference
Attachment: SLA-claims-form.pdf

From: Julian Park <julian@citrine.ai>
To: Lauren Bishop <lauren_bishop@redwood.ai>, Evelyn Hart <evelyn_hart@redwood.ai>
Cc: Alice Navarro <alice@citrine.ai>
Date: Tue, 17 Nov 2026 15:45:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Proposed credit and next steps — Citrine incident 11/12

Lauren / Evelyn — thank you. We accept the 25% credit and the Redwood Optimize offer. Please go ahead and route the credit letter and invoice adjustment. We’ll complete the SLA-claims-form and return it to you within 24 hours.

One small ask: could you include a short technical annex with the root cause summary and the steps taken to prevent recurrence? Our leadership wants that included in the vendor audit.

Appreciate the responsiveness.

— Julian Park

source.subject: Rate lattice and marginal vs bundle construct
messages:
From: Jordan Bates <j.bates@novahealth.com>
To: Irene Choi <irene.choi@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: procurement@novahealth.com
Date: Sun, 15 Nov 2026 09:12:00 -0800
Subject: Pricing construct Q: per-token ladder vs bundled rates

Hi Irene,

Thanks again for the demo last week — the latency numbers look great. Procurement asked me to pull together a proposal request for a committed monthly program (hosted API). Two things they want clarity on before we go to legal:

- How you structure per-token volume discounts (breakpoints) and whether you price on a marginal basis or convert to an effective blended rate on invoices. Practical examples would help.
- We plan to use both generation tokens and embeddings; does Redwood collapse both into one ladder or do you recommend separate bundles (and how does that affect effective rate math)?

We’re looking at a baseline target of ~50M tokens/month for a 12‑month term with a 1Q true-up. Can you provide an example cost model for that usage under both "marginal" and a "bundle/blended" treatment, and call out clawbacks / undercommit protections?

If you can get a short spreadsheet and an executive summary back to procurement by Tuesday that would be ideal.

Thanks,
Jordan
Director, Product
NovaHealth

From: Irene Choi <irene.choi@redwoodinference.com>
To: Jordan Bates <j.bates@novahealth.com>
Cc: Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Sun, 15 Nov 2026 13:05:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Pricing construct Q: per-token ladder vs bundled rates

Jordan —

Appreciate the clear ask. High level first: we support both approaches, and which one we recommend depends on billing simplicity vs economic transparency. Quick exec summary below and I attached a worked spreadsheet (rate_lattice_v2.xlsx).

Executive summary:
- Marginal ladder: each breakpoint has its own per-token rate. This minimizes the list price at the top bands and is straightforward for customers that want true marginal economics.
- Blended/bundle: we calculate an effective blended rate across committed volume. Easier for predictable invoices but can hide marginal benefits for burst usage.
- Bundling embeddings+generation: we can either keep them in a single ladder (tokens aggregated) or split into two ladders with different breakpoints/rates. Aggregation typically lowers the blended rate for customers that do a lot of low-cost embeddings.
- True-up and clawback: standard quarterly true-up. We offer corridor credits for +/- 15% variance in the first two quarters; beyond that we apply standard true-up reconciliation or a pro-rata clawback on credits.

I attached one tab with a 50M-token example showing both marginal math and a blended conversion (you asked for 1Q true-up). The spreadsheet also contains a small sensitivity table for embedding-to-generation token mix.

Next steps: if procurement wants, we can (a) pick a ladder and produce a redlined term sheet, or (b) schedule a 30‑minute rate walk-through with Karthik (he’s cc’d) to go through the spreadsheet live.

Best,
Irene
Head of Partnerships (Commercial)
Redwood Inference
Attachment: rate_lattice_v2.xlsx (spreadsheet, 1.2MB)

From: Jordan Bates <j.bates@novahealth.com>
To: Irene Choi <irene.choi@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Carello AI Procurement <procurement@novahealth.com>
Date: Mon, 16 Nov 2026 08:34:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Pricing construct Q: per-token ladder vs bundled rates

Thanks Irene — this is helpful. A couple quick clarifications after I skimmed the sheet:

1) If we aggregate embeddings and generation tokens into one ladder, how do you show the billing split on the invoice? Procurement insists on seeing line-item visibility for audit reasons.
2) On corridor credits — can you give an example of how credits are applied and then clawed back if we stay below commit three consecutive quarters?

Procurement also asked whether quarterly true-up means retroactive billing at the marginal band rate or if you invoice the blended delta at the end of the quarter. They want to understand cashflow timing.

If Karthik can send a short worked example (numbers) that we can include in the procurement packet that would close the loop.

—Jordan

From: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwoodinference.com>
To: Jordan Bates <j.bates@novahealth.com>, Irene Choi <irene.choi@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Mon, 16 Nov 2026 11:10:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Pricing construct Q: per-token ladder vs bundled rates — worked math

Jordan — looping in the worked math below (pulled into the spreadsheet too). I kept the example intentionally simple so procurement can follow the cashflow. All numbers in USD per token.

Assumptions (example ladder):
- Band A: 0–10M tokens @ $0.00080/token
- Band B: 10M–30M tokens @ $0.00060/token
- Band C: 30M–100M tokens @ $0.00050/token

Scenario: 50M tokens consumed in a month. Two treatments:

1) Marginal pricing (invoice shows each band line-item):
- Band A: 10,000,000 * $0.00080 = $8,000
- Band B: 20,000,000 * $0.00060 = $12,000
- Band C: 20,000,000 * $0.00050 = $10,000
- Total = $30,000
- Effective marginal average = $30,000 / 50,000,000 = $0.00060/token

Invoice behavior: we bill each band as consumed during the month; quarterly true-up reconciles committed vs actual and invoices or credits the delta at the marginal rates used that quarter. This means cashflow tracks consumption (no large retroactive reprice).

2) Blended/bundle treatment (single effective rate applied across total token bucket):
- Blended rate is computed by weighting the marginal bands across forecast/committed volume. Example approach: compute blended rate for 50M using the ladder math above -> $30,000 / 50M = $0.00060 (same numeric result in this example). But if the committed bands differed or you applied a contractual blended discount, the invoice would typically show: (a) line for committed charge using blended rate, (b) true-up delta invoiced as a separate line at the end of the quarter.

Embeddings vs generation bundling:
- If you aggregate tokens, invoice still exposes token-type breakout (we provide line items: "generation tokens", "embedding tokens", "total tokens"). The ladder applies to the total. If you want two ladders, each ladder has its own breakpoints and the invoice shows both ladders and a combined summary.

Corridor credits and clawback example:
- Offer: 15% corridor for Q1 and Q2. Example: committed 50M/month (150M/quarter). If actual quarter usage >= 85% of commit (>=127.5M) -> no clawback. If actual between 70–85% -> apply partial credit amortization (we keep X% of credits). If actual < 70% across three consecutive quarters -> clawback of previously applied credits pro-rata on remaining term. The spreadsheet has a sample calculation (Procurement likes numbers; see tab "Corridor example").

Recommendation: For NovaHealth profile (mix of embeddings and gen, predictable steady baseline), we typically recommend a single aggregated ladder with line-item token breakout on the invoice + quarterly marginal true-up. It gives the best of both worlds: predictable blended invoices for finance, with transparent marginal math on the reconciliation.

I attached a concise PDF summary for procurement to include in the packet. Happy to jump on a quick call to walk through.

Cheers,
Karthik Iyer
Principal Solutions Engineer
Redwood Inference
Attachment: pricing_summary_novahealth.pdf (PDF, 420KB)

From: Irene Choi <irene.choi@redwoodinference.com>
To: Jordan Bates <j.bates@novahealth.com>, procurement@novahealth.com
Cc: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwoodinference.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, 17 Nov 2026 15:03:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Pricing construct Q: per-token ladder vs bundled rates — next steps

Jordan / Procurement —

Great — glad that helps. Proposed next steps:
1) We’ll post the procurement packet (pricing_summary_novahealth.pdf + rate_lattice_v2.xlsx) to your HubSpot record and Drive link.
2) If you confirm the aggregated ladder path, we’ll produce a term sheet and DocuSign-ready SOW for signature.
3) Karthik can run a 30-minute pricing walkthrough on Thursday (11/19) at 10:00am PT if that works for your team.

Please reply with (a) confirmation of the aggregated ladder choice or (b) request for split ladders, and (c) if you want us to draft the PO/DocuSign now.

Thanks,
Irene
Head of Partnerships (Commercial)
Redwood Inference

- Thu 2026-11-19 09:00–10:00

Subject: Re: POC capacity workshop availability and agenda

Thanks for confirming, Olivia. A few technical notes ahead of Tue's session so we can hit the ground running:

- Prompt file: raw prompt text is fine; if you can add a rough token estimate column that helps speed config. We included a sample_prompts.csv template (attached).
- Dedicated provisioning: we can spin a small Dedicated pool in ~20–30 minutes for a short test (non-production). For the workshop we'll pre-stage the image and reserve quota so the flip is <1 minute.
- Baseline numbers (guestimates for your expected profile):
  * Typical request size: 1.5k tokens (prompt+response)
  * Peak concurrency to validate: 50 concurrent requests -> expected ~75k tokens/minute
  * Short Dedicated pool for validation: 2x A100-equivalent GPUs (1–2 minute steady latency target)

Planned session runbook (high-level):
1) Confirm prompt mapping + token estimates (5–10m)
2) Route a 5–10 minute low-traffic replay to validate correctness (10m)
3) Provision pre-staged Dedicated pool and run a 10-minute ramp to peak (15m)
4) Review token spend and identify batching/quantization knobs (10m)

Attachments: poc_capacity_estimate.xlsx, sample_prompts.csv

If there are any IP restrictions, please send the allowlist. Otherwise we'll publish a short postmortem with token accounting and recommendations.

—Vivek
Senior Solutions Engineer, Redwood Inference

source.subject: APAC hosted inference: model-variant partial degradation impacting Zendata — customer notice
messages:
From: Kimberly Park <kimberly_park@redwood.ai>
To: Asha Patel <asha@zendata.ai>; ops@zendata.ai
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.ai>, Dylan Brooks <dylan_brooks@redwood.ai>
Date: Mon, 16 Nov 2026 08:12:00 +0000
Subject: APAC hosted inference: model-variant partial degradation impacting Zendata — customer notice

Hi Asha / Zendata ops,

We are reaching out to let you know we've detected a partial degradation affecting hosted inference in the APAC (ap-southeast-1) region that has impacted some requests from your tenant since ~07:40 UTC. Symptoms we've observed: elevated 502 responses (~1.2% of requests for your traffic), intermittent truncated generations (partial responses), and increased token latencies for the model variant you use (redwood-3b-quant-v2).

What we have done so far (immediate mitigations):
- Began routing new requests to a compatible fallback variant (redwood-3b-float16) for Zendata traffic as of 07:55 UTC.
- Increased capacity in the Dedicated pool serving ap-southeast-1 and enabled temporary connection pinning to reduce rebalancing.
- Enabled aggressive KV cache bypass for affected sessions to avoid stale prefix state.

Impact: We estimate ~1–2% of your API calls between 07:40–08:00 UTC may have received degraded responses. Most traffic is now on the fallback variant; we expect reduced error rates and restored response completeness.

Next steps:
- Our SREs are investigating the root cause; we will provide a complete timeline and a post-incident write-up once validated.
- We will keep your team updated on the status page and via these emails.

Attachments: incident_timeline_kp_20261116.pdf (timeline snapshot).

If you need an immediate rollback to float16-only for all sessions or temporary throughput increases, tell us and we'll enforce it for your tenant.

Best,
Kimberly Park
Customer Success Manager, Redwood Inference
kimberly_park@redwood.ai
+1 415-555-0133

--
Redwood Inference

From: Asha Patel <asha@zendata.ai>
To: Kimberly Park <kimberly_park@redwood.ai>
Cc: ops@zendata.ai
Date: Mon, 16 Nov 2026 08:26:00 +0000
Subject: Re: APAC hosted inference: model-variant partial degradation impacting Zendata — customer notice

Thanks Kimberly — we saw errors starting around 07:42 UTC and had three internal alerts. Appreciate the fallback routing. Two quick asks: 1) can you share any request ids/log snippets for the failed picks (we've captured a few on our side), and 2) what's the ETA for a full resolution and whether we should keep the fallback variant pinned for the next 24 hours?

We prefer not to force a full rollback unless necessary — fallback is fine short term but we'd like an estimate so our on-call can decide whether to open client-facing incident notices.

Asha
Head of Platform, Zendata AI
asha@zendata.ai

From: Kimberly Park <kimberly_park@redwood.ai>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.ai>, Dylan Brooks <dylan_brooks@redwood.ai>, Sean Gallagher <sean_gallagher@redwood.ai>
Date: Mon, 16 Nov 2026 08:33:00 +0000
Subject: Fwd: APAC hosted inference: model-variant partial degradation impacting Zendata — customer notice

Marissa / Dylan / Sean — forwarding Zendata's asks. TL;DR: they want request ids/log snippets and an ETA for full remediation. Immediate impact is limited but customer-visible.

Observed symptoms: elevated 502s, truncated outputs, token p95 up ~3x for variant v2. We switched Zendata to float16 fallback. Please coordinate SRE/serving runtime to: 1) collect sample request ids from 07:35–08:05 UTC; 2) capture kernel selection traces and VM memory fragmentation stats for the affected nodes; 3) confirm whether the 20261115 runtime patch (kernel-selection heuristics) was in the ap-southeast pool.

Attach any initial findings to this thread; I'll share with Asha as soon as we have concrete logs.

Thanks,
Kimberly

> On Mon, 16 Nov 2026 at 08:26, Asha Patel <asha@zendata.ai> wrote:
> > Thanks Kimberly — we saw errors starting around 07:42 UTC and had three internal alerts. Appreciate the fallback routing...

From: Dylan Brooks <dylan_brooks@redwood.ai>
To: Kimberly Park <kimberly_park@redwood.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.ai>, Sean Gallagher <sean_gallagher@redwood.ai>
Date: Mon, 16 Nov 2026 09:05:00 +0000
Subject: Re: Fwd: APAC hosted inference: model-variant partial degradation impacting Zendata — customer notice

Quick update from SRE triage:
- We saw increased memory fragmentation on several inference hosts pinned to the kernel-selection path introduced in 20261115-runtime. The pattern matches bursty short-session traffic that forces frequent realloc of KV caches.
- Kernel selector favored a quantized kernel variant (the v2 path) that increases ephemeral allocation under this workload; fallback float16 path uses a different allocator and did not show fragmentation.
- Collected sample request ids: req_20261116_073942_zd_1, req_20261116_073947_zd_2, req_20261116_074001_zd_3. I attached a small diff with the kernel-selection heuristics (kernel-selection-diff.patch.txt) that shows the recent predicate change.

Immediate actions taken:
- Rolled back the heuristic on a canary subset at 08:50 UTC.
- Drained and restarted 6 hosts in ap-southeast-1 that exhibited fragmentation.
- Increased the per-host KV cache cap temporarily to reduce churn.

ETA: We expect error rates to drop to baseline within 1–2 hours of the host restarts; a full remediation (stable patch + rollout) is targeted within 24 hours pending QA.

I'll push logs to the incident ticket (JIRA-INC-3456) and paste the key traces here when available.

—Dylan
Senior SRE, Redwood Inference

From: Kimberly Park <kimberly_park@redwood.ai>
To: Asha Patel <asha@zendata.ai>; ops@zendata.ai
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.ai>, Dylan Brooks <dylan_brooks@redwood.ai>, Sean Gallagher <sean_gallagher@redwood.ai>
Date: Tue, 17 Nov 2026 14:03:00 +0000
Subject: RE: APAC hosted inference: model-variant partial degradation impacting Zendata — resolved & follow-up items

Hi Asha — quick resolution summary and next steps.

Status: Resolved as of 13:10 UTC on 17 Nov 2026. Error rates returned to baseline and truncation reports have stopped. We kept Zendata on the fallback redwood-3b-float16 path temporarily until the patch was fully validated.

Root cause (preliminary, being finalized in postmortem):
- A recent runtime heuristic (rolled out 2026-11-15) changed kernel-selection predicates for quantized variants. Under bursty short-session traffic typical of real-time prefix use, that heuristic increased ephemeral allocations and accelerated memory fragmentation on a subset of ap-southeast-1 hosts. The fragmentation led to partial response generation and intermittent 502s when allocation attempts failed.

Actions completed:
- Heuristic rolled back to previous selection logic on 2026-11-17 07:20 UTC (canary then full).
- 6 affected hosts were drained and restarted; health checks and KV cache warmups verified.
- We updated the canary and rollout guard in the deployment pipeline to include a synthetic short-session churn test.

Planned follow-ups (postmortem items):
- Engineering: add a memory-fragmentation monitor and alerting rule tied to the serving pool (owner: Dylan).
- Runtime: revisit allocator policy for quantized kernels; add unit tests that simulate heavy short-session churn (owner: Marissa).
- Customer ops: provide Zendata with a targeted runbook to pin fallback variants during maintenance windows and an opt-in for aggressive pre-warming (owner: Kimberly).

We've attached an incident timeline and the minimal patch diff used for triage: incident_timeline_kp_20261116.pdf, kernel-selection-diff.patch.txt. The full postmortem will be published to the internal runbook and linked on the public status page once reviewed (link: https://status.redwood.ai/incidents/INC-20261116-zendata).

Asha — please let us know if you'd like a dedicated post-incident call; we can schedule within 48 hours. Apologies for the disruption and thanks for the rapid signal from your team.

Best,
Kimberly Park
Customer Success Manager, Redwood Inference
kimberly_park@redwood.ai

---
Redwood Inference — Operational notes attached.

Date: Mon, 16 Nov 2026 09:05:00 +0000
Subject: Re: Fwd: APAC hosted inference: model-variant partial degradation impacting Zendata — customer notice

Quick update from SRE triage:
- We saw increased memory fragmentation on several inference hosts pinned to the kernel-selection path introduced in 20261115-runtime. The pattern matches bursty short-session traffic that forces frequent realloc of KV caches.
- Kernel selector favored a quantized kernel variant (the v2 path) that increases ephemeral allocation under this workload; fallback float16 path uses a different allocator and did not show fragmentation.
- Collected sample request ids: req_20261116_073942_zd_1, req_20261116_073947_zd_2, req_20261116_074001_zd_3. I attached a small diff with the kernel-selection heuristics (kernel-selection-diff.patch.txt) that shows the recent predicate change.

Immediate actions taken:
- Rolled back the heuristic on a canary subset at 08:50 UTC.
- Drained and restarted 6 hosts in ap-southeast-1 that exhibited fragmentation.
- Increased the per-host KV cache cap temporarily to reduce churn.

ETA: We expect error rates to drop to baseline within 1–2 hours of the host restarts; a full remediation (stable patch + rollout) is targeted within 24 hours pending QA.

I'll push logs to the incident ticket (JIRA-INC-3456) and paste the key traces here when available.

—Dylan
Senior SRE, Redwood Inference



From: Kimberly Park <kimberly_park@redwood.ai>
To: Asha Patel <asha@zendata.ai>; ops@zendata.ai
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.ai>, Dylan Brooks <dylan_brooks@redwood.ai>, Sean Gallagher <sean_gallagher@redwood.ai>
Date: Tue, 17 Nov 2026 14:03:00 +0000
Subject: RE: APAC hosted inference: model-variant partial degradation impacting Zendata — resolved & follow-up items

Hi Asha — quick resolution summary and next steps.

Status: Resolved as of 13:10 UTC on 17 Nov 2026. Error rates returned to baseline and truncation reports have stopped. We kept Zendata on the fallback redwood-3b-float16 path temporarily until the patch was fully validated.

Root cause (preliminary, being finalized in postmortem):
- A recent runtime heuristic (rolled out 2026-11-15) changed kernel-selection predicates for quantized variants. Under bursty short-session traffic typical of real-time prefix use, that heuristic increased ephemeral allocations and accelerated memory fragmentation on a subset of ap-southeast-1 hosts. The fragmentation led to partial response generation and intermittent 502s when allocation attempts failed.

Actions completed:
- Heuristic rolled back to previous selection logic on 2026-11-17 07:20 UTC (canary then full).
- 6 affected hosts were drained and restarted; health checks and KV cache warmups verified.
- We updated the canary and rollout guard in the deployment pipeline to include a synthetic short-session churn test.

Planned follow-ups (postmortem items):
- Engineering: add a memory-fragmentation monitor and alerting rule tied to the serving pool (owner: Dylan).
- Runtime: revisit allocator policy for quantized kernels; add unit tests that simulate heavy short-session churn (owner: Marissa).
- Customer ops: provide Zendata with a targeted runbook to pin fallback variants during maintenance windows and an opt-in for aggressive pre-warming (owner: Kimberly).

We've attached an incident timeline and the minimal patch diff used for triage: incident_timeline_kp_20261116.pdf, kernel-selection-diff.patch.txt. The full postmortem will be published to the internal runbook and linked on the public status page once reviewed (link: https://status.redwood.ai/incidents/INC-20261116-zendata).

Asha — please let us know if you'd like a dedicated post-incident call; we can schedule within 48 hours. Apologies for the disruption and thanks for the rapid signal from your team.

Best,
Kimberly Park
Customer Success Manager, Redwood Inference
kimberly_park@redwood.ai

---
Redwood Inference — Operational notes attached.

source.subject: Novastat — request for quarterly remit schedule + extended payment window
messages:
From: Elena Morales <elena.morales@novastat.com>
To: Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwood.ai>
Cc: Novastat Procurement <procurement@novastat.com>
Date: Fri, 13 Nov 2026 09:12:00 -0800
Subject: Request: quarterly remit schedule + Net-90 for Redwood contract

Hi Sean,

Quick note from procurement as we're getting the legal/PO process lined up for the contract referenced in the attached SOW. Our finance team runs a quarterly remit schedule and we need invoices consolidated on a quarterly basis with a Net-90 payment window (their standard for contracted software).

This is driven by our fiscal calendar — payments are released on the first business day after quarter close. Can Redwood accommodate: 1) quarterly invoicing (single invoice for the quarter) and 2) Net-90 payment terms?

If needed we can provide the formal PO timeline and a redline of the standard terms. I'm attaching a draft PO timing spreadsheet that shows our payment cycles.

Thanks for checking — Elena

Attachment: Novastat_PO_timing.xlsx
From: Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwood.ai>
To: Elena Morales <elena.morales@novastat.com>
Cc: Priyom Das <priyom.das@redwood.ai>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.ai>
Date: Fri, 13 Nov 2026 09:45:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Request: quarterly remit schedule + Net-90 for Redwood contract

Elena — thanks for the clarity and the timing file.

We can explore this, but quarterly consolidated invoicing + Net-90 is outside our default payment cadence. I've looped in Priyom (AE) and Laura (Finance) to assess whether we can approve an exception and what guardrails we'd need. A couple quick questions so we bring an actionable response to procurement:

- Is the Net-90 applied from invoice date or from quarter-end?
- Do you require a single consolidated invoice for the, say, Oct-Dec quarter or multiple line-item invoices aggregated by you?
- Will Novastat be able to provide a standing PO that covers the quarter?

Priyom/Laura — can you review and advise on required controls (credit hold, initial prepay, carriage fee, or increased late fee) for a potential exception?

I copied the current SOW (v2) for reference.

Best,
Sean

Attachment: Novastat_SOW_v2.pdf
From: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.ai>
To: Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwood.ai>, Priyom Das <priyom.das@redwood.ai>, Jonas Weber <jonas.weber@redwood.ai>
Date: Sat, 14 Nov 2026 10:05:00 -0800
Subject: FW: Request: quarterly remit schedule + Net-90 for Redwood contract

Team —

I pulled Novastat's credit summary and commercial history; they look low risk but our standard is monthly invoices with Net-30. Quarterly + Net-90 materially increases DSO and exposure. If we consider an exception, recommend the following minimum controls:

- Conditional approval only (pilot for first 2 quarters)
- Initial invoice for first month collected up front or placed on a credit hold until PO is received
- Add a 2% financing surcharge or rate uplift to offset extended float (or a fixed platform fee)
- Require a signed amendment/DocuSign prior to going live
- Clear rollback clause: revert to monthly Net-30 if payment is >45 days late for any invoice

If we need an exec-level signoff for deviating from policy, please route to Markus for final approval. I attached the credit summary we ran today.

Laura
Finance Director, Redwood Inference
laura.bennett@redwood.ai | +1 415-555-0123

Attachment: Credit_Summary_Novastat.pdf
From: Markus Klein <markus.klein@redwood.ai>
To: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.ai>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwood.ai>, Priyom Das <priyom.das@redwood.ai>, Jonas Weber <jonas.weber@redwood.ai>
Date: Sun, 15 Nov 2026 16:22:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Request: quarterly remit schedule + Net-90 for Redwood contract

Thanks team — I reviewed the request and Laura's recommended controls. I'm willing to grant an exception for Novastat on the following conditions:

1) Pilot period: two fiscal quarters (Q4 2026 and Q1 2027).
2) Novastat signs an amendment (DocuSign) that documents the quarterly invoicing cadence and Net-90 timing (invoice date = quarter close date).
3) Redwood will collect the first quarter's invoice up front (prepay) or hold services pending the PO for the first billing cycle.
4) Commercial uplift: add a 1.75% financing surcharge to invoices during the pilot.
5) If any invoice is >45 days past due, we immediately revert to monthly Net-30 and reserve right to apply late fees.

If legal can draft a short amendment reflecting the above, Laura you have my signoff to proceed. Priyom/Sean please confirm with Novastat and request a PO and DocuSign signature.

-Markus
VP Sales, Redwood Inference
markus.klein@redwood.ai
From: Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwood.ai>
To: Elena Morales <elena.morales@novastat.com>
Cc: Priyom Das <priyom.das@redwood.ai>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.ai>, Jonas Weber <jonas.weber@redwood.ai>, Markus Klein <markus.klein@redwood.ai>
Date: Mon, 16 Nov 2026 08:10:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Request: quarterly remit schedule + Net-90 for Redwood contract

Elena — good news: we can accommodate Novastat's request on a conditional basis. Summary of what Markus (VP Sales) approved:

- Pilot: two quarters (Q4 2026, Q1 2027)
- Quarterly consolidated invoices; Net-90 measured from quarter close (invoice date = quarter end)
- First-quarter invoice must be pre-paid or we will place services on hold until the PO is received
- 1.75% financing surcharge applied during the pilot period
- Reversion clause: any invoice >45 days past due triggers immediate return to monthly Net-30 and potential late fees

Next steps:
1) Priyom will send the amendment and DocuSign link (I copied the DocuSign reference below).
2) Please provide the standing PO that covers Q4 2026 so we can process the prepay and finalize the start date.

DocuSign (preliminary): https://docusign.com/rc/12345-abcdef
HubSpot deal: https://app.hubspot.com/deals/8421

Elena — can you confirm you have procurement sign the amendment and share the PO timeline by EOD Wednesday? We'll standby to process the prepay and schedule the kickoff.

Regards,
Sean Gallagher
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
sean.gallagher@redwood.ai | +1 415-555-0199

Attachment: DocuSign_Novastat_Agreement.pdf

Quick note from procurement as we're getting the legal/PO process lined up for the contract referenced in the attached SOW. Our finance team runs a quarterly remit schedule and we need invoices consolidated on a quarterly basis with a Net-90 payment window (their standard for contracted software).

This is driven by our fiscal calendar — payments are released on the first business day after quarter close. Can Redwood accommodate: 1) quarterly invoicing (single invoice for the quarter) and 2) Net-90 payment terms?

If needed we can provide the formal PO timeline and a redline of the standard terms. I'm attaching a draft PO timing spreadsheet that shows our payment cycles.

Thanks for checking — Elena

Attachment: Novastat_PO_timing.xlsx


From: Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwood.ai>
To: Elena Morales <elena.morales@novastat.com>
Cc: Priyom Das <priyom.das@redwood.ai>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.ai>
Date: Fri, 13 Nov 2026 09:45:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Request: quarterly remit schedule + Net-90 for Redwood contract

Elena — thanks for the clarity and the timing file.

We can explore this, but quarterly consolidated invoicing + Net-90 is outside our default payment cadence. I've looped in Priyom (AE) and Laura (Finance) to assess whether we can approve an exception and what guardrails we'd need. A couple quick questions so we bring an actionable response to procurement:

- Is the Net-90 applied from invoice date or from quarter-end?
- Do you require a single consolidated invoice for the, say, Oct-Dec quarter or multiple line-item invoices aggregated by you?
- Will Novastat be able to provide a standing PO that covers the quarter?

Priyom/Laura — can you review and advise on required controls (credit hold, initial prepay, carriage fee, or increased late fee) for a potential exception?

I copied the current SOW (v2) for reference.

Best,
Sean

Attachment: Novastat_SOW_v2.pdf


From: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.ai>
To: Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwood.ai>, Priyom Das <priyom.das@redwood.ai>, Jonas Weber <jonas.weber@redwood.ai>
Date: Sat, 14 Nov 2026 10:05:00 -0800
Subject: FW: Request: quarterly remit schedule + Net-90 for Redwood contract

Team —

I pulled Novastat's credit summary and commercial history; they look low risk but our standard is monthly invoices with Net-30. Quarterly + Net-90 materially increases DSO and exposure. If we consider an exception, recommend the following minimum controls:

- Conditional approval only (pilot for first 2 quarters)
- Initial invoice for first month collected up front or placed on a credit hold until PO is received
- Add a 2% financing surcharge or rate uplift to offset extended float (or a fixed platform fee)
- Require a signed amendment/DocuSign prior to going live
- Clear rollback clause: revert to monthly Net-30 if payment is >45 days late for any invoice

If we need an exec-level signoff for deviating from policy, please route to Markus for final approval. I attached the credit summary we ran today.

Laura
Finance Director, Redwood Inference
laura.bennett@redwood.ai | +1 415-555-0123

Attachment: Credit_Summary_Novastat.pdf


From: Markus Klein <markus.klein@redwood.ai>
To: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.ai>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwood.ai>, Priyom Das <priyom.das@redwood.ai>, Jonas Weber <jonas.weber@redwood.ai>
Date: Sun, 15 Nov 2026 16:22:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Request: quarterly remit schedule + Net-90 for Redwood contract

Thanks team — I reviewed the request and Laura's recommended controls. I'm willing to grant an exception for Novastat on the following conditions:

1) Pilot period: two fiscal quarters (Q4 2026 and Q1 2027).
2) Novastat signs an amendment (DocuSign) that documents the quarterly invoicing cadence and Net-90 timing (invoice date = quarter close date).
3) Redwood will collect the first quarter's invoice up front (prepay) or hold services pending the PO for the first billing cycle.
4) Commercial uplift: add a 1.75% financing surcharge to invoices during the pilot.
5) If any invoice is >45 days past due, we immediately revert to monthly Net-30 and reserve right to apply late fees.

If legal can draft a short amendment reflecting the above, Laura you have my signoff to proceed. Priyom/Sean please confirm with Novastat and request a PO and DocuSign signature.

-Markus
VP Sales, Redwood Inference
markus.klein@redwood.ai


From: Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwood.ai>
To: Elena Morales <elena.morales@novastat.com>
Cc: Priyom Das <priyom.das@redwood.ai>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.ai>, Jonas Weber <jonas.weber@redwood.ai>, Markus Klein <markus.klein@redwood.ai>
Date: Mon, 16 Nov 2026 08:10:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Request: quarterly remit schedule + Net-90 for Redwood contract

Elena — good news: we can accommodate Novastat's request on a conditional basis. Summary of what Markus (VP Sales) approved:

- Pilot: two quarters (Q4 2026, Q1 2027)
- Quarterly consolidated invoices; Net-90 measured from quarter close (invoice date = quarter end)
- First-quarter invoice must be pre-paid or we will place services on hold until the PO is received
- 1.75% financing surcharge applied during the pilot period
- Reversion clause: any invoice >45 days past due triggers immediate return to monthly Net-30 and potential late fees

Next steps:
1) Priyom will send the amendment and DocuSign link (I copied the DocuSign reference below).
2) Please provide the standing PO that covers Q4 2026 so we can process the prepay and finalize the start date.

DocuSign (preliminary): https://docusign.com/rc/12345-abcdef
HubSpot deal: https://app.hubspot.com/deals/8421

Elena — can you confirm you have procurement sign the amendment and share the PO timeline by EOD Wednesday? We'll standby to process the prepay and schedule the kickoff.

Regards,
Sean Gallagher
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
sean.gallagher@redwood.ai | +1 415-555-0199

Attachment: DocuSign_Novastat_Agreement.pdf

source.subject: Private hosting egress topology & keystore chain for EU pilot
messages:
From: Liam O'Connor <liam.oconnor@aurorahealth.com>
To: David Kim <david.kim@redwood.ai>, Irene Choi <irene.choi@redwood.ai>, Grace O'Connor <grace.oconnor@redwood.ai>
Date: Mon, 16 Nov 2026 08:12:00 +0100
Subject: Request: private hosting egress topology & keystore chain for EU pilot

Hi David / Irene / Grace,

We're getting our security intake ready for the EU pilot and need a concise package from Redwood to confirm hosting boundaries and key management practices before we sign the pilot SOW. Specifically can you provide the following by EOD Tuesday: 

- VPC egress topology diagram showing all egress paths (including peering, NAT, and any cloud provider control planes) with clear labels that traffic remains within EU regions.
- Keystore/operator chain: list of operator roles that can access customer keystores, their regions, and whether keys are customer-managed (KMS/HSM) or provider-managed.
- A short note on fallback behavior: if an EU node is unavailable, do any failover routes route to non-EU regions? If so, how is customer consent obtained/recorded?
- CAIQ / SIG answers relevant to data residency and crypto key separation (a simple XLS or PDF).

We will treat attachments as evidence in our SIG assessment. If there's anything that is sensitive, let us know and we can accept redacted artifacts via a secure transfer.

Thanks — appreciate a one-page summary + attachments.

Liam
Liam O'Connor
Security Engineer, Aurora Health
liam.oconnor@aurorahealth.com

From: David Kim <david.kim@redwood.ai>
To: Liam O'Connor <liam.oconnor@aurorahealth.com>
Cc: Irene Choi <irene.choi@redwood.ai>, Grace O'Connor <grace.oconnor@redwood.ai>
Date: Mon, 16 Nov 2026 09:05:00 +0100
Subject: Re: Request: private hosting egress topology & keystore chain for EU pilot

Thanks Liam — we can do that. Looping in Sean (ops) and Irene (security) to assemble the package.

Action items for this thread:
- Sean: produce the VPC egress topology diagram (annotated), include peering endpoints and any control-plane calls that cross regions.
- Irene: prepare keystore/operator manifest and CAIQ/SIG extracts relevant to residency + key separation.
- Grace: include product note on rolling-failover behavior and pin the default EU-only routing policy for the pilot.

Deliverables we'll provide: one-page summary PDF, annotated egress diagram (PDF), keystore operator CSV, SOC2 exhibit A excerpt (PDF), CAIQ responses (XLSX). If any item is sensitive, we'll flag and send via secure link.

David Kim
Director, Customer Solutions — Redwood Inference

From: Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwood.ai>
To: Liam O'Connor <liam.oconnor@aurorahealth.com>
Cc: David Kim <david.kim@redwood.ai>, Irene Choi <irene.choi@redwood.ai>, Grace O'Connor <grace.oconnor@redwood.ai>
Date: Mon, 16 Nov 2026 13:37:00 +0100
Subject: Re: Request: private hosting egress topology & keystore chain for EU pilot

Hi Liam — attaching the initial artifacts for your intake. See brief notes inline.

Attachments:
- aurora_vpc_map.pdf (annotated egress topology; all VPCs and peering endpoints are EU-only for the pilot; control-plane telemetry to provider control planes is to eu-central-1 only)
- keystore-operator-list.csv (operator role, region, access method, approval channel — note we support customer-managed KMS; for this pilot we marked keys as customer KMS)
- SOC2-exhibit-A.pdf (excerpt: relevant controls for logical access and key handling)
- CAIQ-responses.xlsx (tabbed answers for data residency and crypto controls)

Key clarifications:
- Failover behavior: by default our routing policy for pinned pilot tenants is EU-preferred with a strict fallback = none. In cases where customer opts into cross-region failover we require explicit written consent and a change record; that policy is visible in the tenant's deployment profile.
- Keystores: For Aurora pilot we've scoped keys to customer-managed KMS in the customer's EU cloud account (customer controlled keys). Redwood can provide HSM-backed key wrapping in-engine if requested; that would be a separate runbook and access manifest.

If you want the topology in a Visio/Cloud-Formation diagram, I can push that as a follow-up.

—Sean
Sean Gallagher
Infrastructure Operations, Redwood Inference
(Attachment stubs included; full pack uploaded to secure transfer link on request)

From: Liam O'Connor <liam.oconnor@aurorahealth.com>
To: Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwood.ai>, David Kim <david.kim@redwood.ai>
Cc: Irene Choi <irene.choi@redwood.ai>, Grace O'Connor <grace.oconnor@redwood.ai>, Aurora Health Security <security@aurorahealth.com>
Date: Mon, 16 Nov 2026 16:45:00 +0100
Subject: Re: Request: private hosting egress topology & keystore chain for EU pilot

Sean / David — received, thank you. Quick clarifying questions so our GRC team can triage: 

1) The keystore-operator CSV shows "Key Admins — Redwood SRE EU Ops" with a service account. Can you confirm whether operator access requires MFA and whether session recordings are retained and where (audit log location)?
2) If we move to HSM-backed wrapping later, will operator access lists change (do you segment HSM ops from runtime SRE)?
3) Your note about control-plane telemetry to eu-central-1 is helpful — can you confirm there are no auxiliary telemetry calls (metrics/phone-home) to non-EU telemetry endpoints (we require that for our data processing agreement).

Also attaching our signed non-prod access form (signed-nonprod-access-form.pdf) — small administrative step so the pilot team can accept the pack. We'll review the artifacts and aim to come back with final SIG pass/fail by Wednesday COB.

Appreciate the quick turnaround.

Liam
Liam O'Connor
Security Engineer, Aurora Health
(Attachment: signed-nonprod-access-form.pdf)

source.subject: Customer performance incident — logs & data handling for executive briefing
messages:
From: Rajiv Menon <rajiv@finservco.com>
To: Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwood.com>, Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-11-10T09:14:00-08:00
Subject: Spike in latency and 500s this morning — urgent

Evelyn / Monica,

We saw a significant latency spike and elevated 500 errors across our production traffic between 09:05–09:52 PST this morning. Error rate peaked ~7% for a 20-minute window; median latency increased ~4x. Our Ops team is asking for:

- Immediate export of request/response logs for 09:00–10:00 PST (all regions handling finservco.com traffic)
- Proof of preservation (who pulled logs, hash manifest, storage location)
- Confirmation whether any PII or account tokens were present in logs and how that data is handled/retained

This incident is currently elevated to our CTO/CSO; they want an exec-level brief and any SOC2/audit evidence we can provide. Please advise on the fastest path to get raw artifacts and a short timeline.

Thanks,
Rajiv
Rajiv Menon
Head of Platform Ops, FinServCo
ops@finservco.com

---
Attachment: sample-error-snippet.txt (text)

From: Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwood.com>
To: Rajiv Menon <rajiv@finservco.com>
Cc: Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.com>, David Kim <david.kim@redwood.com>, Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-11-10T09:28:00-08:00
Subject: Re: Spike in latency and 500s this morning — urgent

Rajiv — thank you for the heads up and apologies for the disruption. We are treating this as highest priority. Quick answers up front:

- We will preserve the full request/response logs and KV cache state for the 09:00–10:00 PST window immediately.
- SRE will generate a SHA256 manifest for exported archives and upload to a restricted Drive folder (read-only to your ops contacts + our incident response).
- We will do a PII scan on the exported logs and redact any low-level tokens before wider distribution; raw artifacts can be made available under an evidence transfer agreement.

Internal: Monica / David / Rishi — please follow the preservation checklist in the internal runbook, export the logs, produce manifest, and confirm where the artifacts land. Monica, please coordinate the exec slides I will draft for the briefing with Rajiv.

Rajiv — timeline: initial export + manifest within 2 hours; PII scan + preliminary analysis within 6 hours. I’ll keep you posted every hour.

Evelyn
Evelyn Hart
Head of Customer Reliability, Redwood Inference
---
Attachment: incident-preservation-checklist.pdf (pdf)

From: Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.com>
To: Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwood.com>, David Kim <david.kim@redwood.com>, Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwood.com>
Cc: Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-11-10T09:46:00-08:00
Subject: Preserve: export job + manifest (INF-11872)

Team — taking point on the export. Actions underway:

1) Kicking an S3 export job for the production ingestion layer for FinServCo (09:00–10:00 PST).
   - Buckets: logs-prod-1 (region us-west-2) and logs-shared-edge
2) Will produce compressed archive: request-log-export-20261110.tar.gz
3) Will generate s3_log_manifest_20261111.txt containing object keys and SHA256 hashes; will submit manifest to Drive folder and to JIRA INF-11872.
4) Running automated PII detector on the raw logs; Qs: Legal — do we provide raw files to customer CSOs or only redacted derivative?

Checklist (short):
- [x] Export job started (09:40 PST)
- [x] Temporary export bucket created with access restricted to incident SRE group
- [ ] Manifest generation (ETA 10:05 PST)
- [ ] Upload to Drive (ETA 10:15 PST)

Notes: For the exec briefing, David please prepare a short one-slide summary of the root-cause timeline and Rishi to provide the security controls/retention bullets.

Monica
Monica Patel
Sr. Customer Solutions Manager, Redwood Inference
---
Attachment: s3_export_request.json (json)

From: David Kim <david.kim@redwood.com>
To: Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwood.com>
Cc: Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.com>, Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwood.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-11-10T10:12:00-08:00
Subject: Fwd: Preserve: export job + manifest — I/O and retention notes

Forwarding internal notes. I pulled the first archive and started verification. Short status:

- request-log-export-20261110.tar.gz created and available in internal incident bucket (s3://incident-exports/fs-3241/).
- Manifest file s3_log_manifest_20261111.txt includes 124k object keys; verifying hashes now.
- Preliminary scan: ~0.8% of entries contain potential account-identifying tokens (session_id, user_id). No full credit-card PANs detected in this window.

Immediate ask for legal/security: confirm whether we can provide raw archive to FinServCo under an evidence transfer (SLA) or if we need to pre-redact. My recommendation: deliver manifest + redacted derivative to FinServCo and make raw artifacts available to their security team via secure transfer with audit logging.

I'll upload verification artifacts (sha256 sums, export job logs) to Drive once hash check completes. ETA for full verification: 2 hours.

-David
David Kim
Principal SRE, Redwood Inference
---
Attachment: s3_log_manifest_20261111.txt (text)

From: Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwood.com>
To: Rajiv Menon <rajiv@finservco.com>
Cc: Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwood.com>, Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-11-10T12:20:00-08:00

title: Customer performance incident — logs & data handling for executive briefing
preview:
From: Rajiv Menon <rajiv@finservco.com>
To: Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwood.com>, Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-11-10T09:14:00-08:00
Subject: Spike in latency and 500s this morning — urgent

Evelyn / Monica,

We saw a significant latency spike and elevated 500 errors across our production traffic between 09:05–09:52 PST this morning. Error rate peaked ~7% for a 20-minute window; median latency increased ~4x. Our Ops team is asking for:

- Immediate export of request/response logs for 09:00–10:00 PST (all regions handling finservco.com traffic)
- Proof of preservation (who pulled logs, hash manifest, storage location)
- Confirmation whether any PII or account tokens were present in logs and how that data is handled/retained

This incident is currently elevated to our CTO/CSO; they want an exec-level brief and any SOC2/audit evidence we can provide. Please advise on the fastest path to get raw artifacts and a short timeline.

Thanks,
Rajiv
Rajiv Menon
Head of Platform Ops, FinServCo
ops@finservco.com

---
Attachment: sample-error-snippet.txt (text)


From: Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwood.com>
To: Rajiv Menon <rajiv@finservco.com>
Cc: Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.com>, David Kim <david.kim@redwood.com>, Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwood.com>

Subject: Re: Spike in latency and 500s this morning — urgent

- We will preserve the full request/response logs and KV cache state for the 09:00–10:00 PST window immediately.
- SRE will generate a SHA256 manifest for exported archives and upload to a restricted Drive folder (read-only to your ops contacts + our incident response).
- We will do a PII scan on the exported logs and redact any low-level tokens before wider distribution; raw artifacts can be made available under an evidence transfer agreement.

Internal: Monica / David / Rishi — please follow the preservation checklist in the internal runbook, export the logs, produce manifest, and confirm where the artifacts land. Monica, please coordinate the exec slides I will draft for the briefing with Rajiv.

Rajiv — timeline: initial export + manifest within 2 hours; PII scan + preliminary analysis within 6 hours. I’ll keep you posted every hour.

Evelyn
Evelyn Hart
Head of Customer Reliability, Redwood Inference
---
Attachment: incident-preservation-checklist.pdf (pdf)

From: Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.com>
To: Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwood.com>, David Kim <david.kim@redwood.com>, Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwood.com>
Cc: Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-11-10T09:46:00-08:00
Subject: Preserve: export job + manifest (INF-11872)

Team — taking point on the export. Actions underway:

4) Running automated PII detector on the raw logs; Qs: Legal — do we provide raw files to customer CSOs or only redacted derivative?

Checklist (short):
- [x] Export job started (09:40 PST)
- [x] Temporary export bucket created with access restricted to incident SRE group
- [ ] Manifest generation (ETA 10:05 PST)
- [ ] Upload to Drive (ETA 10:15 PST)

Notes: For the exec briefing, David please prepare a short one-slide summary of the root-cause timeline and Rishi to provide the security controls/retention bullets.

Monica
Monica Patel
Sr. Customer Solutions Manager, Redwood Inference
---
Attachment: s3_export_request.json (json)


From: David Kim <david.kim@redwood.com>
To: Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwood.com>
